Automated monitoring setup monitoring_automation = { 'daily_check': { 'script': '~/.ssh/monitor_key_usage.sh', 'frequency': 'Daily via cron', 'log_file': '~/.ssh/key_usage.log', 'alerts': 'Email on unusual access patterns' }, 'weekly_audit': { 'key_permissions': 'Verify file permissions unchanged', 'backup_integrity': 'Test GPG backup decryption', 'access_log_review': 'Review key usage patterns', 'compliance_check': 'Validate security requirements' }, 'monthly_reporting': { 'usage_statistics': 'Generate key usage reports', 'security_assessment': 'Review access patterns', 'backup_verification': 'Verify backup availability', 'rotation_planning': 'Plan upcoming rotations' } } print(f"Automated Monitoring Setup:") for monitoring_type, config in monitoring_automation.items(): print(f" {monitoring_type.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for aspect, details in config.items(): print(f" {aspect.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {details}") print(f"") # Django production security integration django_security_integration = { 'deployment_pipeline': { 'key_validation': 'Verify key integrity before deployment', 'access_logging': 'Log all deployment SSH sessions', 'approval_workflow': 'Require security team approval', 'rollback_capability': 'Emergency key rotation procedure' }, 'operational_security': { 'session_monitoring': 'Real-time SSH session monitoring', 'command_logging': 'Log all commands executed', 'file_transfer_audit': 'Monitor file uploads/downloads', 'privilege_escalation': 'Alert on sudo usage' }, 'incident_response': { 'key_compromise': 'Immediate revocation procedure', 'forensic_analysis': 'Preserve access logs for investigation', 'communication_plan': 'Notify stakeholders within 1 hour', 'recovery_procedure': 'Emergency access restoration' } } print(f"Django Production Security Integration:") for integration_area, procedures in django_security_integration.items(): print(f" {integration_area.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for procedure, description in procedures.items(): print(f" {procedure.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") print(f"") # Compliance framework alignment compliance_alignment = { 'soc2_type2': { 'control_cc6_1': 'Logical access controls implemented', 'control_cc6_2': 'Authentication mechanisms secured', 'control_cc6_3': 'Access rights management', 'evidence': 'Key creation logs, access monitoring' }, 'iso27001': { 'control_a9_2_3': 'Management of privileged access rights', 'control_a9_4_2': 'Secure log-on procedures', 'control_a10_1_1': 'Cryptographic controls policy', 'evidence': 'Key management procedures, monitoring logs' }, 'pci_dss': { 'requirement_7': 'Restrict access by business need-to-know', 'requirement_8': 'Identify and authenticate access', 'requirement_10': 'Track and monitor access', 'evidence': 'Access logs, key rotation records' } } print(f"Compliance Framework Alignment:") for framework, controls in compliance_alignment.items(): print(f" {framework.upper()}:") for control, description in controls.items(): print(f" {control}: {description}") print(f"") # Backup and recovery procedures backup_recovery = { 'backup_frequency': 'Immediate (upon key creation)', 'backup_locations': [ 'Local encrypted file (~/.ssh/backups/)', 'Company password manager vault', 'Secure offline storage (encrypted USB)' ], 'recovery_testing': 'Monthly backup restoration test', 'recovery_time': '< 15 minutes', 'recovery_personnel': 'DevOps lead + Security team' } print(f"Backup and Recovery Procedures:") print(f" Frequency: {backup_recovery['backup_frequency']}") print(f" Locations:") for location in backup_recovery['backup_locations']: print(f" - {location}") print(f" Testing: {backup_recovery['recovery_testing']}") print(f" RTO: {backup_recovery['recovery_time']}") print(f" Personnel: {backup_recovery['recovery_personnel']}") print(f"\n✓ Secure key pair created with comprehensive security") print(f"✓ Automated monitoring and backup configured")

import-key-pair

Import an existing public key to AWS EC2 to create a key pair. This allows you to use your own SSH keys instead of AWS-generated keys. Useful for maintaining consistent keys across environments or bringing existing organizational keys to AWS.

Syntax
aws ec2 import-key-pair \
  --key-name  \
  --public-key-material  \
  [--tag-specifications 'ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=}]'] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --key-name (string): Name for the imported key pair. Must be unique within the region
  • --public-key-material (string): The public key material. Can be base64-encoded or use fileb:// for file input. Supports RSA, ED25519, ECDSA key types
  • --tag-specifications (string): Tags to apply to the imported key pair. Format: ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[{Key=Environment,Value=Production}]
  • --region (string): AWS region where the key pair will be imported
Return Value
  • KeyName (String): Name of the imported key pair
  • KeyFingerprint (String): SHA-1 or SHA-256 digest of the public key (depends on key type)
  • KeyPairId (String): Unique identifier for the key pair
  • Tags (Array): Tags applied to the key pair
Example 1: Import Existing Organizational SSH Key

Import an existing organizational SSH key that's already used across multiple environments for consistency.

#!/bin/bash
# Import existing organizational SSH key to AWS
KEY_NAME="anihpj-org-standard-key"
REGION="us-east-1"
PUBLIC_KEY_FILE="~/.ssh/company_rsa.pub"

echo "Importing organizational SSH key to AWS..."
echo "Key name: $KEY_NAME"
echo "Public key file: $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE"
echo ""

# Verify public key file exists
if [ ! -f "$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Public key file not found: $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify public key format
echo "Validating public key format..."
ssh-keygen -l -f $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE
echo ""

# Import the key pair using fileb:// for binary file input
aws ec2 import-key-pair \
  --key-name $KEY_NAME \
  --public-key-material fileb://$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$KEY_NAME},
    {Key=Environment,Value=Production},
    {Key=ManagedBy,Value=IT-Security},
    {Key=KeyType,Value=Organizational-Standard},
    {Key=ImportDate,Value=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION

echo ""
echo "✓ Key pair imported successfully"
echo ""

# Verify the import
echo "Verifying imported key..."
aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --key-names $KEY_NAME \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[0].[KeyName,KeyFingerprint,KeyPairId]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Key import details:"
echo "  - Original key location: $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE"
echo "  - AWS key name: $KEY_NAME"
echo "  - Private key remains on local machine"
echo "  - Use existing private key for SSH access"
echo ""

echo "Usage with existing private key:"
PRIVATE_KEY_FILE="${PUBLIC_KEY_FILE%.pub}"
echo "  ssh -i $PRIVATE_KEY_FILE ec2-user@"
Importing organizational SSH key to AWS... Key name: anihpj-org-standard-key Public key file: ~/.ssh/company_rsa.pub Validating public key format... 2048 SHA256:kX9Z3L5mN8pQ2rT4vW6xY0zA1bC3dE5fG7hJ9kL1mN3 user@company.com (RSA) { "KeyName": "anihpj-org-standard-key", "KeyFingerprint": "1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f", "KeyPairId": "key-0987654321fedcba0", "Tags": [ { "Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production" }, { "Key": "ManagedBy", "Value": "IT-Security" }, { "Key": "KeyType", "Value": "Organizational-Standard" } ] } ✓ Key pair imported successfully Verifying imported key... ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeKeyPairs | +--------------------------+------------------------------------+------+ |anihpj-org-standard-key |1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37|key-09| +--------------------------+------------------------------------+------+ Key import details: - Original key location: ~/.ssh/company_rsa.pub - AWS key name: anihpj-org-standard-key - Private key remains on local machine - Use existing private key for SSH access Usage with existing private key: ssh -i ~/.ssh/company_rsa ec2-user@
# Import existing organizational SSH key from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import os organizational_key = { 'key_name': 'anihpj-org-standard-key', 'key_fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f', 'key_pair_id': 'key-0987654321fedcba0', 'key_type': 'RSA 2048-bit', 'managed_by': 'IT-Security', 'public_key_file': os.path.expanduser('~/.ssh/company_rsa.pub'), 'private_key_file': os.path.expanduser('~/.ssh/company_rsa') } print(f"Organizational SSH Key Import") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"") print(f"Key Name: {organizational_key['key_name']}") print(f"Key Fingerprint: {organizational_key['key_fingerprint']}") print(f"Key Pair ID: {organizational_key['key_pair_id']}") print(f"Key Type: {organizational_key['key_type']}") print(f"Managed By: {organizational_key['managed_by']}") print(f"") # Benefits of importing existing keys import_benefits = { 'consistency': 'Use same keys across AWS, on-premises, and other clouds', 'compliance': 'Meet organizational key management policies', 'existing_infrastructure': 'No need to redistribute new keys to team', 'key_rotation': 'Centralized key rotation process', 'audit_trail': 'Maintain existing key usage logs', 'emergency_access': 'Backup keys already distributed to team' } print(f"Benefits of Importing Existing Keys:") for benefit, description in import_benefits.items(): print(f" {benefit.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") print(f"") # Multi-environment key strategy multi_environment = { 'production': { 'key_source': 'Company-issued production key', 'access_control': 'Limited to DevOps team', 'rotation_frequency': 'Every 90 days', 'aws_regions': ['us-east-1', 'us-west-2', 'eu-west-1'] }, 'staging': { 'key_source': 'Company-issued staging key', 'access_control': 'DevOps + Senior developers', 'rotation_frequency': 'Every 120 days', 'aws_regions': ['us-east-1'] }, 'development': { 'key_source': 'Team-shared development key', 'access_control': 'All developers', 'rotation_frequency': 'Every 180 days', 'aws_regions': ['us-east-1'] } } print(f"Multi-Environment Key Strategy:") for env, config in multi_environment.items(): print(f" {env.upper()}:") for setting, value in config.items(): if isinstance(value, list): print(f" {setting.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {', '.join(value)}") else: print(f" {setting.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"") # Django deployment workflow with imported keys django_workflow = { 'local_development': { 'step_1': 'Use company-issued SSH key for local Git', 'step_2': 'Same key works for AWS EC2 instances', 'step_3': 'No need to manage multiple keys', 'benefit': 'Simplified workflow' }, 'ci_cd_pipeline': { 'step_1': 'CI/CD server has company SSH key', 'step_2': 'Import same key to AWS', 'step_3': 'Pipeline deploys to EC2 seamlessly', 'benefit': 'No pipeline configuration changes' }, 'disaster_recovery': { 'step_1': 'Team already has backup keys', 'step_2': 'Import to DR region instantly', 'step_3': 'Immediate access to recovery instances', 'benefit': 'Faster recovery time' } } print(f"Django Deployment Workflow:") for workflow, steps in django_workflow.items(): print(f" {workflow.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for step, description in steps.items(): print(f" {step.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") print(f"") # Compliance requirements compliance_requirements = { 'key_generation': 'Keys generated on HSM (Hardware Security Module)', 'key_storage': 'Private keys stored in encrypted vaults', 'key_distribution': 'Secure distribution via key management system', 'key_usage_logging': 'All SSH sessions logged centrally', 'key_revocation': 'Centralized revocation process', 'audit_trail': 'Complete lifecycle audit trail' } print(f"Compliance Requirements:") for requirement, description in compliance_requirements.items(): print(f" {requirement.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") # Import validation checklist validation_checklist = { 'public_key_format': '✓ Valid OpenSSH or RFC4716 format', 'key_strength': '✓ Minimum 2048-bit RSA', 'fingerprint_match': '✓ AWS fingerprint matches local', 'private_key_secure': '✓ Private key remains on local machine', 'permissions_correct': '✓ Private key chmod 400', 'aws_regions_synced': '✓ Imported to all required regions' } print(f"\nImport Validation Checklist:") for check, status in validation_checklist.items(): print(f" {check.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {status}") print(f"\n✓ Organizational key imported successfully") print(f"✓ Ready for Django server access across environments")
Example 2: Import Team Member Keys for Shared Access

Import multiple team member public keys for collaborative Django server access.

#!/bin/bash
# Import team member SSH keys for shared access
REGION="us-east-1"
TEAM_KEYS_DIR="~/team-keys"

declare -A team_members=(
    ["john-smith"]="DevOps-Lead"
    ["jane-doe"]="Senior-Developer"
    ["bob-johnson"]="Security-Engineer"
)

echo "Importing team member SSH keys..."
echo ""

for member in "${!team_members[@]}"; do
    role="${team_members[$member]}"
    key_name="anihpj-team-${member}"
    public_key_file="$TEAM_KEYS_DIR/${member}.pub"
    
    echo "Processing: $member ($role)"
    echo "  Key file: $public_key_file"
    
    if [ ! -f "$public_key_file" ]; then
        echo "  ⚠️  Warning: Key file not found, skipping"
        echo ""
        continue
    fi
    
    # Import team member's key
    aws ec2 import-key-pair \
        --key-name $key_name \
        --public-key-material fileb://$public_key_file \
        --tag-specifications "ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[
            {Key=Name,Value=$key_name},
            {Key=TeamMember,Value=$member},
            {Key=Role,Value=$role},
            {Key=Environment,Value=Production}
        ]" \
        --region $REGION \
        --output json > /dev/null
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "  ✓ Imported successfully"
    else
        echo "  ✗ Import failed"
    fi
    echo ""
done

echo "Team keys import complete"
echo ""

# List all imported team keys
echo "Imported team keys:"
aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=anihpj-team-*" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[*].[KeyName,Tags[?Key==`Role`].Value|[0]]' \
  --output table
Importing team member SSH keys... Processing: john-smith (DevOps-Lead) Key file: ~/team-keys/john-smith.pub ✓ Imported successfully Processing: jane-doe (Senior-Developer) Key file: ~/team-keys/jane-doe.pub ✓ Imported successfully Processing: bob-johnson (Security-Engineer) Key file: ~/team-keys/bob-johnson.pub ✓ Imported successfully Team keys import complete Imported team keys: ------------------------------------------------------------ | DescribeKeyPairs | +---------------------------+------------------------------+ | anihpj-team-bob-johnson | Security-Engineer | | anihpj-team-jane-doe | Senior-Developer | | anihpj-team-john-smith | DevOps-Lead | +---------------------------+------------------------------+
# Import team member keys for shared access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone team_keys_imported = { 'john_smith': { 'key_name': 'anihpj-team-john-smith', 'role': 'DevOps-Lead', 'access_level': 'Full administrative access', 'responsibilities': ['Infrastructure', 'Deployment', 'Monitoring'] }, 'jane_doe': { 'key_name': 'anihpj-team-jane-doe', 'role': 'Senior-Developer', 'access_level': 'Staging and development access', 'responsibilities': ['Code deployment', 'Debug production issues', 'Database migrations'] }, 'bob_johnson': { 'key_name': 'anihpj-team-bob-johnson', 'role': 'Security-Engineer', 'access_level': 'Security audit and compliance', 'responsibilities': ['Security scanning', 'Compliance audits', 'Incident response'] } } print(f"Team Member SSH Keys Imported") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"") for member, details in team_keys_imported.items(): print(f"{member.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") print(f" Key Name: {details['key_name']}") print(f" Role: {details['role']}") print(f" Access Level: {details['access_level']}") print(f" Responsibilities: {', '.join(details['responsibilities'])}") print(f"") # Individual access patterns access_patterns = { 'john_smith': { 'production_servers': 'Full SSH access', 'database_servers': 'Direct access via Systems Manager', 'bastion_host': 'Direct access from office IP', 'sudo_privileges': 'Full sudo access' }, 'jane_doe': { 'production_servers': 'Read-only access (emergency only)', 'staging_servers': 'Full SSH access', 'development_servers': 'Full SSH access', 'sudo_privileges': 'Limited sudo (service restart only)' }, 'bob_johnson': { 'production_servers': 'Read-only audit access', 'security_tools': 'Full access to WAF, IDS/IPS', 'log_servers': 'Full access for analysis', 'sudo_privileges': 'No sudo (security policy)' } } print(f"Individual Access Patterns:") for member, access in access_patterns.items(): print(f" {member.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for resource, permission in access.items(): print(f" {resource.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {permission}") print(f"") # Django server access matrix django_access_matrix = { 'web_servers': { 'john_smith': 'Deploy, configure, restart services', 'jane_doe': 'Deploy to staging, debug issues', 'bob_johnson': 'Security scans, log analysis' }, 'database_access': { 'john_smith': 'Full database administration', 'jane_doe': 'Run migrations, data validation', 'bob_johnson': 'Audit queries, compliance checks' }, 'configuration_files': { 'john_smith': 'Edit all configuration files', 'jane_doe': 'Edit staging configs only', 'bob_johnson': 'Read-only access for audits' } } print(f"Django Server Access Matrix:") for resource, team_access in django_access_matrix.items(): print(f" {resource.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for member, permission in team_access.items(): print(f" {member.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {permission}") print(f"") # Team collaboration workflow collaboration_workflow = { 'deployment': { 'initiator': 'jane_doe (Developer)', 'reviewer': 'john_smith (DevOps Lead)', 'approver': 'bob_johnson (Security review)', 'process': 'Staging → Security scan → Production' }, 'incident_response': { 'on_call': 'john_smith (Primary)', 'backup': 'jane_doe (Secondary)', 'security_escalation': 'bob_johnson (If security-related)', 'process': 'Triage → Fix → Document → Review' }, 'maintenance': { 'scheduler': 'john_smith (DevOps Lead)', 'executor': 'jane_doe or john_smith', 'validator': 'bob_johnson (Security checks)', 'process': 'Plan → Notify → Execute → Validate' } } print(f"Team Collaboration Workflow:") for workflow_type, details in collaboration_workflow.items(): print(f" {workflow_type.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for role, person in details.items(): print(f" {role.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {person}") print(f"") # Key management best practices key_management = { 'distribution': 'Team members generate own keys locally', 'collection': 'Public keys collected via secure portal', 'import_process': 'DevOps lead imports to AWS', 'validation': 'Verify fingerprint with team member', 'revocation': 'Immediate deletion upon team member departure', 'rotation': 'Annual key rotation requirement', 'backup': 'Each team member manages own backups' } print(f"Key Management Best Practices:") for practice, description in key_management.items(): print(f" {practice.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") print(f"\n✓ Team member keys imported successfully") print(f"✓ Collaborative Django access configured")
Example 3: Import ED25519 Key for Modern Security

Import an existing ED25519 public key for enhanced security and performance.

#!/bin/bash
# Import existing ED25519 key
KEY_NAME="anihpj-ed25519-prod-key"
REGION="us-east-1"
PUBLIC_KEY_FILE="~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub"

echo "Importing ED25519 public key..."
echo ""

# Verify key type
echo "Verifying key type..."
KEY_TYPE=$(ssh-keygen -l -f $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE | awk '{print $4}' | tr -d '()')

if [ "$KEY_TYPE" != "ED25519" ]; then
    echo "Error: Expected ED25519 key, found: $KEY_TYPE"
    exit 1
fi

echo "✓ Verified ED25519 key type"
echo ""

# Display key information
echo "Key information:"
ssh-keygen -l -f $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE
echo ""

# Import the ED25519 key
aws ec2 import-key-pair \
  --key-name $KEY_NAME \
  --public-key-material fileb://$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$KEY_NAME},
    {Key=KeyType,Value=ED25519},
    {Key=SecurityLevel,Value=High},
    {Key=Purpose,Value=Production-Django}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION

echo ""
echo "✓ ED25519 key imported successfully"
echo ""

# Compare fingerprints
echo "Fingerprint comparison:"
echo "  Local fingerprint:"
ssh-keygen -l -f $PUBLIC_KEY_FILE | awk '{print "    " $2}'

echo "  AWS fingerprint:"
aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --key-names $KEY_NAME \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[0].KeyFingerprint' \
  --output text | awk '{print "    " $0}'

echo ""
echo "ED25519 advantages for Django deployment:"
echo "  ✓ Faster SSH connections"
echo "  ✓ Lower CPU usage during deployments"
echo "  ✓ Better performance on CI/CD pipelines"
echo "  ✓ Quantum-resistant cryptography"
Importing ED25519 public key... Verifying key type... ✓ Verified ED25519 key type Key information: 256 SHA256:2Oo1TcP7DP8+cCfWFbhWRwYBHU4LJAqbYV5JU4jYxQY user@devmachine (ED25519) { "KeyName": "anihpj-ed25519-prod-key", "KeyFingerprint": "SHA256:2Oo1TcP7DP8+cCfWFbhWRwYBHU4LJAqbYV5JU4jYxQY", "KeyPairId": "key-ed25519abcdef123", "Tags": [ { "Key": "KeyType", "Value": "ED25519" }, { "Key": "SecurityLevel", "Value": "High" } ] } ✓ ED25519 key imported successfully Fingerprint comparison: Local fingerprint: SHA256:2Oo1TcP7DP8+cCfWFbhWRwYBHU4LJAqbYV5JU4jYxQY AWS fingerprint: SHA256:2Oo1TcP7DP8+cCfWFbhWRwYBHU4LJAqbYV5JU4jYxQY ED25519 advantages for Django deployment: ✓ Faster SSH connections ✓ Lower CPU usage during deployments ✓ Better performance on CI/CD pipelines ✓ Quantum-resistant cryptography
# Import ED25519 key for modern security from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone ed25519_import = { 'key_name': 'anihpj-ed25519-prod-key', 'key_type': 'ED25519', 'key_size': '256 bits', 'fingerprint': 'SHA256:2Oo1TcP7DP8+cCfWFbhWRwYBHU4LJAqbYV5JU4jYxQY', 'security_level': 'High' } print(f"ED25519 Key Import") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"") print(f"Key Name: {ed25519_import['key_name']}") print(f"Key Type: {ed25519_import['key_type']}") print(f"Key Size: {ed25519_import['key_size']}") print(f"Fingerprint: {ed25519_import['fingerprint']}") print(f"Security Level: {ed25519_import['security_level']}") print(f"") # Performance comparison for Django deployments performance_metrics = { 'ssh_connection': { 'rsa_2048': '~150ms', 'ed25519': '~50ms', 'improvement': '66% faster' }, 'scp_transfer_10mb': { 'rsa_2048': '~5.2s', 'ed25519': '~4.8s', 'improvement': '8% faster' }, 'deployment_authentication': { 'rsa_2048': '~100ms per auth', 'ed25519': '~30ms per auth', 'improvement': '70% faster' }, 'ci_cd_pipeline': { 'rsa_2048': '~10s overhead (100 connections)', 'ed25519': '~3s overhead (100 connections)', 'improvement': '70% faster' } } print(f"Performance Comparison for Django Deployments:") for operation, metrics in performance_metrics.items(): print(f" {operation.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for metric, value in metrics.items(): print(f" {metric.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"") # Django deployment scenarios deployment_scenarios = { 'automated_deployment': { 'frequency': '10-20 times per day', 'ssh_connections': '~500 per day', 'time_saved_daily': '~35 seconds with ED25519', 'annual_time_savings': '~3.5 hours per year' }, 'manual_debugging': { 'frequency': '5-10 times per week', 'ssh_connections': '~200 per week', 'time_saved_weekly': '~20 seconds with ED25519', 'monthly_time_savings': '~1.5 minutes per month' }, 'emergency_hotfix': { 'criticality': 'Every second counts', 'typical_ssh_sessions': '10-20 rapid connections', 'time_saved': '~1-2 seconds per hotfix', 'impact': 'Faster mean time to recovery (MTTR)' } } print(f"Django Deployment Scenarios:") for scenario, details in deployment_scenarios.items(): print(f" {scenario.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for aspect, value in details.items(): print(f" {aspect.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"") # Security advantages security_advantages = { 'quantum_resistance': 'Resistant to quantum computer attacks', 'side_channel_protection': 'Better protection against timing attacks', 'implementation_quality': 'Simpler algorithm, fewer bugs', 'cryptographic_agility': 'Modern algorithm (2011) vs RSA (1977)', 'key_size': 'Smaller keys (256-bit vs 2048-bit)', 'signature_verification': 'Faster verification for Git commits' } print(f"Security Advantages:") for advantage, description in security_advantages.items(): print(f" {advantage.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") # Migration strategy migration_strategy = { 'phase_1': 'Generate ED25519 key locally', 'phase_2': 'Import to AWS staging environment', 'phase_3': 'Test with staging Django servers', 'phase_4': 'Import to production region', 'phase_5': 'Update CI/CD pipelines', 'phase_6': 'Gradually phase out RSA keys', 'timeline': '2-4 weeks for complete migration' } print(f"\nMigration Strategy (RSA → ED25519):") for phase, description in migration_strategy.items(): print(f" {phase.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") # Compatibility verification compatibility = { 'linux_servers': '✓ Full support (OpenSSH 6.5+)', 'macos_workstations': '✓ Full support (10.11+)', 'windows_clients': '✓ Full support (OpenSSH for Windows)', 'ci_cd_tools': '✓ GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins', 'deployment_tools': '✓ Ansible, Terraform, AWS CodeDeploy', 'legacy_systems': '⚠️ May require OpenSSH upgrade' } print(f"\nCompatibility Verification:") for system, status in compatibility.items(): print(f" {system.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {status}") print(f"\n✓ ED25519 key imported for high-performance Django access") print(f"✓ Quantum-resistant security enabled")
Example 4: Import Keys to Multiple Regions for DR

Import the same public key to multiple AWS regions for disaster recovery and multi-region deployments.

#!/bin/bash
# Import key to multiple regions for disaster recovery
KEY_NAME="anihpj-multiregion-key"
PUBLIC_KEY_FILE="~/.ssh/anihpj_multiregion.pub"

# Define regions for multi-region deployment
REGIONS=(
    "us-east-1"     # Primary region
    "us-west-2"     # Failover region
    "eu-west-1"     # European operations
)

echo "Importing key to multiple regions for DR..."
echo "Key: $KEY_NAME"
echo "Regions: ${REGIONS[@]}"
echo ""

# Verify public key exists
if [ ! -f "$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Public key file not found"
    exit 1
fi

# Import to each region
for region in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    echo "Importing to $region..."
    
    aws ec2 import-key-pair \
        --key-name $KEY_NAME \
        --public-key-material fileb://$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE \
        --tag-specifications "ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[
            {Key=Name,Value=$KEY_NAME},
            {Key=Environment,Value=Production},
            {Key=MultiRegion,Value=Yes},
            {Key=Purpose,Value=Disaster-Recovery}
        ]" \
        --region $region \
        --output json > /dev/null
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "  ✓ Import successful"
        
        # Get and display fingerprint
        FINGERPRINT=$(aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
            --key-names $KEY_NAME \
            --region $region \
            --query 'KeyPairs[0].KeyFingerprint' \
            --output text)
        echo "  Fingerprint: $FINGERPRINT"
    else
        echo "  ✗ Import failed"
    fi
    echo ""
done

echo "Multi-region import complete"
echo ""

# Verify consistency across regions
echo "Verifying key consistency across regions..."
for region in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    FINGERPRINT=$(aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
        --key-names $KEY_NAME \
        --region $region \
        --query 'KeyPairs[0].KeyFingerprint' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    echo "  $region: $FINGERPRINT"
done

echo ""
echo "✓ Same private key works in all regions"
echo "✓ Disaster recovery SSH access configured"
Importing key to multiple regions for DR... Key: anihpj-multiregion-key Regions: us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1 Importing to us-east-1... ✓ Import successful Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f Importing to us-west-2... ✓ Import successful Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f Importing to eu-west-1... ✓ Import successful Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f Multi-region import complete Verifying key consistency across regions... us-east-1: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f us-west-2: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f eu-west-1: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f ✓ Same private key works in all regions ✓ Disaster recovery SSH access configured
# Import keys to multiple regions for DR from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone multiregion_import = { 'key_name': 'anihpj-multiregion-key', 'fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f', 'regions': ['us-east-1', 'us-west-2', 'eu-west-1'], 'purpose': 'Disaster Recovery' } print(f"Multi-Region SSH Key Import") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"") print(f"Key Name: {multiregion_import['key_name']}") print(f"Fingerprint: {multiregion_import['fingerprint']}") print(f"Regions: {', '.join(multiregion_import['regions'])}") print(f"Purpose: {multiregion_import['purpose']}") print(f"") # Region-specific deployment strategy region_strategy = { 'us_east_1': { 'designation': 'Primary Production', 'django_instances': 10, 'database': 'RDS Multi-AZ (Primary)', 'traffic_percentage': '70%', 'purpose': 'Main application traffic' }, 'us_west_2': { 'designation': 'Failover / DR', 'django_instances': 5, 'database': 'RDS Read Replica', 'traffic_percentage': '20%', 'purpose': 'West coast traffic + disaster recovery' }, 'eu_west_1': { 'designation': 'European Operations', 'django_instances': 3, 'database': 'RDS Read Replica', 'traffic_percentage': '10%', 'purpose': 'GDPR compliance + low latency for EU users' } } print(f"Region-Specific Deployment Strategy:") for region, config in region_strategy.items(): print(f" {region.upper().replace('_', '-')}:") for setting, value in config.items(): print(f" {setting.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"") # Disaster recovery scenarios dr_scenarios = { 'regional_outage': { 'trigger': 'Primary region (us-east-1) unavailable', 'action': 'Promote us-west-2 to primary', 'ssh_access': 'Same key works in failover region', 'rto': '< 15 minutes', 'rpo': '< 5 minutes' }, 'database_failover': { 'trigger': 'Primary RDS instance failure', 'action': 'Promote read replica in us-west-2', 'ssh_access': 'SSH to us-west-2 Django servers', 'rto': '< 5 minutes', 'rpo': '< 1 minute' }, 'dns_based_failover': { 'trigger': 'Health check failure in primary', 'action': 'Route53 automatically fails over', 'ssh_access': 'SSH to active region servers', 'rto': '< 2 minutes (automatic)', 'rpo': 'Near-zero (continuous replication)' } } print(f"Disaster Recovery Scenarios:") for scenario, details in dr_scenarios.items(): print(f" {scenario.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for aspect, value in details.items(): print(f" {aspect.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"") # Django multi-region considerations django_multiregion = { 'database_replication': 'RDS cross-region read replicas', 'static_assets': 'CloudFront with S3 origin in each region', 'session_storage': 'ElastiCache Global Datastore', 'media_uploads': 'S3 with cross-region replication', 'configuration': 'Same Django settings across regions', 'ssh_access': 'Single key pair works everywhere' } print(f"Django Multi-Region Considerations:") for aspect, implementation in django_multiregion.items(): print(f" {aspect.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {implementation}") # Operational procedures operational_procedures = { 'routine_deployment': { 'target': 'Deploy to all regions simultaneously', 'method': 'CI/CD pipeline with parallel deployment', 'ssh_key': 'Same key authenticates to all regions', 'validation': 'Health checks in each region' }, 'emergency_access': { 'target': 'Quick access to any region', 'method': 'SSH to bastion in affected region', 'ssh_key': 'Pre-imported key available immediately', 'validation': 'No key distribution delay' }, 'regional_maintenance': { 'target': 'Maintenance in one region', 'method': 'Drain traffic, perform maintenance', 'ssh_key': 'Same key for all maintenance tasks', 'validation': 'Restore traffic after validation' } } print(f"\nOperational Procedures:") for procedure, details in operational_procedures.items(): print(f" {procedure.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for aspect, description in details.items(): print(f" {aspect.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") print(f"") # Cost optimization cost_optimization = { 'single_key_pair': 'No cost for multiple imports', 'reduced_complexity': 'Less key management overhead', 'faster_recovery': 'Reduced downtime costs', 'automation_friendly': 'Same automation works everywhere', 'compliance': 'Centralized key management' } print(f"Cost Optimization Benefits:") for benefit, description in cost_optimization.items(): print(f" {benefit.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") # Compliance and security compliance_security = { 'key_consistency': 'Identical fingerprint across all regions', 'audit_trail': 'Single key usage audit trail', 'access_control': 'Consistent access policy everywhere', 'key_rotation': 'Single rotation process for all regions', 'incident_response': 'Single revocation point for emergency' } print(f"\nCompliance and Security:") for aspect, benefit in compliance_security.items(): print(f" {aspect.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {benefit}") print(f"\n✓ Multi-region SSH access configured") print(f"✓ Disaster recovery capabilities enabled")
Example 5: Import Certificate-Based Key for CI/CD Pipeline

Import a CI/CD pipeline SSH key from certificate authority for automated Django deployments.

#!/bin/bash
# Import CI/CD pipeline SSH key from certificate
KEY_NAME="anihpj-cicd-pipeline-key"
REGION="us-east-1"
CERT_DIR="/etc/ssh/ca"
PUBLIC_KEY_FILE="$CERT_DIR/cicd-host-key-cert.pub"

echo "Importing CI/CD pipeline certificate-based key..."
echo ""

# Verify certificate-based key exists
if [ ! -f "$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Certificate public key not found"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract public key from certificate (if needed)
if [[ "$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE" == *"-cert.pub" ]]; then
    echo "Extracting public key from certificate..."
    BASE_KEY="${PUBLIC_KEY_FILE%-cert.pub}.pub"
    
    if [ ! -f "$BASE_KEY" ]; then
        echo "Error: Base public key not found: $BASE_KEY"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    PUBLIC_KEY_TO_IMPORT="$BASE_KEY"
else
    PUBLIC_KEY_TO_IMPORT="$PUBLIC_KEY_FILE"
fi

echo "Using public key: $PUBLIC_KEY_TO_IMPORT"
echo ""

# Display key information
echo "Key information:"
ssh-keygen -l -f $PUBLIC_KEY_TO_IMPORT
echo ""

# Import the CI/CD key
aws ec2 import-key-pair \
  --key-name $KEY_NAME \
  --public-key-material fileb://$PUBLIC_KEY_TO_IMPORT \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=key-pair,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=$KEY_NAME},
    {Key=Environment,Value=Production},
    {Key=Purpose,Value=CI-CD-Automation},
    {Key=ManagedBy,Value=DevOps-Pipeline},
    {Key=CertificateAuthority,Value=Company-CA},
    {Key=AutomationLevel,Value=Fully-Automated}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION

echo ""
echo "✓ CI/CD pipeline key imported successfully"
echo ""

# Document automation scenarios
echo "CI/CD Automation Scenarios:"
echo "  1. Automated Django deployment (main branch)"
echo "  2. Scheduled database migrations"
echo "  3. Automated security patching"
echo "  4. Log collection and analysis"
echo "  5. Backup and disaster recovery tests"
echo ""

# Setup pipeline environment variables
echo "Pipeline Environment Variables:"
echo "  export AWS_KEY_NAME='$KEY_NAME'"
echo "  export AWS_REGION='$REGION'"
echo "  export DJANGO_DEPLOY_USER='ec2-user'"
echo ""

# Document security considerations
echo "Security Considerations:"
echo "  ✓ Key issued by company certificate authority"
echo "  ✓ Limited validity period (auto-rotation)"
echo "  ✓ Automated key rotation via CA"
echo "  ✓ Centralized revocation capability"
echo "  ✓ Audit trail via CA logging"
Importing CI/CD pipeline certificate-based key... Extracting public key from certificate... Using public key: /etc/ssh/ca/cicd-host-key.pub Key information: 2048 SHA256:kX9Z3L5mN8pQ2rT4vW6xY0zA1bC3dE5fG7hJ9kL1mN3 cicd-pipeline@company.com (RSA) { "KeyName": "anihpj-cicd-pipeline-key", "KeyFingerprint": "1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f", "KeyPairId": "key-cicd123456789", "Tags": [ { "Key": "Purpose", "Value": "CI-CD-Automation" }, { "Key": "CertificateAuthority", "Value": "Company-CA" } ] } ✓ CI/CD pipeline key imported successfully CI/CD Automation Scenarios: 1. Automated Django deployment (main branch) 2. Scheduled database migrations 3. Automated security patching 4. Log collection and analysis 5. Backup and disaster recovery tests Pipeline Environment Variables: export AWS_KEY_NAME='anihpj-cicd-pipeline-key' export AWS_REGION='us-east-1' export DJANGO_DEPLOY_USER='ec2-user' Security Considerations: ✓ Key issued by company certificate authority ✓ Limited validity period (auto-rotation) ✓ Automated key rotation via CA ✓ Centralized revocation capability ✓ Audit trail via CA logging
# Import certificate-based key for CI/CD pipeline from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone cicd_key_import = { 'key_name': 'anihpj-cicd-pipeline-key', 'key_fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f', 'key_pair_id': 'key-cicd123456789', 'purpose': 'CI-CD-Automation', 'managed_by': 'DevOps-Pipeline', 'certificate_authority': 'Company-CA' } print(f"CI/CD Pipeline SSH Key Import") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"") print(f"Key Name: {cicd_key_import['key_name']}") print(f"Key Fingerprint: {cicd_key_import['key_fingerprint']}") print(f"Purpose: {cicd_key_import['purpose']}") print(f"Managed By: {cicd_key_import['managed_by']}") print(f"Certificate Authority: {cicd_key_import['certificate_authority']}") print(f"") # CI/CD pipeline stages pipeline_stages = { 'stage_1_build': { 'trigger': 'Git push to main branch', 'actions': ['Run tests', 'Build Docker image', 'Push to ECR'], 'ssh_required': False, 'duration': '~3 minutes' }, 'stage_2_deploy_staging': { 'trigger': 'Build success', 'actions': ['SSH to staging servers', 'Pull Docker image', 'Run migrations', 'Restart services'], 'ssh_required': True, 'duration': '~5 minutes' }, 'stage_3_integration_tests': { 'trigger': 'Staging deployment success', 'actions': ['SSH to staging', 'Run integration tests', 'Collect logs'], 'ssh_required': True, 'duration': '~10 minutes' }, 'stage_4_approval': { 'trigger': 'Tests pass', 'actions': ['Manual approval', 'Security scan review'], 'ssh_required': False, 'duration': '~Variable (manual)' }, 'stage_5_deploy_production': { 'trigger': 'Approval granted', 'actions': ['SSH to production servers', 'Blue-green deployment', 'Health checks'], 'ssh_required': True, 'duration': '~10 minutes' } } print(f"CI/CD Pipeline Stages:") for stage, config in pipeline_stages.items(): print(f" {stage.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") for setting, value in config.items(): if isinstance(value, list): print(f" {setting.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {', '.join(value)}") else: print(f" {setting.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"") # Automated deployment tasks automated_tasks = { 'django_deployment': { 'frequency': '10-15 times per day', 'ssh_commands': [ 'cd /opt/django/anihpj', 'git pull origin main', 'source venv/bin/activate', 'pip install -r requirements.txt', 'python manage.py migrate', 'python manage.py collectstatic --noinput', 'sudo systemctl restart gunicorn', 'sudo systemctl restart nginx' ], 'validation': 'Health check endpoint' }, 'database_migrations': { 'frequency': 'Daily at 2 AM', 'ssh_commands': [ 'cd /opt/django/anihpj', 'source venv/bin/activate', 'python manage.py migrate --check', 'python manage.py migrate', 'python manage.py validate_migration_history' ], 'validation': 'Migration status check' }, 'log_collection': { 'frequency': 'Every hour', 'ssh_commands': [ 'cd /var/log/django', 'tar -czf logs-$(date +%Y%m%d_%H).tar.gz *.log', 'aws s3 cp logs-*.tar.gz s3://anihpj-logs/', 'rm logs-*.tar.gz' ], 'validation': 'S3 upload confirmation' } } print(f"Automated Deployment Tasks:") for task, details in automated_tasks.items(): print(f" {task.replace('_', ' ').title()}:") print(f" Frequency: {details['frequency']}") print(f" SSH Commands:") for cmd in details['ssh_commands']: print(f" - {cmd}") print(f" Validation: {details['validation']}") print(f"") # Certificate-based authentication benefits cert_auth_benefits = { 'centralized_management': 'CA manages all certificates from single point', 'automatic_rotation': 'Certificates auto-renew before expiration', 'immediate_revocation': 'Revoke compromised certificates instantly', 'audit_trail': 'Complete certificate lifecycle logging', 'compliance': 'Meets enterprise security requirements', 'scalability': 'Easy to add new pipeline agents' } print(f"Certificate-Based Authentication Benefits:") for benefit, description in cert_auth_benefits.items(): print(f" {benefit.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {description}") # Pipeline security controls security_controls = { 'ssh_key_storage': 'Stored in CI/CD secrets management', 'access_restriction': 'Only pipeline service account', 'session_logging': 'All SSH sessions logged to SIEM', 'command_auditing': 'All commands logged and reviewed', 'failure_alerting': 'Immediate alerts on auth failure', 'rate_limiting': 'Max 100 SSH connections per hour' } print(f"\nPipeline Security Controls:") for control, implementation in security_controls.items(): print(f" {control.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {implementation}") # Django deployment best practices deployment_best_practices = { 'zero_downtime': 'Blue-green deployment strategy', 'rollback_capability': 'Keep previous version running', 'health_checks': 'Automated health validation', 'database_migrations': 'Run migrations before code deployment', 'static_files': 'Collect static files to S3/CloudFront', 'cache_invalidation': 'Clear application cache after deployment' } print(f"\nDjango Deployment Best Practices:") for practice, implementation in deployment_best_practices.items(): print(f" {practice.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {implementation}") # Monitoring and alerting monitoring_alerting = { 'deployment_success': 'Slack notification on successful deployment', 'deployment_failure': 'PagerDuty alert + Slack notification', 'ssh_failures': 'Email alert after 3 consecutive failures', 'performance_degradation': 'Alert if response time > 500ms', 'error_rate': 'Alert if error rate > 1%', 'pipeline_duration': 'Alert if pipeline exceeds 30 minutes' } print(f"\nMonitoring and Alerting:") for metric, action in monitoring_alerting.items(): print(f" {metric.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {action}") # Pipeline optimization metrics optimization_metrics = { 'deployment_frequency': '10-15 deployments per day', 'mean_time_to_deploy': '~18 minutes (commit to production)', 'deployment_success_rate': '98.5%', 'mean_time_to_recovery': '~8 minutes', 'change_failure_rate': '~1.5%', 'ssh_authentication_time': '< 1 second per connection' } print(f"\nPipeline Optimization Metrics:") for metric, value in optimization_metrics.items(): print(f" {metric.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"\n✓ CI/CD pipeline SSH key imported successfully") print(f"✓ Automated Django deployment enabled")

describe-key-pairs

List and describe EC2 key pairs in your account. Returns key names, fingerprints, IDs, types, and tags. Essential for auditing SSH keys, verifying key availability before instance launches, and managing key lifecycle for Django server access.

Syntax
aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  [--key-names ] \
  [--key-pair-ids ] \
  [--filters Name=,Values=] \
  [--include-public-key] \
  [--query ] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --key-names (list): Specific key pair names to describe. Example: --key-names anihpj-prod-key anihpj-dev-key
  • --key-pair-ids (list): Specific key pair IDs to describe. Example: --key-pair-ids key-1234567890abcdef0
  • --filters (list): Filter results. Supported filters: key-pair-id, fingerprint, key-name, key-type, tag:key=value
  • --include-public-key (boolean): Include the public key material in the response (only for imported keys)
  • --query (string): JMESPath query to filter output. Example: --query 'KeyPairs[*].KeyName'
  • --region (string): AWS region to query
Return Value
  • KeyPairs (Array): List of key pair objects
  • KeyName (String): Name of the key pair
  • KeyPairId (String): Unique identifier for the key pair
  • KeyFingerprint (String): SHA-1 or SHA-256 fingerprint
  • KeyType (String): Key type (rsa or ed25519)
  • Tags (Array): Tags associated with the key pair
  • CreateTime (Timestamp): When the key was created
Example 1: List All Key Pairs for Django Project

List all SSH key pairs in the account with full details for audit and inventory purposes.

#!/bin/bash
# List all key pairs for Django project audit
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Listing all SSH key pairs..."
echo ""

aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[*].[KeyName,KeyType,KeyFingerprint,CreateTime]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Detailed key pair information:"

aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --region $REGION \
  --output json | jq '.KeyPairs[] | {
    Name: .KeyName,
    ID: .KeyPairId,
    Type: .KeyType,
    Fingerprint: .KeyFingerprint,
    Created: .CreateTime,
    Tags: .Tags
  }'
Listing all SSH key pairs... ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeKeyPairs | +---------------------------+----------+------------------------------------------+--+ | anihpj-prod-django-key | rsa | 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d | 2026-01-10 | | anihpj-dev-key | rsa | 2a:62:bf:39:cf:9a:fa:e9:2g:36:6e:48:3e:8e | 2026-01-08 | | anihpj-staging-key | rsa | 3b:73:cg:4a:dg:ab:gb:fa:3h:47:7f:59:4f:9f | 2026-01-09 | | anihpj-ed25519-key | ed25519 | SHA256:2Oo1TcP7DP8+cCfWFbhWRwYBHU4LJAqbYV | 2026-01-11 | +---------------------------+----------+------------------------------------------+--+ Detailed key pair information: { "Name": "anihpj-prod-django-key", "ID": "key-1234567890abcdef0", "Type": "rsa", "Fingerprint": "1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f", "Created": "2026-01-10T10:30:00.000Z", "Tags": [{"Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production"}] }
# List all key pairs for Django project from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone key_pairs_inventory = [ { 'name': 'anihpj-prod-django-key', 'id': 'key-1234567890abcdef0', 'type': 'rsa', 'environment': 'Production', 'created': '2026-01-10', 'usage': 'Production Django servers' }, { 'name': 'anihpj-dev-key', 'id': 'key-0987654321fedcba0', 'type': 'rsa', 'environment': 'Development', 'created': '2026-01-08', 'usage': 'Development testing' }, { 'name': 'anihpj-staging-key', 'id': 'key-staging123456789', 'type': 'rsa', 'environment': 'Staging', 'created': '2026-01-09', 'usage': 'Staging environment' }, { 'name': 'anihpj-ed25519-key', 'id': 'key-ed25519abcdef123', 'type': 'ed25519', 'environment': 'Production', 'created': '2026-01-11', 'usage': 'High-security access' } ] print(f"SSH Key Pairs Inventory - anihpj Django Project") print(f"=" * 60) print(f"Total keys: {len(key_pairs_inventory)}") print(f"") for key in key_pairs_inventory: print(f"Key: {key['name']}") print(f" ID: {key['id']}") print(f" Type: {key['type']}") print(f" Environment: {key['environment']}") print(f" Created: {key['created']}") print(f" Usage: {key['usage']}") print(f"") # Key distribution by environment env_distribution = {} for key in key_pairs_inventory: env = key['environment'] env_distribution[env] = env_distribution.get(env, 0) + 1 print(f"Keys by Environment:") for env, count in env_distribution.items(): print(f" {env}: {count} key(s)") print(f"\n✓ Key inventory complete")
Example 2: Filter Keys by Environment Tag

Filter key pairs by environment tags to find production or development keys.

#!/bin/bash
# Filter keys by environment tag
REGION="us-east-1"
ENVIRONMENT="Production"

echo "Finding $ENVIRONMENT environment keys..."

aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --filters "Name=tag:Environment,Values=$ENVIRONMENT" \
  --region $REGION \
  --output json

echo ""
echo "Production key names only:"
aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --filters "Name=tag:Environment,Values=Production" \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[*].KeyName' \
  --output text
Finding Production environment keys... { "KeyPairs": [ { "KeyName": "anihpj-prod-django-key", "KeyPairId": "key-1234567890abcdef0", "KeyType": "rsa", "Tags": [{"Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production"}] }, { "KeyName": "anihpj-ed25519-key", "KeyPairId": "key-ed25519abcdef123", "KeyType": "ed25519", "Tags": [{"Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production"}] } ] } Production key names only: anihpj-prod-django-key anihpj-ed25519-key
# Filter keys by environment tag from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost production_keys = [ {'name': 'anihpj-prod-django-key', 'type': 'rsa'}, {'name': 'anihpj-ed25519-key', 'type': 'ed25519'} ] print(f"Production Environment Keys:") for key in production_keys: print(f" - {key['name']} ({key['type']})") print(f"\n✓ Found {len(production_keys)} production keys")
Example 3: Verify Key Exists Before Instance Launch

Check if a specific key pair exists before launching EC2 instances for Django deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify key exists before launching instance
KEY_NAME="anihpj-prod-django-key"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Verifying key pair exists: $KEY_NAME"

KEY_EXISTS=$(aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --key-names $KEY_NAME \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[0].KeyName' \
  --output text 2>/dev/null)

if [ "$KEY_EXISTS" = "$KEY_NAME" ]; then
    echo "✓ Key pair verified: $KEY_NAME"
    echo "Proceeding with instance launch..."
    
    # Get key fingerprint for verification
    FINGERPRINT=$(aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
      --key-names $KEY_NAME \
      --region $REGION \
      --query 'KeyPairs[0].KeyFingerprint' \
      --output text)
    
    echo "  Fingerprint: $FINGERPRINT"
else
    echo "✗ Key pair not found: $KEY_NAME"
    echo "Please create the key pair first"
    exit 1
fi
Verifying key pair exists: anihpj-prod-django-key ✓ Key pair verified: anihpj-prod-django-key Proceeding with instance launch... Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f
# Verify key exists before instance launch from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost key_verification = { 'key_name': 'anihpj-prod-django-key', 'exists': True, 'fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f', 'can_launch': True } print(f"Key Verification: {key_verification['key_name']}") print(f" Exists: {key_verification['exists']}") print(f" Fingerprint: {key_verification['fingerprint']}") print(f" Ready for launch: {key_verification['can_launch']}") print(f"\n✓ Key verified - ready for Django server deployment")
Example 4: Audit Key Age and Rotation Status

Check key creation dates to identify keys that need rotation based on security policy.

#!/bin/bash
# Audit key age for rotation compliance
REGION="us-east-1"
MAX_AGE_DAYS=90

echo "Auditing SSH key rotation compliance..."
echo "Policy: Keys older than $MAX_AGE_DAYS days require rotation"
echo ""

CURRENT_DATE=$(date +%s)

aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'KeyPairs[*].[KeyName,CreateTime]' \
  --output text | while read KEY_NAME CREATE_TIME; do
    
    KEY_DATE=$(date -d "$CREATE_TIME" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    AGE_DAYS=$(( (CURRENT_DATE - KEY_DATE) / 86400 ))
    
    if [ $AGE_DAYS -gt $MAX_AGE_DAYS ]; then
        echo "⚠️  $KEY_NAME: $AGE_DAYS days old - ROTATION REQUIRED"
    else
        DAYS_LEFT=$((MAX_AGE_DAYS - AGE_DAYS))
        echo "✓ $KEY_NAME: $AGE_DAYS days old - OK ($DAYS_LEFT days until rotation)"
    fi
done
Auditing SSH key rotation compliance... Policy: Keys older than 90 days require rotation ✓ anihpj-prod-django-key: 3 days old - OK (87 days until rotation) ✓ anihpj-dev-key: 5 days old - OK (85 days until rotation) ✓ anihpj-staging-key: 4 days old - OK (86 days until rotation) ✓ anihpj-ed25519-key: 2 days old - OK (88 days until rotation)
# Audit key age for rotation compliance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta max_age_days = 90 current_date = timezone.now() key_audit = [ {'name': 'anihpj-prod-django-key', 'age_days': 3, 'status': 'OK'}, {'name': 'anihpj-dev-key', 'age_days': 5, 'status': 'OK'}, {'name': 'anihpj-staging-key', 'age_days': 4, 'status': 'OK'}, {'name': 'anihpj-ed25519-key', 'age_days': 2, 'status': 'OK'} ] print(f"SSH Key Rotation Audit") print(f"Policy: {max_age_days} days maximum age") print(f"") compliant = 0 non_compliant = 0 for key in key_audit: days_left = max_age_days - key['age_days'] if key['age_days'] <= max_age_days: compliant += 1 print(f"✓ {key['name']}: {key['age_days']} days ({days_left} days until rotation)") else: non_compliant += 1 print(f"⚠️ {key['name']}: {key['age_days']} days - ROTATION REQUIRED") print(f"\nCompliance Summary:") print(f" Compliant: {compliant}") print(f" Non-compliant: {non_compliant}") print(f" Total: {len(key_audit)}") print(f"\n✓ Audit complete")
Example 5: Compare Keys Across Multiple Regions

Check key pair availability across multiple AWS regions for disaster recovery planning.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare keys across regions for DR planning
KEY_NAME="anihpj-prod-django-key"
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")

echo "Checking key availability across regions..."
echo "Key: $KEY_NAME"
echo ""

for region in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    KEY_EXISTS=$(aws ec2 describe-key-pairs \
      --key-names $KEY_NAME \
      --region $region \
      --query 'KeyPairs[0].KeyFingerprint' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ -n "$KEY_EXISTS" ] && [ "$KEY_EXISTS" != "None" ]; then
        echo "✓ $region: Key available"
        echo "  Fingerprint: $KEY_EXISTS"
    else
        echo "✗ $region: Key NOT found - needs import"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "DR Readiness Summary:"
echo "  Import missing keys to enable multi-region failover"
Checking key availability across regions... Key: anihpj-prod-django-key ✓ us-east-1: Key available Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f ✓ us-west-2: Key available Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f ✓ eu-west-1: Key available Fingerprint: 1f:51:ae:28:bf:89:e9:d8:1f:25:5d:37:2d:7d:b8:ca:9f:f5:f1:6f DR Readiness Summary: All regions have the key - DR ready
# Compare keys across regions for DR planning from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost regions_status = { 'us-east-1': {'available': True, 'fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89...'}, 'us-west-2': {'available': True, 'fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89...'}, 'eu-west-1': {'available': True, 'fingerprint': '1f:51:ae:28:bf:89...'} } print(f"Multi-Region Key Availability") print(f"Key: anihpj-prod-django-key") print(f"") available_count = sum(1 for r in regions_status.values() if r['available']) for region, status in regions_status.items(): if status['available']: print(f"✓ {region}: Available") else: print(f"✗ {region}: Missing - import required") print(f"\nDR Readiness: {available_count}/{len(regions_status)} regions") print(f"✓ Ready for multi-region failover")

allocate-address

Allocate an Elastic IP address (EIP) for your Django application. Elastic IPs are static public IPv4 addresses that persist across instance stops/starts, essential for DNS configuration, SSL certificates, and maintaining consistent access to your anihpj Django servers.

Syntax
aws ec2 allocate-address \
  [--domain vpc|standard] \
  [--address ] \
  [--public-ipv4-pool ] \
  [--network-border-group ] \
  [--tag-specifications 'ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=}]'] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --domain (string): Domain for address allocation. Use 'vpc' for VPC instances (default), 'standard' for EC2-Classic (deprecated)
  • --address (string): Specific IP address from your BYOIP pool (if applicable)
  • --public-ipv4-pool (string): ID of address pool (AWS pool or BYOIP pool)
  • --network-border-group (string): Network border group for Local Zones/Wavelength Zones
  • --tag-specifications (string): Tags for the Elastic IP. Format: ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=anihpj-prod-eip}]
  • --region (string): AWS region to allocate the address in
Return Value
  • PublicIp (String): The allocated Elastic IP address
  • AllocationId (String): Unique identifier for the allocation (use this to associate/release)
  • Domain (String): Whether address is for VPC or EC2-Classic
  • NetworkBorderGroup (String): Network border group name
  • PublicIpv4Pool (String): ID of the pool from which address was allocated
Example 1: Allocate Elastic IP for Production Django ALB

Allocate a static IP for the production Application Load Balancer serving the anihpj Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Allocate Elastic IP for production Django ALB
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Allocating Elastic IP for production Django ALB..."

EIP_RESULT=$(aws ec2 allocate-address \
  --domain vpc \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-prod-alb-eip},
    {Key=Environment,Value=Production},
    {Key=Purpose,Value=ALB-Frontend},
    {Key=Application,Value=anihpj-django}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION \
  --output json)

PUBLIC_IP=$(echo $EIP_RESULT | jq -r '.PublicIp')
ALLOCATION_ID=$(echo $EIP_RESULT | jq -r '.AllocationId')

echo ""
echo "✓ Elastic IP allocated successfully"
echo "  Public IP: $PUBLIC_IP"
echo "  Allocation ID: $ALLOCATION_ID"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Associate with NAT Gateway or instance"
echo "  2. Update DNS A record to point to $PUBLIC_IP"
echo "  3. Configure SSL certificate for the IP"
Allocating Elastic IP for production Django ALB... { "PublicIp": "52.87.123.45", "AllocationId": "eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0", "Domain": "vpc", "NetworkBorderGroup": "us-east-1", "PublicIpv4Pool": "amazon", "Tags": [ {"Key": "Name", "Value": "anihpj-prod-alb-eip"}, {"Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production"} ] } ✓ Elastic IP allocated successfully Public IP: 52.87.123.45 Allocation ID: eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0 Next steps: 1. Associate with NAT Gateway or instance 2. Update DNS A record to point to 52.87.123.45 3. Configure SSL certificate for the IP
# Allocate Elastic IP for production Django ALB from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone elastic_ip = { 'public_ip': '52.87.123.45', 'allocation_id': 'eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0', 'domain': 'vpc', 'environment': 'Production', 'purpose': 'ALB-Frontend' } print(f"Elastic IP Allocation - Production Django") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"Public IP: {elastic_ip['public_ip']}") print(f"Allocation ID: {elastic_ip['allocation_id']}") print(f"Domain: {elastic_ip['domain']}") print(f"Purpose: {elastic_ip['purpose']}") print(f"") # DNS configuration dns_config = { 'domain': 'anihpj.com', 'record_type': 'A', 'record_value': elastic_ip['public_ip'], 'ttl': 300 } print(f"DNS Configuration:") print(f" Domain: {dns_config['domain']}") print(f" Record: {dns_config['record_type']} -> {dns_config['record_value']}") print(f" TTL: {dns_config['ttl']} seconds") print(f"\n✓ Elastic IP ready for Django deployment")
Example 2: Allocate Multiple EIPs for NAT Gateways

Allocate Elastic IPs for NAT Gateways in multiple availability zones for high availability.

#!/bin/bash
# Allocate EIPs for NAT Gateways (HA setup)
REGION="us-east-1"
AZS=("us-east-1a" "us-east-1b")

echo "Allocating Elastic IPs for NAT Gateways..."

for az in "${AZS[@]}"; do
    echo "Allocating EIP for NAT Gateway in $az..."
    
    aws ec2 allocate-address \
      --domain vpc \
      --tag-specifications "ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[
        {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-nat-eip-$az},
        {Key=Purpose,Value=NAT-Gateway},
        {Key=AZ,Value=$az}
      ]" \
      --region $REGION \
      --query '[PublicIp,AllocationId]' \
      --output text
done

echo ""
echo "✓ NAT Gateway EIPs allocated for HA"
Allocating Elastic IPs for NAT Gateways... Allocating EIP for NAT Gateway in us-east-1a... 52.87.123.46 eipalloc-nat1234567890a Allocating EIP for NAT Gateway in us-east-1b... 52.87.123.47 eipalloc-nat0987654321b ✓ NAT Gateway EIPs allocated for HA
# Allocate multiple EIPs for NAT Gateways from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost nat_eips = [ {'az': 'us-east-1a', 'ip': '52.87.123.46', 'allocation_id': 'eipalloc-nat1234567890a'}, {'az': 'us-east-1b', 'ip': '52.87.123.47', 'allocation_id': 'eipalloc-nat0987654321b'} ] print(f"NAT Gateway Elastic IPs (HA Setup)") for eip in nat_eips: print(f" {eip['az']}: {eip['ip']} ({eip['allocation_id']})") print(f"\n✓ {len(nat_eips)} EIPs ready for NAT Gateways")
Example 3: Allocate EIP for Bastion Host

Allocate a static IP for the bastion host to enable consistent SSH access to Django servers.

#!/bin/bash
# Allocate EIP for bastion host
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Allocating EIP for bastion host..."

aws ec2 allocate-address \
  --domain vpc \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-bastion-eip},
    {Key=Purpose,Value=Bastion-Host},
    {Key=SecurityLevel,Value=High}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ Bastion host EIP allocated"
echo "Update SSH config with the new IP"
Allocating EIP for bastion host... { "PublicIp": "52.87.123.48", "AllocationId": "eipalloc-bastion123456", "Domain": "vpc" } ✓ Bastion host EIP allocated Update SSH config with the new IP
# Allocate EIP for bastion host from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost bastion_eip = { 'ip': '52.87.123.48', 'allocation_id': 'eipalloc-bastion123456', 'purpose': 'Bastion Host', 'ssh_config': f'Host bastion.anihpj.com\n HostName 52.87.123.48\n User ec2-user' } print(f"Bastion Host EIP: {bastion_eip['ip']}") print(f"\nSSH Config:\n{bastion_eip['ssh_config']}") print(f"\n✓ Bastion host ready for Django server access")
Example 4: Allocate EIP with Cost Tracking Tags

Allocate an Elastic IP with comprehensive cost tracking tags for billing analysis.

#!/bin/bash
# Allocate EIP with cost tracking
REGION="us-east-1"
COST_CENTER="engineering"
PROJECT="anihpj-django"

aws ec2 allocate-address \
  --domain vpc \
  --tag-specifications "ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[
    {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-web-eip},
    {Key=CostCenter,Value=$COST_CENTER},
    {Key=Project,Value=$PROJECT},
    {Key=Owner,Value=devops-team},
    {Key=BillingCode,Value=PROJ-2026-001}
  ]" \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ EIP allocated with cost tracking tags"
{ "PublicIp": "52.87.123.49", "AllocationId": "eipalloc-cost123456789", "Domain": "vpc", "Tags": [ {"Key": "CostCenter", "Value": "engineering"}, {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj-django"}, {"Key": "BillingCode", "Value": "PROJ-2026-001"} ] } ✓ EIP allocated with cost tracking tags
# Allocate EIP with cost tracking from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost cost_tracked_eip = { 'ip': '52.87.123.49', 'cost_center': 'engineering', 'project': 'anihpj-django', 'monthly_cost': '$3.60 (if unassociated)' } print(f"Cost-Tracked EIP: {cost_tracked_eip['ip']}") print(f"Cost Center: {cost_tracked_eip['cost_center']}") print(f"Project: {cost_tracked_eip['project']}") print(f"Monthly Cost: {cost_tracked_eip['monthly_cost']}") print(f"\n✓ EIP ready with billing visibility")
Example 5: Bulk Allocate EIPs for Multi-Tier Architecture

Allocate multiple EIPs for a complete multi-tier Django architecture.

#!/bin/bash
# Bulk allocate EIPs for multi-tier architecture
REGION="us-east-1"

declare -A tiers=(
    ["web"]="Web-Tier-ALB"
    ["api"]="API-Tier-NLB"
    ["nat-1"]="NAT-Gateway-AZ1"
    ["nat-2"]="NAT-Gateway-AZ2"
)

echo "Allocating EIPs for multi-tier architecture..."

for tier in "${!tiers[@]}"; do
    PURPOSE="${tiers[$tier]}"
    
    RESULT=$(aws ec2 allocate-address \
      --domain vpc \
      --tag-specifications "ResourceType=elastic-ip,Tags=[
        {Key=Name,Value=anihpj-$tier-eip},
        {Key=Tier,Value=$tier},
        {Key=Purpose,Value=$PURPOSE}
      ]" \
      --region $REGION \
      --query '[PublicIp,AllocationId]' \
      --output text)
    
    echo "✓ $tier: $RESULT"
done

echo ""
echo "Multi-tier EIP allocation complete"
Allocating EIPs for multi-tier architecture... ✓ web: 52.87.123.50 eipalloc-web1234567890 ✓ api: 52.87.123.51 eipalloc-api1234567890 ✓ nat-1: 52.87.123.52 eipalloc-nat1234567890 ✓ nat-2: 52.87.123.53 eipalloc-nat0987654321 Multi-tier EIP allocation complete
# Bulk allocate EIPs for multi-tier architecture from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost multi_tier_eips = { 'web': {'ip': '52.87.123.50', 'purpose': 'Web-Tier-ALB'}, 'api': {'ip': '52.87.123.51', 'purpose': 'API-Tier-NLB'}, 'nat-1': {'ip': '52.87.123.52', 'purpose': 'NAT-Gateway-AZ1'}, 'nat-2': {'ip': '52.87.123.53', 'purpose': 'NAT-Gateway-AZ2'} } print(f"Multi-Tier Architecture EIPs") print(f"=" * 40) for tier, config in multi_tier_eips.items(): print(f" {tier}: {config['ip']} - {config['purpose']}") print(f"\nTotal EIPs: {len(multi_tier_eips)}") print(f"Monthly cost (all associated): $0.00") print(f"\n✓ Ready for Django multi-tier deployment")

associate-address

Associate an Elastic IP address with an EC2 instance or network interface. This assigns a static public IP to your Django server, essential for DNS configuration, consistent access, and maintaining IP whitelist rules. Once associated, the EIP persists through instance stops/starts.

Syntax
aws ec2 associate-address \
  --allocation-id  \
  [--instance-id ] \
  [--network-interface-id ] \
  [--private-ip-address ] \
  [--allow-reassociation] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --allocation-id (string, required): The allocation ID of the Elastic IP address (eipalloc-xxxxxxxx)
  • --instance-id (string): The ID of the EC2 instance to associate with
  • --network-interface-id (string): The ID of the network interface (for VPC instances)
  • --private-ip-address (string): The primary or secondary private IP address to associate with
  • --allow-reassociation (boolean): Allow reassociation if EIP is already associated with another resource
  • --region (string): AWS region where the resources are located
Return Value
  • AssociationId (String): The ID of the association (use this to disassociate later)
Example 1: Associate EIP with Django Production Server

Associate an Elastic IP with a production Django EC2 instance for stable public access.

#!/bin/bash
# Associate EIP with production Django server
ALLOCATION_ID="eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0"
INSTANCE_ID="i-0987654321fedcba0"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Associating Elastic IP with Django production server..."
echo "  Allocation ID: $ALLOCATION_ID"
echo "  Instance ID: $INSTANCE_ID"
echo ""

RESULT=$(aws ec2 associate-address \
  --allocation-id $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID \
  --region $REGION)

ASSOCIATION_ID=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.AssociationId')

echo "✓ Elastic IP associated successfully"
echo "  Association ID: $ASSOCIATION_ID"
echo ""

# Get the public IP
PUBLIC_IP=$(aws ec2 describe-addresses \
  --allocation-ids $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Addresses[0].PublicIp' \
  --output text)

echo "Django server now accessible at: $PUBLIC_IP"
echo "  http://$PUBLIC_IP:8000"
echo "  https://$PUBLIC_IP (if SSL configured)"
Associating Elastic IP with Django production server... Allocation ID: eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0 Instance ID: i-0987654321fedcba0 { "AssociationId": "eipassoc-0123456789abcdef0" } ✓ Elastic IP associated successfully Association ID: eipassoc-0123456789abcdef0 Django server now accessible at: 52.87.123.45 http://52.87.123.45:8000 https://52.87.123.45 (if SSL configured)
# Associate EIP with Django production server from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone association = { 'allocation_id': 'eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0', 'instance_id': 'i-0987654321fedcba0', 'association_id': 'eipassoc-0123456789abcdef0', 'public_ip': '52.87.123.45' } print(f"EIP Association - Production Django Server") print(f"=" * 50) print(f"Public IP: {association['public_ip']}") print(f"Instance: {association['instance_id']}") print(f"Association ID: {association['association_id']}") print(f"") # Access URLs print(f"Django Access URLs:") print(f" HTTP: http://{association['public_ip']}:8000") print(f" HTTPS: https://{association['public_ip']}") print(f" Admin: https://{association['public_ip']}/admin/") print(f"\n✓ EIP associated - Django server accessible")
Example 2: Associate EIP with Network Interface

Associate an EIP with a specific network interface for more control over networking.

#!/bin/bash
# Associate EIP with network interface
ALLOCATION_ID="eipalloc-eni123456789"
ENI_ID="eni-0123456789abcdef0"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Associating EIP with network interface..."

aws ec2 associate-address \
  --allocation-id $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --network-interface-id $ENI_ID \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ EIP associated with ENI: $ENI_ID"
Associating EIP with network interface... { "AssociationId": "eipassoc-eni123456789abc" } ✓ EIP associated with ENI: eni-0123456789abcdef0
# Associate EIP with network interface from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost eni_association = { 'eni_id': 'eni-0123456789abcdef0', 'allocation_id': 'eipalloc-eni123456789', 'association_id': 'eipassoc-eni123456789abc' } print(f"EIP Associated with ENI: {eni_association['eni_id']}") print(f"Association ID: {eni_association['association_id']}") print(f"\n✓ Network interface has static public IP")
Example 3: Reassociate EIP to New Instance (Failover)

Reassociate an Elastic IP from a failed instance to a healthy one for failover.

#!/bin/bash
# Reassociate EIP for failover
ALLOCATION_ID="eipalloc-0123456789abcdef0"
NEW_INSTANCE_ID="i-newinstance12345"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Performing EIP failover..."
echo "Moving EIP to healthy instance: $NEW_INSTANCE_ID"

aws ec2 associate-address \
  --allocation-id $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --instance-id $NEW_INSTANCE_ID \
  --allow-reassociation \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ EIP failover complete"
echo "Django now served from: $NEW_INSTANCE_ID"
Performing EIP failover... Moving EIP to healthy instance: i-newinstance12345 { "AssociationId": "eipassoc-failover12345" } ✓ EIP failover complete Django now served from: i-newinstance12345
# Reassociate EIP for failover from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost failover = { 'old_instance': 'i-0987654321fedcba0 (unhealthy)', 'new_instance': 'i-newinstance12345 (healthy)', 'eip': '52.87.123.45', 'downtime': '< 30 seconds' } print(f"EIP Failover Complete") print(f" From: {failover['old_instance']}") print(f" To: {failover['new_instance']}") print(f" EIP: {failover['eip']}") print(f" Downtime: {failover['downtime']}") print(f"\n✓ Django traffic now routed to healthy instance")
Example 4: Associate EIP with Secondary Private IP

Associate an EIP with a secondary private IP for hosting multiple Django sites.

#!/bin/bash
# Associate EIP with secondary private IP
ALLOCATION_ID="eipalloc-secondary123"
INSTANCE_ID="i-multisite123456"
PRIVATE_IP="10.0.1.101"  # Secondary IP
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Associating EIP with secondary private IP..."
echo "  Instance: $INSTANCE_ID"
echo "  Private IP: $PRIVATE_IP"

aws ec2 associate-address \
  --allocation-id $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID \
  --private-ip-address $PRIVATE_IP \
  --region $REGION

echo "✓ EIP associated with secondary IP"
echo "Use for hosting second Django site on same instance"
Associating EIP with secondary private IP... Instance: i-multisite123456 Private IP: 10.0.1.101 { "AssociationId": "eipassoc-secondary12345" } ✓ EIP associated with secondary IP Use for hosting second Django site on same instance
# Associate EIP with secondary private IP from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost multisite_config = { 'primary': {'private_ip': '10.0.1.100', 'public_ip': '52.87.123.45', 'site': 'anihpj.com'}, 'secondary': {'private_ip': '10.0.1.101', 'public_ip': '52.87.123.50', 'site': 'jobs.anihpj.com'} } print(f"Multi-Site Django Configuration") for site_type, config in multisite_config.items(): print(f" {site_type}: {config['site']}") print(f" Private: {config['private_ip']}") print(f" Public: {config['public_ip']}") print(f"\n✓ Multiple Django sites on single instance")
Example 5: Associate EIP and Update DNS

Associate EIP and automatically update Route53 DNS record for the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Associate EIP and update DNS
ALLOCATION_ID="eipalloc-dns123456789"
INSTANCE_ID="i-django123456789"
HOSTED_ZONE_ID="Z1234567890ABC"
DOMAIN="api.anihpj.com"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Associating EIP and updating DNS..."

# Associate EIP
aws ec2 associate-address \
  --allocation-id $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID \
  --region $REGION

# Get the public IP
PUBLIC_IP=$(aws ec2 describe-addresses \
  --allocation-ids $ALLOCATION_ID \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Addresses[0].PublicIp' \
  --output text)

echo "EIP: $PUBLIC_IP"

# Update Route53
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
  --hosted-zone-id $HOSTED_ZONE_ID \
  --change-batch '{
    "Changes": [{
      "Action": "UPSERT",
      "ResourceRecordSet": {
        "Name": "'$DOMAIN'",
        "Type": "A",
        "TTL": 300,
        "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "'$PUBLIC_IP'"}]
      }
    }]
  }'

echo "✓ EIP associated and DNS updated"
echo "  $DOMAIN -> $PUBLIC_IP"
Associating EIP and updating DNS... { "AssociationId": "eipassoc-dns123456789" } EIP: 52.87.123.55 { "ChangeInfo": { "Id": "/change/C1234567890ABC", "Status": "PENDING" } } ✓ EIP associated and DNS updated api.anihpj.com -> 52.87.123.55
# Associate EIP and update DNS from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost dns_update = { 'domain': 'api.anihpj.com', 'eip': '52.87.123.55', 'record_type': 'A', 'ttl': 300, 'status': 'PENDING' } print(f"EIP Association with DNS Update") print(f" Domain: {dns_update['domain']}") print(f" EIP: {dns_update['eip']}") print(f" Record: {dns_update['record_type']}") print(f" TTL: {dns_update['ttl']}s") print(f" DNS Status: {dns_update['status']}") print(f"") print(f"Django API accessible at:") print(f" https://{dns_update['domain']}/api/v1/") print(f"\n✓ EIP and DNS configured")

describe-addresses

List and describe Elastic IP addresses in your account. Returns public IPs, allocation IDs, association status, and associated instances. Essential for auditing EIP usage, identifying unassociated IPs (cost optimization), and managing IP inventory for Django infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ec2 describe-addresses \
  [--allocation-ids ] \
  [--public-ips ] \
  [--filters Name=,Values=] \
  [--query ] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --allocation-ids (list): Specific allocation IDs to describe
  • --public-ips (list): Specific public IP addresses to describe
  • --filters (list): Filter results. Filters: allocation-id, association-id, domain, instance-id, public-ip, tag:key=value
  • --query (string): JMESPath query to filter output
  • --region (string): AWS region to query
Return Value
  • Addresses (Array): List of Elastic IP address objects
  • PublicIp (String): The Elastic IP address
  • AllocationId (String): The allocation ID
  • AssociationId (String): The association ID (if associated)
  • InstanceId (String): Associated instance ID (if any)
  • NetworkInterfaceId (String): Associated ENI ID
  • PrivateIpAddress (String): Private IP associated with EIP
Example 1: List All Elastic IPs with Association Status

List all Elastic IPs and their association status to identify Django infrastructure IPs.

#!/bin/bash
# List all Elastic IPs
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Listing all Elastic IP addresses..."
echo ""

aws ec2 describe-addresses \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Addresses[*].[PublicIp,AllocationId,InstanceId,Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Detailed view:"
aws ec2 describe-addresses \
  --region $REGION \
  --output json | jq '.Addresses[] | {
    PublicIP: .PublicIp,
    AllocationId: .AllocationId,
    Instance: .InstanceId,
    AssociationId: .AssociationId,
    Name: (.Tags // [] | map(select(.Key=="Name")) | .[0].Value // "unnamed")
  }'
Listing all Elastic IP addresses... ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeAddresses | +----------------+---------------------------+----------------------+--+ | 52.87.123.45 | eipalloc-prod123456789 | i-django-prod-001 | anihpj-prod-eip | | 52.87.123.46 | eipalloc-nat1234567890 | None | anihpj-nat-az1 | | 52.87.123.47 | eipalloc-nat0987654321 | None | anihpj-nat-az2 | | 52.87.123.48 | eipalloc-bastion123456 | i-bastion-001 | anihpj-bastion | +----------------+---------------------------+----------------------+--+ Detailed view: { "PublicIP": "52.87.123.45", "AllocationId": "eipalloc-prod123456789", "Instance": "i-django-prod-001", "AssociationId": "eipassoc-prod123456", "Name": "anihpj-prod-eip" }
# List all Elastic IPs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost elastic_ips = [ {'ip': '52.87.123.45', 'name': 'anihpj-prod-eip', 'instance': 'i-django-prod-001', 'associated': True}, {'ip': '52.87.123.46', 'name': 'anihpj-nat-az1', 'instance': None, 'associated': False}, {'ip': '52.87.123.47', 'name': 'anihpj-nat-az2', 'instance': None, 'associated': False}, {'ip': '52.87.123.48', 'name': 'anihpj-bastion', 'instance': 'i-bastion-001', 'associated': True} ] print(f"Elastic IP Inventory") print(f"=" * 50) associated = sum(1 for e in elastic_ips if e['associated']) print(f"Total: {len(elastic_ips)} | Associated: {associated} | Unassociated: {len(elastic_ips) - associated}") print(f"") for eip in elastic_ips: status = "✓" if eip['associated'] else "○" instance = eip['instance'] or 'Not associated' print(f" {status} {eip['ip']} - {eip['name']}") print(f" Instance: {instance}") print(f"\n✓ EIP inventory complete")
Example 2: Find Unassociated EIPs (Cost Optimization)

Identify Elastic IPs not associated with any resource to avoid unnecessary charges.

#!/bin/bash
# Find unassociated EIPs (costing money)
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Finding unassociated Elastic IPs..."
echo "⚠️  Unassociated EIPs cost \$0.005/hour (\$3.60/month)"
echo ""

UNASSOCIATED=$(aws ec2 describe-addresses \
  --region $REGION \
  --query 'Addresses[?AssociationId==`null`].[PublicIp,AllocationId,Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]]' \
  --output text)

if [ -z "$UNASSOCIATED" ]; then
    echo "✓ No unassociated EIPs found - no wasted costs"
else
    echo "⚠️  Unassociated EIPs found:"
    echo "$UNASSOCIATED"
    echo ""
    COUNT=$(echo "$UNASSOCIATED" | wc -l)
    MONTHLY_COST=$(echo "$COUNT * 3.60" | bc)
    echo "Total unassociated: $COUNT"
    echo "Monthly cost: \$$MONTHLY_COST"
    echo ""
    echo "Recommendation: Release or associate these EIPs"
fi
