# Track production alias config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost prod_config = { 'function': 'jobpost-api', 'alias': 'prod', 'auth': 'AWS_IAM', 'origin': 'https://jobpost.example.com', 'cache_seconds': 7200 } print(f"Production URL Config Updated") print(f"\nFunction: {prod_config['function']}:{prod_config['alias']}") print(f"Auth: {prod_config['auth']}") print(f"Origin: {prod_config['origin']}") print(f"CORS Cache: {prod_config['cache_seconds']} seconds ({prod_config['cache_seconds']/3600} hours)") # Production stats prod_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"\nProduction Users: {prod_users}") active_posts = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"Active Job Posts: {active_posts}")
Example 5: Secure URL with Strict CORS

Apply strict CORS policy for security-sensitive endpoints.

#!/bin/bash
# Apply strict CORS for admin endpoints

aws lambda update-function-url-config \
    --function-name jobpost-admin-api \
    --auth-type AWS_IAM \
    --cors '{
        "AllowOrigins": ["https://admin.jobpost.com"],
        "AllowMethods": ["POST", "PUT", "DELETE"],
        "AllowHeaders": ["Content-Type", "Authorization", "X-Admin-Token"],
        "AllowCredentials": true,
        "MaxAge": 300
    }' \
    --region us-east-1

echo "\n✓ Strict CORS applied to admin API"
{ "FunctionUrl": "https://admin789xyz123.lambda-url.us-east-1.on.aws/", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-admin-api", "AuthType": "AWS_IAM", "Cors": { "AllowOrigins": ["https://admin.jobpost.com"], "AllowMethods": ["POST", "PUT", "DELETE"], "AllowHeaders": ["Content-Type", "Authorization", "X-Admin-Token"], "AllowCredentials": true, "MaxAge": 300 }, "LastModifiedTime": "2024-01-15T17:30:00.000+0000", "InvokeMode": "BUFFERED" } ✓ Strict CORS applied to admin API
# Security audit for admin CORS from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost security_config = { 'function': 'jobpost-admin-api', 'auth': 'AWS_IAM', 'single_origin': True, 'no_get': True, 'short_cache': True, 'credentials': True } print(f"Admin API Security Configuration") print(f"\nFunction: {security_config['function']}") print(f"\nSecurity Measures:") print(f" ✓ IAM authentication required") print(f" ✓ Single origin (no wildcard)") print(f" ✓ No GET method (write-only)") print(f" ✓ Short CORS cache (5 min)") print(f" ✓ Credentials required") # Admin users admin_users = User.objects.filter(is_superuser=True) print(f"\nAdmin Users: {admin_users.count()}") for admin in admin_users[:3]: print(f" - {admin.username}")

list-function-event-invoke-configs

Returns a list of function event invoke configurations for asynchronous invocations. Essential for monitoring retry policies and destination configurations for Django background jobs.

Syntax
aws lambda list-function-event-invoke-configs \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    [--max-items <number>] \
    [--marker <token>]
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name of the Lambda function to list configurations for.
  • --max-items (integer, optional): Maximum number of configurations to return (1-50).
  • --marker (string, optional): Pagination token from previous response.
Return Value
  • FunctionEventInvokeConfigs (list): Array of event invoke configuration objects.
  • FunctionArn (string): ARN of the function, version, or alias.
  • MaximumRetryAttempts (integer): Maximum retry attempts (0-2) for failed invocations.
  • MaximumEventAgeInSeconds (integer): Maximum age of events before discarding (60-21600).
  • DestinationConfig (object): Destinations for successful and failed invocations.
  • NextMarker (string): Pagination token for retrieving more results.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: List Async Configs for Job Processor

Retrieve all event invoke configurations for the job processing function.

#!/bin/bash
# List async configs for job processor

aws lambda list-function-event-invoke-configs \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --region us-east-1
{ "FunctionEventInvokeConfigs": [ { "LastModified": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 2, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 3600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnSuccess": { "Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:processed-jobs-queue" }, "OnFailure": { "Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq" } } }, { "LastModified": "2024-01-14T08:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor:prod", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 1800 } ] }
# Track async configurations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost configs = [ { 'function': 'jobpost-processor', 'qualifier': None, 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600, 'on_success': 'processed-jobs-queue', 'on_failure': 'failed-jobs-dlq' }, { 'function': 'jobpost-processor', 'qualifier': 'prod', 'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800, 'on_success': None, 'on_failure': None } ] print(f"Async Invoke Configurations") print(f"\nFunction: jobpost-processor") print(f"Configs found: {len(configs)}\n") for cfg in configs: qualifier = cfg['qualifier'] or 'base' print(f"{qualifier}:") print(f" Retries: {cfg['retries']}") print(f" Max Age: {cfg['max_age']}s ({cfg['max_age']/60}min)") if cfg['on_success']: print(f" On Success: {cfg['on_success']}") if cfg['on_failure']: print(f" On Failure: {cfg['on_failure']}") print()
Example 2: Check Retry Policy Across Versions

List configs to compare retry policies across function versions.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare retry policies

FUNCTION="jobpost-processor"

echo "Retry Policies for $FUNCTION"
echo "=============================\n"

aws lambda list-function-event-invoke-configs \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" \
    --query 'FunctionEventInvokeConfigs[].{
        Version: FunctionArn | split(":") | .[-1],
        Retries: MaximumRetryAttempts,
        MaxAge: MaximumEventAgeInSeconds
    }' \
    --output table
Retry Policies for jobpost-processor ============================= ----------------------------------------------- | ListFunctionEventInvokeConfigs | +---------+---------+------------------------+ | MaxAge | Retries | Version | +---------+---------+------------------------+ | 3600 | 2 | jobpost-processor | | 1800 | 1 | prod | | 7200 | 2 | staging | +---------+---------+------------------------+
# Compare retry configurations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost policies = [ {'version': 'base', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600}, {'version': 'prod', 'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800}, {'version': 'staging', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 7200} ] print(f"Retry Policy Comparison") print(f"\n{'Version':<12} {'Retries':<10} {'Max Age':<15}") print(f"{'-'*40}") for policy in policies: age_mins = policy['max_age'] / 60 print(f"{policy['version']:<12} {policy['retries']:<10} {age_mins:.0f} min") # Recommendations print(f"\nRecommendations:") print(f" - prod: Low retries for faster failures") print(f" - staging: Extended age for debugging") # Job stats pending = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=False).count() print(f"\nPending jobs to process: {pending}")
Example 3: Verify Destination Configurations

List configs to audit success/failure destinations.

#!/bin/bash
# Audit destination configurations

FUNCTION="jobpost-notifications"

echo "Destination Audit for $FUNCTION\n"

CONFIGS=$(aws lambda list-function-event-invoke-configs \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" \
    --output json)

echo "$CONFIGS" | jq '.FunctionEventInvokeConfigs[] | {
    Qualifier: .FunctionArn | split(":") | .[-1],
    OnSuccess: .DestinationConfig.OnSuccess.Destination // "Not configured",
    OnFailure: .DestinationConfig.OnFailure.Destination // "Not configured"
}'

# Check for missing destinations
MISSING=$(echo "$CONFIGS" | jq '[.FunctionEventInvokeConfigs[] | select(.DestinationConfig.OnFailure == null)] | length')
if [ "$MISSING" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "\n⚠️ Warning: $MISSING config(s) without failure destination"
else
    echo "\n✓ All configs have failure destinations"
fi
Destination Audit for jobpost-notifications { "Qualifier": "jobpost-notifications", "OnSuccess": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:notification-sent", "OnFailure": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:notification-dlq" } { "Qualifier": "prod", "OnSuccess": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:prod-notifications", "OnFailure": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:prod-notification-dlq" } ✓ All configs have failure destinations
# Audit notification destinations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost destinations = [ { 'qualifier': 'base', 'on_success': 'notification-sent (SNS)', 'on_failure': 'notification-dlq (SQS)' }, { 'qualifier': 'prod', 'on_success': 'prod-notifications (SNS)', 'on_failure': 'prod-notification-dlq (SQS)' } ] print(f"Destination Configuration Audit") print(f"\nFunction: jobpost-notifications") print(f"\n{'Qualifier':<12} {'On Success':<25} {'On Failure':<25}") print(f"{'-'*62}") for dest in destinations: print(f"{dest['qualifier']:<12} {dest['on_success']:<25} {dest['on_failure']:<25}") print(f"\n✓ All qualifiers have failure handling") # Notification recipients authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nPotential notification recipients: {authors}")
Example 4: Export Configs for Documentation

Generate async config documentation for operations team.

#!/bin/bash
# Export async configs to documentation

FUNCTIONS=("jobpost-processor" "jobpost-notifications" "jobpost-export")
OUTPUT="async-configs-report.txt"

echo "Async Invoke Configuration Report" > "$OUTPUT"
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> "$OUTPUT"
echo "" >> "$OUTPUT"

for func in "${FUNCTIONS[@]}"; do
    echo "\n## $func" >> "$OUTPUT"
    
    CONFIGS=$(aws lambda list-function-event-invoke-configs \
        --function-name "$func" \
        --query 'FunctionEventInvokeConfigs' \
        --output json 2>/dev/null)
    
    COUNT=$(echo "$CONFIGS" | jq 'length')
    echo "Configurations: $COUNT" >> "$OUTPUT"
    
    echo "$CONFIGS" | jq -r '.[] | "  - \(.FunctionArn | split(":") | .[-1]): retries=\(.MaximumRetryAttempts), maxAge=\(.MaximumEventAgeInSeconds)s"' >> "$OUTPUT"
done

echo "\n✓ Report saved to $OUTPUT"
cat "$OUTPUT"
✓ Report saved to async-configs-report.txt Async Invoke Configuration Report Generated: Mon Jan 15 18:00:00 UTC 2024 ## jobpost-processor Configurations: 3 - jobpost-processor: retries=2, maxAge=3600s - prod: retries=1, maxAge=1800s - staging: retries=2, maxAge=7200s ## jobpost-notifications Configurations: 2 - jobpost-notifications: retries=2, maxAge=3600s - prod: retries=2, maxAge=3600s ## jobpost-export Configurations: 1 - jobpost-export: retries=0, maxAge=21600s
# Generate async config report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone report = { 'jobpost-processor': [ {'qualifier': 'base', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600}, {'qualifier': 'prod', 'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800}, {'qualifier': 'staging', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 7200} ], 'jobpost-notifications': [ {'qualifier': 'base', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600}, {'qualifier': 'prod', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600} ], 'jobpost-export': [ {'qualifier': 'base', 'retries': 0, 'max_age': 21600} ] } print(f"Async Configuration Report") print(f"Generated: {timezone.now()}\n") for func, configs in report.items(): print(f"{func}:") for cfg in configs: print(f" {cfg['qualifier']}: retries={cfg['retries']}, maxAge={cfg['max_age']}s") print() total_configs = sum(len(c) for c in report.values()) print(f"Total configurations: {total_configs}")
Example 5: Check for Missing Async Configs

Identify functions that might need async configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Find functions missing async configs

echo "Checking async configs for all job-related functions...\n"

FUNCTIONS=$(aws lambda list-functions \
    --query 'Functions[?contains(FunctionName, `jobpost`)].FunctionName' \
    --output text)

MISSING=()
CONFIGURED=()

for func in $FUNCTIONS; do
    COUNT=$(aws lambda list-function-event-invoke-configs \
        --function-name "$func" \
        --query 'length(FunctionEventInvokeConfigs)' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ] || [ -z "$COUNT" ]; then
        MISSING+=("$func")
    else
        CONFIGURED+=("$func")
    fi
done

echo "Configured (${#CONFIGURED[@]}):"
printf '  ✓ %s\n' "${CONFIGURED[@]}"

echo "\nMissing config (${#MISSING[@]}):"
if [ ${#MISSING[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    printf '  ⚠️ %s\n' "${MISSING[@]}"
else
    echo "  None - all functions configured"
fi
Checking async configs for all job-related functions... Configured (3): ✓ jobpost-processor ✓ jobpost-notifications ✓ jobpost-export Missing config (2): ⚠️ jobpost-api ⚠️ jobpost-health-check
# Check async config coverage from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost configured = ['jobpost-processor', 'jobpost-notifications', 'jobpost-export'] missing = ['jobpost-api', 'jobpost-health-check'] print(f"Async Config Coverage Report") print(f"\nConfigured ({len(configured)}):") for func in configured: print(f" ✓ {func}") print(f"\nMissing ({len(missing)}):") for func in missing: print(f" ⚠️ {func}") coverage = len(configured) / (len(configured) + len(missing)) * 100 print(f"\nCoverage: {coverage:.0f}%") if missing: print(f"\nRecommendation: Configure async handling for:") for func in missing: print(f" - {func}") # System stats job_count = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nTotal job posts in system: {job_count}")

get-function-event-invoke-config

Returns the async invoke configuration for a function, version, or alias. Used to retrieve retry settings and destination configurations for Django background processing.

Syntax
aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    [--qualifier <alias-or-version>]
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name of the Lambda function, version, or ARN.
  • --qualifier (string, optional): Alias name or version number to get configuration for.
Return Value
  • FunctionArn (string): ARN of the function, version, or alias.
  • MaximumRetryAttempts (integer): Maximum retry attempts (0-2) for failed invocations.
  • MaximumEventAgeInSeconds (integer): Maximum event age before discarding (60-21600).
  • DestinationConfig (object): OnSuccess and OnFailure destination ARNs.
  • LastModified (string): Timestamp of last configuration change.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: Get Async Config for Job Processor

Retrieve async invoke settings for Django job processing function.

#!/bin/bash
# Get async config

aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 2, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 3600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnSuccess": { "Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:processed-jobs" }, "OnFailure": { "Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq" } } }
# Monitor async config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost config = { 'function': 'jobpost-processor', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600, 'on_success': 'processed-jobs', 'on_failure': 'failed-jobs-dlq' } print(f"Async Config: {config['function']}") print(f"\nRetry Policy:") print(f" Attempts: {config['retries']}") print(f" Max Age: {config['max_age']}s ({config['max_age']/60} min)") print(f"\nDestinations:") print(f" Success: {config['on_success']}") print(f" Failure: {config['on_failure']}") pending = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=False).count() print(f"\nPending jobs: {pending}")
Example 2: Get Production Alias Config

Retrieve async config for production alias.

#!/bin/bash
# Get production async config

aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --qualifier prod \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T14:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor:prod", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 1800, "DestinationConfig": { "OnFailure": { "Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:prod-dlq" } } }
# Production config analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost prod_config = { 'qualifier': 'prod', 'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800, 'on_failure': 'prod-dlq' } print(f"Production Async Config") print(f"\nRetries: {prod_config['retries']} (conservative)") print(f"Max Age: {prod_config['max_age']}s (30 min)") print(f"DLQ: {prod_config['on_failure']}") active_posts = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"\nActive posts: {active_posts}")
Example 3: Compare Base vs Alias Config

Compare configurations between base function and alias.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare configs

FUNCTION="jobpost-notifications"

echo "Base function config:"
aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" \
    --query '{Retries:MaximumRetryAttempts,MaxAge:MaximumEventAgeInSeconds}'

echo "\nProd alias config:"
aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" \
    --qualifier prod \
    --query '{Retries:MaximumRetryAttempts,MaxAge:MaximumEventAgeInSeconds}'
Base function config: { "Retries": 2, "MaxAge": 3600 } Prod alias config: { "Retries": 1, "MaxAge": 1800 }
# Compare configs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost base = {'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600} prod = {'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800} print(f"Config Comparison") print(f"\n{'Setting':<12} {'Base':<10} {'Prod':<10}") print(f"{'-'*32}") print(f"{'Retries':<12} {base['retries']:<10} {prod['retries']:<10}") print(f"{'Max Age':<12} {base['max_age']}s{'':<5} {prod['max_age']}s") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nNotification recipients: {authors}")
Example 4: Verify DLQ Configuration

Check if failure destination is properly configured.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify DLQ setup

CONFIG=$(aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor)

DLQ=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.DestinationConfig.OnFailure.Destination // "NOT SET"')

if [ "$DLQ" != "NOT SET" ]; then
    echo "✓ DLQ configured: $DLQ"
else
    echo "⚠️ No DLQ configured!"
fi
✓ DLQ configured: arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq
# Verify DLQ from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost dlq = 'failed-jobs-dlq' if dlq: print(f"✓ DLQ configured: {dlq}") print(f" Failed jobs will be preserved") else: print(f"⚠️ No DLQ! Failed events lost") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nTotal jobs: {jobs}")
Example 5: Export Config to JSON

Save configuration for backup or documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export config

FUNCTION="jobpost-processor"
OUTPUT="${FUNCTION}-async-config.json"

aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" \
    --output json > "$OUTPUT"

echo "✓ Config saved to $OUTPUT"
cat "$OUTPUT" | jq '{retries:.MaximumRetryAttempts,maxAge:.MaximumEventAgeInSeconds}'
✓ Config saved to jobpost-processor-async-config.json { "retries": 2, "maxAge": 3600 }
# Export config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json config = {'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600} print(f"Config exported: {json.dumps(config)}") posts = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"Total posts: {posts}")

put-function-event-invoke-config

Configures async invoke settings for a function including retry attempts and destinations. Creates or replaces the entire configuration for Django Lambda functions.

Syntax
aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    [--qualifier <alias-or-version>] \
    [--maximum-retry-attempts <number>] \
    [--maximum-event-age-in-seconds <seconds>] \
    [--destination-config <config>]
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name of the Lambda function.
  • --qualifier (string, optional): Alias or version to configure.
  • --maximum-retry-attempts (integer, optional): Retries for failed async invocations (0-2).
  • --maximum-event-age-in-seconds (integer, optional): Max age before discarding events (60-21600).
  • --destination-config (structure, optional): OnSuccess/OnFailure destination ARNs (SQS, SNS, Lambda, EventBridge).
Return Value
  • FunctionArn (string): ARN of the configured function.
  • MaximumRetryAttempts (integer): Configured retry attempts.
  • MaximumEventAgeInSeconds (integer): Configured max event age.
  • DestinationConfig (object): Configured destinations.
  • LastModified (string): Timestamp of configuration.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: Configure Job Processor with DLQ

Set up async config with dead-letter queue for job processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Configure async with DLQ

aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 2 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 3600 \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:processed-jobs"},
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq"}
    }' \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T16:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 2, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 3600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:processed-jobs"}, "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq"} } }
# Configure async processing from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost config = { 'function': 'jobpost-processor', 'retries': 2, 'max_age': 3600, 'on_success': 'processed-jobs', 'on_failure': 'failed-jobs-dlq' } print(f"Async Config Created") print(f"\nFunction: {config['function']}") print(f"Retries: {config['retries']}") print(f"Max Age: {config['max_age']}s") print(f"\nDestinations:") print(f" Success -> {config['on_success']}") print(f" Failure -> {config['on_failure']}") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nJobs to process: {jobs}")
Example 2: Configure Production with SNS Notification

Set up production alias with SNS for failure alerts.

#!/bin/bash
# Production config with SNS alerts

aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --qualifier prod \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 1 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 1800 \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:prod-alerts"}
    }' \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T16:30:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor:prod", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 1800, "DestinationConfig": { "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:prod-alerts"} } }
# Production alert config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost prod_config = { 'qualifier': 'prod', 'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800, 'alert_topic': 'prod-alerts' } print(f"Production Config") print(f"\nRetries: {prod_config['retries']} (fail fast)") print(f"Max Age: {prod_config['max_age']}s (30 min)") print(f"Alerts: SNS -> {prod_config['alert_topic']}") users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"\nProduction users: {users}")
Example 3: No Retries for Idempotent Operations

Configure zero retries for operations that shouldn't repeat.

#!/bin/bash
# Zero retries for email sending

aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-email-sender \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 0 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 300 \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:email-failures"}
    }' \
    --region us-east-1

echo "\n✓ Zero-retry config for email sender"
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T17:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-email-sender", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 0, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 300, "DestinationConfig": { "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:email-failures"} } } ✓ Zero-retry config for email sender
# Zero-retry config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost email_config = { 'function': 'jobpost-email-sender', 'retries': 0, 'reason': 'Prevent duplicate emails' } print(f"Email Sender Config") print(f"\nRetries: {email_config['retries']}") print(f"Reason: {email_config['reason']}") print(f"\n✓ Emails won't be sent twice") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nPotential recipients: {authors}")
Example 4: Chain to Another Lambda on Success

Configure Lambda-to-Lambda chaining for workflows.

#!/bin/bash
# Chain functions on success

aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-validator \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 2 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 600 \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-publisher"},
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:validation-failures"}
    }'
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T17:30:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-validator", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 2, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-publisher"}, "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:validation-failures"} } }
# Function chaining from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost workflow = { 'step1': 'jobpost-validator', 'step2': 'jobpost-publisher', 'on_failure': 'validation-failures' } print(f"Workflow Configuration") print(f"\n{workflow['step1']} -> {workflow['step2']}") print(f"Failures -> {workflow['on_failure']}") posts = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=False).count() print(f"\nPending validation: {posts}")
Example 5: EventBridge Destination for Analytics

Send async results to EventBridge for analytics pipeline.

#!/bin/bash
# EventBridge for analytics

aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-analytics \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 2 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 21600 \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:events:us-east-1:123456789012:event-bus/analytics-bus"},
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:events:us-east-1:123456789012:event-bus/analytics-bus"}
    }'
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-15T18:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-analytics", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 2, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 21600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:events:us-east-1:123456789012:event-bus/analytics-bus"}, "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:events:us-east-1:123456789012:event-bus/analytics-bus"} } }
# Analytics pipeline config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost analytics = { 'function': 'jobpost-analytics', 'bus': 'analytics-bus', 'max_age': 21600 } print(f"Analytics Config") print(f"\nFunction: {analytics['function']}") print(f"Event Bus: {analytics['bus']}") print(f"Max Age: {analytics['max_age']}s (6 hours)") posts = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nPosts to analyze: {posts}")

update-function-event-invoke-config

Updates async invoke configuration for a function. Only updates specified parameters while preserving others. Use for partial updates to Django Lambda retry and destination settings.

Syntax
aws lambda update-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    [--qualifier <alias-or-version>] \
    [--maximum-retry-attempts <number>] \
    [--maximum-event-age-in-seconds <seconds>] \
    [--destination-config <config>]
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name of the Lambda function.
  • --qualifier (string, optional): Alias or version to update.
  • --maximum-retry-attempts (integer, optional): New retry attempts (0-2).
  • --maximum-event-age-in-seconds (integer, optional): New max event age (60-21600).
  • --destination-config (structure, optional): Updated destination configuration.
Return Value
  • FunctionArn (string): ARN of the updated function.
  • MaximumRetryAttempts (integer): Current retry attempts after update.
  • MaximumEventAgeInSeconds (integer): Current max event age after update.
  • DestinationConfig (object): Current destination configuration.
  • LastModified (string): Timestamp of update.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: Reduce Retry Attempts

Update only the retry count without affecting other settings.

#!/bin/bash
# Reduce retries to 1

aws lambda update-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 1 \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-16T14:20:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 3600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq"} } }
# Update retries from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost update = {'retries': 1, 'reason': 'Reduce retry latency'} print(f"Retry Config Updated") print(f"\nNew retries: {update['retries']}") print(f"Reason: {update['reason']}") print(f"\nOther settings preserved") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"Jobs affected: {jobs}")
Example 2: Update Max Event Age

Extend event retention period.

#!/bin/bash
# Extend max age to 6 hours

aws lambda update-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 21600 \
    --region us-east-1

echo "\n✓ Max age extended to 6 hours"
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-16T15:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 21600 } ✓ Max age extended to 6 hours
# Update max age from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost old_age = 3600 new_age = 21600 print(f"Max Age Updated") print(f"\nOld: {old_age}s ({old_age/3600} hour)") print(f"New: {new_age}s ({new_age/3600} hours)") users = User.objects.count() print(f"\nUsers: {users}")
Example 3: Add Success Destination

Add success destination without removing failure destination.

#!/bin/bash
# Add success destination

aws lambda update-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:success-queue"},
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq"}
    }' \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-16T15:30:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 21600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnSuccess": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:success-queue"}, "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:failed-jobs-dlq"} } }
# Add success destination from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost destinations = { 'on_success': 'success-queue', 'on_failure': 'failed-jobs-dlq' } print(f"Destinations Updated") print(f"\nSuccess -> {destinations['on_success']}") print(f"Failure -> {destinations['on_failure']}") posts = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"\nActive posts: {posts}")
Example 4: Update Production Alias Config

Modify production alias settings for tighter SLA.

#!/bin/bash
# Tighten production config

aws lambda update-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --qualifier prod \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 0 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 900

echo "\n✓ Prod: 0 retries, 15 min max age"
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-16T16:00:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor:prod", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 0, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 900 } ✓ Prod: 0 retries, 15 min max age
# Update prod alias from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost prod_update = { 'qualifier': 'prod', 'retries': 0, 'max_age': 900, 'reason': 'Fail fast in production' } print(f"Production Config Updated") print(f"\nRetries: {prod_update['retries']} (no retries)") print(f"Max Age: {prod_update['max_age']}s (15 min)") print(f"Reason: {prod_update['reason']}") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nAuthors: {authors}")
Example 5: Change DLQ to SNS Topic

Switch failure destination from SQS to SNS for alerts.

#!/bin/bash
# Switch to SNS for alerts

aws lambda update-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --destination-config '{
        "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:failure-alerts"}
    }' \
    --region us-east-1
{ "LastModified": "2024-01-16T16:30:00.000+0000", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-processor", "MaximumRetryAttempts": 1, "MaximumEventAgeInSeconds": 21600, "DestinationConfig": { "OnFailure": {"Destination": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:failure-alerts"} } }
# Switch to SNS from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost change = { 'old': 'SQS DLQ', 'new': 'SNS Topic', 'topic': 'failure-alerts' } print(f"Failure Destination Changed") print(f"\nFrom: {change['old']}") print(f"To: {change['new']} ({change['topic']})") print(f"\n✓ Now sends alerts on failure") skills = Skills.objects.count() print(f"\nSkills tracked: {skills}")

delete-function-event-invoke-config

Deletes async invoke configuration for a function, version, or alias. Resets to default Lambda behavior with 2 retries and 6-hour max age. Use when cleaning up Django Lambda configurations.

Syntax
aws lambda delete-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    [--qualifier <alias-or-version>]
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the Lambda function.
  • --qualifier (string, optional): Alias or version to delete config from.
Return Value
  • No output on success (HTTP 204).
  • Function reverts to default async invoke behavior.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: Delete Base Function Config

Remove async config from base function.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete async config

aws lambda delete-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Async config deleted, using defaults"
✓ Async config deleted, using defaults
# Delete async config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost config_deleted = True defaults = {'retries': 2, 'max_age': 21600} print(f"Async Config Deleted") print(f"\nFunction now uses defaults:") print(f" Retries: {defaults['retries']}") print(f" Max Age: {defaults['max_age']}s (6 hours)") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nJobs: {jobs}")
Example 2: Delete Alias Config

Remove async config from a specific alias.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete prod alias config

aws lambda delete-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name jobpost-processor \
    --qualifier prod \
    --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Prod alias config removed"
✓ Prod alias config removed
# Delete alias config from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost alias_deleted = 'prod' print(f"Alias Config Deleted: {alias_deleted}") print(f"\nProd alias inherits base config") print(f"or uses Lambda defaults") users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"\nActive users: {users}")
Example 3: Clean Up All Async Configs

Remove async configs from all functions.

#!/bin/bash
# Clean up all async configs

FUNCTIONS=("jobpost-processor" "jobpost-notifications" "jobpost-analytics")

for func in "${FUNCTIONS[@]}"; do
    aws lambda delete-function-event-invoke-config \
        --function-name "$func" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "✓ Deleted: $func" || \
        echo "- No config: $func"
done
✓ Deleted: jobpost-processor ✓ Deleted: jobpost-notifications - No config: jobpost-analytics
# Clean up configs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost functions = ['jobpost-processor', 'jobpost-notifications', 'jobpost-analytics'] deleted = 2 no_config = 1 print(f"Cleanup Complete") print(f"\nDeleted: {deleted}") print(f"No config: {no_config}") for func in functions[:deleted]: print(f" ✓ {func}") posts = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nTotal posts: {posts}")
Example 4: Delete Before Reconfiguring

Delete old config before applying new settings.

#!/bin/bash
# Reset and reconfigure

FUNCTION="jobpost-processor"

# Delete existing
aws lambda delete-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" 2>/dev/null

echo "Existing config removed"

# Apply new
aws lambda put-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" \
    --maximum-retry-attempts 1 \
    --maximum-event-age-in-seconds 1800

echo "✓ New config applied"
Existing config removed ✓ New config applied
# Reset and reconfigure from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost steps = ['Delete old config', 'Apply new config'] print(f"Config Reset") for i, step in enumerate(steps, 1): print(f"\n{i}. {step} ✓") new_config = {'retries': 1, 'max_age': 1800} print(f"\nNew Settings:") print(f" Retries: {new_config['retries']}") print(f" Max Age: {new_config['max_age']}s") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nAuthors: {authors}")
Example 5: Verify Config Deleted

Confirm config deletion by checking for resource.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete and verify

FUNCTION="jobpost-processor"

aws lambda delete-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION"

# Verify deletion
if aws lambda get-function-event-invoke-config \
    --function-name "$FUNCTION" 2>&1 | grep -q "ResourceNotFound"; then
    echo "✓ Config successfully deleted"
else
    echo "⚠️ Config still exists"
fi
✓ Config successfully deleted
# Verify deletion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost deleted = True verified = True if deleted and verified: print(f"✓ Config successfully deleted") print(f"\nFunction uses defaults:") print(f" - 2 retries") print(f" - 6 hour max age") print(f" - No custom destinations") locations = Location.objects.count() print(f"\nLocations: {locations}")

get-provisioned-concurrency-config

Returns the provisioned concurrency configuration for a function alias or version. Use to monitor warm instance status and allocation for Django Lambda functions requiring consistent cold start elimination.

Syntax
aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    --qualifier <alias-or-version>
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the Lambda function.
  • --qualifier (string, required): Alias name or version number (cannot use $LATEST).
Return Value
  • RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions (integer): Number of instances requested.
  • AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions (integer): Ready instances available for requests.
  • AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions (integer): Total instances allocated.
  • Status (string): IN_PROGRESS, READY, or FAILED.
  • StatusReason (string): Reason if status is FAILED.
  • LastModified (string): Timestamp of last configuration change.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: Check Production Provisioned Concurrency

Verify warm instances are ready for production traffic.

#!/bin/bash
# Check prod provisioned concurrency

aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier prod \
    --region us-east-1
{ "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "Status": "READY", "LastModified": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000+0000" }
# Check provisioned concurrency from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost pc_config = { 'requested': 100, 'available': 100, 'allocated': 100, 'status': 'READY' } print(f"Provisioned Concurrency Status") print(f"\nRequested: {pc_config['requested']}") print(f"Available: {pc_config['available']}") print(f"Allocated: {pc_config['allocated']}") print(f"Status: {pc_config['status']} ✓") active_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"\nActive jobs to serve: {active_jobs}")
Example 2: Monitor Provisioning Progress

Check status during provisioning allocation.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor provisioning progress

while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
        --function-name jobpost-api \
        --qualifier prod \
        --query 'Status' --output text)
    
    AVAIL=$(aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
        --function-name jobpost-api \
        --qualifier prod \
        --query 'AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions')
    
    echo "Status: $STATUS | Available: $AVAIL"
    
    [ "$STATUS" = "READY" ] && break
    sleep 10
done

echo "✓ Provisioning complete"
Status: IN_PROGRESS | Available: 25 Status: IN_PROGRESS | Available: 50 Status: IN_PROGRESS | Available: 75 Status: READY | Available: 100 ✓ Provisioning complete
# Monitor provisioning from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost progress = [25, 50, 75, 100] print(f"Provisioning Progress") for p in progress: status = 'READY' if p == 100 else 'IN_PROGRESS' print(f" Available: {p} | Status: {status}") print(f"\n✓ Provisioning complete") users = User.objects.count() print(f"Users ready to serve: {users}")
Example 3: Verify Capacity for High Traffic

Ensure sufficient capacity before traffic spike.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify capacity

REQUIRED=200

AVAILABLE=$(aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier prod \
    --query 'AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions')

if [ "$AVAILABLE" -ge "$REQUIRED" ]; then
    echo "✓ Capacity OK: $AVAILABLE >= $REQUIRED"
else
    echo "⚠️ Insufficient: $AVAILABLE < $REQUIRED"
fi
✓ Capacity OK: 200 >= 200
# Verify capacity from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost required = 200 available = 200 if available >= required: print(f"✓ Capacity OK: {available} >= {required}") else: print(f"⚠️ Insufficient: {available} < {required}") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nJobs to handle: {jobs}")
Example 4: Compare Dev vs Prod Config

Check provisioned concurrency across environments.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare environments

echo "Dev environment:"
aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier dev \
    --query '{Requested:RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions,Status:Status}'

echo "\nProd environment:"
aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier prod \
    --query '{Requested:RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions,Status:Status}'
Dev environment: { "Requested": 10, "Status": "READY" } Prod environment: { "Requested": 100, "Status": "READY" }
# Compare environments from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost envs = { 'dev': {'requested': 10, 'status': 'READY'}, 'prod': {'requested': 100, 'status': 'READY'} } print(f"Provisioned Concurrency by Environment") for env, config in envs.items(): print(f"\n{env.upper()}:") print(f" Requested: {config['requested']}") print(f" Status: {config['status']}") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nAuthors to notify: {authors}")
Example 5: Check for Provisioning Failures

Detect and report provisioning issues.

#!/bin/bash
# Check for failures

RESULT=$(aws lambda get-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier prod)

STATUS=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.Status')
REASON=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.StatusReason // "N/A"')

if [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "✗ Provisioning failed: $REASON"
    exit 1
else
    echo "✓ Status: $STATUS"
fi
✓ Status: READY
# Check for failures from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost status = 'READY' reason = None if status == 'FAILED': print(f"✗ Provisioning failed: {reason}") else: print(f"✓ Status: {status}") print(f"\nAll instances ready") skills = Skills.objects.count() print(f"\nSkills indexed: {skills}")

put-provisioned-concurrency-config

Configures provisioned concurrency for a function alias or version to eliminate cold starts. Essential for Django Lambda functions requiring consistent low-latency responses.

Syntax
aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    --qualifier <alias-or-version> \
    --provisioned-concurrent-executions <number>
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the Lambda function.
  • --qualifier (string, required): Alias name or version number (cannot use $LATEST).
  • --provisioned-concurrent-executions (integer, required): Number of instances to keep warm (1-unreserved account concurrency).
Return Value
  • RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions (integer): Number of instances requested.
  • AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions (integer): Instances currently ready.
  • AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions (integer): Instances being allocated.
  • Status (string): IN_PROGRESS while provisioning, READY when complete.
  • LastModified (string): Timestamp of configuration.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: Provision Production Instances

Allocate warm instances for production traffic.

#!/bin/bash
# Provision 100 instances for prod

aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier prod \
    --provisioned-concurrent-executions 100 \
    --region us-east-1
{ "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 0, "AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 0, "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "LastModified": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000+0000" }
# Provision production from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost pc_request = { 'function': 'jobpost-api', 'qualifier': 'prod', 'requested': 100, 'status': 'IN_PROGRESS' } print(f"Provisioning Started") print(f"\nFunction: {pc_request['function']}:{pc_request['qualifier']}") print(f"Requested: {pc_request['requested']} instances") print(f"Status: {pc_request['status']}") print(f"\nWarm instances eliminate cold starts") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"Jobs to serve: {jobs}")
Example 2: Scale for Traffic Spike

Increase provisioned concurrency before expected traffic.

#!/bin/bash
# Scale up for traffic spike

echo "Scaling provisioned concurrency for job fair event..."

aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier prod \
    --provisioned-concurrent-executions 500

echo "✓ Scaling to 500 instances initiated"
Scaling provisioned concurrency for job fair event... { "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 500, "AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "LastModified": "2024-01-15T12:00:00.000+0000" } ✓ Scaling to 500 instances initiated
# Scale for event from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost scale_up = { 'event': 'Job Fair', 'current': 100, 'target': 500, 'status': 'IN_PROGRESS' } print(f"Scaling for {scale_up['event']}") print(f"\nCurrent: {scale_up['current']}") print(f"Target: {scale_up['target']}") print(f"Status: {scale_up['status']}") users = User.objects.count() print(f"\nExpected users: {users * 5}")
Example 3: Provision Version-Specific Concurrency

Set concurrency for a specific function version.

#!/bin/bash
# Provision specific version

VERSION="5"

aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier "$VERSION" \
    --provisioned-concurrent-executions 50

echo "✓ Version $VERSION provisioned with 50 instances"
{ "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 50, "AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 0, "AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 0, "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "LastModified": "2024-01-15T13:00:00.000+0000" } ✓ Version 5 provisioned with 50 instances
# Provision version from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost version_config = { 'version': 5, 'instances': 50, 'use_case': 'Canary deployment' } print(f"Version Provisioning") print(f"\nVersion: {version_config['version']}") print(f"Instances: {version_config['instances']}") print(f"Use case: {version_config['use_case']}") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nAuthors: {authors}")
Example 4: Minimal Provisioning for Dev

Set small provisioned concurrency for development.

#!/bin/bash
# Dev environment - minimal warm instances

aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --qualifier dev \
    --provisioned-concurrent-executions 5

echo "✓ Dev: 5 warm instances (cost-effective)"
{ "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 5, "AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 0, "AllocatedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 0, "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "LastModified": "2024-01-15T14:00:00.000+0000" } ✓ Dev: 5 warm instances (cost-effective)
# Dev provisioning from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost dev_config = { 'env': 'dev', 'instances': 5, 'cost': 'Low' } print(f"Dev Environment") print(f"\nInstances: {dev_config['instances']}") print(f"Cost tier: {dev_config['cost']}") print(f"\nSufficient for testing") skills = Skills.objects.count() print(f"\nSkills: {skills}")
Example 5: Provision Multiple Aliases

Set up provisioned concurrency across deployment stages.

#!/bin/bash
# Provision all environments

declare -A CONFIGS=(
    ["dev"]=10
    ["staging"]=25
    ["prod"]=100
)

for alias in "${!CONFIGS[@]}"; do
    count="${CONFIGS[$alias]}"
    aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
        --function-name jobpost-api \
        --qualifier "$alias" \
        --provisioned-concurrent-executions "$count"
    echo "✓ $alias: $count instances"
done
✓ dev: 10 instances ✓ staging: 25 instances ✓ prod: 100 instances
# Provision all environments from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost configs = { 'dev': 10, 'staging': 25, 'prod': 100 } print(f"Multi-Environment Provisioning") for env, count in configs.items(): print(f"\n✓ {env}: {count} instances") total = sum(configs.values()) print(f"\nTotal provisioned: {total}") locations = Location.objects.count() print(f"Locations served: {locations}")

list-provisioned-concurrency-configs

Lists provisioned concurrency configurations for a function. Review all warm instance allocations across Django Lambda function versions and aliases.

Syntax
aws lambda list-provisioned-concurrency-configs \
    --function-name <function-name> \
    [--max-items <number>] \
    [--marker <token>]
Parameters Description
  • --function-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the Lambda function.
  • --max-items (integer, optional): Maximum configurations to return.
  • --marker (string, optional): Pagination token from previous response.
Return Value
  • ProvisionedConcurrencyConfigs (array): List of provisioned concurrency configurations.
  • NextMarker (string): Token for next page if more results exist.
5 Detailed Examples
Example 1: List All Provisioned Configs

Get all provisioned concurrency settings for a function.

#!/bin/bash
# List provisioned configs

aws lambda list-provisioned-concurrency-configs \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --region us-east-1
{ "ProvisionedConcurrencyConfigs": [ { "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-api:prod", "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "AvailableProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 100, "Status": "READY" }, { "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-api:staging", "RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions": 25, "Status": "READY" } ] }
# List configs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost configs = [('prod', 100), ('staging', 25)] print(f"Provisioned Concurrency Configs") for alias, count in configs: print(f"\n{alias}: {count} instances") jobs = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"\nJobs: {jobs}")
Example 2: Calculate Total Cost

Sum up all provisioned instances for cost estimation.

#!/bin/bash
# Calculate total provisioned

TOTAL=$(aws lambda list-provisioned-concurrency-configs \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --query 'sum(ProvisionedConcurrencyConfigs[].RequestedProvisionedConcurrentExecutions)')

echo "Total provisioned instances: $TOTAL"
Total provisioned instances: 125
# Calculate total from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost total = 125 print(f"Total provisioned: {total}") users = User.objects.count() print(f"\nUsers: {users}")
Example 3: Check Ready Status

Verify all configs are in READY state.

#!/bin/bash
# Check status

aws lambda list-provisioned-concurrency-configs \
    --function-name jobpost-api \
    --query 'ProvisionedConcurrencyConfigs[].{Alias:FunctionArn,Status:Status}' \
    --output table
------------------------------------------- | ListProvisionedConcurrencyConfigs | +--------------------------+--------------+ | Alias | Status | +--------------------------+--------------+ | jobpost-api:prod | READY | | jobpost-api:staging | READY | +--------------------------+--------------+
# Check status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost statuses = [('prod', 'READY'), ('staging', 'READY')] print(f"Config Status") for alias, status in statuses: print(f"\n{alias}: {status}") authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"\nAuthors: {authors}")
Example 4: Export Config Report

Save configurations to JSON for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
