Manually starts an execution of a CodePipeline pipeline. This command triggers your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline on demand, initiating the build, test, and deployment process for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes without waiting for automatic triggers. Useful for emergency deployments, testing pipeline configurations, and manual release management.

Syntax
aws codepipeline start-pipeline-execution \
  --name {pipeline-name} \
  [--variables name={var-name},value={var-value}] \
  [--client-request-token {token}] \
  [--source-revisions {source-revisions}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): The name of the pipeline to start
  • --variables (list): List of pipeline variables to override for this execution. Format: name=string,value=string
  • --client-request-token (string): Unique token for idempotency to prevent duplicate executions
  • --source-revisions (list): Specific source revisions to use for this execution
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipelineExecutionId: Unique identifier for the started pipeline execution (UUID format)

stop-pipeline-execution

Stops a running CodePipeline execution before it completes. This command halts your Django jobpost application deployment in progress, useful for emergency rollbacks, preventing problematic deployments from reaching production, or stopping accidentally triggered pipelines before changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models are deployed.

Syntax
aws codepipeline stop-pipeline-execution \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --pipeline-execution-id {execution-id} \
  [--abandon | --no-abandon] \
  [--reason {stop-reason}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline to stop
  • --pipeline-execution-id (string, required): The unique ID of the pipeline execution to stop
  • --abandon | --no-abandon (boolean): If true, abandons in-progress actions immediately. If false (default), allows in-progress actions to complete before stopping
  • --reason (string): Human-readable reason for stopping the execution (for audit trails)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipelineExecutionId: ID of the stopped execution confirming the stop request was processed

list-pipelines

Returns a list of all CodePipeline pipelines in your AWS account. This command enumerates all CI/CD pipelines available for your Django jobpost application deployments, showing pipeline names, creation dates, and update times to help manage and organize your deployment infrastructure for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model releases.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-pipelines \
  [--next-token {pagination-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from a previous request for retrieving additional results
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of pipelines to return per request (1-1000)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipelines: Array of pipeline summary objects containing name, version, pipelineType, executionMode, created, and updated timestamps
  • nextToken: Pagination token for retrieving additional results

list-pipeline-executions

Returns a list of execution history for a specific CodePipeline pipeline. This command retrieves the deployment history of your Django jobpost application, showing execution IDs, statuses, start times, and trigger information to track and audit all CI/CD runs that deployed changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-pipeline-executions \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  [--filter pipelineExecutionId={id}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--next-token {pagination-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline to get execution history for
  • --filter (structure): Filter criteria for executions including pipelineExecutionId
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of execution summaries to return (1-100)
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from a previous request
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipelineExecutionSummaries: Array of execution summaries containing pipelineExecutionId, status, startTime, lastUpdateTime, sourceRevisions, trigger, and executionMode
  • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

disable-stage-transition

Disables the flow of artifacts from one stage to another in a CodePipeline pipeline. This command pauses your Django jobpost application deployment pipeline, preventing artifacts from transitioning to subsequent stages until manually re-enabled. Useful for holding deployments for manual approval, testing, or preventing changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models from reaching production during maintenance windows.

Syntax
aws codepipeline disable-stage-transition \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --transition-type {Inbound|Outbound} \
  --reason {reason-text} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the stage
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage where transition should be disabled
  • --transition-type (string, required): Type of transition to disable - Inbound (into stage) or Outbound (from stage)
  • --reason (string, required): Human-readable reason for disabling the transition (for audit and team communication)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful disable. The transition is immediately blocked.

enable-stage-transition

Re-enables the flow of artifacts from one stage to another in a CodePipeline pipeline. This command resumes your Django jobpost application deployment pipeline after it was paused, allowing artifacts to continue transitioning through stages and enabling deployments of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes to proceed to production environments.

Syntax
aws codepipeline enable-stage-transition \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --transition-type {Inbound|Outbound} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the stage
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage where transition should be enabled
  • --transition-type (string, required): Type of transition to enable - Inbound (into stage) or Outbound (from stage)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful enable. The transition is immediately unblocked and artifacts can flow.

update-pipeline

Updates an existing CodePipeline pipeline with new configuration. This command modifies your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline structure, allowing you to add, remove, or modify stages and actions for deploying Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes. Changes take effect on the next pipeline execution.

Syntax
aws codepipeline update-pipeline \
  --pipeline {pipeline-structure} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline (structure, required): Complete updated pipeline structure including name, roleArn, artifactStore, and stages array with actions
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file (recommended for complex configurations)
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipeline: Updated pipeline configuration

put-webhook

Creates or updates a webhook that triggers pipeline executions from external events. This command configures automatic CI/CD triggers for your Django jobpost application, enabling automatic deployments when code changes are pushed to GitHub, GitLab, or other source providers for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model updates.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-webhook \
  --webhook name={webhook-name},targetPipeline={pipeline-name},targetAction={action-name},filters=[{jsonPath={path},matchEquals={value}}],authentication={type},authenticationConfiguration={AllowedIPRange={range},SecretToken={token}} \
  [--tags Key={key},Value={value}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --webhook (structure, required): Webhook configuration containing name, targetPipeline, targetAction, filters array, authentication type, and authenticationConfiguration
  • --tags (list): Tags to apply to the webhook for organization and cost allocation
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file (recommended for complex configurations)
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • webhook: Created webhook object containing definition, url, errorMessage, errorCode, lastTriggered, and arn

delete-webhook

Permanently deletes a webhook from CodePipeline. This command removes automatic trigger configurations for your Django jobpost application deployments, disabling automatic pipeline executions from external events like GitHub pushes for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline delete-webhook \
  --name {webhook-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): The name of the webhook to delete
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful deletion.

list-webhooks

Returns a list of all webhooks configured in your AWS account. This command enumerates all automatic trigger configurations for your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipelines, showing webhook URLs, authentication settings, and target pipelines for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployment automation.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-webhooks \
  [--next-token {pagination-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from a previous request
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of webhooks to return per request
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • webhooks: Array of webhook objects containing definition, url, arn, errorCode, errorMessage, and lastTriggered
  • NextToken: Pagination token for additional results

put-approval-result

Submits the result of a manual approval action to CodePipeline. This command approves or rejects deployments of your Django jobpost application at manual approval gates, controlling when changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models can proceed to production environments after human review.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-approval-result \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --action-name {action-name} \
  --result summary={summary-text},status={Approved|Rejected} \
  --token {approval-token} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the approval action
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage containing the approval action
  • --action-name (string, required): The name of the manual approval action
  • --result (structure, required): The approval result containing summary (reason text) and status (Approved or Rejected)
  • --token (string, required): The approval token from the pending approval action (retrieved from get-pipeline-state)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • approvedAt: Timestamp when the approval was recorded

delete-custom-action-type

Removes a custom action type from the available actions list in CodePipeline. This command deletes custom Django-specific actions you created for your jobpost application CI/CD workflows, cleaning up unused action types for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployment processes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline delete-custom-action-type \
  --category {Source|Build|Deploy|Test|Invoke|Approval} \
  --provider {provider-name} \
  --version {version-number} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --category (string, required): The category of the custom action type (Source, Build, Deploy, Test, Invoke, Approval)
  • --provider (string, required): The provider name of the custom action type
  • --version (string, required): The version identifier of the custom action type
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful deletion.

get-job-details

Returns detailed information about a job for custom action workers. This command retrieves job data, input artifacts, and configuration for custom Django jobpost deployment actions, enabling custom workers to process CI/CD jobs for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments.

Syntax
aws codepipeline get-job-details \
  --job-id {job-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --job-id (string, required): The unique system-generated ID of the job to retrieve details for
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • jobDetails: Job details object containing id, data (actionTypeId, actionConfiguration, pipelineContext, inputArtifacts, outputArtifacts, artifactCredentials), and accountId

get-third-party-job-details

Returns detailed information about a job for third-party action workers. This command retrieves job data and configuration for external integration actions in your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipelines, enabling third-party tools to process deployments of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline get-third-party-job-details \
  --job-id {job-id} \
  --client-token {client-token} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --job-id (string, required): The unique system-generated ID of the third-party job
  • --client-token (string, required): The client token associated with the third-party integration
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • jobDetails: Third-party job details containing id, data (actionTypeId, actionConfiguration, pipelineContext, inputArtifacts, outputArtifacts), and nonce

list-action-executions

Returns a list of action executions for a specified pipeline. This command provides detailed execution history of individual actions in your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline, showing input artifacts, output artifacts, status, and timing for each action that deploys Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-action-executions \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  [--filter pipelineExecutionId={execution-id}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--next-token {pagination-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline to get action executions for
  • --filter (structure): Filter criteria for action executions including pipelineExecutionId
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of action execution results to return (1-100)
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from a previous request
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • actionExecutionDetails: Array of action execution details containing pipelineExecutionId, actionExecutionId, pipelineVersion, stageName, actionName, startTime, lastUpdateTime, status, input, and output
  • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

list-action-types

Returns a list of all action types available for use in pipelines. This command shows all available actions for your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipelines, including AWS-provided actions, third-party integrations, and custom actions for building, testing, and deploying Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model applications.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-action-types \
  [--action-owner-filter {AWS|ThirdParty|Custom}] \
  [--region-filter {region}] \
  [--next-token {pagination-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --action-owner-filter (string): Filter action types by owner (AWS, ThirdParty, Custom)
  • --region-filter (string): Filter action types by region availability
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from a previous request
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • actionTypes: Array of action type objects containing id (category, owner, provider, version), settings, actionConfigurationProperties, inputArtifactDetails, and outputArtifactDetails
  • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

get-action-type

Returns detailed information about a specific action type. This command retrieves configuration requirements, input/output artifact specifications, and settings for actions used in your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipelines for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments.

Syntax
aws codepipeline get-action-type \
  --category {Source|Build|Deploy|Test|Invoke|Approval} \
  --owner {AWS|ThirdParty|Custom} \
  --provider {provider-name} \
  --version {version-number} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --category (string, required): The category of the action type (Source, Build, Deploy, Test, Invoke, Approval)
  • --owner (string, required): The creator/owner of the action type (AWS, ThirdParty, Custom)
  • --provider (string, required): The provider name of the action type (e.g., CodeBuild, S3, GitHub)
  • --version (string, required): The version of the action type
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • actionType: Action type details containing id, executor, actionConfigurationProperties, permissions, urls, and description

poll-for-jobs

Returns available jobs for custom action workers to process. This command polls for pending jobs in your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline custom actions, enabling custom workers to pick up and process deployment tasks for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline poll-for-jobs \
  --action-type-id category={category},owner={owner},provider={provider},version={version} \
  [--max-batch-size {max-batch-size}] \
  [--query-param key={key},value={value}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --action-type-id (structure, required): The action type identifier containing category, owner, provider, and version
  • --max-batch-size (integer): Maximum number of jobs to return in a single poll (1-20)
  • --query-param (map): Key-value pairs to filter jobs for this poll request
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • jobs: Array of available job objects containing id, data (actionTypeId, actionConfiguration, pipelineContext, inputArtifacts, outputArtifacts, artifactCredentials), nonce, and accountId

poll-for-third-party-jobs

Returns available jobs for third-party action workers to process. This command polls for pending third-party integration jobs in your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline, enabling external tools to process deployment tasks for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline poll-for-third-party-jobs \
  --action-type-id category={category},owner={owner},provider={provider},version={version} \
  [--max-batch-size {max-batch-size}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --action-type-id (structure, required): Action type identifier containing category, owner, provider, and version for third-party actions
  • --max-batch-size (integer): Maximum number of third-party jobs to return (1-20)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • jobs: Array of available third-party job objects containing clientId, jobId, and data

put-job-success-result

Reports the successful completion of a job to CodePipeline. This command signals that a custom action job for your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline has completed successfully, allowing the pipeline to proceed with deploying Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes to subsequent stages.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-job-success-result \
  --job-id {job-id} \
  [--current-revision revision={revision},changeIdentifier={change-id},created={timestamp},revisionSummary={summary}] \
  [--continuation-token {token}] \
  [--execution-details summary={summary},percentComplete={percent},externalExecutionId={id}] \
  [--output-variables key={key},value={value}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --job-id (string, required): The unique system-generated ID of the completed job
  • --current-revision (structure): The revision of the artifact being processed with revision ID, change identifier, created timestamp, and summary
  • --continuation-token (string): Token to allow the action to continue in a later execution
  • --execution-details (structure): Execution details containing summary, percentComplete, and externalExecutionId
  • --output-variables (map): Key-value pairs of variables to pass to subsequent actions
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful result submission.

put-job-failure-result

Reports the failure of a job to CodePipeline. This command signals that a custom action job for your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline has failed, providing failure details and stopping the pipeline from deploying potentially broken Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-job-failure-result \
  --job-id {job-id} \
  --failure-details type={JobFailed|ConfigurationError|PermissionError|RevisionOutOfSync|RevisionUnavailable|SystemUnavailable},message={error-message},externalExecutionId={id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --job-id (string, required): The unique system-generated ID of the failed job
  • --failure-details (structure, required): Details about the failure containing type (JobFailed, ConfigurationError, etc.), message, and externalExecutionId
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful failure result submission.

register-webhook-with-third-party

Registers a webhook with a third-party source provider (like GitHub). This command configures the connection between your Django jobpost application repository and CodePipeline, enabling automatic pipeline triggers when code changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models are pushed.

Syntax
aws codepipeline register-webhook-with-third-party \
  --webhook-name {webhook-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --webhook-name (string, required): The name of the webhook to register with the third-party provider
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful registration.

deregister-webhook-with-third-party

Removes the registration between a webhook and a third-party source provider. This command disconnects your Django jobpost application repository from the CodePipeline webhook, disabling automatic triggers for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline deregister-webhook-with-third-party \
  --webhook-name {webhook-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --webhook-name (string, required): The name of the webhook to deregister from the third-party provider
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful deregistration.

retry-stage-execution

Retries a failed stage in a pipeline execution. This command re-runs failed actions in your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipeline without restarting the entire execution, allowing quick recovery from transient failures during Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments.

Syntax
aws codepipeline retry-stage-execution \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --pipeline-execution-id {execution-id} \
  --retry-mode {FAILED_ACTIONS|ALL_ACTIONS} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the stage to retry
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage with failed actions to retry
  • --pipeline-execution-id (string, required): The ID of the pipeline execution containing the failed stage
  • --retry-mode (string, required): The retry mode - FAILED_ACTIONS (retry only failed) or ALL_ACTIONS (retry all actions in stage)
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipelineExecutionId: The ID of the pipeline execution that was retried

rollback-stage

Rolls back a stage to a previous successful execution. This command reverts your Django jobpost application deployment to a known good state, quickly undoing problematic changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models by re-deploying the artifacts from a previous successful execution.

Syntax
aws codepipeline rollback-stage \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --target-pipeline-execution-id {target-execution-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the stage to roll back
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage to roll back
  • --target-pipeline-execution-id (string, required): The ID of the successful pipeline execution to roll back to
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value
  • pipelineExecutionId: The ID of the new pipeline execution created for the rollback

tag-resource

Adds or updates tags for a CodePipeline resource. This command applies metadata tags to your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipelines and webhooks, enabling organization, cost allocation, and access control for resources managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments.

Syntax
aws codepipeline tag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tags key={key},value={value} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string, required): The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline or webhook to tag
  • --tags (list, required): List of key-value pairs to add as tags. Maximum 50 tags per resource
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from a JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string): Generates a JSON skeleton for input parameters
Return Value

No output returned on successful tagging.

untag-resource

Removes tags from a CodePipeline resource. This command deletes metadata tags from your Django jobpost application CI/CD pipelines and webhooks, cleaning up obsolete organization and cost allocation tags from resources managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments.

Syntax
aws codepipeline untag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tag-keys {key1} {key2} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string, required): The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource
  • --tag-keys (list, required): The tag keys to remove
Return Value
  • No return value: Operation returns empty response when successful

list-tags-for-resource

Gets the set of key-value pairs that are used to manage the resource. View all tags applied to Django jobpost pipeline resources for cost tracking and organization of deployments involving Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-tags-for-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string, required): The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource to list tags for
  • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed the maximum
  • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return per page
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • tags: List of tags associated with the resource
    • key: Tag key name
    • value: Tag value
  • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

put-action-revision

Updates action revision information for custom actions. Modify Django jobpost deployment action configurations to trigger pipeline execution with new code revisions containing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-action-revision \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --action-name {action-name} \
  --action-revision {action-revision-json} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the action
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage containing the action
  • --action-name (string, required): The name of the action to update revision for
  • --action-revision (structure, required): Information about the action revision
    • revisionId: Unique identifier for the revision
    • revisionChangeId: String representing change identifier
    • created: Timestamp when revision was created
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • newRevision: Boolean indicating if a new revision was created
  • pipelineExecutionId: The ID of the pipeline execution if triggered

put-third-party-job-success-result

Represents success result of a third-party job as complete. Mark external integration actions in Django jobpost pipelines as successfully completed after processing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments through external tools.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-third-party-job-success-result \
  --job-id {job-id} \
  --client-token {client-token} \
  [--current-revision {current-revision-json}] \
  [--continuation-token {continuation-token}] \
  [--execution-details {execution-details-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --job-id (string, required): The ID of the third-party job being reported
  • --client-token (string, required): The client token associated with the third-party job
  • --current-revision (structure, optional): Current revision of processed artifact
    • revision: Revision identifier string
    • changeIdentifier: Change identifier for the revision
    • created: Timestamp of revision creation
    • revisionSummary: Summary description of the revision
  • --continuation-token (string, optional): Token to continue job processing
  • --execution-details (structure, optional): Execution details
    • summary: Execution summary
    • externalExecutionId: External execution identifier
    • percentComplete: Completion percentage (0-100)
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • No return value: Operation returns empty response when successful

put-third-party-job-failure-result

Represents failure result of a third-party job to CodePipeline. Report external integration action failures in Django jobpost pipelines with detailed error information when Skills, User, Author, Location, or JobPost model deployments fail through external tools.

Syntax
aws codepipeline put-third-party-job-failure-result \
  --job-id {job-id} \
  --client-token {client-token} \
  --failure-details {failure-details-json} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --job-id (string, required): The ID of the third-party job to report failure for
  • --client-token (string, required): The client token associated with the third-party job
  • --failure-details (structure, required): Details about the failure
    • type: Type of failure (JobFailed, ConfigurationError, PermissionError, RevisionOutOfSync, RevisionUnavailable, SystemUnavailable)
    • message: Detailed failure message
    • externalExecutionId: External execution identifier where failure occurred
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • No return value: Operation returns empty response when successful

update-action-type

Updates an action type that was created with any supported integration model. Modify Django jobpost-specific custom action configurations for processing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments in CI/CD workflows.

Syntax
aws codepipeline update-action-type \
  --action-type {action-type-json} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --action-type (structure, required): Updated action type configuration
    • description: Description of the action type
    • executor: Executor configuration (Lambda or JobWorker)
    • id: Action type identifier with category, owner, provider, version
    • inputArtifactDetails: Input artifact requirements
    • outputArtifactDetails: Output artifact requirements
    • permissions: Permission configuration for the action type
    • properties: Properties displayed to users
    • urls: URLs for configuration and execution details
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • No return value: Operation returns empty response when successful

override-stage-condition

Forces a stage to proceed past a condition even if the condition has failed. Skip failed entry or success conditions for Django jobpost deployments when deploying urgent fixes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

Syntax
aws codepipeline override-stage-condition \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --pipeline-execution-id {execution-id} \
  --condition-type {condition-type} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline containing the stage
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage with the condition to override
  • --pipeline-execution-id (string, required): The ID of the pipeline execution to override condition for
  • --condition-type (string, required): The condition type to override (ENTRY, ON_SUCCESS)
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • No return value: Operation returns empty response when successful

list-rule-executions

Lists the rule executions for a pipeline. Monitor Django jobpost deployment rule enforcement and compliance checks for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model migrations.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-rule-executions \
  --pipeline-name {pipeline-name} \
  [--filter {filter-json}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-name (string, required): The name of the pipeline to list rule executions for
  • --filter (structure, optional): Filter criteria for rule executions
    • pipelineExecutionId: Filter by specific pipeline execution
  • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return per page
  • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • ruleExecutionDetails: List of rule execution details
    • pipelineExecutionId: Pipeline execution identifier
    • ruleExecutionId: Rule execution identifier
    • input: Input configuration for the rule
    • output: Output from rule execution
    • stageName: Stage where rule was executed
    • ruleName: Name of the executed rule
  • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

list-rule-types

Lists the available rule types that can be applied to pipelines. View rule types available for Django jobpost pipeline governance and compliance when deploying Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-rule-types \
  [--rule-owner-filter {rule-owner}] \
  [--region-filter {region}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --rule-owner-filter (string, optional): Filter by rule owner (AWS for AWS-owned rules)
  • --region-filter (string, optional): Filter by AWS region for regional rules
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • ruleTypes: List of available rule types
    • id: Rule type identifier with category, owner, provider, version
    • settings: Configuration settings for the rule type
    • ruleConfigurationProperties: Properties for configuring the rule
    • inputArtifactDetails: Input artifact requirements

list-deploy-action-execution-targets

Lists deployment action execution targets for a specific execution. View Django jobpost deployment targets and their status when deploying Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes to EC2 instances or ECS clusters.

Syntax
aws codepipeline list-deploy-action-execution-targets \
  --pipeline-execution-id {execution-id} \
  --stage-name {stage-name} \
  --action-name {action-name} \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
Parameters Description
  • --pipeline-execution-id (string, required): The ID of the pipeline execution to list targets for
  • --stage-name (string, required): The name of the stage containing the deployment action
  • --action-name (string, required): The name of the deployment action to list targets for
  • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
  • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return per page
  • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
  • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
Return Value
  • deployActionExecutionTargetDetails: List of deployment targets and execution status
    • targetId: Identifier of the deployment target
    • targetType: Type of deployment target (EC2, ECS, Lambda)
    • status: Current status of the deployment to this target
    • lastUpdatedAt: Timestamp of last status update
    • resourceArn: ARN of the deployment target resource
  • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

AWS CodeBuild

Fully managed continuous integration service for building Django applications

📋 CodeBuild Overview

AWS CodeBuild is a fully managed continuous integration service that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces software packages. Build your anihpj Django application with automatic dependency installation, testing of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models, and artifact generation for deployment to EC2, ECS, or Elastic Beanstalk.

Core CodeBuild Operations for Django Development

create-project - Create new build project
start-build - Start build execution
batch-get-builds - Get build status information
list-builds - List all builds
stop-build - Stop running build
update-project - Update project configuration
delete-project - Delete build project
list-projects - List all build projects

📝 CodeBuild Commands

  • update-project
  • delete-project

  • start-sandbox
  • stop-sandbox
  • list-sandboxes
  • list-sandboxes-for-project
  • batch-get-sandboxes
  • start-sandbox-connection
  • start-command-execution
  • list-command-executions-for-sandbox
  • batch-get-command-executions
    • 🔗 Webhooks & Integration
  • create-webhook
  • update-webhook
  • delete-webhook
  • import-source-credentials
  • delete-source-credentials
  • list-source-credentials
    • 🔐 Security & Access
  • put-resource-policy
  • get-resource-policy
  • delete-resource-policy
    • 🎯 Utilities
  • list-curated-environment-images

    • create-project

    Creates a build project for your Django jobpost application. Define build environment, source location, build commands, and artifact output configuration for compiling and testing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild create-project \
  --name {project-name} \
  --source {source-json} \
  --artifacts {artifacts-json} \
  --environment {environment-json} \
  --service-role {service-role-arn} \
  [--description {description}] \
  [--source-version {source-version}] \
  [--secondary-sources {secondary-sources-json}] \
  [--secondary-artifacts {secondary-artifacts-json}] \
  [--cache {cache-json}] \
  [--timeout-in-minutes {timeout}] \
  [--queued-timeout-in-minutes {queued-timeout}] \
  [--encryption-key {kms-key-arn}] \
  [--tags {tags-json}] \
  [--vpc-config {vpc-config-json}] \
  [--badge-enabled] \
  [--logs-config {logs-config-json}] \
  [--file-system-locations {file-system-json}] \
  [--build-batch-config {batch-config-json}] \
  [--concurrent-build-limit {limit}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --name (string, required): The name of the build project (must be unique)
    • --source (structure, required): Information about the build input source code location
    • --artifacts (structure, required): Information about the build output artifacts
    • --environment (structure, required): Information about the build environment including type, image, and compute
    • --service-role (string, required): The ARN of the IAM role for CodeBuild to assume
    • --description (string, optional): A description that makes the project easy to identify
    • --source-version (string, optional): A version identifier for the source code (branch, tag, commit)
    • --cache (structure, optional): Build caching configuration (NO_CACHE, S3, LOCAL)
    • --timeout-in-minutes (integer, optional): Build timeout in minutes (5-480, default 60)
    • --vpc-config (structure, optional): VPC configuration for builds in private subnets
    • --tags (list, optional): Resource tags for the project
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • project: Information about the created build project
      • name: Project name
      • arn: Project ARN
      • source: Source configuration
      • artifacts: Artifacts configuration
      • environment: Environment configuration
      • serviceRole: IAM service role ARN
      • created: Creation timestamp
    5 Detailed CodeBuild Examples
    Example 1: Basic Django Build Project

    Create a simple CodeBuild project for the anihpj Django jobpost application with GitHub source and S3 artifacts.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create basic Django build project
PROJECT_NAME="anihpj-django-build"
SERVICE_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeBuildRole"
ARTIFACT_BUCKET="anihpj-build-artifacts"

aws codebuild create-project \
  --name $PROJECT_NAME \
  --description "Django jobpost application build project" \
  --source '{
    "type": "GITHUB",
    "location": "https://github.com/anihpj/django-jobpost.git",
    "buildspec": "version: 0.2\nphases:\n  pre_build:\n    commands:\n      - echo Installing dependencies...\n      - pip install -r requirements.txt\n  build:\n    commands:\n      - echo Running Django tests...\n      - python manage.py test jobpost\n      - echo Building Django static files...\n      - python manage.py collectstatic --noinput\n  post_build:\n    commands:\n      - echo Build completed\nartifacts:\n  files:\n    - '**/*'"
  }' \
  --artifacts '{
    "type": "S3",
    "location": "'$ARTIFACT_BUCKET'/django-builds",
    "packaging": "ZIP"
  }' \
  --environment '{
    "type": "LINUX_CONTAINER",
    "image": "aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:3.0",
    "computeType": "BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL",
    "environmentVariables": [
      {"name": "DJANGO_ENV", "value": "production"},
      {"name": "PYTHONPATH", "value": "/codebuild/output/src"}
    ]
  }' \
  --service-role $SERVICE_ROLE \
  --timeout-in-minutes 30 \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=App,Value=jobpost Key=Environment,Value=build
    { "project": { "name": "anihpj-django-build", "arn": "arn:aws:codebuild:us-east-1:123456789012:project/anihpj-django-build", "description": "Django jobpost application build project", "source": { "type": "GITHUB", "location": "https://github.com/anihpj/django-jobpost.git", "buildspec": "version: 0.2..." }, "artifacts": { "type": "S3", "location": "anihpj-build-artifacts/django-builds", "packaging": "ZIP" }, "environment": { "type": "LINUX_CONTAINER", "image": "aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:3.0", "computeType": "BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL", "environmentVariables": [ { "name": "DJANGO_ENV", "value": "production" }, { "name": "PYTHONPATH", "value": "/codebuild/output/src" } ] }, "serviceRole": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeBuildRole", "timeoutInMinutes": 30, "created": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z", "lastModified": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z" } }
    # Django Build Project Created from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime print(f"Django CodeBuild project created successfully") print(f"Project name: anihpj-django-build") print(f"Target application: Django jobpost") print(f"Models to be tested: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Build project configuration build_config = { 'project_name': 'anihpj-django-build', 'source_type': 'GITHUB', 'compute_type': 'BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL', 'environment': 'amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:3.0', 'timeout_minutes': 30, 'django_app': 'jobpost', 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'artifact_location': 'anihpj-build-artifacts/django-builds', 'created_at': datetime.now().isoformat() } print(f"Build configuration: {build_config}") print(f"✓ GitHub source integration") print(f"✓ S3 artifact storage") print(f"✓ Django test execution") print(f"✓ Static file collection") # Build phases build_phases = [ {'phase': 'pre_build', 'action': 'Install Django dependencies'}, {'phase': 'build', 'action': 'Run Django model tests'}, {'phase': 'build', 'action': 'Collect static files'}, {'phase': 'post_build', 'action': 'Package build artifacts'} ] for phase in build_phases: print(f"✓ {phase['phase']}: {phase['action']}")
    Example 2: Docker-Based Django Build with PostgreSQL

    Create a CodeBuild project using Docker for Django with PostgreSQL database testing, suitable for production-like testing environment.

    #!/bin/bash
# Docker-based Django build with PostgreSQL
cat > buildspec-docker.yml << 'EOF'
version: 0.2

phases:
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo Logging into Amazon ECR...
      - aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
      - echo Starting PostgreSQL service...
      - docker run -d --name postgres-test -e POSTGRES_DB=anihpj_test -e POSTGRES_USER=django -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=testpass123 -p 5432:5432 postgres:13
      - sleep 10
  build:
    commands:
      - echo Building Django Docker image...
      - docker build -f Dockerfile.build -t anihpj-django-test .
      - echo Running Django tests with PostgreSQL...
      - docker run --rm --link postgres-test:postgres -e DATABASE_URL=postgresql://django:testpass123@postgres:5432/anihpj_test anihpj-django-test python manage.py test jobpost
      - echo Testing Django models...
      - docker run --rm --link postgres-test:postgres -e DATABASE_URL=postgresql://django:testpass123@postgres:5432/anihpj_test anihpj-django-test python manage.py check
      - echo Building production image...
      - docker build -t 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django:latest .
      - docker push 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django:latest
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo Cleaning up...
      - docker stop postgres-test
      - docker rm postgres-test
      - echo Build completed on `date`

artifacts:
  files:
    - '**/*'
  exclude-paths:
    - '.git/**/*'
    - '__pycache__/**/*'
    - '*.pyc'
EOF

aws codebuild create-project \
  --name "anihpj-django-docker-build" \
  --description "Docker-based Django build with PostgreSQL testing" \
  --source '{
    "type": "CODECOMMIT",
    "location": "https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-django",
    "buildspec": "buildspec-docker.yml"
  }' \
  --artifacts '{
    "type": "S3",
    "location": "anihpj-docker-builds/artifacts",
    "packaging": "ZIP"
  }' \
  --environment '{
    "type": "LINUX_CONTAINER",
    "image": "aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:4.0",
    "computeType": "BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM",
    "privilegedMode": true,
    "environmentVariables": [
      {"name": "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION", "value": "us-east-1"},
      {"name": "AWS_ACCOUNT_ID", "value": "123456789012"},
      {"name": "IMAGE_REPO_NAME", "value": "anihpj-django"},
      {"name": "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", "value": "jobpost.settings.test"}
    ]
  }' \
  --service-role "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeBuildDockerRole" \
  --timeout-in-minutes 45
    { "project": { "name": "anihpj-django-docker-build", "arn": "arn:aws:codebuild:us-east-1:123456789012:project/anihpj-django-docker-build", "description": "Docker-based Django build with PostgreSQL testing", "source": { "type": "CODECOMMIT", "location": "https://git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/anihpj-django", "buildspec": "buildspec-docker.yml" }, "environment": { "type": "LINUX_CONTAINER", "image": "aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:4.0", "computeType": "BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM", "privilegedMode": true, "environmentVariables": [ { "name": "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION", "value": "us-east-1" }, { "name": "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", "value": "jobpost.settings.test" } ] }, "serviceRole": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeBuildDockerRole", "timeoutInMinutes": 45, "created": "2025-01-15T14:35:00Z" } }
    # Docker Django Build with PostgreSQL from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.conf import settings from django.test import TestCase print(f"Docker-based Django build project created") print(f"Project: anihpj-django-docker-build") print(f"Database: PostgreSQL 13 for testing") print(f"Container registry: Amazon ECR") # Docker build configuration docker_config = { 'base_image': 'amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:4.0', 'compute_type': 'BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM', 'privileged_mode': True, 'database': 'PostgreSQL 13', 'ecr_repository': '123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django', 'timeout_minutes': 45, 'django_settings': 'jobpost.settings.test' } print(f"\nDocker configuration:") for key, value in docker_config.items(): print(f"{key}: {value}") # Django models for testing test_models = [Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost] print(f"\nDjango models for testing:") for model_class in test_models: print(f"✓ {model_class.__name__} - Table: {model_class._meta.db_table}") # Build phases build_phases = [ {'phase': 'pre_build', 'action': 'ECR login and PostgreSQL startup'}, {'phase': 'build', 'action': 'Django Docker image build'}, {'phase': 'build', 'action': 'PostgreSQL-based model testing'}, {'phase': 'build', 'action': 'Production image push to ECR'}, {'phase': 'post_build', 'action': 'Cleanup and artifact packaging'} ] print(f"\nBuild phases:") for phase in build_phases: print(f"✓ {phase['phase']}: {phase['action']}") # Testing configuration test_config = { 'database_engine': 'postgresql', 'test_database': 'anihpj_test', 'test_user': 'django', 'models_tested': len(test_models), 'container_testing': True, 'production_ready': True } print(f"\nTesting configuration: {test_config}")

    start-build

    Starts running a build for your Django jobpost project. Triggers the build process with optional source version override and environment variable customization for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild start-build \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--source-version {source-version}] \
  [--artifacts-override {artifacts-json}] \
  [--environment-variables-override {env-vars-json}] \
  [--source-type-override {source-type}] \
  [--source-location-override {source-location}] \
  [--source-auth-override {source-auth-json}] \
  [--git-clone-depth-override {depth}] \
  [--git-submodules-config-override {submodules-json}] \
  [--buildspec-override {buildspec}] \
  [--insecure-ssl-override] \
  [--report-build-status-override] \
  [--build-status-config-override {status-config-json}] \
  [--environment-type-override {env-type}] \
  [--image-override {image}] \
  [--compute-type-override {compute-type}] \
  [--certificate-override {certificate}] \
  [--cache-override {cache-json}] \
  [--service-role-override {service-role-arn}] \
  [--privileged-mode-override] \
  [--timeout-in-minutes-override {timeout}] \
  [--queued-timeout-in-minutes-override {queued-timeout}] \
  [--encryption-key-override {kms-key-arn}] \
  [--idempotency-token {token}] \
  [--logs-config-override {logs-config-json}] \
  [--registry-credential-override {registry-cred-json}] \
  [--image-pull-credentials-type-override {pull-type}] \
  [--debug-session-enabled] \
  [--fleet-override {fleet-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): The name of the build project to start
    • --source-version (string, optional): Version of the source code (commit ID, branch name, or tag)
    • --artifacts-override (structure, optional): Build output artifacts configuration override
    • --environment-variables-override (list, optional): Environment variables for this build
    • --buildspec-override (string, optional): Override the buildspec file or inline spec
    • --timeout-in-minutes-override (integer, optional): Build timeout override (5-480 minutes)
    • --image-override (string, optional): Docker image override for build environment
    • --compute-type-override (string, optional): Compute type override (BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • build: Information about the build that started
      • id: Build identifier
      • arn: Build ARN
      • buildNumber: Build sequence number
      • startTime: Build start timestamp
      • currentPhase: Current build phase
      • buildStatus: Build status (IN_PROGRESS, etc.)
    5 Detailed CodeBuild Examples
    Example 1: Start Basic Django Build

    Start a build for the anihpj Django jobpost application with default settings and specific source version.

    #!/bin/bash
# Start basic Django build
PROJECT_NAME="anihpj-django-build"
SOURCE_VERSION="main"
BUILD_ID=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)

echo "Starting Django build for project: $PROJECT_NAME"
echo "Source version: $SOURCE_VERSION"
echo "Build timestamp: $BUILD_ID"

aws codebuild start-build \
  --project-name $PROJECT_NAME \
  --source-version $SOURCE_VERSION \
  --environment-variables-override \
    name=BUILD_ID,value=$BUILD_ID \
    name=DJANGO_ENV,value=production \
    name=MODELS_TO_TEST,value="Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost" \
  --region us-east-1
    { "build": { "id": "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "arn": "arn:aws:codebuild:us-east-1:123456789012:build/anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "buildNumber": 42, "startTime": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z", "currentPhase": "SUBMITTED", "buildStatus": "IN_PROGRESS", "sourceVersion": "main", "projectName": "anihpj-django-build", "phases": [ { "phaseType": "SUBMITTED", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "startTime": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z", "endTime": "2025-01-15T14:30:01Z" } ], "environment": { "type": "LINUX_CONTAINER", "image": "aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:3.0", "computeType": "BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL", "environmentVariables": [ { "name": "BUILD_ID", "value": "20250115143000" }, { "name": "DJANGO_ENV", "value": "production" }, { "name": "MODELS_TO_TEST", "value": "Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost" } ] } } }
    # Django Build Started from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime print(f"Django build started successfully") print(f"Build ID: anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012") print(f"Project: anihpj-django-build") print(f"Source version: main") print(f"Build number: 42") print(f"Status: IN_PROGRESS") # Build environment build_env = { 'build_id': '20250115143000', 'django_env': 'production', 'project_name': 'anihpj-django-build', 'source_version': 'main', 'models_to_test': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'started_at': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'compute_type': 'BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL' } print(f"\nBuild environment: {build_env}") print(f"\nModels scheduled for testing:") for model_name in build_env['models_to_test']: print(f"✓ {model_name}") # Expected build phases expected_phases = [ {'phase': 'SUBMITTED', 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'description': 'Build queued'}, {'phase': 'QUEUED', 'status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'description': 'Waiting for capacity'}, {'phase': 'PROVISIONING', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Setting up environment'}, {'phase': 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Downloading Django source'}, {'phase': 'INSTALL', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Installing dependencies'}, {'phase': 'PRE_BUILD', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Pre-build setup'}, {'phase': 'BUILD', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Django tests and build'}, {'phase': 'POST_BUILD', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Post-build cleanup'}, {'phase': 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Uploading build artifacts'}, {'phase': 'FINALIZING', 'status': 'PENDING', 'description': 'Finalizing build'} ] print(f"\nExpected build phases:") for phase in expected_phases: print(f"• {phase['phase']}: {phase['description']}")
    Example 3: Start Build with Buildspec Override

    Start Django build with custom buildspec for production deployment including Docker image creation and ECR push.

    #!/bin/bash
# Start build with custom buildspec for production
PROJECT_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
ECR_REPO="123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django"
IMAGE_TAG="v$(date +%Y.%m.%d)-$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)"

cat > custom-buildspec.yml << EOF
version: 0.2

phases:
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Production Django build starting..."
      - echo "Logging into Amazon ECR..."
      - aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $ECR_REPO
      - echo "Installing production dependencies..."
      - pip install -r requirements/production.txt
      - echo "Setting up test database..."
      - python manage.py migrate --settings=jobpost.settings.test
      
  build:
    commands:
      - echo "Running comprehensive Django tests..."
      - python -m pytest jobpost/tests/ -v --tb=short --cov=jobpost --cov-report=term-missing
      - echo "Testing all Django models..."
      - python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models.SkillsTestCase
      - python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models.UserTestCase  
      - python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models.AuthorTestCase
      - python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models.LocationTestCase
      - python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models.JobPostTestCase
      - echo "Collecting static files for production..."
      - python manage.py collectstatic --noinput --settings=jobpost.settings.production
      - echo "Building production Docker image..."
      - docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t $ECR_REPO:$IMAGE_TAG .
      - docker tag $ECR_REPO:$IMAGE_TAG $ECR_REPO:latest
      
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Pushing Docker images to ECR..."
      - docker push $ECR_REPO:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker push $ECR_REPO:latest
      - echo "Production build completed successfully"
      - echo "Image tag: $IMAGE_TAG"
      
artifacts:
  files:
    - '**/*'
  exclude-paths:
    - '.git/**/*'
    - '__pycache__/**/*'
    - '*.pyc'
    - 'node_modules/**/*'
EOF

aws codebuild start-build \
  --project-name $PROJECT_NAME \
  --source-version "release/v2.1" \
  --buildspec-override file://custom-buildspec.yml \
  --environment-variables-override \
    name=ECR_REPO,value=$ECR_REPO \
    name=IMAGE_TAG,value=$IMAGE_TAG \
    name=DJANGO_SECRET_KEY,value="${DJANGO_SECRET_KEY}" \
    name=DATABASE_URL,value="postgresql://user:pass@prod-db:5432/anihpj_prod"
    { "build": { "id": "anihpj-django-production:11223344-5566-7788-9900-aabbccddeeff", "buildNumber": 28, "startTime": "2025-01-15T14:40:00Z", "currentPhase": "SUBMITTED", "buildStatus": "IN_PROGRESS", "sourceVersion": "release/v2.1", "projectName": "anihpj-django-production", "environment": { "environmentVariables": [ {"name": "ECR_REPO", "value": "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django"}, {"name": "IMAGE_TAG", "value": "v2025.01.15-abc1234"}, {"name": "DJANGO_SECRET_KEY", "value": "***"}, {"name": "DATABASE_URL", "value": "postgresql://user:***@prod-db:5432/anihpj_prod"} ] }, "source": { "buildspec": "version: 0.2\nphases:..." } } }
    # Production Django Build from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.conf import settings import docker print(f"Production Django build started") print(f"Build: anihpj-django-production:11223344-5566-7788-9900-aabbccddeeff") print(f"Release version: v2.1") print(f"Docker image tag: v2025.01.15-abc1234") print(f"Target: Production ECR repository") # Production build configuration prod_config = { 'environment': 'production', 'source_version': 'release/v2.1', 'docker_registry': 'Amazon ECR', 'image_tag': 'v2025.01.15-abc1234', 'database_engine': 'postgresql', 'static_files': True, 'comprehensive_testing': True } print(f"\nProduction configuration:") for key, value in prod_config.items(): print(f"{key}: {value}") # Model test coverage for production model_tests = { 'Skills': { 'test_class': 'SkillsTestCase', 'tests': ['creation', 'validation', 'search', 'categories'], 'critical': True }, 'User': { 'test_class': 'UserTestCase', 'tests': ['authentication', 'permissions', 'profile', 'security'], 'critical': True }, 'Author': { 'test_class': 'AuthorTestCase', 'tests': ['creation', 'validation', 'job_relationship'], 'critical': False }, 'Location': { 'test_class': 'LocationTestCase', 'tests': ['geocoding', 'search', 'validation', 'proximity'], 'critical': True }, 'JobPost': { 'test_class': 'JobPostTestCase', 'tests': ['creation', 'search', 'filtering', 'relationships', 'validation'], 'critical': True } } print(f"\nModel test execution:") for model_name, test_info in model_tests.items(): critical_flag = "❗" if test_info['critical'] else "✓" test_count = len(test_info['tests']) print(f"{critical_flag} {model_name} ({test_info['test_class']}): {test_count} tests") # Production build phases build_phases = [ {'phase': 'pre_build', 'duration': '2m', 'action': 'ECR login, deps install, test DB setup'}, {'phase': 'build', 'duration': '8m', 'action': 'Comprehensive tests, static files, Docker build'}, {'phase': 'post_build', 'duration': '3m', 'action': 'ECR push, cleanup'} ] print(f"\nProduction build phases:") total_time = 0 for phase in build_phases: duration_min = int(phase['duration'][:-1]) total_time += duration_min print(f"• {phase['phase']} ({phase['duration']}): {phase['action']}") print(f"\nEstimated total build time: {total_time} minutes")
    Example 4: Parallel Build with Multiple Environments

    Start multiple parallel builds for different environments (dev, staging, production) with environment-specific configurations.

    #!/bin/bash
# Start parallel builds for multiple environments
BASE_PROJECT="anihpj-django"
SOURCE_VERSION="main"
BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)

# Array of environments and their configurations
declare -A ENVIRONMENTS=(
    ["dev"]="development"
    ["staging"]="staging" 
    ["prod"]="production"
)

declare -A COMPUTE_TYPES=(
    ["dev"]="BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL"
    ["staging"]="BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM"
    ["prod"]="BUILD_GENERAL1_LARGE"
)

declare -A TIMEOUT_MINUTES=(
    ["dev"]="15"
    ["staging"]="30"
    ["prod"]="45"
)

# Start builds for each environment
for ENV in "${!ENVIRONMENTS[@]}"; do
    PROJECT_NAME="${BASE_PROJECT}-${ENV}"
    DJANGO_SETTINGS="jobpost.settings.${ENVIRONMENTS[$ENV]}"
    
    echo "Starting build for $ENV environment..."
    echo "Project: $PROJECT_NAME"
    echo "Settings: $DJANGO_SETTINGS"
    
    BUILD_RESULT=$(aws codebuild start-build \
        --project-name $PROJECT_NAME \
        --source-version $SOURCE_VERSION \
        --compute-type-override ${COMPUTE_TYPES[$ENV]} \
        --timeout-in-minutes-override ${TIMEOUT_MINUTES[$ENV]} \
        --environment-variables-override \
            name=ENVIRONMENT,value=$ENV \
            name=DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE,value=$DJANGO_SETTINGS \
            name=BUILD_TIMESTAMP,value=$BUILD_TIMESTAMP \
            name=PARALLEL_BUILD,value=true \
            name=TARGET_MODELS,value="Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost" \
            name=TEST_LEVEL,value=$([ "$ENV" = "prod" ] && echo "comprehensive" || echo "standard") \
        --query 'build.id' --output text)
    
    echo "Build started: $BUILD_RESULT"
    echo "Environment: $ENV"
    echo "Expected duration: ${TIMEOUT_MINUTES[$ENV]} minutes"
    echo "---"
done

echo "All parallel builds initiated!"
echo "Monitor builds with: aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids "
    Starting build for dev environment... Project: anihpj-django-dev Settings: jobpost.settings.development Build started: anihpj-django-dev:aabbccdd-1111-2222-3333-444455556666 Environment: dev Expected duration: 15 minutes --- Starting build for staging environment... Project: anihpj-django-staging Settings: jobpost.settings.staging Build started: anihpj-django-staging:bbccddee-2222-3333-4444-555566667777 Environment: staging Expected duration: 30 minutes --- Starting build for prod environment... Project: anihpj-django-prod Settings: jobpost.settings.production Build started: anihpj-django-prod:ccddeeff-3333-4444-5555-666677778888 Environment: prod Expected duration: 45 minutes --- All parallel builds initiated! Monitor builds with: aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids
    # Parallel Multi-Environment Builds from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.conf import settings from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Parallel Django builds initiated") print(f"Source version: main") print(f"Build timestamp: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}") print(f"Total environments: 3") # Build configurations per environment build_configs = { 'dev': { 'build_id': 'anihpj-django-dev:aabbccdd-1111-2222-3333-444455556666', 'settings': 'jobpost.settings.development', 'compute_type': 'BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL', 'timeout_minutes': 15, 'test_level': 'standard', 'database': 'sqlite3', 'debug_mode': True }, 'staging': { 'build_id': 'anihpj-django-staging:bbccddee-2222-3333-4444-555566667777', 'settings': 'jobpost.settings.staging', 'compute_type': 'BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM', 'timeout_minutes': 30, 'test_level': 'standard', 'database': 'postgresql', 'debug_mode': False }, 'prod': { 'build_id': 'anihpj-django-prod:ccddeeff-3333-4444-5555-666677778888', 'settings': 'jobpost.settings.production', 'compute_type': 'BUILD_GENERAL1_LARGE', 'timeout_minutes': 45, 'test_level': 'comprehensive', 'database': 'postgresql', 'debug_mode': False } } print(f"\nBuild configurations:") for env, config in build_configs.items(): print(f"\n{env.upper()} Environment:") print(f"• Build ID: {config['build_id']}") print(f"• Compute: {config['compute_type']}") print(f"• Timeout: {config['timeout_minutes']} minutes") print(f"• Test Level: {config['test_level']}") print(f"• Database: {config['database']}") # Model testing matrix models_tested = ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'] test_matrix = { 'dev': {'models': models_tested, 'tests_per_model': 3, 'coverage_min': 70}, 'staging': {'models': models_tested, 'tests_per_model': 5, 'coverage_min': 80}, 'prod': {'models': models_tested, 'tests_per_model': 8, 'coverage_min': 95} } print(f"\nTest execution matrix:") for env, test_info in test_matrix.items(): total_tests = len(test_info['models']) * test_info['tests_per_model'] print(f"{env}: {total_tests} tests, {test_info['coverage_min']}% coverage minimum") # Parallel execution timeline start_time = datetime.now() estimated_completion = { 'dev': start_time + timedelta(minutes=15), 'staging': start_time + timedelta(minutes=30), 'prod': start_time + timedelta(minutes=45) } print(f"\nEstimated completion times:") for env, completion_time in estimated_completion.items(): print(f"{env}: {completion_time.strftime('%H:%M:%S')}")
    Example 5: Conditional Build with Feature Flags

    Start Django build with conditional execution based on feature flags and git branch patterns, enabling selective testing and deployment.

    #!/bin/bash
# Conditional build with feature flags
PROJECT_NAME="anihpj-django-conditional"
CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
COMMIT_MESSAGE=$(git log -1 --pretty=%B)
FEATURE_FLAGS=""

# Determine feature flags based on branch and commit message
if [[ $CURRENT_BRANCH == "feature/"* ]]; then
    FEATURE_FLAGS="$FEATURE_FLAGS,FEATURE_BRANCH"
fi

if [[ $COMMIT_MESSAGE == *"[skip-tests]"* ]]; then
    FEATURE_FLAGS="$FEATURE_FLAGS,SKIP_TESTS"
fi

if [[ $COMMIT_MESSAGE == *"[full-coverage]"* ]]; then
    FEATURE_FLAGS="$FEATURE_FLAGS,FULL_COVERAGE"
fi

if [[ $CURRENT_BRANCH == "hotfix/"* ]]; then
    FEATURE_FLAGS="$FEATURE_FLAGS,HOTFIX,FAST_BUILD"
fi

if [[ $COMMIT_MESSAGE == *"[models-only]"* ]]; then
    FEATURE_FLAGS="$FEATURE_FLAGS,MODELS_ONLY"
fi

echo "Detected branch: $CURRENT_BRANCH"
echo "Commit message: $COMMIT_MESSAGE"
echo "Feature flags: $FEATURE_FLAGS"

# Custom buildspec based on feature flags
cat > conditional-buildspec.yml << EOF
version: 0.2

phases:
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Conditional Django build starting..."
      - echo "Branch: $CURRENT_BRANCH"
      - echo "Feature flags: $FEATURE_FLAGS"
      - pip install -r requirements.txt
      - |
        if [[ "$FEATURE_FLAGS" == *"FAST_BUILD"* ]]; then
          echo "Fast build mode - minimal dependencies"
          pip install -r requirements/minimal.txt
        else
          pip install -r requirements/full.txt
        fi
      
  build:
    commands:
      - |
        if [[ "$FEATURE_FLAGS" == *"SKIP_TESTS"* ]]; then
          echo "Skipping tests as requested"
        elif [[ "$FEATURE_FLAGS" == *"MODELS_ONLY"* ]]; then
          echo "Running model tests only"
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models -v 2
        elif [[ "$FEATURE_FLAGS" == *"FULL_COVERAGE"* ]]; then
          echo "Running full coverage tests"
          python -m pytest jobpost/ --cov=jobpost --cov-report=html --cov-fail-under=95
        else
          echo "Running standard test suite"
          python manage.py test jobpost -v 2
        fi
      - |
        if [[ "$FEATURE_FLAGS" == *"FEATURE_BRANCH"* ]]; then
          echo "Feature branch - running additional integration tests"
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.integration -v 2
        fi
      - |
        if [[ "$FEATURE_FLAGS" != *"HOTFIX"* ]]; then
          echo "Collecting static files"
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
        fi
        
  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "Conditional build completed"
      - echo "Branch: $CURRENT_BRANCH processed with flags: $FEATURE_FLAGS"
EOF

aws codebuild start-build \
  --project-name $PROJECT_NAME \
  --source-version $CURRENT_BRANCH \
  --buildspec-override file://conditional-buildspec.yml \
  --environment-variables-override \
    name=CURRENT_BRANCH,value="$CURRENT_BRANCH" \
    name=FEATURE_FLAGS,value="$FEATURE_FLAGS" \
    name=COMMIT_SHA,value="$(git rev-parse HEAD)" \
    name=COMMIT_MESSAGE,value="$COMMIT_MESSAGE" \
    name=BUILD_TYPE,value="conditional" \
    name=DJANGO_MODELS,value="Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost"
    Detected branch: feature/enhanced-search Commit message: Add advanced search functionality [full-coverage] Feature flags: ,FEATURE_BRANCH,FULL_COVERAGE { "build": { "id": "anihpj-django-conditional:ffeeddcc-9988-7766-5544-332211009988", "buildNumber": 73, "startTime": "2025-01-15T14:45:00Z", "currentPhase": "SUBMITTED", "buildStatus": "IN_PROGRESS", "sourceVersion": "feature/enhanced-search", "projectName": "anihpj-django-conditional", "environment": { "environmentVariables": [ {"name": "CURRENT_BRANCH", "value": "feature/enhanced-search"}, {"name": "FEATURE_FLAGS", "value": ",FEATURE_BRANCH,FULL_COVERAGE"}, {"name": "COMMIT_SHA", "value": "a1b2c3d4e5f6789012345678901234567890abcd"}, {"name": "COMMIT_MESSAGE", "value": "Add advanced search functionality [full-coverage]"}, {"name": "BUILD_TYPE", "value": "conditional"}, {"name": "DJANGO_MODELS", "value": "Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost"} ] } } }
    # Conditional Django Build from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.conf import settings from django.test import TestCase print(f"Conditional Django build initiated") print(f"Build: anihpj-django-conditional:ffeeddcc-9988-7766-5544-332211009988") print(f"Branch: feature/enhanced-search") print(f"Build type: conditional") print(f"Feature flags detected: FEATURE_BRANCH, FULL_COVERAGE") # Parse feature flags and determine execution plan feature_flags = ['FEATURE_BRANCH', 'FULL_COVERAGE'] execution_plan = { 'skip_tests': False, 'models_only': False, 'full_coverage': True, 'feature_branch': True, 'fast_build': False, 'hotfix_mode': False } print(f"\nExecution plan based on flags:") for flag, enabled in execution_plan.items(): status = "✓ ENABLED" if enabled else "✗ DISABLED" print(f"{flag}: {status}") # Test execution strategy if execution_plan['full_coverage']: test_strategy = { 'type': 'full_coverage', 'tool': 'pytest', 'coverage_target': 95, 'report_format': 'html', 'fail_under': 95, 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'additional_tests': ['integration', 'api', 'views'] if execution_plan['feature_branch'] else [] } elif execution_plan['models_only']: test_strategy = { 'type': 'models_only', 'tool': 'django_test', 'target': 'jobpost.tests.test_models', 'verbosity': 2 } else: test_strategy = { 'type': 'standard', 'tool': 'django_test', 'target': 'jobpost', 'verbosity': 2 } print(f"\nTest strategy:") for key, value in test_strategy.items(): print(f"{key}: {value}") #
