Build optimizations optimizations = { 'dependency_installation': 'full' if not execution_plan['fast_build'] else 'minimal', 'static_file_collection': not execution_plan['hotfix_mode'], 'integration_tests': execution_plan['feature_branch'], 'coverage_reporting': execution_plan['full_coverage'], 'estimated_duration': '12-15 minutes' if execution_plan['full_coverage'] else '5-8 minutes' } print(f"\nBuild optimizations:") for key, value in optimizations.items(): print(f"{key}: {value}") # Conditional outcomes print(f"\nConditional build outcomes:") print(f"• Dependencies: {optimizations['dependency_installation']} installation") print(f"• Test coverage: {'95% minimum' if execution_plan['full_coverage'] else 'standard'}") print(f"• Integration tests: {'included' if optimizations['integration_tests'] else 'skipped'}") print(f"• Static files: {'collected' if optimizations['static_file_collection'] else 'skipped'}") print(f"• Duration: {optimizations['estimated_duration']}")

    batch-get-builds

    Gets information about one or more builds. Retrieve detailed information about Django jobpost application build executions, including status, logs, and artifacts for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
  --ids {build-id} [{build-id}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --ids (list, required): The IDs of the builds to retrieve information about
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • builds: Array of build information objects
      • id: Build identifier
      • arn: Build ARN
      • buildStatus: Build status (SUCCEEDED, FAILED, IN_PROGRESS, etc.)
      • currentPhase: Current phase of the build
      • phases: Array of build phase details
      • logs: CloudWatch logs information
    • buildsNotFound: Array of build IDs that were not found
    5 Detailed CodeBuild Examples
    Example 1: Get Multiple Django Build Details

    Retrieve detailed information about multiple Django build executions to monitor the anihpj jobpost application build status.

    #!/bin/bash
# Get details for multiple Django builds
BUILD_IDS=(
    "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012"
    "anihpj-django-build:87654321-4321-4321-4321-210987654321"
    "anihpj-django-build:11223344-5566-7788-9900-aabbccddeeff"
)

echo "Retrieving Django build details..."
echo "Total builds to check: ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}"

# Convert array to space-separated string for AWS CLI
BUILD_LIST=""
for BUILD_ID in "${BUILD_IDS[@]}"; do
    BUILD_LIST="$BUILD_LIST $BUILD_ID"
done

echo "Build IDs: $BUILD_LIST"

aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
  --ids $BUILD_LIST \
  --query 'builds[*].{ID:id,Status:buildStatus,Phase:currentPhase,Project:projectName,StartTime:startTime,Duration:endTime}' \
  --output table
    { "builds": [ { "id": "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "arn": "arn:aws:codebuild:us-east-1:123456789012:build/anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "buildNumber": 42, "startTime": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z", "endTime": "2025-01-15T14:45:30Z", "currentPhase": "COMPLETED", "buildStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "sourceVersion": "main", "projectName": "anihpj-django-build", "environment": { "environmentVariables": [ {"name": "DJANGO_ENV", "value": "production"}, {"name": "MODELS_TO_TEST", "value": "Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost"} ] }, "logs": { "groupName": "/aws/codebuild/anihpj-django-build", "streamName": "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012" }, "phases": [ {"phaseType": "SUBMITTED", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 1}, {"phaseType": "PROVISIONING", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 45}, {"phaseType": "DOWNLOAD_SOURCE", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 15}, {"phaseType": "INSTALL", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 120}, {"phaseType": "PRE_BUILD", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 60}, {"phaseType": "BUILD", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 480}, {"phaseType": "POST_BUILD", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 30}, {"phaseType": "UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 25}, {"phaseType": "FINALIZING", "phaseStatus": "SUCCEEDED", "durationInSeconds": 5} ] }, { "id": "anihpj-django-build:87654321-4321-4321-4321-210987654321", "buildNumber": 43, "currentPhase": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "IN_PROGRESS", "startTime": "2025-01-15T14:50:00Z", "projectName": "anihpj-django-build" }, { "id": "anihpj-django-build:11223344-5566-7788-9900-aabbccddeeff", "buildNumber": 44, "currentPhase": "FAILED", "buildStatus": "FAILED", "startTime": "2025-01-15T15:00:00Z", "endTime": "2025-01-15T15:05:30Z", "projectName": "anihpj-django-build" } ], "buildsNotFound": [] }
    # Django Build Details Retrieved from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django build details retrieved") print(f"Total builds checked: 3") print(f"Project: anihpj-django-build") print(f"Models tested: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Build status summary build_summary = { 'build_42': { 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'duration': timedelta(minutes=15, seconds=30), 'phase': 'COMPLETED', 'source_version': 'main', 'test_results': 'All Django model tests passed' }, 'build_43': { 'status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'current_phase': 'BUILD', 'started': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 14, 50, 0), 'estimated_completion': 'In progress' }, 'build_44': { 'status': 'FAILED', 'duration': timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=30), 'phase': 'FAILED', 'failure_reason': 'Test failure or build error' } } print(f"\nBuild status summary:") for build_id, details in build_summary.items(): print(f"\n{build_id.upper()}:") for key, value in details.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Phase duration analysis (Build 42) phase_durations = { 'SUBMITTED': 1, 'PROVISIONING': 45, 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE': 15, 'INSTALL': 120, 'PRE_BUILD': 60, 'BUILD': 480, 'POST_BUILD': 30, 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS': 25, 'FINALIZING': 5 } print(f"\nBuild 42 - Phase duration analysis:") total_seconds = sum(phase_durations.values()) for phase, duration in phase_durations.items(): percentage = (duration / total_seconds) * 100 print(f"{phase}: {duration}s ({percentage:.1f}%)") print(f"\nTotal build time: {total_seconds}s ({total_seconds // 60}m {total_seconds % 60}s)") print(f"Django models successfully tested in Build 42") print(f"Test coverage: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost models")
    Example 2: Monitor Build Status with Loop

    Continuously monitor Django build progress with automatic status checking and notification when builds complete.

    #!/bin/bash
# Monitor Django build status with automated checking
BUILD_IDS=(
    "anihpj-django-staging:aabbccdd-1111-2222-3333-444455556666"
    "anihpj-django-prod:bbccddee-2222-3333-4444-555566667777"
)

MONITOR_INTERVAL=30  # Check every 30 seconds
MAX_WAIT_TIME=3600   # Maximum wait time of 1 hour
START_TIME=$(date +%s)

echo "Starting Django build monitoring..."
echo "Builds to monitor: ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}"
echo "Check interval: ${MONITOR_INTERVAL}s"
echo "Maximum wait time: ${MAX_WAIT_TIME}s"
echo "="

while true; do
    CURRENT_TIME=$(date +%s)
    ELAPSED_TIME=$((CURRENT_TIME - START_TIME))
    
    if [ $ELAPSED_TIME -gt $MAX_WAIT_TIME ]; then
        echo "Maximum wait time exceeded. Stopping monitoring."
        break
    fi
    
    echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] Checking build status (Elapsed: ${ELAPSED_TIME}s)..."
    
    # Get build status for all monitored builds
    BUILD_STATUSES=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids "${BUILD_IDS[@]}" \
        --query 'builds[*].{ID:id,Status:buildStatus,Phase:currentPhase,Progress:phases[-1].phaseType}' \
        --output json)
    
    echo "Build status update:"
    
    # Parse and display status
    COMPLETED_BUILDS=0
    TOTAL_BUILDS=${#BUILD_IDS[@]}
    
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        if [[ $line == *"Status"* ]]; then
            STATUS=$(echo $line | grep -o '"[^"]*"' | tail -1 | tr -d '"')
            if [[ $STATUS == "SUCCEEDED" || $STATUS == "FAILED" || $STATUS == "STOPPED" ]]; then
                ((COMPLETED_BUILDS++))
            fi
            echo "  Status: $STATUS"
        elif [[ $line == *"Phase"* ]]; then
            PHASE=$(echo $line | grep -o '"[^"]*"' | tail -1 | tr -d '"')
            echo "  Phase: $PHASE"
        fi
    done <<< "$BUILD_STATUSES"
    
    echo "Progress: $COMPLETED_BUILDS/$TOTAL_BUILDS builds completed"
    echo "---"
    
    # Check if all builds are completed
    if [ $COMPLETED_BUILDS -eq $TOTAL_BUILDS ]; then
        echo "All Django builds completed!"
        # Send notification (example)
        aws sns publish \
            --topic-arn "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:django-build-notifications" \
            --message "All Django builds for anihpj jobpost application completed. Total elapsed time: ${ELAPSED_TIME}s"
        break
    fi
    
    sleep $MONITOR_INTERVAL
done

echo "Build monitoring completed."
    Starting Django build monitoring... Builds to monitor: 2 Check interval: 30s Maximum wait time: 3600s = [14:30:00] Checking build status (Elapsed: 0s)... Build status update: Status: IN_PROGRESS Phase: BUILD Status: IN_PROGRESS Phase: INSTALL Progress: 0/2 builds completed --- [14:30:30] Checking build status (Elapsed: 30s)... Build status update: Status: IN_PROGRESS Phase: POST_BUILD Status: IN_PROGRESS Phase: BUILD Progress: 0/2 builds completed --- [14:31:00] Checking build status (Elapsed: 60s)... Build status update: Status: SUCCEEDED Phase: COMPLETED Status: IN_PROGRESS Phase: POST_BUILD Progress: 1/2 builds completed --- [14:31:30] Checking build status (Elapsed: 90s)... Build status update: Status: SUCCEEDED Phase: COMPLETED Status: SUCCEEDED Phase: COMPLETED Progress: 2/2 builds completed --- All Django builds completed! Build monitoring completed.
    # Django Build Monitoring from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import time print(f"Django build monitoring session") print(f"Monitored builds: 2") print(f"Total monitoring time: 90 seconds") print(f"Check interval: 30 seconds") print(f"All builds completed successfully") # Build monitoring timeline monitoring_timeline = [ {'time': '14:30:00', 'staging_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'staging_phase': 'BUILD', 'prod_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'prod_phase': 'INSTALL'}, {'time': '14:30:30', 'staging_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'staging_phase': 'POST_BUILD', 'prod_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'prod_phase': 'BUILD'}, {'time': '14:31:00', 'staging_status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'staging_phase': 'COMPLETED', 'prod_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'prod_phase': 'POST_BUILD'}, {'time': '14:31:30', 'staging_status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'staging_phase': 'COMPLETED', 'prod_status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'prod_phase': 'COMPLETED'} ] print(f"\nMonitoring timeline:") for check in monitoring_timeline: print(f"\n{check['time']}:") print(f"• Staging: {check['staging_status']} ({check['staging_phase']})") print(f"• Production: {check['prod_status']} ({check['prod_phase']})") # Build completion analysis completion_analysis = { 'staging_build': { 'completed_at': '14:31:00', 'total_duration': '1m 0s', 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'result': 'SUCCESS' }, 'production_build': { 'completed_at': '14:31:30', 'total_duration': '1m 30s', 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'result': 'SUCCESS' } } print(f"\nCompletion analysis:") for build_name, analysis in completion_analysis.items(): print(f"\n{build_name.upper()}:") for key, value in analysis.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Notification sent notification = { 'service': 'Amazon SNS', 'topic': 'django-build-notifications', 'message': 'All Django builds for anihpj jobpost application completed', 'total_elapsed_time': '90 seconds', 'builds_completed': 2, 'success_rate': '100%' } print(f"\nNotification sent: {notification}")
    Example 3: Analyze Failed Django Builds

    Analyze failed Django builds to identify common issues and patterns in the anihpj jobpost application build process.

    #!/bin/bash
# Analyze failed Django builds for troubleshooting
FAILED_BUILD_IDS=(
    "anihpj-django-build:failed1-1111-1111-1111-111111111111"
    "anihpj-django-build:failed2-2222-2222-2222-222222222222" 
    "anihpj-django-build:failed3-3333-3333-3333-333333333333"
)

echo "Analyzing failed Django builds..."
echo "Failed builds to analyze: ${#FAILED_BUILD_IDS[@]}"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "="

# Get detailed failure information
FAILURE_DETAILS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids "${FAILED_BUILD_IDS[@]}" \
    --query 'builds[*].{ID:id,Status:buildStatus,FailurePhase:phases[?phaseStatus==`FAILED`].phaseType|[0],StartTime:startTime,EndTime:endTime,SourceVersion:sourceVersion,Logs:logs.s3DeepLink}' \
    --output json)

echo "$FAILURE_DETAILS" | jq '.'

# Extract common failure patterns
echo "\nFailure pattern analysis:"
echo "Checking for common Django build failures..."

# Check for dependency failures
echo "\n1. Dependency Installation Failures:"
aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "/aws/codebuild/anihpj-django-build" \
    --filter-pattern "[timestamp, request_id, level=ERROR, ...msg=*requirements*]" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 day ago' +%s)000 \
    --query 'events[*].message' \
    --output text | head -5

# Check for Django model test failures
echo "\n2. Django Model Test Failures:"
aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "/aws/codebuild/anihpj-django-build" \
    --filter-pattern "[timestamp, request_id, level=ERROR, ...msg=*test*django*model*]" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 day ago' +%s)000 \
    --query 'events[*].message' \
    --output text | head -5

# Check for database connection issues
echo "\n3. Database Connection Issues:"
aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "/aws/codebuild/anihpj-django-build" \
    --filter-pattern "[timestamp, request_id, level=ERROR, ...msg=*database*connection*]" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 day ago' +%s)000 \
    --query 'events[*].message' \
    --output text | head -5

echo "\nFailure analysis complete. Check CloudWatch logs for detailed error messages."
    [ { "ID": "anihpj-django-build:failed1-1111-1111-1111-111111111111", "Status": "FAILED", "FailurePhase": "INSTALL", "StartTime": "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z", "EndTime": "2025-01-15T12:03:45Z", "SourceVersion": "feature/new-jobpost-model", "Logs": "https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/object/anihpj-build-logs/failed1-1111-1111-1111-111111111111" }, { "ID": "anihpj-django-build:failed2-2222-2222-2222-222222222222", "Status": "FAILED", "FailurePhase": "BUILD", "StartTime": "2025-01-15T13:15:00Z", "EndTime": "2025-01-15T13:20:30Z", "SourceVersion": "feature/skills-model-update", "Logs": "https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/object/anihpj-build-logs/failed2-2222-2222-2222-222222222222" }, { "ID": "anihpj-django-build:failed3-3333-3333-3333-333333333333", "Status": "FAILED", "FailurePhase": "PRE_BUILD", "StartTime": "2025-01-15T14:00:00Z", "EndTime": "2025-01-15T14:02:15Z", "SourceVersion": "hotfix/user-authentication", "Logs": "https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/object/anihpj-build-logs/failed3-3333-3333-3333-333333333333" } ] Failure pattern analysis: Checking for common Django build failures... 1. Dependency Installation Failures: ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement Django==4.2.8 ERROR: No matching distribution found for psycopg2-binary==2.9.7 ERROR: Failed building wheel for pillow ERROR: pip install failed with exit code 1 ERROR: requirements.txt installation failed 2. Django Model Test Failures: ERROR: test_skills_model_creation (jobpost.tests.test_models.SkillsModelTest) ... FAIL ERROR: test_user_model_fields (jobpost.tests.test_models.UserModelTest) ... FAIL ERROR: test_jobpost_location_relationship (jobpost.tests.test_models.JobPostModelTest) ... FAIL ERROR: AssertionError: Skills model validation failed ERROR: Django model tests failed with 3 failures 3. Database Connection Issues: ERROR: django.db.utils.OperationalError: could not connect to server ERROR: FATAL: password authentication failed for user "django_user" ERROR: connection to server at "localhost" (127.0.0.1), port 5432 failed ERROR: Database migration failed during pre_build phase ERROR: PostgreSQL connection timeout Failure analysis complete. Check CloudWatch logs for detailed error messages.
    # Django Build Failure Analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django build failure analysis") print(f"Failed builds analyzed: 3") print(f"Project: anihpj jobpost application") print(f"Models affected: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Failure summary by phase failure_summary = { 'failed1': { 'failure_phase': 'INSTALL', 'duration': timedelta(minutes=3, seconds=45), 'source_version': 'feature/new-jobpost-model', 'primary_issue': 'Dependency installation failure', 'affected_models': ['JobPost'], 'error_pattern': 'Django version mismatch' }, 'failed2': { 'failure_phase': 'BUILD', 'duration': timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=30), 'source_version': 'feature/skills-model-update', 'primary_issue': 'Model test failures', 'affected_models': ['Skills', 'User', 'JobPost'], 'error_pattern': 'Model validation errors' }, 'failed3': { 'failure_phase': 'PRE_BUILD', 'duration': timedelta(minutes=2, seconds=15), 'source_version': 'hotfix/user-authentication', 'primary_issue': 'Database connection failure', 'affected_models': ['User', 'Author'], 'error_pattern': 'PostgreSQL authentication' } } print(f"\nFailure breakdown:") for build_id, details in failure_summary.items(): print(f"\n{build_id.upper()}:") for key, value in details.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Error pattern frequency error_patterns = { 'Dependency Issues': { 'frequency': 1, 'affected_builds': ['failed1'], 'solutions': ['Update requirements.txt', 'Check Python version compatibility'] }, 'Model Test Failures': { 'frequency': 1, 'affected_builds': ['failed2'], 'solutions': ['Review model changes', 'Update test cases', 'Check model relationships'] }, 'Database Connection': { 'frequency': 1, 'affected_builds': ['failed3'], 'solutions': ['Check database credentials', 'Verify network connectivity', 'Update connection string'] } } print(f"\nError pattern analysis:") for pattern, data in error_patterns.items(): print(f"\n{pattern}:") print(f"• Frequency: {data['frequency']}/3 builds") print(f"• Affected builds: {', '.join(data['affected_builds'])}") print(f"• Solutions: {', '.join(data['solutions'])}") # Recommendations recommendations = [ 'Pin exact dependency versions in requirements.txt', 'Add comprehensive model validation tests', 'Implement database health checks in pre_build phase', 'Set up build environment validation', 'Add retry logic for transient failures' ] print(f"\nRecommendations:") for i, rec in enumerate(recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") print(f"\nModels requiring attention: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") print(f"Most common failure phase: INSTALL, BUILD, PRE_BUILD (tied)") print(f"Average failure time: {sum([details['duration'] for details in failure_summary.values()], timedelta()) / 3}")
    Example 4: Compare Build Performance Metrics

    Compare performance metrics across multiple Django builds to optimize the anihpj jobpost application build process.

    #!/bin/bash
# Compare Django build performance metrics
BUILD_IDS_FOR_COMPARISON=(
    "anihpj-django-build:perf1-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-ddddeeeefffff1"
    "anihpj-django-build:perf2-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-ddddeeeefffff2"
    "anihpj-django-build:perf3-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-ddddeeeefffff3"
    "anihpj-django-build:perf4-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-ddddeeeefffff4"
    "anihpj-django-build:perf5-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-ddddeeeefffff5"
)

echo "Django Build Performance Comparison"
echo "====================================="
echo "Builds to analyze: ${#BUILD_IDS_FOR_COMPARISON[@]}"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo ""

# Get build details for performance analysis
BUILD_DETAILS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_COMPARISON[@]}" \
    --query 'builds[*].{ID:id,Status:buildStatus,StartTime:startTime,EndTime:endTime,Phases:phases,BuildNumber:buildNumber,SourceVersion:sourceVersion}' \
    --output json)

echo "Build performance data retrieved. Analyzing..."
echo ""

# Calculate performance metrics
echo "Performance Metrics Summary:"
echo "----------------------------"

# Process each build and calculate metrics
echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r '.[] | 
    "Build #" + (.BuildNumber|tostring) + " (" + .SourceVersion + "):\n" +
    "  Status: " + .buildStatus + "\n" +
    "  Duration: " + (if .EndTime then 
        ((.EndTime | fromdateiso8601) - (.StartTime | fromdateiso8601) | tostring) + "s" 
    else "In Progress" end) + "\n" +
    "  Phases: " + (.Phases | length | tostring) + "\n" +
    "---"'

echo ""
echo "Phase Duration Analysis:"
echo "----------------------"

# Analyze phase durations across builds
aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_COMPARISON[@]}" \
    --query 'builds[*].phases[*].{Phase:phaseType,Duration:durationInSeconds,Status:phaseStatus}' \
    --output table

echo ""
echo "Build Optimization Recommendations:"
echo "----------------------------------"

# Generate optimization recommendations based on analysis
cat << EOF
1. INSTALL Phase Optimization:
   • Use Docker layer caching for Django dependencies
   • Pre-build base image with common Python packages
   • Implement pip cache between builds

2. BUILD Phase Optimization:
   • Parallelize Django model tests (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost)
   • Use pytest-xdist for concurrent test execution
   • Optimize database migrations for test environment

3. POST_BUILD Phase Optimization:
   • Compress static assets before upload
   • Use multi-part uploads for large Django media files
   • Implement artifact caching strategy

4. Overall Build Optimization:
   • Consider using larger compute instances for complex builds
   • Implement incremental builds for unchanged components
   • Use build artifacts from previous successful builds
EOF

echo ""
echo "Performance comparison complete."
    Django Build Performance Comparison ===================================== Builds to analyze: 5 Project: anihpj jobpost application Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Build performance data retrieved. Analyzing... Performance Metrics Summary: ---------------------------- Build #45 (main): Status: SUCCEEDED Duration: 420s Phases: 9 --- Build #46 (feature/skills-optimization): Status: SUCCEEDED Duration: 385s Phases: 9 --- Build #47 (feature/user-model-refactor): Status: SUCCEEDED Duration: 455s Phases: 9 --- Build #48 (hotfix/location-validation): Status: SUCCEEDED Duration: 325s Phases: 9 --- Build #49 (feature/jobpost-enhancement): Status: SUCCEEDED Duration: 410s Phases: 9 --- Phase Duration Analysis: ---------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | Phase | Duration | Status | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | SUBMITTED | 1 | SUCCEEDED | | PROVISIONING | 45 | SUCCEEDED | | DOWNLOAD_SOURCE| 12 | SUCCEEDED | | INSTALL | 125 | SUCCEEDED | | PRE_BUILD | 35 | SUCCEEDED | | BUILD | 180 | SUCCEEDED | | POST_BUILD | 15 | SUCCEEDED | | UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS| 5 | SUCCEEDED | | FINALIZING | 2 | SUCCEEDED | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Build Optimization Recommendations: ---------------------------------- 1. INSTALL Phase Optimization: • Use Docker layer caching for Django dependencies • Pre-build base image with common Python packages • Implement pip cache between builds 2. BUILD Phase Optimization: • Parallelize Django model tests (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost) • Use pytest-xdist for concurrent test execution • Optimize database migrations for test environment 3. POST_BUILD Phase Optimization: • Compress static assets before upload • Use multi-part uploads for large Django media files • Implement artifact caching strategy 4. Overall Build Optimization: • Consider using larger compute instances for complex builds • Implement incremental builds for unchanged components • Use build artifacts from previous successful builds Performance comparison complete.
    # Django Build Performance Analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import statistics print(f"Django build performance analysis") print(f"Builds analyzed: 5") print(f"Project: anihpj jobpost application") print(f"Models tested: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Build performance data performance_data = { 'build_45': { 'branch': 'main', 'duration': timedelta(seconds=420), 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'phase_durations': { 'SUBMITTED': 1, 'PROVISIONING': 45, 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE': 12, 'INSTALL': 125, 'PRE_BUILD': 35, 'BUILD': 180, 'POST_BUILD': 15, 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS': 5, 'FINALIZING': 2 } }, 'build_46': { 'branch': 'feature/skills-optimization', 'duration': timedelta(seconds=385), 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'phase_durations': { 'SUBMITTED': 1, 'PROVISIONING': 40, 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE': 10, 'INSTALL': 115, 'PRE_BUILD': 30, 'BUILD': 165, 'POST_BUILD': 18, 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS': 4, 'FINALIZING': 2 } }, 'build_47': { 'branch': 'feature/user-model-refactor', 'duration': timedelta(seconds=455), 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'phase_durations': { 'SUBMITTED': 1, 'PROVISIONING': 50, 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE': 15, 'INSTALL': 140, 'PRE_BUILD': 40, 'BUILD': 195, 'POST_BUILD': 12, 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS': 5, 'FINALIZING': 2 } }, 'build_48': { 'branch': 'hotfix/location-validation', 'duration': timedelta(seconds=325), 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'phase_durations': { 'SUBMITTED': 1, 'PROVISIONING': 35, 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE': 8, 'INSTALL': 95, 'PRE_BUILD': 25, 'BUILD': 145, 'POST_BUILD': 13, 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS': 3, 'FINALIZING': 2 } }, 'build_49': { 'branch': 'feature/jobpost-enhancement', 'duration': timedelta(seconds=410), 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'phase_durations': { 'SUBMITTED': 1, 'PROVISIONING': 42, 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE': 11, 'INSTALL': 120, 'PRE_BUILD': 32, 'BUILD': 175, 'POST_BUILD': 24, 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS': 5, 'FINALIZING': 2 } } } print(f"\nPerformance summary:") duration_list = [data['duration'].total_seconds() for data in performance_data.values()] print(f"• Average build time: {statistics.mean(duration_list):.1f}s") print(f"• Fastest build: {min(duration_list):.0f}s") print(f"• Slowest build: {max(duration_list):.0f}s") print(f"• Standard deviation: {statistics.stdev(duration_list):.1f}s") # Phase analysis all_phases = ['SUBMITTED', 'PROVISIONING', 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE', 'INSTALL', 'PRE_BUILD', 'BUILD', 'POST_BUILD', 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS', 'FINALIZING'] phase_stats = {} for phase in all_phases: phase_durations = [data['phase_durations'][phase] for data in performance_data.values()] phase_stats[phase] = { 'average': statistics.mean(phase_durations), 'min': min(phase_durations), 'max': max(phase_durations) } print(f"\nPhase performance analysis:") for phase, stats in phase_stats.items(): print(f"\n{phase}:") print(f"• Average: {stats['average']:.1f}s") print(f"• Range: {stats['min']}s - {stats['max']}s") # Optimization opportunities optimization_opportunities = { 'BUILD': { 'current_avg': phase_stats['BUILD']['average'], 'optimization_target': '30% reduction', 'methods': ['Parallel testing', 'Optimized database setup', 'Cached dependencies'] }, 'INSTALL': { 'current_avg': phase_stats['INSTALL']['average'], 'optimization_target': '25% reduction', 'methods': ['Docker layer caching', 'Pre-built base images', 'pip caching'] }, 'PROVISIONING': { 'current_avg': phase_stats['PROVISIONING']['average'], 'optimization_target': '20% reduction', 'methods': ['Reserved capacity', 'Larger instance types', 'Regional optimization'] } } print(f"\nOptimization opportunities:") for phase, opp in optimization_opportunities.items(): print(f"\n{phase}:") print(f"• Current average: {opp['current_avg']:.1f}s") print(f"• Target reduction: {opp['optimization_target']}") print(f"• Methods: {', '.join(opp['methods'])}") print(f"\nModels consistently tested: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") print(f"All builds completed successfully (100% success rate)")
    Example 5: Export Build Data for Reporting

    Export comprehensive Django build data to generate reports and dashboards for the anihpj jobpost application development team.

    #!/bin/bash
# Export Django build data for comprehensive reporting
BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT=(
    "anihpj-django-build:report1-1a1a-2b2b-3c3c-4d4d5e5e6f6f1"
    "anihpj-django-build:report2-1a1a-2b2b-3c3c-4d4d5e5e6f6f2"
    "anihpj-django-build:report3-1a1a-2b2b-3c3c-4d4d5e5e6f6f3"
    "anihpj-django-build:report4-1a1a-2b2b-3c3c-4d4d5e5e6f6f4"
    "anihpj-django-build:report5-1a1a-2b2b-3c3c-4d4d5e5e6f6f5"
)

# Set up export directory and files
EXPORT_DIR="./django-build-reports"
EXPORT_DATE=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S")
REPORT_FILE="${EXPORT_DIR}/django-builds-${EXPORT_DATE}.json"
CSV_FILE="${EXPORT_DIR}/django-builds-${EXPORT_DATE}.csv"
SUMMARY_FILE="${EXPORT_DIR}/summary-${EXPORT_DATE}.txt"

echo "Django Build Data Export"
echo "======================="
echo "Export date: $(date)"
echo "Builds to export: ${#BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "Export directory: $EXPORT_DIR"
echo ""

# Create export directory
mkdir -p "$EXPORT_DIR"

# Export detailed build data to JSON
echo "Exporting detailed build data..."
aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}" \
    --output json > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "JSON report saved: $REPORT_FILE"

# Create CSV summary for spreadsheet analysis
echo "Creating CSV summary..."
echo "ID,BuildNumber,Status,StartTime,EndTime,Duration,SourceVersion,Project,TotalPhases,SuccessfulPhases,FailedPhases" > "$CSV_FILE"

# Process builds and create CSV entries
aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}" \
    --query 'builds[*].[id,buildNumber,buildStatus,startTime,endTime,sourceVersion,projectName,phases[?phaseStatus==`SUCCEEDED`] | length(@),phases[?phaseStatus==`FAILED`] | length(@),phases | length(@)]' \
    --output text | while read -r id build_num status start_time end_time source_version project_name successful_phases failed_phases total_phases; do
    
    # Calculate duration if both times are available
    if [[ "$end_time" != "None" && "$start_time" != "None" ]]; then
        start_epoch=$(date -d "$start_time" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
        end_epoch=$(date -d "$end_time" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
        duration=$((end_epoch - start_epoch))
    else
        duration="N/A"
    fi
    
    echo "$id,$build_num,$status,$start_time,$end_time,$duration,$source_version,$project_name,$total_phases,$successful_phases,$failed_phases" >> "$CSV_FILE"
done

echo "CSV report saved: $CSV_FILE"

# Create executive summary
echo "Generating executive summary..."
cat << EOF > "$SUMMARY_FILE"
Django Build Report Summary
==========================
Report generated: $(date)
Project: anihpj jobpost application
Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost
Builds analyzed: ${#BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}

Build Status Summary:
$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}" --query 'builds[*].buildStatus' --output text | sort | uniq -c | while read count status; do echo "- $status: $count builds"; done)

Average Phase Durations:
$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}" --query 'builds[].phases[*].{Phase:phaseType,Duration:durationInSeconds}' --output json | jq -r 'group_by(.Phase) | .[] | "- " + .[0].Phase + ": " + (map(.Duration) | add / length | floor | tostring) + "s avg"' | head -10)

Source Version Analysis:
$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids "${BUILD_IDS_FOR_EXPORT[@]}" --query 'builds[*].sourceVersion' --output text | sort | uniq -c | while read count version; do echo "- $version: $count builds"; done)

Files Generated:
- Detailed JSON: $(basename "$REPORT_FILE")
- CSV Summary: $(basename "$CSV_FILE")
- Executive Summary: $(basename "$SUMMARY_FILE")

Next Steps:
1. Import CSV into spreadsheet application for analysis
2. Review failed builds and identify patterns
3. Share summary with development team
4. Schedule regular build performance reviews

Report generated by AWS CodeBuild batch-get-builds automation
EOF

echo "Executive summary saved: $SUMMARY_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Export completed successfully!"
echo "Files generated:"
echo "1. $REPORT_FILE - Detailed JSON data"
echo "2. $CSV_FILE - Spreadsheet-ready CSV"
echo "3. $SUMMARY_FILE - Executive summary"
echo ""
echo "To view summary:"
echo "cat \"$SUMMARY_FILE\""
echo ""
echo "To analyze in Excel/Sheets:"
echo "Open \"$CSV_FILE\" in your preferred spreadsheet application"
    Django Build Data Export ======================= Export date: Wed Jan 15 14:45:30 UTC 2025 Builds to export: 5 Project: anihpj jobpost application Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Export directory: ./django-build-reports Exporting detailed build data... JSON report saved: ./django-build-reports/django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.json Creating CSV summary... CSV report saved: ./django-build-reports/django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.csv Generating executive summary... Executive summary saved: ./django-build-reports/summary-2025-01-15_14-45-30.txt Export completed successfully! Files generated: 1. ./django-build-reports/django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.json - Detailed JSON data 2. ./django-build-reports/django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.csv - Spreadsheet-ready CSV 3. ./django-build-reports/summary-2025-01-15_14-45-30.txt - Executive summary To view summary: cat "./django-build-reports/summary-2025-01-15_14-45-30.txt" To analyze in Excel/Sheets: Open "./django-build-reports/django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.csv" in your preferred spreadsheet application
    # Django Build Data Export Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import json import csv print(f"Django build data export report") print(f"Export timestamp: {datetime.now()}") print(f"Builds exported: 5") print(f"Project: anihpj jobpost application") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Export summary export_summary = { 'total_builds': 5, 'export_date': '2025-01-15', 'export_time': '14:45:30 UTC', 'project_name': 'anihpj-django-build', 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'export_formats': ['JSON', 'CSV', 'TXT'] } print(f"\nExport summary: {export_summary}") # File generation details files_generated = { 'json_report': { 'filename': 'django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.json', 'size': '45.2 KB', 'content': 'Complete build details with phases, logs, artifacts', 'purpose': 'Detailed analysis and API integration' }, 'csv_summary': { 'filename': 'django-builds-2025-01-15_14-45-30.csv', 'size': '2.1 KB', 'content': 'Tabular data with key metrics and durations', 'purpose': 'Spreadsheet analysis and charts' }, 'executive_summary': { 'filename': 'summary-2025-01-15_14-45-30.txt', 'size': '1.8 KB', 'content': 'High-level overview and recommendations', 'purpose': 'Management reporting and decision making' } } print(f"\nFiles generated:") for file_type, details in files_generated.items(): print(f"\n{file_type.upper()}:") for key, value in details.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Build statistics from export build_statistics = { 'status_distribution': { 'SUCCEEDED': 4, 'FAILED': 1, 'IN_PROGRESS': 0 }, 'average_durations': { 'INSTALL': '115s', 'BUILD': '170s', 'POST_BUILD': '16s', 'TOTAL': '398s' }, 'source_versions': { 'main': 2, 'feature/skills-optimization': 1, 'feature/user-model-refactor': 1, 'hotfix/location-validation': 1 } } print(f"\nBuild statistics:") for category, stats in build_statistics.items(): print(f"\n{category.upper()}:") for key, value in stats.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Usage recommendations usage_recommendations = [ 'Import CSV file into Excel/Google Sheets for trend analysis', 'Use JSON data for automated dashboard integration', 'Share executive summary with stakeholders', 'Schedule weekly build performance reviews', 'Set up automated reporting pipeline', 'Monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost model test performance', 'Track build duration trends over time' ] print(f"\nUsage recommendations:") for i, rec in enumerate(usage_recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") # Export metrics export_metrics = { 'data_completeness': '100%', 'export_duration': '45 seconds', 'file_formats': 3, 'total_file_size': '49.1 KB', 'builds_with_complete_data': 5, 'models_coverage': '100% (all 5 models)' } print(f"\nExport metrics:") for metric, value in export_metrics.items(): print(f"• {metric}: {value}") print(f"\nExport completed successfully for anihpj Django jobpost application") print(f"Ready for analysis and reporting")

    list-builds

    Gets a list of build IDs. View all Django jobpost application build executions across projects, with optional sorting and pagination for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model builds.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-builds \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • ids: Array of build IDs in the account
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available
    5 Detailed CodeBuild Examples
    Example 1: List Recent Django Builds

    List the most recent Django builds for the anihpj jobpost application to monitor development progress across all models.

    #!/bin/bash
# List recent Django builds for monitoring
MAX_BUILDS=20

echo "Listing recent Django builds..."
echo "Maximum builds: $MAX_BUILDS"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "="

# List builds with detailed output
RECENT_BUILDS=$(aws codebuild list-builds \
    --sort-order DESCENDING \
    --max-items $MAX_BUILDS \
    --output json)

echo "$RECENT_BUILDS" | jq '.ids | length' | xargs -I {} echo "Total builds found: {}"
echo ""

# Get detailed information for recent builds
echo "Recent build summary:"
echo "-------------------"

# Extract build IDs and get their details
BUILD_IDS=$(echo "$RECENT_BUILDS" | jq -r '.ids[]' | head -5)

if [ -n "$BUILD_IDS" ]; then
    echo "Getting details for 5 most recent builds..."
    
    aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids $BUILD_IDS \
        --query 'builds[*].{BuildNumber:buildNumber,Status:buildStatus,Project:projectName,StartTime:startTime,Duration:endTime,SourceVersion:sourceVersion}' \
        --output table
else
    echo "No builds found"
fi

echo ""
echo "Build listing completed."
    { "ids": [ "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789013", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789014", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789015", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789016", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789017", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789018", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789019", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789020", "anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789021" ], "nextToken": "eyJOZXh0VG9rZW4iOiJudWxsIiwiYm90b190cnVuY2F0ZV9hbW91bnQiOjIwfQ==" } Total builds found: 10 Recent build summary: ------------------- Getting details for 5 most recent builds... ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | BatchGetBuilds | +-------------+------------------+--------------------+---------------------------+-----------------------------+-------------------+ | BuildNumber | Duration | Project | SourceVersion | StartTime | Status | +-------------+------------------+--------------------+---------------------------+-----------------------------+-------------------+ | 156 | 2025-01-15T15:15:30Z| anihpj-django-build| feature/jobpost-search | 2025-01-15T15:08:00Z | SUCCEEDED | | 155 | 2025-01-15T15:05:45Z| anihpj-django-build| main | 2025-01-15T14:58:00Z | SUCCEEDED | | 154 | 2025-01-15T14:52:30Z| anihpj-django-build| feature/skills-filter | 2025-01-15T14:45:00Z | SUCCEEDED | | 153 | 2025-01-15T14:40:15Z| anihpj-django-build| hotfix/user-validation | 2025-01-15T14:33:00Z | SUCCEEDED | | 152 | 2025-01-15T14:28:20Z| anihpj-django-build| feature/location-search | 2025-01-15T14:20:00Z | FAILED | +-------------+------------------+--------------------+---------------------------+-----------------------------+-------------------+ Build listing completed.
    # Django Recent Builds List from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django recent builds analysis") print(f"Total builds retrieved: 10") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Recent builds summary recent_builds = { 'build_156': { 'source_version': 'feature/jobpost-search', 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 15, 8, 0), 'duration': timedelta(minutes=7, seconds=30), 'models_affected': ['JobPost', 'Skills', 'Location'] }, 'build_155': { 'source_version': 'main', 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 14, 58, 0), 'duration': timedelta(minutes=7, seconds=45), 'models_affected': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'] }, 'build_154': { 'source_version': 'feature/skills-filter', 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 14, 45, 0), 'duration': timedelta(minutes=7, seconds=30), 'models_affected': ['Skills', 'JobPost'] }, 'build_153': { 'source_version': 'hotfix/user-validation', 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 14, 33, 0), 'duration': timedelta(minutes=7, seconds=15), 'models_affected': ['User', 'Author'] }, 'build_152': { 'source_version': 'feature/location-search', 'status': 'FAILED', 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 14, 20, 0), 'duration': timedelta(minutes=8, seconds=20), 'models_affected': ['Location', 'JobPost'] } } print(f"\n5 Most recent builds:") for build_id, details in recent_builds.items(): print(f"\n{build_id.upper()}:") for key, value in details.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Build frequency analysis build_frequency = { 'today': 5, 'this_week': 10, 'success_rate': '80%', # 4/5 recent builds succeeded 'average_duration': timedelta(minutes=7, seconds=38), 'most_active_branch': 'feature branches' } print(f"\nBuild frequency analysis:") for metric, value in build_frequency.items(): print(f"• {metric}: {value}") # Model coverage in recent builds model_coverage = { 'JobPost': 4, # in 4 out of 5 builds 'Skills': 3, # in 3 out of 5 builds 'Location': 3, # in 3 out of 5 builds 'User': 2, # in 2 out of 5 builds 'Author': 2 # in 2 out of 5 builds } print(f"\nModel test coverage in recent builds:") for model, count in sorted(model_coverage.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True): percentage = (count / 5) * 100 print(f"• {model}: {count}/5 builds ({percentage:.0f}%)") print(f"\nNext actions:") print(f"1. Investigate failed Location search functionality (build 152)") print(f"2. Monitor JobPost model changes (highest test frequency)") print(f"3. Review main branch stability (100% success rate)")
    Example 2: Paginate Through All Builds

    Use pagination to retrieve all Django builds in the anihpj jobpost application, handling large result sets efficiently.

    #!/bin/bash
# Paginate through all Django builds
TOTAL_BUILDS=0
PAGE_COUNT=0
NEXT_TOKEN=""
MAX_ITEMS_PER_PAGE=10
ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE="./all_django_builds.txt"

echo "Paginating through all Django builds..."
echo "Page size: $MAX_ITEMS_PER_PAGE builds"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "Output file: $ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE"
echo "="

# Clear the output file
> "$ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE"

# Loop through all pages
while true; do
    ((PAGE_COUNT++))
    echo "Fetching page $PAGE_COUNT..."
    
    # Build the command with optional next-token
    CMD="aws codebuild list-builds --sort-order DESCENDING --max-items $MAX_ITEMS_PER_PAGE --output json"
    if [ -n "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        CMD="$CMD --starting-token '$NEXT_TOKEN'"
    fi
    
    # Execute the command
    PAGE_RESULT=$(eval $CMD)
    
    # Extract build IDs and next token
    BUILD_IDS=$(echo "$PAGE_RESULT" | jq -r '.ids[]?' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    NEW_NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$PAGE_RESULT" | jq -r '.NextToken // empty' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    # Count builds in this page
    PAGE_BUILD_COUNT=$(echo "$BUILD_IDS" | grep -c . 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    TOTAL_BUILDS=$((TOTAL_BUILDS + PAGE_BUILD_COUNT))
    
    echo "  Builds in page $PAGE_COUNT: $PAGE_BUILD_COUNT"
    echo "  Total builds so far: $TOTAL_BUILDS"
    
    # Save build IDs to file
    if [ -n "$BUILD_IDS" ]; then
        echo "$BUILD_IDS" >> "$ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE"
        echo "  Build IDs saved to file"
    fi
    
    # Check if we have more pages
    if [ -z "$NEW_NEXT_TOKEN" ] || [ "$NEW_NEXT_TOKEN" = "null" ]; then
        echo "  No more pages. Pagination complete."
        break
    fi
    
    NEXT_TOKEN="$NEW_NEXT_TOKEN"
    echo "  Next token: ${NEXT_TOKEN:0:50}..."
    echo "---"
    
    # Add a small delay to avoid rate limiting
    sleep 1
done

echo ""
echo "Pagination Summary:"
echo "=================="
echo "Total pages processed: $PAGE_COUNT"
echo "Total builds found: $TOTAL_BUILDS"
echo "Build IDs saved to: $ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE"

# Display sample of collected build IDs
echo ""
echo "Sample of collected build IDs:"
echo "------------------------------"
head -5 "$ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE" | nl

echo ""
echo "To get detailed information for all builds:"
echo "cat '$ALL_BUILD_IDS_FILE' | xargs aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids"
echo ""
echo "Total Django builds discovered: $TOTAL_BUILDS"
    Paginating through all Django builds... Page size: 10 builds Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Output file: ./all_django_builds.txt = Fetching page 1... Builds in page 1: 10 Total builds so far: 10 Build IDs saved to file Next token: eyJOZXh0VG9rZW4iOiJudWxsIiwiYm90b190cnVuY2F0ZV9hbW91bnQiOjEwfQ... --- Fetching page 2... Builds in page 2: 10 Total builds so far: 20 Build IDs saved to file Next token: eyJOZXh0VG9rZW4iOiJudWxsIiwiYm90b190cnVuY2F0ZV9hbW91bnQiOjIwfQ... --- Fetching page 3... Builds in page 3: 10 Total builds so far: 30 Build IDs saved to file Next token: eyJOZXh0VG9rZW4iOiJudWxsIiwiYm90b190cnVuY2F0ZV9hbW91bnQiOjMwfQ... --- Fetching page 4... Builds in page 4: 8 Total builds so far: 38 Build IDs saved to file No more pages. Pagination complete. Pagination Summary: ================== Total pages processed: 4 Total builds found: 38 Build IDs saved to: ./all_django_builds.txt Sample of collected build IDs: ------------------------------ 1 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012 2 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789013 3 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789014 4 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789015 5 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789016 To get detailed information for all builds: cat './all_django_builds.txt' | xargs aws codebuild batch-get-builds --ids Total Django builds discovered: 38
    # Django Builds Pagination Analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django builds pagination analysis") print(f"Total pages processed: 4") print(f"Total builds discovered: 38") print(f"Page size: 10 builds per page") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Pagination statistics pagination_stats = { 'page_1': {'builds': 10, 'status': 'full_page'}, 'page_2': {'builds': 10, 'status': 'full_page'}, 'page_3': {'builds': 10, 'status': 'full_page'}, 'page_4': {'builds': 8, 'status': 'partial_page'} } print(f"\nPagination breakdown:") for page, stats in pagination_stats.items(): print(f"{page}: {stats['builds']} builds ({stats['status']})") # Build collection summary collection_summary = { 'total_api_calls': 4, 'average_builds_per_page': 38 / 4, 'pagination_efficiency': '95%', # 3/4 pages were full 'data_completeness': '100%', 'file_output': './all_django_builds.txt', 'estimated_time': '4 seconds (1s delay between pages)' } print(f"\nCollection summary:") for metric, value in collection_summary.items(): print(f"• {metric}: {value}") # Build ID patterns analysis build_id_patterns = { 'prefix_pattern': 'anihpj-django-build:', 'uuid_format': 'standard UUID format', 'total_unique_builds': 38, 'estimated_project_age': 'Several weeks (38 builds)', 'build_naming_consistent': True } print(f"\nBuild ID analysis:") for pattern, value in build_id_patterns.items(): print(f"• {pattern}: {value}") # Usage recommendations usage_recommendations = [ 'Use collected build IDs for batch analysis', 'Import into spreadsheet for trend analysis', 'Set up automated collection for monitoring', 'Filter by date ranges for specific periods', 'Combine with batch-get-builds for full details', 'Monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost model testing frequency' ] print(f"\nRecommended next steps:") for i, rec in enumerate(usage_recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") # Performance insights performance_insights = { 'pagination_needed': True, # 38 > default page size 'api_efficiency': 'Good (4 calls vs 38 individual calls)', 'memory_usage': 'Low (streaming to file)', 'rate_limit_safe': True, # 1s delay between calls 'scalability': 'Excellent for large datasets' } print(f"\nPerformance insights:") for insight, value in performance_insights.items(): print(f"• {insight}: {value}") print(f"\nTotal Django builds in anihpj jobpost application: 38") print(f"All build IDs successfully collected and saved") print(f"Ready for detailed analysis of Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost models")
    Example 3: Sort and Filter Build Status

    List Django builds with custom sorting and filtering to analyze build patterns for the anihpj jobpost application models.

    #!/bin/bash
# Sort and filter Django builds by status
SORT_ORDER="DESCENDING"  # Get newest first
MAX_ITEMS=50
TEMP_FILE="./temp_build_analysis.json"
STATUS_REPORT="./build_status_report.txt"

echo "Django Build Status Analysis"
echo "============================="
echo "Sort order: $SORT_ORDER"
echo "Maximum items: $MAX_ITEMS"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "="

# Get recent builds
echo "Retrieving recent builds..."
aws codebuild list-builds \
    --sort-order $SORT_ORDER \
    --max-items $MAX_ITEMS \
    --output json > "$TEMP_FILE"

# Extract build IDs
BUILD_IDS=$(cat "$TEMP_FILE" | jq -r '.ids[]')
TOTAL_BUILDS=$(echo "$BUILD_IDS" | wc -l)

echo "Total builds retrieved: $TOTAL_BUILDS"
echo ""

if [ $TOTAL_BUILDS -gt 0 ]; then
    # Get detailed status for all builds
    echo "Analyzing build statuses..."
    BUILD_DETAILS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids $BUILD_IDS \
        --query 'builds[*].{ID:id,Status:buildStatus,Project:projectName,StartTime:startTime,SourceVersion:sourceVersion,BuildNumber:buildNumber}' \
        --output json)
    
    # Create status report
    cat << EOF > "$STATUS_REPORT"
Django Build Status Report
=========================
Generated: $(date)
Project: anihpj-django-build
Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost
Total builds analyzed: $TOTAL_BUILDS

STATUS BREAKDOWN:
$(echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r 'group_by(.Status) | .[] | "- " + .[0].Status + ": " + (. | length | tostring) + " builds"' | sort)

SOURCE VERSION ANALYSIS:
$(echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r 'group_by(.SourceVersion) | .[] | "- " + .[0].SourceVersion + ": " + (. | length | tostring) + " builds"' | sort | head -10)

RECENT FAILED BUILDS:
$(echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r '.[] | select(.Status == "FAILED") | "- Build #" + (.BuildNumber|tostring) + " (" + .SourceVersion + ") - " + .StartTime' | head -5)

RECENT SUCCESSFUL BUILDS:
$(echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r '.[] | select(.Status == "SUCCEEDED") | "- Build #" + (.BuildNumber|tostring) + " (" + .SourceVersion + ") - " + .StartTime' | head -5)

RECOMMENDATIONS:
EOF

    # Calculate success rate
    SUCCESS_COUNT=$(echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq '[.[] | select(.Status == "SUCCEEDED")] | length')
    FAILED_COUNT=$(echo "$BUILD_DETAILS" | jq '[.[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length')
    SUCCESS_RATE=$(echo "scale=1; $SUCCESS_COUNT * 100 / $TOTAL_BUILDS" | bc)
    
    echo "1. Current success rate: $SUCCESS_RATE% ($SUCCESS_COUNT/$TOTAL_BUILDS)" >> "$STATUS_REPORT"
    
    if [ "$FAILED_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
        echo "2. Investigate $FAILED_COUNT failed builds for common issues" >> "$STATUS_REPORT"
    fi
    
    echo "3. Monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost model test stability" >> "$STATUS_REPORT"
    echo "4. Consider build optimization for consistent performance" >> "$STATUS_REPORT"
    
    # Display summary
    echo "Status analysis complete!"
    echo "Report saved: $STATUS_REPORT"
    echo ""
    echo "Quick Summary:"
    echo "- Total builds: $TOTAL_BUILDS"
    echo "- Success rate: $SUCCESS_RATE%"
    echo "- Successful builds: $SUCCESS_COUNT"
    echo "- Failed builds: $FAILED_COUNT"
    
    # Clean up
    rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"
else
    echo "No builds found to analyze."
fi

echo ""
echo "Analysis completed."
    Django Build Status Analysis ============================= Sort order: DESCENDING Maximum items: 50 Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost = Retrieving recent builds... Total builds retrieved: 42 Analyzing build statuses... Status analysis complete! Report saved: ./build_status_report.txt Quick Summary: - Total builds: 42 - Success rate: 85.7% - Successful builds: 36 - Failed builds: 6 Analysis completed. --- Contents of build_status_report.txt --- Django Build Status Report ========================= Generated: Wed Jan 15 15:30:45 UTC 2025 Project: anihpj-django-build Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Total builds analyzed: 42 STATUS BREAKDOWN: - FAILED: 6 builds - SUCCEEDED: 36 builds SOURCE VERSION ANALYSIS: - feature/author-profile: 3 builds - feature/jobpost-enhancement: 4 builds - feature/jobpost-search: 5 builds - feature/location-autocomplete: 3 builds - feature/location-search: 2 builds - feature/skills-filter: 4 builds - feature/skills-validation: 3 builds - feature/user-registration: 5 builds - hotfix/jobpost-display: 2 builds - hotfix/user-validation: 3 builds - main: 8 builds RECENT FAILED BUILDS: - Build #148 (hotfix/jobpost-display) - 2025-01-15T10:00:00Z - Build #152 (feature/location-search) - 2025-01-15T14:20:00Z - Build #139 (feature/skills-validation) - 2025-01-14T09:30:00Z - Build #127 (feature/user-registration) - 2025-01-13T16:45:00Z - Build #115 (main) - 2025-01-12T14:20:00Z RECENT SUCCESSFUL BUILDS: - Build #156 (feature/jobpost-search) - 2025-01-15T15:08:00Z - Build #155 (main) - 2025-01-15T14:58:00Z - Build #154 (feature/skills-filter) - 2025-01-15T14:45:00Z - Build #153 (hotfix/user-validation) - 2025-01-15T14:33:00Z - Build #151 (feature/author-profile) - 2025-01-15T13:45:00Z RECOMMENDATIONS: 1. Current success rate: 85.7% (36/42) 2. Investigate 6 failed builds for common issues 3. Monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost model test stability 4. Consider build optimization for consistent performance
    # Django Build Status Analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django build status analysis") print(f"Total builds analyzed: 42") print(f"Success rate: 85.7%") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Status breakdown status_breakdown = { 'SUCCEEDED': 36, 'FAILED': 6, 'IN_PROGRESS': 0, 'STOPPED': 0 } print(f"\nStatus distribution:") for status, count in status_breakdown.items(): percentage = (count / 42) * 100 print(f"• {status}: {count} builds ({percentage:.1f}%)") # Source version analysis source_version_analysis = { 'main': {'builds': 8, 'success_rate': 87.5}, 'feature/jobpost-search': {'builds': 5, 'success_rate': 100.0}, 'feature/user-registration': {'builds': 5, 'success_rate': 80.0}, 'feature/jobpost-enhancement': {'builds': 4, 'success_rate': 75.0}, 'feature/skills-filter': {'builds': 4, 'success_rate': 100.0}, 'feature/author-profile': {'builds': 3, 'success_rate': 100.0}, 'feature/skills-validation': {'builds': 3, 'success_rate': 66.7}, 'feature/location-autocomplete': {'builds': 3, 'success_rate': 100.0}, 'hotfix/user-validation': {'builds': 3, 'success_rate': 100.0}, 'feature/location-search': {'builds': 2, 'success_rate': 50.0}, 'hotfix/jobpost-display': {'builds': 2, 'success_rate': 50.0} } print(f"\nSource version performance:") for version, data in sorted(source_version_analysis.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['builds'], reverse=True): print(f"• {version}: {data['builds']} builds ({data['success_rate']:.1f}% success)") # Failed builds analysis failed_builds = [ {'build_number': 148, 'branch': 'hotfix/jobpost-display', 'date': '2025-01-15', 'models_affected': ['JobPost']}, {'build_number': 152, 'branch': 'feature/location-search', 'date': '2025-01-15', 'models_affected': ['Location', 'JobPost']}, {'build_number': 139, 'branch': 'feature/skills-validation', 'date': '2025-01-14', 'models_affected': ['Skills']}, {'build_number': 127, 'branch': 'feature/user-registration', 'date': '2025-01-13', 'models_affected': ['User', 'Author']}, {'build_number': 115, 'branch': 'main', 'date': '2025-01-12', 'models_affected': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost']} ] print(f"\nFailed builds analysis:") for build in failed_builds: print(f"• Build #{build['build_number']} ({build['branch']}) - {build['date']}") print(f" Models affected: {', '.join(build['models_affected'])}") # Model impact analysis model_impact = { 'JobPost': {'total_tests': 25, 'failures': 3, 'failure_rate': 12.0}, 'Skills': {'total_tests': 18, 'failures': 2, 'failure_rate': 11.1}, 'Location': {'total_tests': 15, 'failures': 2, 'failure_rate': 13.3}, 'User': {'total_tests': 20, 'failures': 2, 'failure_rate': 10.0}, 'Author': {'total_tests': 12, 'failures': 2, 'failure_rate': 16.7} } print(f"\nModel test stability:") for model, data in sorted(model_impact.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['failure_rate'], reverse=True): print(f"• {model}: {data['failures']}/{data['total_tests']} failures ({data['failure_rate']:.1f}% failure rate)") # Recommendations recommendations = [ 'Focus on Author model stability (highest failure rate: 16.7%)', 'Investigate Location and JobPost model integration issues', 'Review Skills validation logic for edge cases', 'Improve User registration flow testing', 'Consider hotfix branch testing before merge', 'Monitor main branch quality (1 failure in 8 builds)' ] print(f"\nRecommendations:") for i, rec in enumerate(recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") print(f"\nOverall project health: Good (85.7% success rate)") print(f"Areas for improvement: Author and Location model testing")
    Example 4: Export Build List to CSV

    Export Django build list to CSV format for analysis and reporting of the anihpj jobpost application development metrics.

    #!/bin/bash
# Export Django build list to CSV for analysis
CSV_OUTPUT="./django_builds_export.csv"
TEMP_JSON="./temp_builds.json"
MAX_BUILDS=100
EXPORT_DATE=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S")
FINAL_CSV="./django_builds_${EXPORT_DATE}.csv"

echo "Django Build Export to CSV"
echo "==========================="
echo "Export date: $(date)"
echo "Maximum builds: $MAX_BUILDS"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "Output file: $FINAL_CSV"
echo "="

# Get build list
echo "Retrieving build list..."
aws codebuild list-builds \
    --sort-order DESCENDING \
    --max-items $MAX_BUILDS \
    --output json > "$TEMP_JSON"

# Extract build IDs
BUILD_IDS=$(cat "$TEMP_JSON" | jq -r '.ids[]')
BUILD_COUNT=$(echo "$BUILD_IDS" | wc -l)

echo "Build IDs retrieved: $BUILD_COUNT"
echo ""

if [ $BUILD_COUNT -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Getting detailed build information..."
    
    # Create CSV header
    cat << EOF > "$FINAL_CSV"
BuildID,BuildNumber,Status,ProjectName,SourceVersion,StartTime,EndTime,Duration,BuildPhases,Environment,Region
EOF

    # Get detailed build information and process in batches
    echo "$BUILD_IDS" | split -l 20 - batch_
    
    for BATCH_FILE in batch_*; do
        BATCH_IDS=$(cat "$BATCH_FILE" | tr '\n' ' ')
        echo "Processing batch: $BATCH_FILE..."
        
        aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
            --ids $BATCH_IDS \
            --query 'builds[*].[id,buildNumber,buildStatus,projectName,sourceVersion,startTime,endTime,phases[?phaseStatus==`SUCCEEDED`] | length(@),environment.type]' \
            --output text | while IFS=$'\t' read -r id build_num status project_name source_version start_time end_time phases env_type; do
            
            # Calculate duration if both times are available
            if [[ "$end_time" != "None" && "$start_time" != "None" ]]; then
                start_epoch=$(date -d "$start_time" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
                end_epoch=$(date -d "$end_time" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
                duration=$((end_epoch - start_epoch))
            else
                duration="N/A"
            fi
            
            # Default values for missing data
            project_name=${project_name:-"anihpj-django-build"}
            env_type=${env_type:-"LINUX_CONTAINER"}
            region=$(aws configure get region 2>/dev/null || echo "us-east-1")
            
            # Write CSV row
            echo "$id,$build_num,$status,$project_name,$source_version,$start_time,$end_time,$duration,$phases,$env_type,$region"
        done >> "$FINAL_CSV"
        
        # Clean up batch file
        rm -f "$BATCH_FILE"
        
        # Small delay to avoid rate limiting
        sleep 0.5
    done
    
    # Generate summary statistics
    SUMMARY_FILE="./django_builds_summary_${EXPORT_DATE}.txt"
    cat << EOF > "$SUMMARY_FILE"
Django Build Export Summary
==========================
Export Date: $(date)
Project: anihpj jobpost application
Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost

Export Statistics:
- Total builds exported: $BUILD_COUNT
- CSV file: $FINAL_CSV
- File size: $(ls -lh "$FINAL_CSV" | awk '{print $5}' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")

CSV Structure:
- BuildID: Unique identifier for each build
- BuildNumber: Sequential build number
- Status: Build result (SUCCEEDED, FAILED, etc.)
- ProjectName: CodeBuild project name
- SourceVersion: Git branch/commit
- StartTime: Build start timestamp
- EndTime: Build completion timestamp
- Duration: Build duration in seconds
- BuildPhases: Number of successful phases
- Environment: Build environment type
- Region: AWS region

Usage Instructions:
1. Import CSV into Excel/Google Sheets for analysis
2. Create charts for build trends and success rates
3. Filter by SourceVersion for branch-specific analysis
4. Use Duration column for performance optimization

Next Steps:
- Analyze build duration trends
- Identify patterns in failed builds
- Monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost model test performance
- Create automated reporting dashboard
EOF

    echo "Export completed successfully!"
    echo "Files generated:"
    echo "1. CSV export: $FINAL_CSV"
    echo "2. Summary report: $SUMMARY_FILE"
    echo ""
    echo "CSV rows exported: $(wc -l < "$FINAL_CSV")" 
    echo "(Including header row)"
    echo ""
    echo "To view first few rows:"
    echo "head -5 '$FINAL_CSV'"
else
    echo "No builds found to export."
fi

# Clean up temporary files
rm -f "$TEMP_JSON"

echo ""
echo "Export process completed."
    Django Build Export to CSV =========================== Export date: Wed Jan 15 15:45:30 UTC 2025 Maximum builds: 100 Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Output file: ./django_builds_2025-01-15_15-45-30.csv = Retrieving build list... Build IDs retrieved: 78 Getting detailed build information... Processing batch: batch_aa... Processing batch: batch_ab... Processing batch: batch_ac... Processing batch: batch_ad... Summary report: ./django_builds_summary_2025-01-15_15-45-30.txt CSV rows exported: 79 (Including header row) To view first few rows: head -5 './django_builds_2025-01-15_15-45-30.csv' Export process completed. --- Sample CSV Content --- BuildID,BuildNumber,Status,ProjectName,SourceVersion,StartTime,EndTime,Duration,BuildPhases,Environment,Region anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012,156,SUCCEEDED,anihpj-django-build,feature/jobpost-search,2025-01-15T15:08:00Z,2025-01-15T15:15:30Z,450,9,LINUX_CONTAINER,us-east-1 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789013,155,SUCCEEDED,anihpj-django-build,main,2025-01-15T14:58:00Z,2025-01-15T15:05:45Z,465,9,LINUX_CONTAINER,us-east-1 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789014,154,SUCCEEDED,anihpj-django-build,feature/skills-filter,2025-01-15T14:45:00Z,2025-01-15T14:52:30Z,450,9,LINUX_CONTAINER,us-east-1 anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789015,153,SUCCEEDED,anihpj-django-build,hotfix/user-validation,2025-01-15T14:33:00Z,2025-01-15T14:40:15Z,435,9,LINUX_CONTAINER,us-east-1

Export statistics export_stats = { 'total_builds': 78, 'csv_rows': 79, # Including header 'batches_processed': 4, 'builds_per_batch': 20, 'export_duration': '2 minutes', 'file_size': '15.2 KB', 'success_rate': '95%', 'api_calls_made': 4 } print(f"\nExport statistics:") for stat, value in export_stats.items(): print(f"• {stat}: {value}") # Sample data analysis sample_data = { 'build_156': { 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'source_version': 'feature/jobpost-search', 'duration': 450, 'phases': 9, 'models_tested': ['JobPost', 'Skills', 'Location'] }, 'build_155': { 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'source_version': 'main', 'duration': 465, 'phases': 9, 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'] }, 'build_154': { 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'source_version': 'feature/skills-filter', 'duration': 450, 'phases': 9, 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'JobPost'] }, 'build_153': { 'status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'source_version': 'hotfix/user-validation', 'duration': 435, 'phases': 9, 'models_tested': ['User', 'Author'] } } print(f"\nSample build data:") for build_id, data in sample_data.items(): print(f"\n{build_id.upper()}:") for key, value in data.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Analysis recommendations analysis_recommendations = [ 'Create pivot tables for status distribution analysis', 'Generate duration trend charts over time', 'Filter by SourceVersion for branch performance comparison', 'Calculate average build times by environment type', 'Identify peak build times during the day', 'Analyze correlation between build phases and success rate', 'Monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost model test patterns', 'Set up automated weekly CSV exports' ] print(f"\nRecommended CSV analysis:") for i, rec in enumerate(analysis_recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") # File usage insights usage_insights = { 'import_compatibility': 'Excel, Google Sheets, Python pandas, R', 'recommended_charts': ['Build duration over time', 'Status distribution', 'Branch success rates'], 'filtering_options': ['By date range', 'By source version', 'By status', 'By duration'], 'aggregation_possibilities': ['Daily build counts', 'Average durations', 'Success rates by branch'], 'dashboard_potential': 'High - suitable for automated reporting' } print(f"\nFile usage insights:") for insight, value in usage_insights.items(): print(f"• {insight}: {value}") print(f"\nCSV export successful for anihpj Django jobpost application") print(f"Ready for comprehensive build analysis and reporting") print(f"All Django model testing data (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost) included")
    Example 5: Monitor Live Build Activity

    Monitor live Django build activity with real-time updates for the anihpj jobpost application development process.

    #!/bin/bash
# Monitor live Django build activity
MONITOR_INTERVAL=15  # Check every 15 seconds
MAX_MONITORING_TIME=3600  # Monitor for 1 hour
LAST_BUILD_FILE="./last_build_check.txt"
LOG_FILE="./build_activity_log.txt"

echo "Django Live Build Activity Monitor"
echo "==================================="
echo "Check interval: ${MONITOR_INTERVAL} seconds"
echo "Maximum monitoring time: $((MAX_MONITORING_TIME / 60)) minutes"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
echo "="

# Initialize log file
echo "Django Build Activity Log - Started at $(date)" > "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "=" >> "$LOG_FILE"

# Get initial state
echo "Getting initial build state..."
INITIAL_BUILDS=$(aws codebuild list-builds --sort-order DESCENDING --max-items 5 --output json)
LAST_BUILD_ID=$(echo "$INITIAL_BUILDS" | jq -r '.ids[0]')
echo "$LAST_BUILD_ID" > "$LAST_BUILD_FILE"

echo "Initial last build: $LAST_BUILD_ID"
echo "Starting live monitoring..."
echo ""

# Monitor loop
START_TIME=$(date +%s)
CHECK_COUNT=0
NEW_BUILDS_DETECTED=0

while true; do
    CURRENT_TIME=$(date +%s)
    ELAPSED_TIME=$((CURRENT_TIME - START_TIME))
    
    # Check if we've exceeded max monitoring time
    if [ $ELAPSED_TIME -gt $MAX_MONITORING_TIME ]; then
        echo "Maximum monitoring time reached. Stopping monitor."
        break
    fi
    
    ((CHECK_COUNT++))
    echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] Check #$CHECK_COUNT (Elapsed: ${ELAPSED_TIME}s)"
    
    # Get current latest builds
    CURRENT_BUILDS=$(aws codebuild list-builds --sort-order DESCENDING --max-items 10 --output json)
    CURRENT_LATEST=$(echo "$CURRENT_BUILDS" | jq -r '.ids[0]')
    
    # Compare with last known build
    LAST_KNOWN=$(cat "$LAST_BUILD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [ "$CURRENT_LATEST" != "$LAST_KNOWN" ]; then
        echo "  🔥 NEW BUILD ACTIVITY DETECTED!"
        ((NEW_BUILDS_DETECTED++))
        
        # Get details of new builds
        NEW_BUILD_IDS=$(echo "$CURRENT_BUILDS" | jq -r '.ids[]' | head -5)
        echo "  Analyzing new builds..."
        
        NEW_BUILD_DETAILS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
            --ids $NEW_BUILD_IDS \
            --query 'builds[*].{ID:id,BuildNumber:buildNumber,Status:buildStatus,Project:projectName,StartTime:startTime,SourceVersion:sourceVersion}' \
            --output json)
        
        # Log the new activity
        echo "\n$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - NEW BUILD ACTIVITY" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo "$NEW_BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r '.[] | "- Build #" + (.BuildNumber|tostring) + " (" + .SourceVersion + ") - Status: " + .Status + " - Started: " + .StartTime' >> "$LOG_FILE"
        
        # Display new builds
        echo "  New builds found:"
        echo "$NEW_BUILD_DETAILS" | jq -r '.[] | "    - Build #" + (.BuildNumber|tostring) + " (" + .SourceVersion + ") - " + .Status'
        
        # Check for in-progress builds
        IN_PROGRESS_COUNT=$(echo "$NEW_BUILD_DETAILS" | jq '[.[] | select(.Status == "IN_PROGRESS")] | length')
        if [ "$IN_PROGRESS_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "  📊 $IN_PROGRESS_COUNT build(s) currently in progress"
            
            # Log in-progress builds
            echo "    Active builds: $IN_PROGRESS_COUNT" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        fi
        
        # Update last known build
        echo "$CURRENT_LATEST" > "$LAST_BUILD_FILE"
    else
        echo "  ✓ No new builds detected"
    fi
    
    # Display monitoring stats
    echo "  Total new builds detected: $NEW_BUILDS_DETECTED"
    echo "  Next check in ${MONITOR_INTERVAL}s..."
    echo "---"
    
    sleep $MONITOR_INTERVAL
done

echo ""
echo "Monitoring Summary:"
echo "=================="
echo "Total monitoring time: $((ELAPSED_TIME / 60)) minutes"
echo "Total checks performed: $CHECK_COUNT"
echo "New builds detected: $NEW_BUILDS_DETECTED"
echo "Average check interval: $((ELAPSED_TIME / CHECK_COUNT)) seconds"

# Append summary to log
echo "\nMonitoring Summary:" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Ended at: $(date)" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Duration: $((ELAPSED_TIME / 60)) minutes" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Checks performed: $CHECK_COUNT" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "New builds detected: $NEW_BUILDS_DETECTED" >> "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Activity log saved: $LOG_FILE"
echo "Live monitoring completed."
    Django Live Build Activity Monitor =================================== Check interval: 15 seconds Maximum monitoring time: 60 minutes Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Log file: ./build_activity_log.txt = Getting initial build state... Initial last build: anihpj-django-build:12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012 Starting live monitoring... [15:50:00] Check #1 (Elapsed: 0s) ✓ No new builds detected Total new builds detected: 0 Next check in 15s... --- [15:50:15] Check #2 (Elapsed: 15s) ✓ No new builds detected Total new builds detected: 0 Next check in 15s... --- [15:50:30] Check #3 (Elapsed: 30s) 🔥 NEW BUILD ACTIVITY DETECTED! Analyzing new builds... New builds found: - Build #157 (feature/location-optimization) - IN_PROGRESS - Build #156 (feature/jobpost-search) - SUCCEEDED 📊 1 build(s) currently in progress Total new builds detected: 1 Next check in 15s... --- [15:50:45] Check #4 (Elapsed: 45s) ✓ No new builds detected Total new builds detected: 1 Next check in 15s... --- [15:51:00] Check #5 (Elapsed: 60s) 🔥 NEW BUILD ACTIVITY DETECTED! Analyzing new builds... New builds found: - Build #158 (main) - IN_PROGRESS - Build #157 (feature/location-optimization) - SUCCEEDED 📊 1 build(s) currently in progress Total new builds detected: 2 Next check in 15s... --- [15:51:15] Check #6 (Elapsed: 75s) ✓ No new builds detected Total new builds detected: 2 Next check in 15s... --- Monitoring Summary: ================== Total monitoring time: 1 minutes Total checks performed: 6 New builds detected: 2 Average check interval: 12 seconds Activity log saved: ./build_activity_log.txt Live monitoring completed. --- Sample Log Content --- Django Build Activity Log - Started at Wed Jan 15 15:50:00 UTC 2025 Project: anihpj jobpost application Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost = 2025-01-15 15:50:30 - NEW BUILD ACTIVITY - Build #157 (feature/location-optimization) - Status: IN_PROGRESS - Started: 2025-01-15T15:50:25Z - Build #156 (feature/jobpost-search) - Status: SUCCEEDED - Started: 2025-01-15T15:08:00Z Active builds: 1 2025-01-15 15:51:00 - NEW BUILD ACTIVITY - Build #158 (main) - Status: IN_PROGRESS - Started: 2025-01-15T15:50:55Z - Build #157 (feature/location-optimization) - Status: SUCCEEDED - Started: 2025-01-15T15:50:25Z Active builds: 1 Monitoring Summary: Ended at: Wed Jan 15 15:51:15 UTC 2025 Duration: 1 minutes Checks performed: 6 New builds detected: 2
    # Django Live Build Activity Monitor from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django live build activity monitoring session") print(f"Session duration: 1 minute 15 seconds") print(f"Total checks: 6") print(f"Check interval: 15 seconds") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Monitoring session data monitoring_session = { 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 15, 50, 0), 'end_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 15, 51, 15), 'total_duration': timedelta(minutes=1, seconds=15), 'checks_performed': 6, 'new_builds_detected': 2, 'detection_rate': '33.3%' # 2 out of 6 checks found new builds } print(f"\nMonitoring session summary:") for metric, value in monitoring_session.items(): print(f"• {metric}: {value}") # Build activity detected build_activity = { 'build_157': { 'branch': 'feature/location-optimization', 'detected_at': '15:50:30', 'initial_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'final_status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'duration': timedelta(minutes=30), 'models_tested': ['Location', 'JobPost'] }, 'build_158': { 'branch': 'main', 'detected_at': '15:51:00', 'initial_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'final_status': 'Unknown (monitoring ended)', 'estimated_duration': timedelta(minutes=7), 'models_tested': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'] } } print(f"\nBuild activity detected:") for build_id, details in build_activity.items(): print(f"\n{build_id.upper()}:") for key, value in details.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Check timeline check_timeline = [ {'time': '15:50:00', 'check': 1, 'result': 'no_activity', 'builds_detected': 0}, {'time': '15:50:15', 'check': 2, 'result': 'no_activity', 'builds_detected': 0}, {'time': '15:50:30', 'check': 3, 'result': 'activity_detected', 'builds_detected': 1}, {'time': '15:50:45', 'check': 4, 'result': 'no_activity', 'builds_detected': 0}, {'time': '15:51:00', 'check': 5, 'result': 'activity_detected', 'builds_detected': 1}, {'time': '15:51:15', 'check': 6, 'result': 'no_activity', 'builds_detected': 0} ] print(f"\nCheck timeline:") for check in check_timeline: status_icon = '🔥' if check['result'] == 'activity_detected' else '✓' print(f"{status_icon} {check['time']} (Check #{check['check']}): {check['result']} - {check['builds_detected']} builds") # Activity patterns activity_patterns = { 'peak_activity_window': '15:50:30 - 15:51:00', 'activity_frequency': '30 seconds apart', 'most_active_branch_type': 'feature branches', 'concurrent_builds': 'Yes (build 158 started while 157 was completing)', 'build_completion_rate': 'High (1 completed during monitoring)', 'development_velocity': 'Active (2 builds in 75 seconds)' } print(f"\nActivity patterns:") for pattern, observation in activity_patterns.items(): print(f"• {pattern}: {observation}") # Monitoring effectiveness monitoring_effectiveness = { 'detection_accuracy': '100%', 'false_positives': 0, 'missed_builds': 0, 'real_time_capability': 'Excellent (15s max delay)', 'resource_efficiency': 'Good (API rate limit safe)', 'log_completeness': 'Comprehensive' } print(f"\nMonitoring effectiveness:") for metric, score in monitoring_effectiveness.items(): print(f"• {metric}: {score}") # Development insights development_insights = [ 'Active development on Location model optimization', 'Regular main branch integration (build 158)', 'Parallel development workflows supported', 'Feature branches completing successfully', 'Consistent build naming patterns', 'All Django models being actively tested' ] print(f"\nDevelopment insights:") for i, insight in enumerate(development_insights, 1): print(f"{i}. {insight}") print(f"\nLive monitoring successful for anihpj Django jobpost application") print(f"Real-time build activity tracking operational") print(f"Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost models actively monitored")

    stop-build

    Attempts to stop a running build for your Django jobpost project. Use when you need to halt a problematic or long-running build involving Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild stop-build \
  --id {build-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --id (string, required): The ID of the build to stop
