    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • build: Information about the stopped build
      • id: Build identifier
      • buildStatus: Updated status (STOPPED)
      • endTime: Build end timestamp
    5 Detailed CodeBuild Examples
    Example 1: Stop a Problematic Django Build

    Stop a Django build that is experiencing issues or taking too long to complete for the anihpj jobpost application.

    #!/bin/bash
# Stop a problematic Django build
BUILD_ID="anihpj-django-build:problem-1111-2222-3333-444444444444"

echo "Stopping problematic Django build..."
echo "Build ID: $BUILD_ID"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "="

# Get build status before stopping
echo "Checking current build status..."
BUILD_STATUS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
    --ids "$BUILD_ID" \
    --query 'builds[0].{Status:buildStatus,Phase:currentPhase,StartTime:startTime,Project:projectName}' \
    --output json)

echo "Current build information:"
echo "$BUILD_STATUS" | jq '.'

# Check if build is actually running
CURRENT_STATUS=$(echo "$BUILD_STATUS" | jq -r '.Status')
if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "IN_PROGRESS" ]]; then
    echo "\nBuild is currently running. Proceeding with stop..."
    
    # Stop the build
    echo "Executing stop command..."
    STOP_RESULT=$(aws codebuild stop-build \
        --id "$BUILD_ID" \
        --output json)
    
    echo "Stop command executed successfully!"
    echo "\nStopped build details:"
    echo "$STOP_RESULT" | jq '.build | {ID: .id, Status: .buildStatus, EndTime: .endTime, Project: .projectName}'
    
    # Wait a moment and check final status
    echo "\nWaiting 10 seconds for status to update..."
    sleep 10
    
    FINAL_STATUS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids "$BUILD_ID" \
        --query 'builds[0].{Status:buildStatus,EndTime:endTime}' \
        --output json)
    
    echo "Final build status:"
    echo "$FINAL_STATUS" | jq '.'
    
    # Log the action
    echo "\n$(date): Build $BUILD_ID stopped due to issues" >> ./django_build_stops.log
    echo "Build stop completed for Django project."
else
    echo "\nBuild is not currently running (Status: $CURRENT_STATUS)."
    echo "Cannot stop a build that is not in progress."
    
    case $CURRENT_STATUS in
        "SUCCEEDED")
            echo "Build has already completed successfully."
            ;;
        "FAILED")
            echo "Build has already failed."
            ;;
        "STOPPED")
            echo "Build has already been stopped."
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Build is in an unexpected state: $CURRENT_STATUS"
            ;;
    esac
fi

echo "\nOperation completed."
    Stopping problematic Django build... Build ID: anihpj-django-build:problem-1111-2222-3333-444444444444 Project: anihpj jobpost application Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost = Checking current build status... Current build information: { "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "Phase": "BUILD", "StartTime": "2025-01-15T16:00:00Z", "Project": "anihpj-django-build" } Build is currently running. Proceeding with stop... Executing stop command... Stop command executed successfully! Stopped build details: { "ID": "anihpj-django-build:problem-1111-2222-3333-444444444444", "Status": "STOPPED", "EndTime": "2025-01-15T16:15:30Z", "Project": "anihpj-django-build" } Waiting 10 seconds for status to update... Final build status: { "Status": "STOPPED", "EndTime": "2025-01-15T16:15:30Z" } Operation completed.
    # Django Build Stop Operation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django build stop operation analysis") print(f"Build ID: anihpj-django-build:problem-1111-2222-3333-444444444444") print(f"Project: anihpj jobpost application") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Stop operation details stop_operation = { 'build_id': 'problem-1111-2222-3333-444444444444', 'initial_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'initial_phase': 'BUILD', 'start_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 16, 0, 0), 'stop_time': datetime(2025, 1, 15, 16, 15, 30), 'final_status': 'STOPPED', 'duration_before_stop': timedelta(minutes=15, seconds=30), 'reason': 'Problematic build taking too long' } print(f"\nStop operation details:") for key, value in stop_operation.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Phase analysis at time of stop phase_analysis = { 'stopped_during': 'BUILD phase', 'completed_phases': ['SUBMITTED', 'PROVISIONING', 'DOWNLOAD_SOURCE', 'INSTALL', 'PRE_BUILD'], 'incomplete_phases': ['BUILD', 'POST_BUILD', 'UPLOAD_ARTIFACTS', 'FINALIZING'], 'estimated_completion': '70% complete when stopped', 'time_saved': timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=30) # Estimated remaining time } print(f"\nPhase analysis:") for analysis, detail in phase_analysis.items(): print(f"• {analysis}: {detail}") # Impact on Django models model_impact = { 'Skills': {'status': 'tested', 'result': 'passed'}, 'User': {'status': 'tested', 'result': 'passed'}, 'Author': {'status': 'testing_interrupted', 'result': 'incomplete'}, 'Location': {'status': 'not_reached', 'result': 'not_tested'}, 'JobPost': {'status': 'not_reached', 'result': 'not_tested'} } print(f"\nModel testing impact:") for model, impact in model_impact.items(): print(f"• {model}: {impact['status']} - {impact['result']}") # Stop operation statistics stop_stats = { 'response_time': '< 1 second', 'status_change_delay': '10 seconds', 'data_preservation': 'All logs and artifacts preserved', 'cost_impact': 'Positive (stopped before completion)', 'resource_cleanup': 'Automatic', 'retry_recommended': True } print(f"\nStop operation statistics:") for stat, value in stop_stats.items(): print(f"• {stat}: {value}") # Recommendations recommendations = [ 'Review build logs to identify the issue causing delays', 'Check Author and Location model tests for potential infinite loops', 'Consider increasing build timeout if issue is environmental', 'Restart build with --source-version to retry from specific commit', 'Monitor Skills and User model tests (completed successfully)', 'Investigate Django test configuration for performance optimization' ] print(f"\nRecommendations:") for i, rec in enumerate(recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") print(f"\nBuild stop successful for anihpj Django jobpost application") print(f"Resources freed, ready for retry with fixes") print(f"Partial test results preserved for Skills and User models")
    Example 2: Stop Multiple Builds in Batch

    Stop multiple Django builds simultaneously to clean up resources or halt problematic builds for the anihpj jobpost application.

    #!/bin/bash
# Stop multiple Django builds in batch
BUILD_IDS=(
    "anihpj-django-build:batch1-1111-2222-3333-444444444444"
    "anihpj-django-build:batch2-2222-3333-4444-555555555555"
    "anihpj-django-build:batch3-3333-4444-5555-666666666666"
    "anihpj-django-build:batch4-4444-5555-6666-777777777777"
)

BATCH_STOP_LOG="./batch_stop_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

echo "Django Batch Build Stop Operation"
echo "=================================="
echo "Builds to stop: ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application"
echo "Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost"
echo "Log file: $BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "="

# Initialize log
echo "Django Batch Stop Log - $(date)" > "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Project: anihpj jobpost application" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Builds to stop: ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "=" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"

# Check status of all builds first
echo "Checking status of all builds before stopping..."
echo "BUILD_ID,CURRENT_STATUS,CURRENT_PHASE" > ./pre_stop_status.csv

for BUILD_ID in "${BUILD_IDS[@]}"; do
    echo "Checking: $BUILD_ID"
    
    BUILD_INFO=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids "$BUILD_ID" \
        --query 'builds[0].{Status:buildStatus,Phase:currentPhase}' \
        --output json 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ "$(echo "$BUILD_INFO" | jq -r '.Status')" != "null" ]; then
        STATUS=$(echo "$BUILD_INFO" | jq -r '.Status')
        PHASE=$(echo "$BUILD_INFO" | jq -r '.Phase // "N/A"')
        echo "  Status: $STATUS, Phase: $PHASE"
        echo "$BUILD_ID,$STATUS,$PHASE" >> ./pre_stop_status.csv
        
        # Log to batch file
        echo "$BUILD_ID - Pre-stop status: $STATUS ($PHASE)" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
    else
        echo "  Error: Could not retrieve status"
        echo "$BUILD_ID,ERROR,ERROR" >> ./pre_stop_status.csv
        echo "$BUILD_ID - Error retrieving status" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Starting batch stop operations..."
echo "\nBatch Stop Operations Started - $(date)" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"

# Stop builds in parallel
STOPPED_COUNT=0
SKIPPED_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0

for BUILD_ID in "${BUILD_IDS[@]}"; do
    echo "\nStopping build: $BUILD_ID"
    
    # Check if build is actually running
    CURRENT_STATUS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids "$BUILD_ID" \
        --query 'builds[0].buildStatus' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "IN_PROGRESS" ]]; then
        echo "  Build is running, stopping..."
        
        # Stop the build
        STOP_RESULT=$(aws codebuild stop-build --id "$BUILD_ID" --output json 2>/dev/null)
        
        if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            ((STOPPED_COUNT++))
            echo "  ✓ Successfully stopped"
            
            STOP_TIME=$(echo "$STOP_RESULT" | jq -r '.build.endTime')
            echo "$BUILD_ID - Successfully stopped at $STOP_TIME" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
        else
            ((ERROR_COUNT++))
            echo "  ✗ Failed to stop"
            echo "$BUILD_ID - Failed to stop (API error)" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
        fi
    else
        ((SKIPPED_COUNT++))
        echo "  Skipped (Status: $CURRENT_STATUS)"
        echo "$BUILD_ID - Skipped (Status: $CURRENT_STATUS)" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
    fi
    
    # Small delay to avoid rate limiting
    sleep 1
done

echo ""
echo "Batch Stop Summary:"
echo "=================="
echo "Total builds processed: ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}"
echo "Successfully stopped: $STOPPED_COUNT"
echo "Skipped (not running): $SKIPPED_COUNT"
echo "Errors encountered: $ERROR_COUNT"
echo "Success rate: $(echo "scale=1; $STOPPED_COUNT * 100 / ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}" | bc)%"

# Final status check
echo "\nFinal status verification..."
for BUILD_ID in "${BUILD_IDS[@]}"; do
    FINAL_STATUS=$(aws codebuild batch-get-builds \
        --ids "$BUILD_ID" \
        --query 'builds[0].buildStatus' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null)
    echo "$BUILD_ID: $FINAL_STATUS"
done

# Log summary
echo "\nBatch Stop Summary:" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Total processed: ${#BUILD_IDS[@]}" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Stopped: $STOPPED_COUNT" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Skipped: $SKIPPED_COUNT" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Errors: $ERROR_COUNT" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"
echo "Completed: $(date)" >> "$BATCH_STOP_LOG"

echo "\nBatch stop operation completed."
echo "Log saved: $BATCH_STOP_LOG"
    Django Batch Build Stop Operation ================================== Builds to stop: 4 Project: anihpj jobpost application Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost Log file: ./batch_stop_20250115_161500.log = Checking status of all builds before stopping... Checking: anihpj-django-build:batch1-1111-2222-3333-444444444444 Status: IN_PROGRESS, Phase: BUILD Checking: anihpj-django-build:batch2-2222-3333-4444-555555555555 Status: IN_PROGRESS, Phase: INSTALL Checking: anihpj-django-build:batch3-3333-4444-5555-666666666666 Status: SUCCEEDED, Phase: COMPLETED Checking: anihpj-django-build:batch4-4444-5555-6666-777777777777 Status: IN_PROGRESS, Phase: PRE_BUILD Starting batch stop operations... Stopping build: anihpj-django-build:batch1-1111-2222-3333-444444444444 Build is running, stopping... ✓ Successfully stopped Stopping build: anihpj-django-build:batch2-2222-3333-4444-555555555555 Build is running, stopping... ✓ Successfully stopped Stopping build: anihpj-django-build:batch3-3333-4444-5555-666666666666 Skipped (Status: SUCCEEDED) Stopping build: anihpj-django-build:batch4-4444-5555-6666-777777777777 Build is running, stopping... ✓ Successfully stopped Batch Stop Summary: ================== Total builds processed: 4 Successfully stopped: 3 Skipped (not running): 1 Errors encountered: 0 Success rate: 75.0% Final status verification... anihpj-django-build:batch1-1111-2222-3333-444444444444: STOPPED anihpj-django-build:batch2-2222-3333-4444-555555555555: STOPPED anihpj-django-build:batch3-3333-4444-5555-666666666666: SUCCEEDED anihpj-django-build:batch4-4444-5555-6666-777777777777: STOPPED Batch stop operation completed. Log saved: ./batch_stop_20250115_161500.log
    # Django Batch Build Stop Analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta print(f"Django batch build stop analysis") print(f"Total builds processed: 4") print(f"Operation timestamp: 2025-01-15 16:15:00") print(f"Project: anihpj jobpost application") print(f"Models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost") # Batch operation results batch_results = { 'batch1': { 'id': 'batch1-1111-2222-3333-444444444444', 'initial_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'initial_phase': 'BUILD', 'operation': 'STOPPED', 'result': 'SUCCESS', 'models_affected': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author'] }, 'batch2': { 'id': 'batch2-2222-3333-4444-555555555555', 'initial_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'initial_phase': 'INSTALL', 'operation': 'STOPPED', 'result': 'SUCCESS', 'models_affected': ['Skills'] }, 'batch3': { 'id': 'batch3-3333-4444-5555-666666666666', 'initial_status': 'SUCCEEDED', 'initial_phase': 'COMPLETED', 'operation': 'SKIPPED', 'result': 'NO_ACTION', 'models_affected': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'] }, 'batch4': { 'id': 'batch4-4444-5555-6666-777777777777', 'initial_status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'initial_phase': 'PRE_BUILD', 'operation': 'STOPPED', 'result': 'SUCCESS', 'models_affected': ['Skills'] } } print(f"\nBatch operation results:") for batch_id, details in batch_results.items(): print(f"\n{batch_id.upper()}:") for key, value in details.items(): print(f"• {key}: {value}") # Operation statistics operation_stats = { 'total_processed': 4, 'successfully_stopped': 3, 'skipped_builds': 1, 'error_count': 0, 'success_rate': 75.0, 'operation_duration': timedelta(seconds=15), 'average_stop_time': timedelta(seconds=1) } print(f"\nOperation statistics:") for stat, value in operation_stats.items(): print(f"• {stat}: {value}") # Phase impact analysis phase_impact = { 'BUILD': {'builds_stopped': 1, 'impact': 'High - testing interrupted'}, 'INSTALL': {'builds_stopped': 1, 'impact': 'Medium - dependency installation halted'}, 'PRE_BUILD': {'builds_stopped': 1, 'impact': 'Low - minimal work lost'}, 'COMPLETED': {'builds_stopped': 0, 'impact': 'None - already finished'} } print(f"\nPhase impact analysis:") for phase, impact in phase_impact.items(): print(f"• {phase}: {impact['builds_stopped']} builds stopped - {impact['impact']}") # Resource savings calculation resource_savings = { 'compute_minutes_saved': 25, # Estimated based on remaining time 'cost_savings': '$2.50', # Approximate based on instance type 'builds_freed_for_retry': 3, 'queue_capacity_freed': 'Yes', 'concurrent_build_slots': 3 } print(f"\nResource savings:") for resource, savings in resource_savings.items(): print(f"• {resource}: {savings}") # Model testing impact model_testing_impact = { 'Skills': {'tests_interrupted': 3, 'tests_completed': 1, 'impact': 'Medium'}, 'User': {'tests_interrupted': 2, 'tests_completed': 1, 'impact': 'Medium'}, 'Author': {'tests_interrupted': 2, 'tests_completed': 1, 'impact': 'Medium'}, 'Location': {'tests_interrupted': 0, 'tests_completed': 1, 'impact': 'None'}, 'JobPost': {'tests_interrupted': 0, 'tests_completed': 1, 'impact': 'None'} } print(f"\nModel testing impact:") for model, impact in model_testing_impact.items(): print(f"• {model}: {impact['tests_interrupted']} interrupted, {impact['tests_completed']} completed ({impact['impact']} impact)") # Recommendations recommendations = [ 'Review interrupted builds for common issues before retry', 'Consider staggered restart to avoid resource contention', 'Monitor Skills model tests (most frequently interrupted)', 'Preserve batch3 success for reference testing', 'Implement build queue management for better resource allocation', 'Set up automated alerts for problematic build patterns' ] print(f"\nRecommendations:") for i, rec in enumerate(recommendations, 1): print(f"{i}. {rec}") print(f"\nBatch stop operation successful for anihpj Django jobpost application") print(f"3/4 builds successfully stopped, resources freed for optimization")
    Return Value
    • build: Information about the build that was stopped

    update-project

    Updates the configuration of an existing build project. Modify Django jobpost build settings, environment variables, or buildspec configuration for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild update-project \
  --name {project-name} \
  [--description {description}] \
  [--source {source-json}] \
  [--secondary-sources {secondary-sources-json}] \
  [--source-version {source-version}] \
  [--secondary-source-versions {secondary-versions-json}] \
  [--artifacts {artifacts-json}] \
  [--secondary-artifacts {secondary-artifacts-json}] \
  [--cache {cache-json}] \
  [--environment {environment-json}] \
  [--service-role {service-role-arn}] \
  [--timeout-in-minutes {timeout}] \
  [--queued-timeout-in-minutes {queued-timeout}] \
  [--encryption-key {kms-key-arn}] \
  [--tags {tags-json}] \
  [--vpc-config {vpc-config-json}] \
  [--badge-enabled] \
  [--logs-config {logs-config-json}] \
  [--file-system-locations {file-system-json}] \
  [--build-batch-config {batch-config-json}] \
  [--concurrent-build-limit {limit}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --name (string, required): The name of the build project to update
    • --description (string, optional): New description for the project
    • --source (structure, optional): Updated build input source configuration
    • --artifacts (structure, optional): Updated build output artifacts configuration
    • --environment (structure, optional): Updated build environment configuration
    • --service-role (string, optional): Updated IAM service role ARN
    • --timeout-in-minutes (integer, optional): Updated build timeout (5-480 minutes)
    • --cache (structure, optional): Updated build caching configuration
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • project: Information about the updated build project
      • name: Project name
      • arn: Project ARN
      • lastModified: Last modification timestamp

    list-builds-for-project

    Gets a list of build IDs for the specified build project. Monitor builds for your Django jobpost application development, tracking CI/CD runs that compile and test Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-builds-for-project \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): The name of the CodeBuild project to list builds for
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order for results (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • ids: Array of build IDs for the specified project
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    retry-build

    Restarts a previously failed build. Useful for retrying Django jobpost application builds after transient failures or after fixing issues in Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild retry-build \
  --id {build-id} \
  [--idempotency-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --id (string, required): The ID of the build to retry
    • --idempotency-token (string, optional): Unique token to prevent duplicate retry requests
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • build: Information about the retried build
      • id: Unique build identifier
      • buildStatus: Current status of the retried build
      • startTime: Timestamp when retry started

    delete-project

    Deletes a build project and all associated resources. Remove obsolete Django jobpost build projects when they are no longer needed for building Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code. This action cannot be undone.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-project \
  --name {project-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --name (string, required): The name of the build project to delete
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value: Operation returns empty response on successful deletion

    list-projects

    Gets a list of build project names in your AWS account. Discover all CodeBuild projects including Django jobpost application builds for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model development.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-projects \
  [--sort-by {sort-criteria}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-by (string, optional): Sort criteria (NAME, CREATED_TIME, LAST_MODIFIED_TIME)
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • projects: Array of project names in your account
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    batch-get-projects

    Gets information about multiple build projects in a single request. Retrieve detailed configuration for Django jobpost build projects handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model compilation and testing.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-projects \
  --names {project-name-1} {project-name-2} ... \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --names (list, required): List of project names to retrieve information for
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • projects: Array of project information objects
      • name: Project name
      • arn: Project ARN
      • source: Source configuration
      • environment: Build environment settings
    • projectsNotFound: Array of project names that were not found

    batch-delete-builds

    Deletes multiple builds in a single request. Efficiently clean up old or failed Django jobpost builds to manage storage costs and remove obsolete build artifacts for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model compilations.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-delete-builds \
  --ids {build-id-1} {build-id-2} ... \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --ids (list, required): List of build IDs to delete (maximum 100)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • buildsDeleted: Array of successfully deleted build IDs
    • buildsNotDeleted: Array of builds that could not be deleted
      • id: Build ID that failed to delete
      • statusCode: Error status code

    invalidate-project-cache

    Resets the build cache for a project. Use when Django jobpost dependencies change or cache corruption is suspected during Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model builds.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild invalidate-project-cache \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): The name of the project to invalidate cache for
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value: Operation returns empty response on successful cache invalidation

    start-build-batch

    Starts multiple builds as a batch operation. Efficient for building multiple Django jobpost components or environments simultaneously, running parallel builds for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model testing.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild start-build-batch \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--source-version {source-version}] \
  [--artifacts-override {artifacts-json}] \
  [--build-batch-config-override {config-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): The name of the build project
    • --source-version (string, optional): Source version (commit ID, branch, or tag) to build
    • --artifacts-override (structure, optional): Override artifact configuration
    • --build-batch-config-override (structure, optional): Override batch configuration
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • buildBatch: Information about the started build batch
      • id: Unique batch identifier
      • arn: Batch ARN
      • buildBatchStatus: Current status of the batch
      • buildGroups: Array of build groups in the batch

    stop-build-batch

    Stops a build batch that is currently in progress. Use to cancel long-running Django jobpost batch operations when building Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models no longer needed.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild stop-build-batch \
  --id {batch-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --id (string, required): The ID of the build batch to stop
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • buildBatch: Information about the stopped build batch
      • id: Batch identifier
      • buildBatchStatus: Updated status (STOPPED)
      • endTime: Timestamp when batch was stopped

    retry-build-batch

    Retries a failed build batch operation. Restart Django jobpost batch builds after fixing issues in configuration or source code for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model testing.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild retry-build-batch \
  --id {batch-id} \
  [--idempotency-token {token}] \
  [--retry-type {retry-type}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --id (string, required): The ID of the build batch to retry
    • --idempotency-token (string, optional): Unique token to prevent duplicate retry requests
    • --retry-type (string, optional): Type of retry (RETRY_ALL_BUILDS or RETRY_FAILED_BUILDS)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • buildBatch: Information about the retried build batch
      • id: Batch identifier
      • buildBatchStatus: Updated status
      • buildGroups: Array of build groups being retried

    batch-get-build-batches

    Gets information about multiple build batches in a single request. Monitor status of several Django jobpost batch operations simultaneously for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model builds.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-build-batches \
  --ids {batch-id-1} {batch-id-2} ... \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --ids (list, required): List of build batch IDs to retrieve (maximum 100)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • buildBatches: Array of build batch information objects
      • id: Batch identifier
      • arn: Batch ARN
      • buildBatchStatus: Current status
      • buildGroups: Array of build groups
    • buildBatchesNotFound: Array of batch IDs that were not found

    list-build-batches

    Gets a list of build batch IDs in your AWS account. View all batch operations across Django jobpost projects including Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model builds.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-build-batches \
  [--filter {filter-json}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --filter (structure, optional): Filter criteria with status field (FAILED, IN_PROGRESS, STOPPED, SUCCEEDED)
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • ids: Array of build batch IDs
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    list-build-batches-for-project

    Gets build batch IDs for a specific project. Monitor batch operations for a particular Django jobpost application building Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-build-batches-for-project \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--filter {filter-json}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): The name of the project to list batches for
    • --filter (structure, optional): Filter criteria with status field
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • ids: Array of build batch IDs for the specified project
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    create-fleet

    Creates a dedicated compute fleet for builds. Improve Django jobpost build performance with reserved capacity for compiling and testing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild create-fleet \
  --name {fleet-name} \
  --base-capacity {capacity} \
  --environment-type {environment-type} \
  --compute-type {compute-type} \
  [--scaling-configuration {scaling-json}] \
  [--overflow-behavior {behavior}] \
  [--tags {tags-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --name (string, required): Name of the compute fleet
    • --base-capacity (integer, required): Base capacity for the fleet (minimum instances)
    • --environment-type (string, required): Environment type (LINUX_CONTAINER, LINUX_GPU_CONTAINER, ARM_CONTAINER, WINDOWS_SERVER_2019_CONTAINER)
    • --compute-type (string, required): Compute type (BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL, BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM, BUILD_GENERAL1_LARGE, BUILD_GENERAL1_2XLARGE)
    • --scaling-configuration (structure, optional): Auto-scaling configuration
    • --overflow-behavior (string, optional): Behavior when capacity exceeded (QUEUE or ON_DEMAND)
    • --tags (list, optional): Tags to apply to the fleet
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • fleet: Information about the created fleet
      • arn: Fleet ARN
      • name: Fleet name
      • id: Fleet identifier
      • status: Fleet status object with statusCode
      • baseCapacity: Base capacity configured

    delete-fleet

    Deletes a dedicated compute fleet. Remove unused fleets to reduce costs for Django jobpost build infrastructure used for compiling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-fleet \
  --arn {fleet-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --arn (string, required): ARN of the fleet to delete
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value: Operation returns empty response on successful deletion

    batch-get-fleets

    Gets information about multiple compute fleets in a single request. Monitor capacity and status of Django jobpost build fleets for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model compilation.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-fleets \
  --names {fleet-name-1} {fleet-name-2} ... \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --names (list, required): List of fleet names or ARNs to retrieve
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • fleets: Array of fleet information objects
      • arn: Fleet ARN
      • name: Fleet name
      • status: Fleet status with statusCode
      • baseCapacity: Base capacity
    • fleetsNotFound: Array of fleet names that were not found

    list-fleets

    Lists all compute fleets in your AWS account. View available dedicated capacity for Django jobpost builds handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-fleets \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--sort-by {sort-by}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --sort-by (string, optional): Sort criteria (NAME, CREATED_TIME, LAST_MODIFIED_TIME)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • fleets: Array of fleet names in your account
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    update-fleet

    Updates configuration of a compute fleet. Modify capacity or settings for Django jobpost build infrastructure used for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model compilation.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild update-fleet \
  --arn {fleet-arn} \
  [--base-capacity {capacity}] \
  [--environment-type {environment-type}] \
  [--compute-type {compute-type}] \
  [--scaling-configuration {scaling-json}] \
  [--overflow-behavior {behavior}] \
  [--tags {tags-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --arn (string, required): ARN of the fleet to update
    • --base-capacity (integer, optional): New base capacity for the fleet
    • --environment-type (string, optional): New environment type
    • --compute-type (string, optional): New compute type
    • --scaling-configuration (structure, optional): New auto-scaling configuration
    • --overflow-behavior (string, optional): New overflow behavior
    • --tags (list, optional): Updated tags for the fleet
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • fleet: Updated fleet information
      • arn: Fleet ARN
      • name: Fleet name
      • status: Updated status
      • baseCapacity: Updated base capacity

    create-report-group

    Creates a report group for organizing test results. Track Django jobpost testing metrics across builds for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model unit tests and integration tests.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild create-report-group \
  --name {report-group-name} \
  --type {report-type} \
  --export-config {export-config-json} \
  [--tags {tags-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --name (string, required): Name of the report group
    • --type (string, required): Type of report (TEST or CODE_COVERAGE)
    • --export-config (structure, required): Export configuration for reports
      • exportConfigType: Type of export (S3 or NO_EXPORT)
      • s3Destination: S3 bucket configuration if using S3 export
    • --tags (list, optional): Tags to apply to the report group
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reportGroup: Information about the created report group
      • arn: Report group ARN
      • name: Report group name
      • type: Report type
      • exportConfig: Export configuration

    delete-report

    Deletes a specific test report. Clean up old Django jobpost test reports to manage storage costs after reviewing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model test results.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-report \
  --arn {report-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --arn (string, required): ARN of the report to delete
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value: Operation returns empty response on successful deletion

    delete-report-group

    Deletes a test report group and optionally all associated reports. Remove unused Django jobpost report groups to clean up resources after Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model testing is complete.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-report-group \
  --arn {report-group-arn} \
  [--delete-reports | --no-delete-reports] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --arn (string, required): ARN of the report group to delete
    • --delete-reports (boolean, optional): Also delete all reports in the group
    • --no-delete-reports (boolean, optional): Keep reports when deleting group
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value: Operation returns empty response on successful deletion

    batch-get-reports

    Gets information about multiple test reports in a single request. Retrieve detailed Django jobpost testing metrics for analysis of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model test results.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-reports \
  --report-arns {report-arn-1} {report-arn-2} ... \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --report-arns (list, required): List of report ARNs to retrieve (maximum 100)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reports: Array of report information objects
      • arn: Report ARN
      • type: Report type (TEST or CODE_COVERAGE)
      • status: Report status
      • testSummary: Test summary with pass/fail counts
    • reportsNotFound: Array of report ARNs that were not found

    batch-get-report-groups

    Gets information about multiple report groups in a single request. Monitor Django jobpost test reporting configuration across projects for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model testing.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-report-groups \
  --report-group-arns {group-arn-1} {group-arn-2} ... \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --report-group-arns (list, required): List of report group ARNs to retrieve (maximum 100)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reportGroups: Array of report group information objects
      • arn: Report group ARN
      • name: Report group name
      • type: Report type
      • exportConfig: Export configuration
    • reportGroupsNotFound: Array of report group ARNs that were not found

    describe-test-cases

    Gets detailed information about test cases from a build report. Analyze individual Django jobpost test results and failures for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model unit tests.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild describe-test-cases \
  --report-arn {report-arn} \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--filter {filter-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --report-arn (string, required): ARN of the test report to describe
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --filter (structure, optional): Filter criteria with status field (SUCCEEDED, FAILED, ERROR, SKIPPED, UNKNOWN)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • testCases: Array of test case results
      • reportArn: Report ARN
      • testRawDataPath: Path to raw test data
      • prefix: Test case prefix
      • name: Test case name
      • status: Test status
      • durationInNanoSeconds: Test duration
      • message: Status message
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    describe-code-coverages

    Gets code coverage data for application builds. Monitor Django jobpost test coverage metrics and identify untested code in Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild describe-code-coverages \
  --report-arn {report-arn} \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--sort-by {sort-by}] \
  [--min-line-coverage-percentage {min-percentage}] \
  [--max-line-coverage-percentage {max-percentage}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --report-arn (string, required): ARN of the code coverage report
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --sort-by (string, optional): Sort criteria (LINE_COVERAGE_PERCENTAGE or FILE_PATH)
    • --min-line-coverage-percentage (double, optional): Filter for minimum coverage percentage
    • --max-line-coverage-percentage (double, optional): Filter for maximum coverage percentage
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • codeCoverages: Array of code coverage data
      • id: Coverage identifier
      • reportARN: Report ARN
      • filePath: Path to the source file
      • lineCoveragePercentage: Line coverage percentage
      • linesCovered: Number of lines covered
      • linesMissed: Number of lines not covered
      • branchCoveragePercentage: Branch coverage percentage
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    list-reports

    Lists all test reports in your AWS account. Browse available Django jobpost testing metrics and results for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model builds.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-reports \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--filter {filter-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --filter (structure, optional): Filter criteria with status field (GENERATING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, INCOMPLETE, DELETING)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reports: Array of report ARNs
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    list-reports-for-report-group

    Lists test reports for a specific report group. View Django jobpost testing metrics organized by project or category for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-reports-for-report-group \
  --report-group-arn {report-group-arn} \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--filter {filter-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --report-group-arn (string, required): ARN of the report group to list reports for
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --filter (structure, optional): Filter criteria with status field
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reports: Array of report ARNs for the specified group
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    list-report-groups

    Lists all report groups in your AWS account. Browse available Django jobpost test reporting configurations for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model testing.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-report-groups \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--sort-by {sort-by}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --sort-by (string, optional): Sort criteria (NAME, CREATED_TIME, LAST_MODIFIED_TIME)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reportGroups: Array of report group ARNs
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    update-report-group

    Updates configuration of a report group. Modify export settings or tags for Django jobpost test reporting of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model test results.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild update-report-group \
  --arn {report-group-arn} \
  [--export-config {export-config-json}] \
  [--tags {tags-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --arn (string, required): ARN of the report group to update
    • --export-config (structure, optional): New export configuration for reports
    • --tags (list, optional): Updated tags for the report group
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reportGroup: Updated report group information
      • arn: Report group ARN
      • name: Report group name
      • type: Report type
      • exportConfig: Updated export configuration

    get-report-group-trend

    Gets trend data for test metrics over time. Analyze Django jobpost testing performance and quality trends for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model test suites.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild get-report-group-trend \
  --report-group-arn {report-group-arn} \
  --num-of-reports {count} \
  --trend-field {trend-field} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --report-group-arn (string, required): ARN of the report group to analyze
    • --num-of-reports (integer, required): Number of reports to include in trend analysis (1-100)
    • --trend-field (string, required): Trend field to analyze (PASS_RATE, DURATION, TOTAL, LINE_COVERAGE, LINES_COVERED, LINES_MISSED, BRANCH_COVERAGE, BRANCHES_COVERED, BRANCHES_MISSED)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • stats: Trend statistics for the specified field
      • average: Average value
      • max: Maximum value
      • min: Minimum value
    • rawData: Array of raw data points for the trend with reportArn and data values

    create-webhook

    Creates a webhook for a build project to trigger builds automatically. Enable CI/CD workflows for Django jobpost application from Git repositories for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild create-webhook \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--branch-filter {branch-filter}] \
  [--filter-groups {filter-groups-json}] \
  [--build-type {build-type}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): Name of the CodeBuild project to create webhook for
    • --branch-filter (string, optional): Regex filter for branches (deprecated, use filter-groups)
    • --filter-groups (list, optional): Array of webhook filter groups for triggering builds
    • --build-type (string, optional): Build type to trigger (BUILD, BUILD_BATCH)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • webhook: Created webhook information
      • url: Webhook URL to configure in source repository
      • payloadUrl: URL for webhook payload delivery
      • secret: Secret token for webhook authentication
      • branchFilter: Branch filter pattern
      • filterGroups: Configured filter groups
      • buildType: Build type
      • lastModifiedSecret: Last secret modification timestamp

    delete-webhook

    Deletes a webhook for a build project. Remove automatic build triggers for Django jobpost application when CI/CD integration is no longer needed for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-webhook \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): Name of the CodeBuild project to delete webhook for
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value on successful deletion

    update-webhook

    Updates webhook configuration for a build project. Modify build triggers and filtering rules for Django jobpost application's CI/CD pipeline handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild update-webhook \
  --project-name {project-name} \
  [--branch-filter {branch-filter}] \
  [--rotate-secret] \
  [--no-rotate-secret] \
  [--filter-groups {filter-groups-json}] \
  [--build-type {build-type}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --project-name (string, required): Name of the CodeBuild project to update webhook for
    • --branch-filter (string, optional): Updated branch filter pattern (deprecated, use filter-groups)
    • --rotate-secret | --no-rotate-secret (boolean, optional): Rotate webhook secret for enhanced security
    • --filter-groups (list, optional): Updated webhook filter groups configuration
    • --build-type (string, optional): Build type (BUILD, BUILD_BATCH)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • webhook: Updated webhook information
      • url: Webhook URL
      • payloadUrl: URL for webhook payload delivery
      • secret: Secret token (if rotated)
      • filterGroups: Updated filter groups
      • buildType: Build type

    import-source-credentials

    Imports source control credentials for accessing private repositories. Authenticate with GitHub, Bitbucket, or GitHub Enterprise for Django jobpost application source code containing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild import-source-credentials \
  --username {username} \
  --token {access-token} \
  --server-type {server-type} \
  --auth-type {auth-type} \
  [--should-overwrite] \
  [--no-should-overwrite] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --username (string, required): Username for source control provider (not used for OAUTH)
    • --token (string, required): Access token, app password, or personal access token
    • --server-type (string, required): Source control provider (GITHUB, BITBUCKET, GITHUB_ENTERPRISE)
    • --auth-type (string, required): Authentication type (OAUTH, BASIC_AUTH, PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN)
    • --should-overwrite | --no-should-overwrite (boolean, optional): Overwrite existing credentials for this server type
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • arn: ARN of the imported source credentials resource

    delete-source-credentials

    Deletes source control credentials. Remove authentication for repositories no longer in use for Django jobpost application builds involving Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-source-credentials \
  --arn {source-credentials-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --arn (string, required): ARN of the source credentials resource to delete
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • arn: ARN of the deleted source credentials resource

    list-source-credentials

    Lists all source control credentials in your account. View available repository authentication configurations for Django jobpost application builds handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-source-credentials \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • sourceCredentialsInfos: Array of source credentials information
      • arn: Source credentials ARN
      • serverType: Source provider (GITHUB, BITBUCKET, GITHUB_ENTERPRISE)
      • authType: Authentication type

    put-resource-policy

    Creates or updates a resource policy for sharing build projects or report groups. Control cross-account access to Django jobpost application build resources for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild put-resource-policy \
  --policy {policy-document} \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --policy (string, required): Resource policy document in JSON format defining permissions
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the project or report group to attach policy to
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • resourceArn: ARN of the resource the policy was applied to

    get-resource-policy

    Gets the resource policy for a build project or report group. View current cross-account sharing permissions for Django jobpost application resources handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild get-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the project or report group to get policy for
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • policy: Resource policy document in JSON format defining permissions

    delete-resource-policy

    Deletes the resource policy for a project or report group. Remove cross-account sharing permissions for Django jobpost application build resources handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild delete-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the project or report group to delete policy from
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • No return value on successful deletion

    list-curated-environment-images

    Lists AWS-curated Docker images available for CodeBuild. Browse pre-configured build environments for Django jobpost application development with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-curated-environment-images \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • platforms: Array of available platforms with curated images
      • platform: Platform type (AMAZON_LINUX, UBUNTU, WINDOWS_SERVER, etc.)
      • languages: Array of available languages with images
        • language: Programming language (PYTHON, RUBY, JAVA, etc.)
        • images: Array of Docker images for that language/platform

    list-shared-projects

    Lists build projects shared from other AWS accounts. View cross-account accessible build projects for Django jobpost application development with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-shared-projects \
  [--sort-by {sort-by}] \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-by (string, optional): Sort criteria (ARN, MODIFIED_TIME)
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • projects: Array of shared project ARNs accessible to your account
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    list-shared-report-groups

    Lists report groups shared from other AWS accounts. Access cross-account test reporting data for Django jobpost application testing of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild list-shared-report-groups \
  [--sort-order {sort-order}] \
  [--sort-by {sort-by}] \
  [--next-token {next-token}] \
  [--max-results {max-results}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-order (string, optional): Sort order (ASCENDING or DESCENDING)
    • --sort-by (string, optional): Sort criteria (ARN, MODIFIED_TIME)
    • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination when results exceed maximum
    • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • reportGroups: Array of shared report group ARNs accessible to your account
    • nextToken: Token for retrieving additional results if available

    batch-get-build-batches

    Gets information about multiple build batches. Monitor batch build operations for Django jobpost application complex build pipelines involving Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild batch-get-build-batches \
  --ids {build-batch-id} [{build-batch-id}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --ids (list, required): Array of build batch IDs to retrieve information for
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • buildBatches: Array of build batch information
      • id: Build batch identifier
      • arn: Build batch ARN
      • buildBatchStatus: Current status of the build batch
      • buildGroups: Build groups in the batch
    • buildBatchesNotFound: Array of build batch IDs that were not found

    get-current-user-permission

    Gets current user permissions for CodeBuild operations on a resource. Verify access rights for Django jobpost application build management involving Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws codebuild get-current-user-permission \
  [--resource-arn {resource-arn}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {output-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, optional): ARN of the specific resource to check permissions for
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Return Value
    • resourceArn: ARN of the resource permissions were checked for
    • policies: Array of applicable permission policies

    AWS CodeDeploy

    Automated application deployment service for consistent Django deployments

    add-tags-to-on-premises-instances

    Adds tags to one or more on-premises instances running your Django jobpost application. This command enables you to organize and manage on-premises servers hosting Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models by applying metadata tags for categorization, environment identification, cost allocation, and deployment tracking across your hybrid cloud infrastructure.

    Syntax
    aws deploy add-tags-to-on-premises-instances \
  --instance-names {instance-name} [{instance-name}...] \
  --tags Key={key},Value={value} [{Key={key},Value={value}}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --instance-names (list, required): Names of on-premises instances to tag. Use descriptive names like "django-prod-web-01" or "jobpost-api-server"
    • --tags (list, required): Key-value pairs to apply as tags. Examples: Key=Environment,Value=production Key=Application,Value=django-jobpost Key=Role,Value=web-server
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful execution.

    batch-get-application-revisions

    Retrieves information about multiple application revisions for your Django jobpost application. This command provides detailed revision metadata including commit information, deployment bundle details, and version history for tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments across different application versions and environments.

    Syntax
    aws deploy batch-get-application-revisions \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  --revisions revisionType={revision-type},s3Location={bucket-info} [{revision-info}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --application-name (string, required): Name of the CodeDeploy application containing the revisions to retrieve
    • --revisions (list, required): List of revision specifications to retrieve information about
    Return Value
    • applicationName: Name of the Django application
    • revisions: Array of revision details with metadata
    • errorMessage: Error details if retrieval fails

    batch-get-applications

    Retrieves detailed information about multiple CodeDeploy applications simultaneously. This command efficiently gathers comprehensive application metadata for Django jobpost applications, including configuration details, deployment history, and settings for applications managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments across your infrastructure.

    Syntax
    aws deploy batch-get-applications \
  --application-names {application-name} [{application-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --application-names (list, required): Names of CodeDeploy applications to retrieve. Use application names like "django-jobpost-prod" or "skills-management-app"
    Return Value
    • applicationsInfo: Array of application details including creation time, revision locations
    • applicationName: Name of each Django application
    • createTime: Application creation timestamp
    • linkedToGitHub: GitHub integration status

    batch-get-deployment-targets

    Retrieves detailed information about multiple deployment targets in a CodeDeploy deployment. This command provides comprehensive target metadata including instance details, deployment status, and lifecycle event information for monitoring Django jobpost application deployments across Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services on various compute platforms.

    Syntax
    aws deploy batch-get-deployment-targets \
  --deployment-id {deployment-id} \
  --target-ids {target-id} [{target-id}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --deployment-id (string, required): Unique identifier of the deployment containing the targets
    • --target-ids (list, required): List of target identifiers to retrieve information about
    Return Value
    • deploymentTargets: Array of deployment target details
    • targetId: Unique identifier of each deployment target
    • targetType: Type of deployment target (EC2, Lambda, ECS)
    • deploymentStatus: Current deployment status
    • lifecycleEvents: Array of lifecycle event information

    continue-deployment

    Resumes a paused deployment for your Django jobpost application. This command continues a deployment that was stopped due to manual approval requirements or deployment triggers, allowing you to proceed with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model updates after verification or approval processes are complete.

    Syntax
    aws deploy continue-deployment \
  --deployment-id {deployment-id} \
  --deployment-wait-type {wait-type} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --deployment-id (string, required): Unique identifier of the paused deployment to continue
    • --deployment-wait-type (string, required): Type of wait condition to override. Values: READY_WAIT, TERMINATION_WAIT
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful execution. The deployment resumes normal processing.

    📋 CodeDeploy Overview

    AWS CodeDeploy automates application deployments to EC2 instances, on-premises servers, serverless Lambda functions, and Amazon ECS services. It helps you rapidly release new features while avoiding downtime during deployment. CodeDeploy coordinates deployments of your Django jobpost application across multiple environments with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Core CodeDeploy Operations for Django Deployment

    create-application - Create deployment application
    create-deployment - Start new deployment
    create-deployment-group - Create target group
    create-deployment-config - Define deployment strategy
    get-deployment - Check deployment status
    stop-deployment - Halt running deployment
    list-deployments - List all deployments
    register-application-revision - Register app version
    5 Detailed CodeDeploy create-application Examples
    Example 1: Create Basic Django Application for Development

    Create a simple CodeDeploy application for deploying the Django jobpost application to EC2 instances in development environment.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create development CodeDeploy application
aws deploy create-application \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-dev" \
  --compute-platform Server \
  --tags Key=Environment,Value=development Key=Project,Value=jobpost Key=Models,Value="Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost" \
  --region us-east-1
    { "applicationId": "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012" }
    # Django Models that will be deployed via this application from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Application configuration for deployment deployment_app = { 'application_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-dev', 'application_id': '12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012', 'compute_platform': 'Server', 'target_environment': 'development', 'models_included': [ 'Skills', # Skill categories and levels 'User', # Django authentication users 'Author', # Job post authors/recruiters 'Location', # Geographic locations for jobs 'JobPost' # Main job posting model ] } print(f"Created CodeDeploy application: {deployment_app['application_name']}") print(f"Ready to deploy Django models: {', '.join(deployment_app['models_included'])}")
    Example 2: Create Production Application with Advanced Tagging

    Create a production CodeDeploy application with comprehensive tagging for the Django jobpost application deployment across multiple environments.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create production CodeDeploy application with detailed tags
aws deploy create-application \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-production" \
  --compute-platform Server \
  --tags Key=Environment,Value=production \
         Key=Project,Value=jobpost \
         Key=Team,Value=django-dev \
         Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering \
         Key=Backup,Value=required \
         Key=Models,Value="Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost" \
         Key=Framework,Value=Django \
         Key=Version,Value=5.2.5 \
  --region us-east-1
    { "applicationId": "87654321-4321-4321-4321-210987654321" }
    # Production deployment configuration production_config = { 'application_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-production', 'application_id': '87654321-4321-4321-4321-210987654321', 'environment': 'production', 'django_version': '5.2.5', 'high_availability': True, 'backup_required': True, 'models_schema': { 'Skills': 'Manages job skill requirements and levels', 'User': 'Django user authentication and profiles', 'Author': 'Job post creators and company recruiters', 'Location': 'Geographic data for job locations', 'JobPost': 'Main job posting with foreign keys to other models' } } # Verify production readiness print(f"Production app created: {production_config['application_name']}") print(f"Models ready for HA deployment: {list(production_config['models_schema'].keys())}")
    Example 3: Create Lambda Application for Serverless Django Functions

    Create a CodeDeploy application targeting Lambda compute platform for serverless Django functions handling jobpost API operations.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create Lambda-based CodeDeploy application for Django functions
aws deploy create-application \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-lambda" \
  --compute-platform Lambda \
  --tags Key=Environment,Value=serverless \
         Key=Project,Value=jobpost-api \
         Key=Functions,Value="skills-api,user-auth,job-search" \
         Key=Runtime,Value=python3.11 \
  --region us-east-1
    { "applicationId": "abcd1234-5678-9012-3456-789012345678" }
    # Serverless Django functions deployment serverless_config = { 'application_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-lambda', 'application_id': 'abcd1234-5678-9012-3456-789012345678', 'compute_platform': 'Lambda', 'runtime': 'python3.11', 'django_functions': { 'skills-api': { 'model': 'Skills', 'endpoints': ['GET /skills', 'POST /skills', 'PUT /skills/{id}'] }, 'user-auth': { 'model': 'User', 'endpoints': ['POST /auth/login', 'POST /auth/register', 'GET /auth/profile'] }, 'job-search': { 'models': ['JobPost', 'Location', 'Skills'], 'endpoints': ['GET /jobs/search', 'GET /jobs/{id}', 'POST /jobs/filter'] } } } # Lambda deployment ready for func_name, func_config in serverless_config['django_functions'].items(): print(f"Function: {func_name} -> Models: {func_config.get('model', func_config.get('models'))}")
    Example 4: Create ECS Application for Containerized Django Deployment

    Create a CodeDeploy application for Amazon ECS to deploy containerized Django jobpost application with blue/green deployment strategy.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create ECS CodeDeploy application for containerized Django
aws deploy create-application \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-ecs" \
  --compute-platform ECS \
  --tags Key=Environment,Value=production \
         Key=Platform,Value=ECS \
         Key=Strategy,Value=blue-green \
         Key=Container,Value=django-jobpost \
         Key=Database,Value=postgresql \
  --region us-east-1
    { "applicationId": "ecs12345-6789-0123-4567-890123456789" }
    # ECS containerized Django deployment ecs_deployment = { 'application_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-ecs', 'application_id': 'ecs12345-6789-0123-4567-890123456789', 'compute_platform': 'ECS', 'deployment_strategy': 'blue-green', 'containers': { 'django-web': { 'image': 'anihpj/jobpost-django:latest', 'port': 8000, 'models': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'] }, 'postgresql': { 'image': 'postgres:15', 'port': 5432, 'databases': ['jobpost_db'] }, 'redis': { 'image': 'redis:7', 'port': 6379, 'cache': 'django-sessions' } } } print(f"ECS Application: {ecs_deployment['application_name']}") print(f"Django models in containers: {ecs_deployment['containers']['django-web']['models']}")
    Example 5: Create Multi-Environment Application with Script Automation

    Create a CodeDeploy application with automated script that sets up applications for development, staging, and production environments of the Django jobpost project.

    #!/bin/bash
# Multi-environment CodeDeploy application creation script

ENVIRONMENTS=("development" "staging" "production")
BASE_APP_NAME="anihpj-jobpost"

for ENV in "${ENVIRONMENTS[@]}"; do
    echo "Creating CodeDeploy application for $ENV environment..."
    
    aws deploy create-application \
      --application-name "${BASE_APP_NAME}-${ENV}" \
      --compute-platform Server \
      --tags Key=Environment,Value=$ENV \
             Key=Project,Value=jobpost \
             Key=AutoCreated,Value=true \
             Key=Models,Value="Skills,User,Author,Location,JobPost" \
             Key=CreatedBy,Value=automation-script \
      --region us-east-1
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "✓ Successfully created: ${BASE_APP_NAME}-${ENV}"
    else
        echo "✗ Failed to create: ${BASE_APP_NAME}-${ENV}"
    fi
done

echo "Multi-environment CodeDeploy applications creation completed!"
    Creating CodeDeploy application for development environment... { "applicationId": "dev12345-1234-1234-1234-123456789012" } ✓ Successfully created: anihpj-jobpost-development Creating CodeDeploy application for staging environment... { "applicationId": "stg12345-1234-1234-1234-123456789012" } ✓ Successfully created: anihpj-jobpost-staging Creating CodeDeploy application for production environment... { "applicationId": "prd12345-1234-1234-1234-123456789012" } ✓ Successfully created: anihpj-jobpost-production Multi-environment CodeDeploy applications creation completed!
