    # Multi-environment Django deployment tracking environments = { 'development': { 'app_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-development', 'app_id': 'dev12345-1234-1234-1234-123456789012', 'models_active': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'debug_mode': True, 'database': 'sqlite3' }, 'staging': { 'app_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-staging', 'app_id': 'stg12345-1234-1234-1234-123456789012', 'models_active': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'debug_mode': False, 'database': 'postgresql' }, 'production': { 'app_name': 'anihpj-jobpost-production', 'app_id': 'prd12345-1234-1234-1234-123456789012', 'models_active': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'debug_mode': False, 'database': 'postgresql', 'high_availability': True } } # Verify all environments ready for env_name, config in environments.items(): print(f"{env_name.upper()}: {config['app_name']} -> Models: {len(config['models_active'])}")

    create-deployment

    Creates and starts a new CodeDeploy deployment for your Django jobpost application. This command initiates the deployment process to push your application code, including Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models, to target instances or compute platforms. The deployment coordinates application updates across multiple environments while maintaining availability and data consistency for your Django models and database migrations.

    Syntax
    aws deploy create-deployment \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  --deployment-config-name {deployment-config} \
  --deployment-group-name {deployment-group} \
  --revision revisionType={revision-type},s3Location={bucket-info} \
  --description {deployment-description} \
  --ignore-application-stop-failures \
  --update-outdated-instances-only \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled={boolean},events={event-types}
    Parameters Description
    • --application-name (string, required): Name of the CodeDeploy application containing your Django jobpost project. This application serves as the container for all deployment configurations and manages the deployment of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models across your infrastructure.
    • --deployment-config-name (string, optional): Deployment configuration that controls how the deployment proceeds. Options include CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce for development, CodeDeployDefault.HalfAtATime for staged production deployments, or custom configurations for Django-specific deployment strategies with database migration coordination.
    • --deployment-group-name (string, required): Target deployment group containing the EC2 instances, Auto Scaling groups, or Lambda functions where your Django application with jobpost models will be deployed. This group defines the infrastructure that will receive the new application version.
    • --revision (structure, required): Source code location and type for deployment. For Django applications, typically points to S3 bucket containing your packaged application with models, templates, static files, and database migrations. Include revisionType (S3, GitHub, or local), and location details for source retrieval.
    • --description (string, optional): Human-readable description of the deployment for tracking and auditing purposes. Include information about Django version, model changes, feature updates, or database schema modifications being deployed.
    • --ignore-application-stop-failures (boolean, optional): Continue deployment even if application stop lifecycle events fail on target instances. Useful for Django applications where graceful shutdown may timeout during database connection cleanup or model state persistence.
    • --update-outdated-instances-only (boolean, optional): Deploy only to instances that don't already have the target application revision. Optimizes deployment time for Django applications by skipping instances that already have the current version of models and application code.
    • --auto-rollback-configuration (structure, optional): Automatic rollback settings triggered by deployment failures or monitoring alerts. Critical for Django applications to maintain data consistency when model migrations fail or application health checks indicate issues with database connectivity.
    Attributes
    • deploymentId: Unique identifier assigned to the deployment operation for tracking and monitoring progress
    • deploymentGroupName: Name of the target deployment group receiving the Django application update
    • applicationName: CodeDeploy application name containing the Django jobpost project
    • revisionLocation: Source location details including S3 bucket, key, and bundleType for Django application package
    • deploymentConfigName: Configuration strategy controlling deployment rollout pattern and failure handling
    • status: Current deployment state (Created, Queued, InProgress, Succeeded, Failed, Stopped)
    • errorInformation: Detailed error messages and codes when deployment encounters Django-specific issues like migration failures
    • startTime: Timestamp when deployment initiated
    • completeTime: Timestamp when deployment finished (success or failure)
    • deploymentOverview: Summary statistics of deployment progress across target instances
    Methods
    get-deployment

    Syntax: aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id {deployment-id}

    Parameters: deployment-id (string, required) - Unique identifier returned from create-deployment operation to retrieve detailed status, progress metrics, and error information for Django application deployment monitoring

    stop-deployment

    Syntax: aws deploy stop-deployment --deployment-id {deployment-id} --auto-rollback-enabled

    Parameters: deployment-id (string, required) - Deployment to halt; auto-rollback-enabled (boolean, optional) - Whether to automatically revert Django application to previous working state when stopping deployment

    list-deployment-targets

    Syntax: aws deploy list-deployment-targets --deployment-id {deployment-id}

    Parameters: deployment-id (string, required) - Show all target instances, containers, or Lambda functions receiving the Django deployment with individual status and lifecycle event progress

    Return Value
    • deploymentId (string): Unique identifier for the created deployment that can be used to monitor progress, retrieve status, and manage the Django application deployment lifecycle
    5 Detailed CodeDeploy create-deployment Examples
    Example 1: Deploy Django Development Build to Single EC2 Instance

    Deploy the latest development version of the Django jobpost application with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models to a single EC2 development instance. This deployment includes database migrations, static file collection, and model schema updates for testing new features before production release.

    #!/bin/bash
# Deploy Django development build to EC2 development instance
aws deploy create-deployment \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-dev" \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce" \
  --deployment-group-name "dev-django-servers" \
  --revision revisionType=S3,s3Location='{bucket=anihpj-deployments,key=jobpost-dev-v1.2.3.zip,bundleType=zip}' \
  --description "Django jobpost dev deployment v1.2.3 with Skills model updates and new Location fields" \
  --ignore-application-stop-failures \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
  --region us-east-1
    { "deploymentId": "d-1234567890abcdef0" } # Check deployment status aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id d-1234567890abcdef0 { "deploymentInfo": { "applicationName": "anihpj-jobpost-dev", "deploymentGroupName": "dev-django-servers", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce", "deploymentId": "d-1234567890abcdef0", "revision": { "revisionType": "S3", "s3Location": { "bucket": "anihpj-deployments", "key": "jobpost-dev-v1.2.3.zip", "bundleType": "zip" } }, "status": "Succeeded", "startTime": "2026-01-15T10:30:00.000Z", "completeTime": "2026-01-15T10:35:22.000Z", "deploymentOverview": { "Pending": 0, "InProgress": 0, "Succeeded": 1, "Failed": 0, "Skipped": 0, "Ready": 0 } } }
    # Django models deployed and migrated after successful deployment from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import User as DjangoUser # Verify deployment success by checking model availability deployment_status = { 'deployment_id': 'd-1234567890abcdef0', 'application_version': 'v1.2.3', 'environment': 'development', 'models_deployed': ['Skills', 'User', 'Author', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'migration_status': 'completed' } # Create test data to verify models work after deployment print("=== Post-Deployment Model Verification ===") # Create Skills python_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Python', level='Advanced', description='Python programming for Django development' ) django_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Django', level='Expert', description='Django web framework mastery' ) print(f"Skills created: {Skills.objects.count()} total") print(f"Latest skill: {Skills.objects.last().name} - {Skills.objects.last().level}") # Create User and Author dev_user = DjangoUser.objects.create_user( username='dev_recruiter', email='dev@anihpj.com', password='dev_password123' ) # Create Location sf_location = Location.objects.create( city='San Francisco', state='California', country='USA', zip_code='94105', is_remote=False ) print(f"Location created: {sf_location.city}, {sf_location.state}") # Create Author tech_author = Author.objects.create( user=dev_user, bio='Senior Technical Recruiter', company='TechCorp', location=sf_location ) print(f"Author created: {tech_author.user.username} at {tech_author.company}") # Create JobPost senior_dev_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Senior Python Developer', description='Looking for experienced Python/Django developer for jobpost platform', company='TechCorp', location=sf_location, author=tech_author, salary_min=120000, salary_max=180000, is_active=True ) senior_dev_job.skills.add(python_skill, django_skill) print(f"JobPost created: {senior_dev_job.title} by {senior_dev_job.author.user.username}") # Verify relationships work print(f"Job skills: {[skill.name for skill in senior_dev_job.skills.all()]}") print(f"Jobs by author: {JobPost.objects.filter(author=tech_author).count()}") print(f"Jobs in {sf_location.city}: {JobPost.objects.filter(location=sf_location).count()}") print("=== Deployment Verification Complete ===") print(f"Deployment {deployment_status['deployment_id']} successful") print(f"All {len(deployment_status['models_deployed'])} models working correctly")
    Example 2: Blue/Green Production Deployment with Auto Rollback

    Deploy Django jobpost application to production environment using blue/green deployment strategy. This ensures zero-downtime deployment with automatic rollback capabilities if health checks fail. Includes comprehensive monitoring for database migrations, model integrity, and application performance metrics.

    #!/bin/bash
# Production blue/green deployment with enhanced monitoring
aws deploy create-deployment \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-production" \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenAllAtOnce" \
  --deployment-group-name "prod-django-cluster" \
  --revision revisionType=S3,s3Location='{bucket=anihpj-prod-deployments,key=jobpost-prod-v2.1.0.zip,bundleType=zip}' \
  --description "Production deployment v2.1.0: Enhanced Skills matching, new JobPost fields, Location hierarchy updates" \
  --update-outdated-instances-only \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_INSTANCE_FAILURE \
  --region us-east-1

# Monitor deployment progress
DEPLOYMENT_ID=$(aws deploy list-deployments --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-production" --query 'deployments[0]' --output text)
echo "Monitoring deployment: $DEPLOYMENT_ID"

# Check deployment targets
aws deploy list-deployment-targets --deployment-id $DEPLOYMENT_ID
    { "deploymentId": "d-abcdef1234567890a" } # Deployment monitoring output aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id d-abcdef1234567890a { "deploymentInfo": { "applicationName": "anihpj-jobpost-production", "deploymentGroupName": "prod-django-cluster", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenAllAtOnce", "deploymentId": "d-abcdef1234567890a", "status": "InProgress", "startTime": "2026-01-15T14:20:00.000Z", "deploymentOverview": { "Pending": 0, "InProgress": 3, "Succeeded": 0, "Failed": 0, "Skipped": 0, "Ready": 2 }, "blueGreenDeploymentConfiguration": { "terminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess": { "action": "TERMINATE", "terminationWaitTimeInMinutes": 15 }, "deploymentReadyOption": { "actionOnTimeout": "CONTINUE_DEPLOYMENT" }, "greenFleetProvisioningOption": { "action": "COPY_AUTO_SCALING_GROUP" } } } } # Final successful deployment status { "deploymentInfo": { "status": "Succeeded", "completeTime": "2026-01-15T14:45:30.000Z", "deploymentOverview": { "Pending": 0, "InProgress": 0, "Succeeded": 5, "Failed": 0, "Skipped": 0, "Ready": 0 } } }
    # Production deployment verification with enhanced model testing from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import User as DjangoUser from django.db import transaction import json production_deployment = { 'deployment_id': 'd-abcdef1234567890a', 'version': 'v2.1.0', 'environment': 'production', 'deployment_type': 'blue_green', 'models_updated': ['Skills', 'Location', 'JobPost'], 'new_features': ['skills_matching_algorithm', 'location_hierarchy', 'job_recommendations'] } print("=== Production Deployment v2.1.0 Verification ===") # Test new Skills enhancements advanced_skills = [ {'name': 'Machine Learning', 'level': 'Expert', 'category': 'AI/ML'}, {'name': 'Docker', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'DevOps'}, {'name': 'PostgreSQL', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'Database'}, {'name': 'React', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'Frontend'} ] for skill_data in advanced_skills: skill, created = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=skill_data['name'], defaults={ 'level': skill_data['level'], 'category': skill_data.get('category', 'General') } ) print(f"Skill {'created' if created else 'updated'}: {skill.name} - {skill.level}") # Test Location hierarchy (new feature) locations = [ {'city': 'New York', 'state': 'New York', 'country': 'USA', 'zip_code': '10001'}, {'city': 'London', 'state': 'England', 'country': 'UK', 'zip_code': 'SW1A 1AA'}, {'city': 'Tokyo', 'state': 'Tokyo', 'country': 'Japan', 'zip_code': '100-0001'}, {'city': 'Remote', 'state': 'Global', 'country': 'Remote', 'zip_code': '00000', 'is_remote': True} ] for loc_data in locations: location, created = Location.objects.get_or_create( city=loc_data['city'], state=loc_data['state'], defaults=loc_data ) print(f"Location {'created' if created else 'exists'}: {location.city}, {location.country}") # Create production users and authors prod_users_data = [ {'username': 'hr_manager', 'email': 'hr@techcorp.com', 'first_name': 'Sarah', 'last_name': 'Johnson'}, {'username': 'startup_founder', 'email': 'founder@innovate.io', 'first_name': 'Alex', 'last_name': 'Chen'}, {'username': 'enterprise_recruiter', 'email': 'talent@bigcorp.com', 'first_name': 'Michael', 'last_name': 'Smith'} ] with transaction.atomic(): for user_data in prod_users_data: user, created = DjangoUser.objects.get_or_create( username=user_data['username'], defaults=user_data ) if created: user.set_password('prod_secure_pass_2026') user.save() # Create corresponding Author author_location = Location.objects.filter(city='New York').first() author = Author.objects.create( user=user, bio=f'Professional recruiter - {user.first_name} {user.last_name}', company=f'{user.first_name} Corp', location=author_location ) print(f"Created user & author: {user.username} -> {author.company}") # Test JobPost with new features (v2.1.0) job_posts_data = [ { 'title': 'Senior ML Engineer', 'description': 'Lead machine learning initiatives for job matching algorithm', 'company': 'TechCorp', 'salary_min': 150000, 'salary_max': 220000, 'skills': ['Machine Learning', 'Python'], 'location': 'New York' }, { 'title': 'DevOps Engineer', 'description': 'Container orchestration and CI/CD pipeline management', 'company': 'Innovate Corp', 'salary_min': 120000, 'salary_max': 180000, 'skills': ['Docker', 'PostgreSQL'], 'location': 'Remote' }, { 'title': 'Full Stack Developer', 'description': 'React frontend with Django backend development', 'company': 'BigCorp', 'salary_min': 100000, 'salary_max': 150000, 'skills': ['React', 'PostgreSQL'], 'location': 'London' } ] for job_data in job_posts_data: location = Location.objects.filter(city=job_data['location']).first() author = Author.objects.filter(company__icontains=job_data['company'].split()[0]).first() if location and author: job_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=job_data['title'], description=job_data['description'], company=job_data['company'], location=location, author=author, salary_min=job_data['salary_min'], salary_max=job_data['salary_max'], is_active=True ) # Add skills to job post for skill_name in job_data['skills']: skill = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).first() if skill: job_post.skills.add(skill) print(f"Created job: {job_post.title} at {job_post.location.city}") print(f" Skills: {[s.name for s in job_post.skills.all()]}") print(f" Salary: ${job_post.salary_min:,} - ${job_post.salary_max:,}") # Production deployment statistics stats = { 'total_skills': Skills.objects.count(), 'total_locations': Location.objects.count(), 'total_users': DjangoUser.objects.count(), 'total_authors': Author.objects.count(), 'total_jobs': JobPost.objects.count(), 'active_jobs': JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count(), 'remote_jobs': JobPost.objects.filter(location__is_remote=True).count() } print("=== Production Deployment Statistics ===") for key, value in stats.items(): print(f"{key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"\nDeployment {production_deployment['deployment_id']} completed successfully") print(f"New features deployed: {', '.join(production_deployment['new_features'])}") print("All models and relationships verified in production")
    Example 3: Multi-Region Staging Deployment with Database Migration Validation

    Deploy Django jobpost application across multiple AWS regions for staging environment with comprehensive database migration testing. This deployment validates model schema changes, tests data consistency across regions, and ensures Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models maintain integrity during geographic distribution.

    #!/bin/bash
# Multi-region staging deployment with migration validation
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")
DEPLOYMENT_VERSION="v1.5.0"

for REGION in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    echo "Deploying to region: $REGION"
    
    aws deploy create-deployment \
      --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-staging-${REGION}" \
      --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.HalfAtATime" \
      --deployment-group-name "staging-django-cluster-${REGION}" \
      --revision revisionType=S3,s3Location="{bucket=anihpj-staging-${REGION},key=jobpost-staging-${DEPLOYMENT_VERSION}.zip,bundleType=zip}" \
      --description "Staging deployment ${DEPLOYMENT_VERSION} to ${REGION}: Model migrations, Location geo-indexing, Skills categorization" \
      --update-outdated-instances-only \
      --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE \
      --region $REGION
      
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "✓ Deployment initiated successfully in $REGION"
    else
        echo "✗ Deployment failed in $REGION"
        exit 1
    fi
done
    Deploying to region: us-east-1 { "deploymentId": "d-eastcoast123456789" } ✓ Deployment initiated successfully in us-east-1 Deploying to region: us-west-2 { "deploymentId": "d-westcoast987654321" } ✓ Deployment initiated successfully in us-west-2 Deploying to region: eu-west-1 { "deploymentId": "d-europe456789123" } ✓ Deployment initiated successfully in eu-west-1
    # Multi-region deployment verification with geographic data validation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import User as DjangoUser from django.db import models, connection import datetime staging_deployment = { 'version': 'v1.5.0', 'regions': ['us-east-1', 'us-west-2', 'eu-west-1'], 'deployment_ids': { 'us-east-1': 'd-eastcoast123456789', 'us-west-2': 'd-westcoast987654321', 'eu-west-1': 'd-europe456789123' }, 'features': ['geo_indexing', 'skills_categorization', 'location_hierarchy'] } print("=== Multi-Region Staging Deployment Verification ===") # Test Skills categorization (new feature in v1.5.0) skill_categories = [ {'name': 'Cloud Architecture', 'level': 'Expert', 'category': 'Cloud Computing'}, {'name': 'Kubernetes', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'Container Orchestration'}, {'name': 'Microservices', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'Architecture'}, {'name': 'GraphQL', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'API Development'}, {'name': 'MongoDB', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'NoSQL Database'} ] print("Creating skills with new categorization system...") for skill_data in skill_categories: skill, created = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=skill_data['name'], defaults=skill_data ) print(f" {skill.name} [{skill.category}] - {skill.level}") # Test geo-indexed Locations (new feature) regional_locations = [ # US East Coast {'city': 'Boston', 'state': 'Massachusetts', 'country': 'USA', 'region': 'us-east-1', 'timezone': 'America/New_York'}, {'city': 'Atlanta', 'state': 'Georgia', 'country': 'USA', 'region': 'us-east-1', 'timezone': 'America/New_York'}, # US West Coast {'city': 'Seattle', 'state': 'Washington', 'country': 'USA', 'region': 'us-west-2', 'timezone': 'America/Los_Angeles'}, {'city': 'Portland', 'state': 'Oregon', 'country': 'USA', 'region': 'us-west-2', 'timezone': 'America/Los_Angeles'}, # Europe {'city': 'Dublin', 'state': 'Leinster', 'country': 'Ireland', 'region': 'eu-west-1', 'timezone': 'Europe/Dublin'}, {'city': 'Berlin', 'state': 'Berlin', 'country': 'Germany', 'region': 'eu-west-1', 'timezone': 'Europe/Berlin'} ] print("\nCreating geo-indexed locations...") for loc_data in regional_locations: location, created = Location.objects.get_or_create( city=loc_data['city'], state=loc_data['state'], defaults=loc_data ) print(f" {location.city}, {location.country} [{loc_data['region']}] - {loc_data['timezone']}") # Create regional recruitment teams regional_teams = [ {'region': 'us-east-1', 'username': 'east_coast_recruiter', 'email': 'recruiter.east@anihpj.com', 'company': 'East Coast Tech'}, {'region': 'us-west-2', 'username': 'west_coast_recruiter', 'email': 'recruiter.west@anihpj.com', 'company': 'West Coast Innovation'}, {'region': 'eu-west-1', 'username': 'europe_recruiter', 'email': 'recruiter.eu@anihpj.com', 'company': 'European Tech Hub'} ] print("\nCreating regional recruitment teams...") for team_data in regional_teams: user, created = DjangoUser.objects.get_or_create( username=team_data['username'], defaults={'email': team_data['email'], 'first_name': team_data['region'].upper()} ) if created: user.set_password('staging_secure_2026') user.save() # Assign to regional location region_location = Location.objects.filter(region=team_data['region']).first() if region_location: author = Author.objects.create( user=user, bio=f'Regional recruiter for {team_data["region"]}', company=team_data['company'], location=region_location ) print(f" Created: {author.user.username} -> {author.company} in {region_location.city}") # Test multi-regional job posting with location hierarchy regional_jobs = [ { 'title': 'Senior Cloud Architect', 'region': 'us-east-1', 'skills': ['Cloud Architecture', 'Kubernetes'], 'salary_range': (180000, 250000) }, { 'title': 'Microservices Developer', 'region': 'us-west-2', 'skills': ['Microservices', 'GraphQL'], 'salary_range': (140000, 200000) }, { 'title': 'Database Engineer', 'region': 'eu-west-1', 'skills': ['MongoDB', 'Cloud Architecture'], 'salary_range': (70000, 120000) # EUR equivalent } ] print("\nCreating region-specific job postings...") for job_data in regional_jobs: # Get regional author and location author = Author.objects.filter( user__username__contains=job_data['region'].replace('-', '_') ).first() location = Location.objects.filter(region=job_data['region']).first() if author and location: job_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=job_data['title'], description=f'Seeking {job_data["title"].lower()} for our {location.city} office', company=author.company, location=location, author=author, salary_min=job_data['salary_range'][0], salary_max=job_data['salary_range'][1], is_active=True, posted_date=datetime.datetime.now() ) # Add required skills for skill_name in job_data['skills']: skill = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).first() if skill: job_post.skills.add(skill) print(f" {job_post.title} in {location.city} ({job_data['region']})") print(f" Skills: {[s.name for s in job_post.skills.all()]}") print(f" Salary: {job_post.salary_min:,} - {job_post.salary_max:,}") # Verify geographic distribution regional_stats = {} for region in staging_deployment['regions']: regional_stats[region] = { 'locations': Location.objects.filter(region=region).count(), 'authors': Author.objects.filter(location__region=region).count(), 'jobs': JobPost.objects.filter(location__region=region).count() } print("\n=== Regional Distribution Statistics ===") for region, stats in regional_stats.items(): print(f"{region.upper()}:") print(f" Locations: {stats['locations']}") print(f" Authors: {stats['authors']}") print(f" Job Posts: {stats['jobs']}") # Test cross-regional queries (new geo-indexing capability) total_stats = { 'skills_by_category': {}, 'jobs_by_region': {}, 'global_salary_ranges': {} } # Skills by category for category in ['Cloud Computing', 'Container Orchestration', 'Architecture']: count = Skills.objects.filter(category=category).count() total_stats['skills_by_category'][category] = count # Jobs by region for region in staging_deployment['regions']: count = JobPost.objects.filter(location__region=region).count() total_stats['jobs_by_region'][region] = count print("\n=== Global Statistics (Cross-Regional Queries) ===") print("Skills by Category:") for category, count in total_stats['skills_by_category'].items(): print(f" {category}: {count} skills") print("Jobs by Region:") for region, count in total_stats['jobs_by_region'].items(): print(f" {region}: {count} jobs") print(f"\nTotal deployment success across {len(staging_deployment['regions'])} regions") print(f"Deployment IDs: {list(staging_deployment['deployment_ids'].values())}") print("Geo-indexing and skills categorization verified successfully")
    Example 4: Serverless Lambda Deployment for Django API Microservices

    Deploy Django REST API microservices as Lambda functions using CodeDeploy for serverless architecture. This deployment creates individual Lambda functions for Skills management, User authentication, Job search, and Location services, maintaining model relationships while enabling independent scaling and deployment of each microservice component.

    #!/bin/bash
# Serverless Lambda deployment for Django API microservices
MICROSERVICES=("skills-api" "user-auth-api" "job-search-api" "location-api")
DEPLOYMENT_VERSION="v3.0.0"

echo "Deploying Django microservices to Lambda..."

for SERVICE in "${MICROSERVICES[@]}"; do
    echo "Deploying microservice: $SERVICE"
    
    aws deploy create-deployment \
      --application-name "anihpj-${SERVICE}-lambda" \
      --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.LambdaCanary10Percent5Minutes" \
      --revision revisionType=S3,s3Location="{bucket=anihpj-lambda-deployments,key=${SERVICE}-${DEPLOYMENT_VERSION}.zip,bundleType=zip}" \
      --description "Lambda deployment ${DEPLOYMENT_VERSION} for ${SERVICE}: Django REST API microservice with enhanced model operations" \
      --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
      --region us-east-1
      
    sleep 5  # Allow time between deployments
done

# Monitor Lambda deployments
echo "Monitoring all Lambda deployments..."
for SERVICE in "${MICROSERVICES[@]}"; do
    DEPLOYMENT_ID=$(aws deploy list-deployments \
        --application-name "anihpj-${SERVICE}-lambda" \
        --query 'deployments[0]' --output text)
    echo "Service: $SERVICE, Deployment: $DEPLOYMENT_ID"
    
    # Check deployment status
    aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id $DEPLOYMENT_ID
done
    Deploying Django microservices to Lambda... Deploying microservice: skills-api { "deploymentId": "d-lambda-skills-abc123" } Deploying microservice: user-auth-api { "deploymentId": "d-lambda-auth-def456" } Deploying microservice: job-search-api { "deploymentId": "d-lambda-jobs-ghi789" } Deploying microservice: location-api { "deploymentId": "d-lambda-location-jkl012" } Monitoring all Lambda deployments... Service: skills-api, Deployment: d-lambda-skills-abc123 { "deploymentInfo": { "applicationName": "anihpj-skills-api-lambda", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.LambdaCanary10Percent5Minutes", "status": "Succeeded", "startTime": "2026-01-15T18:15:00.000Z", "completeTime": "2026-01-15T18:25:30.000Z" } } Service: job-search-api, Deployment: d-lambda-jobs-ghi789 { "deploymentInfo": { "applicationName": "anihpj-job-search-api-lambda", "status": "Succeeded", "deploymentOverview": { "Succeeded": 1, "Failed": 0 } } }
    # Serverless Lambda microservices verification with Django models from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import User as DjangoUser from django.http import JsonResponse import json import boto3 lambda_deployment = { 'version': 'v3.0.0', 'architecture': 'serverless_microservices', 'services': { 'skills-api': 'd-lambda-skills-abc123', 'user-auth-api': 'd-lambda-auth-def456', 'job-search-api': 'd-lambda-jobs-ghi789', 'location-api': 'd-lambda-location-jkl012' }, 'deployment_strategy': 'canary_10_percent' } print("=== Serverless Lambda Microservices Verification ===") # Simulate Skills API Lambda function def skills_api_handler(event, context): """Lambda function for Skills management API""" if event['httpMethod'] == 'GET' and event['path'] == '/skills': # List all skills with categorization skills = Skills.objects.all().values('name', 'level', 'category') return { 'statusCode': 200, 'body': json.dumps(list(skills)), 'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'} } elif event['httpMethod'] == 'POST' and event['path'] == '/skills': # Create new skill skill_data = json.loads(event['body']) skill = Skills.objects.create(**skill_data) return { 'statusCode': 201, 'body': json.dumps({ 'id': skill.id, 'name': skill.name, 'level': skill.level, 'category': skill.category }) } # Test Skills API functionality print("Testing Skills API microservice...") skills_test_data = [ {'name': 'Serverless Architecture', 'level': 'Expert', 'category': 'Cloud Computing'}, {'name': 'API Gateway', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'API Development'}, {'name': 'DynamoDB', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'NoSQL Database'}, {'name': 'Lambda Functions', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'Serverless'} ] for skill_data in skills_test_data: skill = Skills.objects.create(**skill_data) print(f" Created skill: {skill.name} [{skill.category}] - {skill.level}") # Simulate User Authentication API Lambda function def user_auth_api_handler(event, context): """Lambda function for User authentication and management""" if event['path'] == '/auth/register': user_data = json.loads(event['body']) user = DjangoUser.objects.create_user(**user_data) return { 'statusCode': 201, 'body': json.dumps({ 'user_id': user.id, 'username': user.username, 'email': user.email }) } elif event['path'] == '/auth/profile': # Get user profile with author information user_id = event['pathParameters']['user_id'] user = DjangoUser.objects.get(id=user_id) try: author = Author.objects.get(user=user) return { 'statusCode': 200, 'body': json.dumps({ 'username': user.username, 'bio': author.bio, 'company': author.company, 'location': author.location.city }) } except Author.DoesNotExist: return {'statusCode': 404, 'body': 'Author profile not found'} # Test User Auth API functionality print("\nTesting User Auth API microservice...") api_users = [ {'username': 'lambda_user1', 'email': 'user1@lambda.com', 'first_name': 'Lambda', 'last_name': 'User1'}, {'username': 'serverless_recruiter', 'email': 'recruiter@serverless.io', 'first_name': 'Serverless', 'last_name': 'Recruiter'} ] for user_data in api_users: user = DjangoUser.objects.create_user(**user_data) user.set_password('lambda_secure_2026') user.save() print(f" Created user: {user.username} ({user.email})") # Simulate Location API Lambda function def location_api_handler(event, context): """Lambda function for Location management and geo-queries""" if event['path'] == '/locations/search': query_params = event['queryStringParameters'] locations = Location.objects.filter( city__icontains=query_params.get('city', ''), country__icontains=query_params.get('country', '') ) return { 'statusCode': 200, 'body': json.dumps([ { 'city': loc.city, 'state': loc.state, 'country': loc.country, 'is_remote': loc.is_remote } for loc in locations ]) } # Test Location API functionality print("\nTesting Location API microservice...") serverless_locations = [ {'city': 'Global Remote', 'state': 'Virtual', 'country': 'Remote', 'is_remote': True}, {'city': 'AWS Cloud', 'state': 'Distributed', 'country': 'Serverless', 'is_remote': True}, {'city': 'Edge Locations', 'state': 'Multi-AZ', 'country': 'Global', 'is_remote': False} ] for loc_data in serverless_locations: location = Location.objects.create(**loc_data) print(f" Created location: {location.city}, {location.country}") # Simulate Job Search API Lambda function def job_search_api_handler(event, context): """Lambda function for Job search and filtering""" if event['path'] == '/jobs/search': query_params = event.get('queryStringParameters', {}) jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) if query_params.get('skills'): skills_list = query_params['skills'].split(',') jobs = jobs.filter(skills__name__in=skills_list).distinct() if query_params.get('location'): jobs = jobs.filter(location__city__icontains=query_params['location']) return { 'statusCode': 200, 'body': json.dumps([ { 'title': job.title, 'company': job.company, 'location': job.location.city, 'salary_min': job.salary_min, 'salary_max': job.salary_max, 'skills': [skill.name for skill in job.skills.all()] } for job in jobs[:10] # Limit to 10 results ]) } # Test Job Search API with microservices data print("\nTesting Job Search API microservice...") # Create Authors for serverless jobs serverless_location = Location.objects.filter(city='AWS Cloud').first() lambda_user = DjangoUser.objects.filter(username='serverless_recruiter').first() if lambda_user and serverless_location: serverless_author = Author.objects.create( user=lambda_user, bio='Serverless architecture recruiter', company='CloudTech Serverless', location=serverless_location ) print(f" Created author: {serverless_author.user.username} at {serverless_author.company}") # Create serverless job postings serverless_jobs = [ { 'title': 'Senior Lambda Developer', 'description': 'Expert in AWS Lambda and serverless architectures', 'skills': ['Serverless Architecture', 'Lambda Functions'], 'salary_range': (130000, 200000) }, { 'title': 'API Gateway Specialist', 'description': 'Design and implement API Gateway solutions', 'skills': ['API Gateway', 'Lambda Functions'], 'salary_range': (110000, 170000) }, { 'title': 'DynamoDB Engineer', 'description': 'NoSQL database design for serverless applications', 'skills': ['DynamoDB', 'Serverless Architecture'], 'salary_range': (100000, 160000) } ] for job_data in serverless_jobs: job = JobPost.objects.create( title=job_data['title'], description=job_data['description'], company='CloudTech Serverless', location=serverless_location, author=serverless_author, salary_min=job_data['salary_range'][0], salary_max=job_data['salary_range'][1], is_active=True ) # Add skills to job for skill_name in job_data['skills']: skill = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).first() if skill: job.skills.add(skill) print(f" Created job: {job.title}") print(f" Skills: {[s.name for s in job.skills.all()]}") print(f" Salary: ${job.salary_min:,} - ${job.salary_max:,}") # Microservices deployment statistics microservice_stats = { 'skills_api': { 'skills_managed': Skills.objects.filter(category__in=['Cloud Computing', 'Serverless']).count(), 'categories': Skills.objects.values_list('category', flat=True).distinct().count() }, 'user_auth_api': { 'users_managed': DjangoUser.objects.filter(username__contains='lambda').count(), 'authors_with_profiles': Author.objects.filter(user__username__contains='serverless').count() }, 'location_api': { 'locations_managed': Location.objects.filter(is_remote=True).count(), 'serverless_locations': Location.objects.filter(country='Serverless').count() }, 'job_search_api': { 'searchable_jobs': JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True, company__contains='Serverless').count(), 'serverless_jobs': JobPost.objects.filter(skills__category='Serverless').distinct().count() } } print("\n=== Microservices Statistics ===") for service, stats in microservice_stats.items(): print(f"{service.upper().replace('_', ' ')}:") for key, value in stats.items(): print(f" {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"\nLambda deployment successful: {lambda_deployment['version']}") print(f"Microservices deployed: {len(lambda_deployment['services'])}") print(f"Deployment strategy: {lambda_deployment['deployment_strategy']}") print("All Django models accessible via serverless API endpoints")
    Example 5: Auto Scaling Group Deployment with Health Monitoring and Rolling Updates

    Deploy Django jobpost application to Auto Scaling Groups with comprehensive health monitoring, rolling updates, and automatic scaling based on application load. This deployment ensures high availability for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models while maintaining performance during traffic spikes and providing automatic recovery from failures.

    #!/bin/bash
# Auto Scaling Group deployment with health monitoring and rolling updates
APPLICATION_NAME="anihpj-jobpost-autoscaling"
DEPLOYMENT_VERSION="v2.5.0"

echo "Deploying Django application to Auto Scaling Groups..."

# Create deployment with rolling update configuration
aws deploy create-deployment \
  --application-name $APPLICATION_NAME \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.AutoScalingGroupOneAtATime" \
  --deployment-group-name "django-asg-production" \
  --revision revisionType=S3,s3Location='{bucket=anihpj-asg-deployments,key=jobpost-asg-v2.5.0.zip,bundleType=zip}' \
  --description "ASG deployment v2.5.0: Enhanced auto-scaling, health checks, model performance optimization" \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_INSTANCE_FAILURE \
  --region us-east-1

# Get deployment ID for monitoring
DEPLOYMENT_ID=$(aws deploy list-deployments --application-name $APPLICATION_NAME --query 'deployments[0]' --output text)
echo "Deployment ID: $DEPLOYMENT_ID"

# Monitor deployment progress with detailed tracking
echo "Monitoring Auto Scaling Group deployment..."
while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id $DEPLOYMENT_ID --query 'deploymentInfo.status' --output text)
    echo "Current status: $STATUS ($(date))"
    
    if [ "$STATUS" = "Succeeded" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "Failed" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "Stopped" ]; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 30
done

# Check final deployment status with targets
aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id $DEPLOYMENT_ID
aws deploy list-deployment-targets --deployment-id $DEPLOYMENT_ID

# Verify Auto Scaling Group health
ASG_NAME=$(aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --query 'AutoScalingGroups[?contains(Tags[?Key==`Application`].Value, `anihpj-jobpost`)].AutoScalingGroupName' --output text)
echo "Auto Scaling Group: $ASG_NAME"

aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --auto-scaling-group-names $ASG_NAME
    Deploying Django application to Auto Scaling Groups... { "deploymentId": "d-asg-production-xyz789" } Deployment ID: d-asg-production-xyz789 Monitoring Auto Scaling Group deployment... Current status: InProgress (Tue Jan 15 20:15:00 UTC 2026) Current status: InProgress (Tue Jan 15 20:15:30 UTC 2026) Current status: InProgress (Tue Jan 15 20:16:00 UTC 2026) Current status: Succeeded (Tue Jan 15 20:16:30 UTC 2026) # Final deployment status { "deploymentInfo": { "applicationName": "anihpj-jobpost-autoscaling", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.AutoScalingGroupOneAtATime", "deploymentId": "d-asg-production-xyz789", "status": "Succeeded", "startTime": "2026-01-15T20:15:00.000Z", "completeTime": "2026-01-15T20:45:22.000Z", "deploymentOverview": { "Pending": 0, "InProgress": 0, "Succeeded": 6, "Failed": 0, "Skipped": 0, "Ready": 0 }, "autoScalingGroupConfiguration": { "minimumHealthyInstances": 4, "rollingUpdatePolicy": "OneAtATime" } } } Auto Scaling Group: django-jobpost-asg-prod { "AutoScalingGroups": [ { "AutoScalingGroupName": "django-jobpost-asg-prod", "MinSize": 2, "MaxSize": 10, "DesiredCapacity": 6, "HealthCheckType": "ELB", "HealthCheckGracePeriod": 300, "Instances": [ { "InstanceId": "i-asg001", "HealthStatus": "Healthy", "LifecycleState": "InService" }, { "InstanceId": "i-asg002", "HealthStatus": "Healthy", "LifecycleState": "InService" }, { "InstanceId": "i-asg003", "HealthStatus": "Healthy", "LifecycleState": "InService" } ] } ] }
    # Auto Scaling Group deployment verification with load testing from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import User as DjangoUser from django.db import transaction, connections from django.core.cache import cache import time import random asg_deployment = { 'deployment_id': 'd-asg-production-xyz789', 'version': 'v2.5.0', 'infrastructure': 'auto_scaling_group', 'instances': { 'min_size': 2, 'max_size': 10, 'current_capacity': 6, 'healthy_instances': 6 }, 'features': ['auto_scaling', 'health_monitoring', 'rolling_updates', 'load_balancing'] } print("=== Auto Scaling Group Deployment Verification ===") # Simulate high-load data creation for scaling test def create_bulk_test_data(): """Create large datasets to test auto-scaling performance""" print("Creating bulk test data for auto-scaling verification...") # Create diverse skill set for load testing bulk_skills = [] skill_categories = ['Software Development', 'Data Science', 'DevOps', 'UI/UX Design', 'Project Management'] skill_levels = ['Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced', 'Expert'] for category in skill_categories: for i in range(20): # 20 skills per category for level in skill_levels: skill_name = f"{category} Skill {i+1}" skill = Skills( name=skill_name, level=level, category=category, description=f"Professional {skill_name.lower()} at {level.lower()} level" ) bulk_skills.append(skill) # Bulk create skills Skills.objects.bulk_create(bulk_skills, batch_size=100) print(f" Created {len(bulk_skills)} skills across {len(skill_categories)} categories") # Create geographical locations for global scaling def create_global_locations(): """Create locations worldwide for global auto-scaling test""" global_cities = [ # North America ('New York', 'New York', 'USA', '10001', False), ('San Francisco', 'California', 'USA', '94105', False), ('Toronto', 'Ontario', 'Canada', 'M5H 2N2', False), # Europe ('London', 'England', 'UK', 'SW1A 1AA', False), ('Paris', 'Île-de-France', 'France', '75001', False), ('Berlin', 'Berlin', 'Germany', '10115', False), # Asia Pacific ('Tokyo', 'Tokyo', 'Japan', '100-0001', False), ('Sydney', 'New South Wales', 'Australia', '2000', False), ('Singapore', 'Singapore', 'Singapore', '018989', False), # Remote locations ('Remote Worldwide', 'Global', 'Remote', '00000', True), ('Hybrid Office', 'Flexible', 'Hybrid', '00001', True) ] locations = [] for city, state, country, zip_code, is_remote in global_cities: location = Location( city=city, state=state, country=country, zip_code=zip_code, is_remote=is_remote ) locations.append(location) Location.objects.bulk_create(locations, ignore_conflicts=True) print(f" Created {len(locations)} global locations") # Create enterprise-level user base def create_enterprise_users(): """Create large user base to test auto-scaling under load""" companies = [ 'TechCorp Global', 'Innovation Industries', 'StartupHub Inc', 'Enterprise Solutions', 'Digital Dynamics', 'CloudTech Systems', 'AI Innovations', 'DataDriven Corp', 'ScaleUp Solutions', 'GlobalReach Ltd' ] roles = ['Senior Recruiter', 'Talent Acquisition Manager', 'HR Director', 'Technical Recruiter', 'Executive Search', 'People Operations'] users_created = 0 authors_created = 0 with transaction.atomic(): for company in companies: for i in range(10): # 10 users per company username = f"{company.lower().replace(' ', '_')}_{i+1}" email = f"user{i+1}@{company.lower().replace(' ', '')}.com" user = DjangoUser.objects.create_user( username=username, email=email, first_name=f"User{i+1}", last_name=company.split()[0], password='asg_secure_2026' ) users_created += 1 # Create corresponding Author location = Location.objects.order_by('?').first() # Random location role = random.choice(roles) author = Author.objects.create( user=user, bio=f'{role} at {company} with expertise in talent acquisition', company=company, location=location ) authors_created += 1 print(f" Created {users_created} users and {authors_created} authors") # Create high-volume job postings for load testing def create_enterprise_job_postings(): """Create large number of job postings to test search performance""" job_titles = [ 'Senior Software Engineer', 'Data Scientist', 'DevOps Engineer', 'Product Manager', 'UX Designer', 'Frontend Developer', 'Backend Developer', 'Machine Learning Engineer', 'Solutions Architect', 'Technical Lead', 'Engineering Manager', 'Site Reliability Engineer' ] job_descriptions = [ 'Seeking experienced professional for challenging role in growing team', 'Join our innovative team building next-generation solutions', 'Opportunity to work with cutting-edge technology and talented professionals', 'Make impact in fast-paced environment with excellent growth opportunities', 'Lead technical initiatives in collaborative and supportive environment' ] jobs_created = 0 # Get all authors and locations for job creation authors = list(Author.objects.all()) locations = list(Location.objects.all()) skills = list(Skills.objects.all()) with transaction.atomic(): for author in authors[:50]: # Use first 50 authors for i in range(5): # 5 jobs per author title = random.choice(job_titles) description = random.choice(job_descriptions) location = random.choice(locations) # Generate realistic salary range based on role base_salary = random.randint(80000, 200000) salary_range = (base_salary, base_salary + random.randint(20000, 50000)) job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"{title} - {author.company}", description=description, company=author.company, location=location, author=author, salary_min=salary_range[0], salary_max=salary_range[1], is_active=True ) # Add 2-4 random skills to each job job_skills = random.sample(skills, random.randint(2, 4)) job.skills.set(job_skills) jobs_created += 1 print(f" Created {jobs_created} job postings with skill requirements") # Execute load testing data creation print("Generating enterprise-scale test data...") create_bulk_test_data() create_global_locations() create_enterprise_users() create_enterprise_job_postings() # Test database performance under auto-scaling load def performance_test_queries(): """Test query performance that would trigger auto-scaling""" print("\nExecuting performance test queries...") start_time = time.time() # Test 1: Complex skill-based job search complex_search = JobPost.objects.filter( skills__category='Software Development', location__country='USA', is_active=True, salary_min__gte=100000 ).distinct().select_related('location', 'author').prefetch_related('skills') result_count = complex_search.count() search_time = time.time() - start_time print(f" Complex search: {result_count} results in {search_time:.3f}s") # Test 2: Cross-model aggregation start_time = time.time() skill_stats = Skills.objects.values('category').annotate( job_count=models.Count('jobpost'), avg_salary_min=models.Avg('jobpost__salary_min') ).order_by('-job_count')[:10] stats_time = time.time() - start_time print(f" Skill statistics: {len(skill_stats)} categories in {stats_time:.3f}s") # Test 3: Location-based filtering start_time = time.time() location_jobs = Location.objects.annotate( job_count=models.Count('jobpost'), company_count=models.Count('jobpost__company', distinct=True) ).filter(job_count__gt=0).order_by('-job_count')[:15] location_time = time.time() - start_time print(f" Location analysis: {len(location_jobs)} locations in {location_time:.3f}s") performance_test_queries() # Auto Scaling Group deployment statistics final_stats = { 'deployment_info': { 'deployment_id': asg_deployment['deployment_id'], 'version': asg_deployment['version'], 'instances': asg_deployment['instances'], 'deployment_success': True }, 'data_volume': { 'total_skills': Skills.objects.count(), 'total_locations': Location.objects.count(), 'total_users': DjangoUser.objects.count(), 'total_authors': Author.objects.count(), 'total_jobs': JobPost.objects.count(), 'active_jobs': JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() }, 'performance_metrics': { 'database_connections': len(connections.all()), 'cache_status': 'enabled' if cache.get('test_key') is not None else 'configured', 'avg_response_time': '< 200ms', 'throughput': '1000+ req/min' } } print("\n=== Auto Scaling Group Final Statistics ===") print("Deployment Information:") for key, value in final_stats['deployment_info'].items(): print(f" {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print("\nData Volume:") for key, value in final_stats['data_volume'].items(): print(f" {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value:,}") print("\nPerformance Metrics:") for key, value in final_stats['performance_metrics'].items(): print(f" {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") print(f"\n=== Deployment {asg_deployment['deployment_id']} Complete ===") print(f"Auto Scaling Group successfully handles enterprise-scale Django application") print(f"All 5 models (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost) performing optimally") print(f"Health monitoring active, rolling updates successful") print(f"Current capacity: {asg_deployment['instances']['current_capacity']} instances") print("System ready for production traffic and automatic scaling")

    create-deployment-group

    Creates a new deployment group that defines the target infrastructure for your Django jobpost application deployments. A deployment group specifies the EC2 instances, Auto Scaling groups, or on-premises servers that will receive your application updates. This command establishes the target environment where Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models will be deployed, including load balancer configurations, instance tagging, and deployment monitoring settings for maintaining application availability during updates.

    Syntax
    aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  --deployment-group-name {deployment-group-name} \
  --service-role-arn {service-role-arn} \
  --ec2-tag-filters Key={key},Value={value},Type={type} \
  --auto-scaling-groups {auto-scaling-group-names} \
  --deployment-config-name {deployment-config} \
  --load-balancer-info {load-balancer-configuration} \
  --alarm-configuration enabled={boolean},alarms={alarm-list}
    Parameters Description
    • --application-name (string, required): Name of the CodeDeploy application that contains this deployment group. This application serves as the container for all Django jobpost deployments and manages the lifecycle of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models across your infrastructure environments.
    • --deployment-group-name (string, required): Unique name for the deployment group within the application. Use descriptive names that identify the environment and purpose, such as "production-web-servers" or "staging-django-cluster" for managing your jobpost application deployments.
    • --service-role-arn (string, required): ARN of the IAM service role that grants CodeDeploy permissions to access your AWS resources. This role must have policies allowing EC2 instance access, Auto Scaling group management, and load balancer operations for Django application deployments.
    • --ec2-tag-filters (list, optional): EC2 instance tag filters that identify target instances for deployment. Use tags like Environment=production, Application=jobpost, or Role=django-web to specify which instances should receive your Django application updates with model deployments.
    • --auto-scaling-groups (list, optional): Names of Auto Scaling groups that contain target instances for deployment. These groups provide automatic capacity management for your Django application, ensuring availability during traffic spikes while maintaining model data consistency.
    • --deployment-config-name (string, optional): Deployment configuration that controls rollout strategy. Options include CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime for minimal risk, CodeDeployDefault.HalfAtATime for balanced updates, or custom configurations for Django-specific deployment patterns with database migration coordination.
    • --load-balancer-info (structure, optional): Load balancer configuration for managing traffic during deployments. Specifies target groups, listeners, and health check settings to ensure zero-downtime updates for your Django jobpost application while maintaining user access to Skills and JobPost data.
    • --alarm-configuration (structure, optional): CloudWatch alarm settings for deployment monitoring and automatic rollback. Configure alarms for application health metrics, database connection errors, or custom Django model operation failures to trigger automatic deployment rollbacks when issues are detected.
    Attributes
    • deploymentGroupId: Unique identifier assigned to the deployment group for tracking and management operations
    • deploymentGroupName: Name of the deployment group as specified during creation
    • applicationName: CodeDeploy application name that contains this deployment group
    • serviceRoleArn: IAM service role ARN used by CodeDeploy for AWS resource access
    • targetRevision: Current application revision deployed to this group's target instances
    • ec2TagFilters: Tag filters used to identify target EC2 instances for Django deployments
    • autoScalingGroups: List of Auto Scaling groups associated with this deployment group
    • deploymentConfigName: Active deployment configuration controlling rollout strategy
    • loadBalancerInfo: Load balancer settings for traffic management during deployments
    • alarmConfiguration: CloudWatch alarm settings for deployment monitoring and rollback triggers
    • autoRollbackConfiguration: Automatic rollback settings for failure scenarios
    • lastSuccessfulDeployment: Information about the most recent successful deployment to this group
    Methods
    get-deployment-group

    Syntax: aws deploy get-deployment-group --application-name {app-name} --deployment-group-name {group-name}

    Parameters: application-name (string, required) - Name of CodeDeploy application; deployment-group-name (string, required) - Name of deployment group to retrieve configuration details, target instances, load balancer settings, and alarm configurations

    update-deployment-group

    Syntax: aws deploy update-deployment-group --application-name {app-name} --current-deployment-group-name {current-name} --deployment-config-name {config}

    Parameters: application-name (string, required) - Application containing the group; current-deployment-group-name (string, required) - Current group name; deployment-config-name (string, optional) - New deployment configuration; ec2-tag-filters (list, optional) - Updated instance targeting rules

    delete-deployment-group

    Syntax: aws deploy delete-deployment-group --application-name {app-name} --deployment-group-name {group-name}

    Parameters: application-name (string, required) - Application name; deployment-group-name (string, required) - Deployment group to delete; this removes the target configuration but does not affect deployed Django applications on target instances

    list-deployment-groups

    Syntax: aws deploy list-deployment-groups --application-name {app-name}

    Parameters: application-name (string, required) - List all deployment groups within the specified CodeDeploy application, showing group names and basic configuration summary for Django application deployment management

    Return Value
    • deploymentGroupId (string): Unique identifier for the created deployment group that can be used for monitoring, updates, and deployment operations targeting the Django jobpost application infrastructure
    5 Detailed CodeDeploy create-deployment-group Examples
    Example 1: Create Basic EC2 Deployment Group for Django Development Environment

    Create a deployment group targeting EC2 instances in the development environment for Django jobpost application. This group uses instance tags to identify target servers and configures basic deployment settings for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments with minimal downtime during development testing.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create development deployment group for Django EC2 instances
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-dev" \
  --deployment-group-name "dev-django-servers" \
  --service-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole" \
  --ec2-tag-filters Key=Environment,Value=development,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
                     Key=Application,Value=jobpost,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
                     Key=Framework,Value=django,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce" \
  --region us-east-1

# Verify deployment group creation
aws deploy get-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-dev" \
  --deployment-group-name "dev-django-servers"
    { "deploymentGroupId": "12345678-dev-group-abcd-ef1234567890" } # Deployment group verification { "deploymentGroupInfo": { "applicationName": "anihpj-jobpost-dev", "deploymentGroupId": "12345678-dev-group-abcd-ef1234567890", "deploymentGroupName": "dev-django-servers", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce", "serviceRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole", "ec2TagFilters": [ { "Key": "Environment", "Value": "development", "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE" }, { "Key": "Application", "Value": "jobpost", "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE" }, { "Key": "Framework", "Value": "django", "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE" } ], "targetInstances": { "tagFilters": [ { "Key": "Environment", "Value": "development" } ] } } }
    # Development deployment group verification with Django models setup from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import User as DjangoUser from django.conf import settings dev_deployment_group = { 'group_id': '12345678-dev-group-abcd-ef1234567890', 'group_name': 'dev-django-servers', 'environment': 'development', 'target_filters': { 'Environment': 'development', 'Application': 'jobpost', 'Framework': 'django' }, 'deployment_strategy': 'all_at_once' } print("=== Development Deployment Group Verification ===") # Prepare development environment data for deployment testing print("Setting up development test data...") # Create development skills for testing deployment dev_skills = [ {'name': 'Django Development', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'Web Development'}, {'name': 'Python Scripting', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'Programming'}, {'name': 'PostgreSQL', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'Database'}, {'name': 'Git Version Control', 'level': 'Advanced', 'category': 'Development Tools'}, {'name': 'Linux Administration', 'level': 'Intermediate', 'category': 'System Administration'} ] for skill_data in dev_skills: skill, created = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=skill_data['name'], defaults=skill_data ) print(f" {'Created' if created else 'Found'} skill: {skill.name} - {skill.level}") # Create development locations dev_locations = [ {'city': 'Development Lab', 'state': 'Virtual', 'country': 'Local', 'zip_code': '00000', 'is_remote': False}, {'city': 'Remote Dev', 'state': 'Distributed', 'country': 'Global', 'zip_code': '00001', 'is_remote': True} ] for loc_data in dev_locations: location, created = Location.objects.get_or_create( city=loc_data['city'], state=loc_data['state'], defaults=loc_data ) print(f" {'Created' if created else 'Found'} location: {location.city}, {location.country}") # Create development users for testing dev_users = [ {'username': 'dev_lead', 'email': 'lead@dev.anihpj.com', 'first_name': 'Dev', 'last_name': 'Lead'}, {'username': 'test_recruiter', 'email': 'recruiter@dev.anihpj.com', 'first_name': 'Test', 'last_name': 'Recruiter'} ] created_users = [] for user_data in dev_users: user, created = DjangoUser.objects.get_or_create( username=user_data['username'], defaults=user_data ) if created: user.set_password('dev_password_2026') user.save() created_users.append(user) print(f" Created user: {user.username} ({user.email})") # Create authors for development testing dev_location = Location.objects.filter(city='Development Lab').first() for user in created_users: author, created = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=user, defaults={ 'bio': f'Development team member: {user.first_name} {user.last_name}', 'company': 'AnihpjDev Corp', 'location': dev_location } ) if created: print(f" Created author: {author.user.username} -> {author.company}") # Create development job postings for deployment testing dev_jobs = [ { 'title': 'Junior Django Developer', 'description': 'Entry-level Django developer for development team', 'skills': ['Django Development', 'Python Scripting'], 'salary_range': (60000, 80000) }, { 'title': 'DevOps Engineer', 'description': 'System administration and deployment automation', 'skills': ['Linux Administration', 'Git Version Control'], 'salary_range': (70000, 90000) } ] for job_data in dev_jobs: author = Author.objects.filter(company='AnihpjDev Corp').first() if author: job = JobPost.objects.create( title=job_data['title'], description=job_data['description'], company='AnihpjDev Corp', location=dev_location, author=author, salary_min=job_data['salary_range'][0], salary_max=job_data['salary_range'][1], is_active=True ) # Add skills to job for skill_name in job_data['skills']: skill = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).first() if skill: job.skills.add(skill) print(f" Created job: {job.title}") print(f" Skills required: {[s.name for s in job.skills.all()]}") # Test deployment group readiness deployment_readiness = { 'models_ready': { 'skills': Skills.objects.count(), 'locations': Location.objects.count(), 'users': DjangoUser.objects.count(), 'authors': Author.objects.count(), 'jobs': JobPost.objects.count() }, 'environment_config': { 'debug_mode': getattr(settings, 'DEBUG', True), 'database_backend': settings.DATABASES['default']['ENGINE'], 'deployment_group': dev_deployment_group['group_name'], 'target_environment': dev_deployment_group['environment'] } } print("\n=== Deployment Group Readiness Check ===") print("Django Models Status:") for model, count in deployment_readiness['models_ready'].items(): print(f" {model.title()}: {count}") print("\nEnvironment Configuration:") for key, value in deployment_readiness['environment_config'].items(): print(f" {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") # Verify model relationships for deployment print("\n=== Model Relationship Verification ===") for job in JobPost.objects.all(): print(f"Job: {job.title}") print(f" Company: {job.company}") print(f" Location: {job.location.city}, {job.location.country}") print(f" Author: {job.author.user.username}") print(f" Skills: {', '.join([s.name for s in job.skills.all()])}") print(f" Salary: ${job.salary_min:,} - ${job.salary_max:,}") print(f"\nDeployment group '{dev_deployment_group['group_name']}' ready for Django deployments") print(f"Group ID: {dev_deployment_group['group_id']}") print("All models configured and relationships verified")
    Example 2: Create Production Auto Scaling Group Deployment with Load Balancer Integration

    Create a production deployment group using Auto Scaling Groups with Application Load Balancer integration for high-availability Django jobpost application deployment. This configuration ensures zero-downtime updates for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models while maintaining traffic distribution and automatic scaling based on application load.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create production deployment group with Auto Scaling and Load Balancer
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-production" \
  --deployment-group-name "prod-django-asg-cluster" \
  --service-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole" \
  --auto-scaling-groups "django-jobpost-asg-prod" "django-workers-asg-prod" \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenAllAtOnce" \
  --load-balancer-info 'targetGroupInfoList=[{name=django-jobpost-tg-prod}],elbInfoList=[]' \
  --alarm-configuration enabled=true,alarms='[{name=DjangoAppErrors},{name=DatabaseConnections},{name=ResponseTime}]' \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
  --blue-green-deployment-configuration 'terminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess={action=TERMINATE,terminationWaitTimeInMinutes=10},deploymentReadyOption={actionOnTimeout=CONTINUE_DEPLOYMENT},greenFleetProvisioningOption={action=COPY_AUTO_SCALING_GROUP}' \
  --region us-east-1

# Verify Auto Scaling Groups exist
aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --auto-scaling-group-names "django-jobpost-asg-prod" "django-workers-asg-prod"

# Verify Load Balancer Target Group
aws elbv2 describe-target-groups --names "django-jobpost-tg-prod"

# Get deployment group details
aws deploy get-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-production" \
  --deployment-group-name "prod-django-asg-cluster"
    { "deploymentGroupId": "87654321-prod-group-xyz9-0123456789ab" } # Auto Scaling Groups verification { "AutoScalingGroups": [ { "AutoScalingGroupName": "django-jobpost-asg-prod", "MinSize": 3, "MaxSize": 12, "DesiredCapacity": 6, "HealthCheckType": "ELB", "Instances": [ {"InstanceId": "i-prod001", "HealthStatus": "Healthy"}, {"InstanceId": "i-prod002", "HealthStatus": "Healthy"}, {"InstanceId": "i-prod003", "HealthStatus": "Healthy"} ] }, { "AutoScalingGroupName": "django-workers-asg-prod", "MinSize": 2, "MaxSize": 8, "DesiredCapacity": 4 } ] } # Load Balancer Target Group verification { "TargetGroups": [ { "TargetGroupName": "django-jobpost-tg-prod", "Protocol": "HTTP", "Port": 8000, "VpcId": "vpc-prod123", "HealthCheckPath": "/health/", "HealthCheckIntervalSeconds": 30, "HealthyThresholdCount": 3 } ] } # Final deployment group configuration { "deploymentGroupInfo": { "deploymentGroupName": "prod-django-asg-cluster", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenAllAtOnce", "autoScalingGroups": [ {"name": "django-jobpost-asg-prod"}, {"name": "django-workers-asg-prod"} ], "loadBalancerInfo": { "targetGroupInfoList": [ {"name": "django-jobpost-tg-prod"} ] }, "alarmConfiguration": { "enabled": true, "alarms": [ {"name": "DjangoAppErrors"}, {"name": "DatabaseConnections"}, {"name": "ResponseTime"} ] } } }

    Django Queryset Examples for Production Auto Scaling Environment

    # Production Django Models Setup for Auto Scaling Deployment Group
from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Create production environment skills
production_skills = [
    Skills.objects.create(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        category='Infrastructure',
        skill_type='production',
        skill_description='Auto Scaling Group Management for high-traffic Django applications with CodeDeploy integration',
        name='AWS Auto Scaling',
        proficiency_level='Expert',
        required=True,
        years_experience=5,
        certification_required=True,
        last_updated=datetime.now()
    ),
    Skills.objects.create(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        category='Load Balancing',
        skill_type='production',
        skill_description='Application Load Balancer configuration for Django applications with health check optimization',
        name='AWS ALB Management',
        proficiency_level='Advanced',
        required=True,
        years_experience=4,
        certification_required=True
    ),
    Skills.objects.create(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        category='Blue-Green Deployment',
        skill_type='production',
        skill_description='Zero-downtime deployment strategies for critical Django jobpost applications',
        name='Blue-Green Strategy',
        proficiency_level='Expert',
        required=True,
        years_experience=5
    )
]

# Create production environment users
production_users = [
    User.objects.create(
        username='prod-devops-lead',
        email='devops.lead@anihpj.com',
        first_name='Production',
        last_name='DevOps Lead',
        role='DevOps Engineer',
        department='Infrastructure',
        phone_number='+1-555-0101',
        location='Production Data Center',
        hire_date=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1800),
        is_active=True,

    ),

    )
]

# Find all auto scaling related skills

    name__icontains='Auto Scaling'
).order_by('proficiency_level')

for skill in auto_scaling_skills:

    Example 3: Create Blue-Green Deployment Group with Lambda Hooks and Custom Validation

    Create an advanced blue-green deployment group with Lambda hooks for custom validation logic and automated Django model migration checks. This configuration includes pre-deployment validation, post-deployment verification, and custom rollback triggers specifically designed for Django jobpost applications with complex database relationships between Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create blue-green deployment group with Lambda hooks for Django validation
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-bluegreen" \
  --deployment-group-name "bg-django-validation-cluster" \
  --service-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole" \
  --auto-scaling-groups "django-jobpost-bg-green" \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenAllAtOnceWithAutoScaling" \
  --blue-green-deployment-configuration 'terminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess={action=TERMINATE,terminationWaitTimeInMinutes=15},deploymentReadyOption={actionOnTimeout=STOP_DEPLOYMENT,waitTimeInMinutes=20},greenFleetProvisioningOption={action=COPY_AUTO_SCALING_GROUP}' \
  --load-balancer-info 'targetGroupInfoList=[{name=django-jobpost-bg-tg}]' \
  --trigger-configurations '[{"triggerName":"PreDeploymentHook","triggerTargetArn":"arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-pre-deployment-validation","triggerEvents":["DeploymentStart"]},{"triggerName":"PostDeploymentHook","triggerTargetArn":"arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-post-deployment-verification","triggerEvents":["DeploymentSuccess"]},{"triggerName":"FailureHook","triggerTargetArn":"arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-deployment-failure-handler","triggerEvents":["DeploymentFailure","DeploymentRollback"]}]' \
  --alarm-configuration enabled=true,ignorePollAlarmFailure=false,alarms='[{name=DjangoModelValidationErrors},{name=DatabaseMigrationFailures},{name=UserSessionErrors},{name=JobPostCreationErrors}]' \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_INSTANCE_FAILURE \
  --region us-east-1

# Create Lambda functions for Django validation hooks
aws lambda create-function \
  --function-name "django-pre-deployment-validation" \
  --runtime python3.11 \
  --role "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/LambdaExecutionRole" \
  --handler lambda_function.lambda_handler \
  --zip-file fileb://django-validation-hooks.zip \
  --description "Pre-deployment validation for Django models and database schema"

aws lambda create-function \
  --function-name "django-post-deployment-verification" \
  --runtime python3.11 \
  --role "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/LambdaExecutionRole" \
  --handler verification.verify_deployment \
  --zip-file fileb://django-verification-hooks.zip \
  --description "Post-deployment verification for Django application health and model operations"

# Get deployment group configuration
aws deploy get-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-bluegreen" \
  --deployment-group-name "bg-django-validation-cluster"
    { "deploymentGroupId": "bg-validation-456-xyz-789abc" } # Lambda functions created successfully { "FunctionName": "django-pre-deployment-validation", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-pre-deployment-validation", "Runtime": "python3.11", "Handler": "lambda_function.lambda_handler", "Role": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/LambdaExecutionRole", "State": "Active" } { "FunctionName": "django-post-deployment-verification", "FunctionArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-post-deployment-verification", "Runtime": "python3.11", "Handler": "verification.verify_deployment", "State": "Active" } # Blue-Green deployment group configuration { "deploymentGroupInfo": { "deploymentGroupName": "bg-django-validation-cluster", "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenAllAtOnceWithAutoScaling", "blueGreenDeploymentConfiguration": { "terminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess": { "action": "TERMINATE", "terminationWaitTimeInMinutes": 15 }, "deploymentReadyOption": { "actionOnTimeout": "STOP_DEPLOYMENT", "waitTimeInMinutes": 20 }, "greenFleetProvisioningOption": { "action": "COPY_AUTO_SCALING_GROUP" } }, "triggerConfigurations": [ { "triggerName": "PreDeploymentHook", "triggerTargetArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-pre-deployment-validation", "triggerEvents": ["DeploymentStart"] }, { "triggerName": "PostDeploymentHook", "triggerTargetArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-post-deployment-verification", "triggerEvents": ["DeploymentSuccess"] }, { "triggerName": "FailureHook", "triggerTargetArn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:django-deployment-failure-handler", "triggerEvents": ["DeploymentFailure", "DeploymentRollback"] } ], "alarmConfiguration": { "enabled": true, "ignorePollAlarmFailure": false, "alarms": [ {"name": "DjangoModelValidationErrors"}, {"name": "DatabaseMigrationFailures"}, {"name": "UserSessionErrors"}, {"name": "JobPostCreationErrors"} ] } } }

    Django Queryset Examples for Blue-Green Deployment with Lambda Validation

    # Blue-Green Django Models Setup with Lambda Validation Hooks

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from django.db.models import Q

# Create blue-green deployment skills with validation focus
bluegreen_skills = [

        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        category='Deployment Strategy',
        skill_type='blue-green',
        skill_description='Blue-Green deployment implementation with Lambda validation hooks for Django applications',

        certification_required=True,

        id=uuid.uuid4(),

]

# Create blue-green deployment environment users

]

# Create blue-green deployment authors

]

# Add skills to job posts (Many-to-Many relationship)
for job in bluegreen_jobposts:
    job.required_skills.add(*bluegreen_skills)

print("Blue-Green deployment group with Lambda validation configured successfully")
    Example 4: Create Multi-Region Deployment Group with Cross-Region Replication

    Create a deployment group that spans multiple AWS regions for Django jobpost application disaster recovery and load distribution. This configuration includes cross-region deployment coordination, database replication validation, and region-specific rollback capabilities while maintaining data consistency across Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models in multiple geographic locations.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create multi-region deployment group for Django disaster recovery
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-multiregion" \
  --deployment-group-name "multiregion-django-cluster" \
  --service-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployMultiRegionRole" \
  --ec2-tag-filters Key=Environment,Value=production,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
                     Key=Region,Value=primary,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
                     Key=Application,Value=jobpost-dr,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATimeMultiRegion" \
  --trigger-configurations '[{"triggerName":"CrossRegionSync","triggerTargetArn":"arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:cross-region-sync-handler","triggerEvents":["DeploymentStart","DeploymentSuccess"]}]' \
  --alarm-configuration enabled=true,alarms='[{name=CrossRegionLatency},{name=DatabaseReplicationLag},{name=RegionHealthCheck}]' \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
  --region us-east-1

# Create secondary region deployment group  
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-multiregion" \
  --deployment-group-name "multiregion-django-cluster-west" \
  --service-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployMultiRegionRole" \
  --ec2-tag-filters Key=Environment,Value=production,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
                     Key=Region,Value=secondary,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
  --deployment-config-name "CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATimeMultiRegion" \
  --region us-west-2

# Verify cross-region setup
aws deploy list-deployment-groups --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-multiregion" --region us-east-1
aws deploy list-deployment-groups --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-multiregion" --region us-west-2
    { "deploymentGroupId": "multiregion-789-primary-abc-def123" } # Secondary region deployment group { "deploymentGroupId": "multiregion-789-secondary-ghi-jkl456" } # Primary region deployment groups { "deploymentGroups": [ "multiregion-django-cluster" ] } # Secondary region deployment groups { "deploymentGroups": [ "multiregion-django-cluster-west" ] }
    Example 5: Create Staging Deployment Group with Automated Testing Integration

    Create a comprehensive staging deployment group with integrated automated testing, performance benchmarking, and Django model validation. This configuration includes test automation triggers, performance regression detection, and comprehensive validation of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations before promoting deployments to production environments.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create staging deployment group with automated testing integration
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
  --application-name "anihpj-jobpost-staging" \
  --deployment-group-name "staging-django-test-cluster" \
  --service-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployTestingRole" \
  --ec2-tag-filters Key=Environment,Value=staging,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
                     Key=Testing,Value=automated,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
