        'web_server': 'healthy',
        'database_connection': 'healthy',
        'cache_connection': 'healthy',
        'static_files': 'accessible'
    }
    
    # Check infrastructure services
    health_verification['infrastructure_checks'] = {
        'nginx': 'running',
        'gunicorn': 'running',
        'supervisord': 'running',
        'system_resources': 'normal'
    }
    
    return health_verification

def update_development_documentation(cleanup_config: Dict):
    """Update development documentation"""
    
    documentation_updates = {
        'documentation_updated': [],
        'runbooks_revised': [],
        'team_wikis_updated': []
    }
    
    print("Updating development documentation...")
    
    documentation_updates['documentation_updated'] = [
        'Development Environment Setup Guide',
        'Experimental Features Documentation',
        'Cleanup Procedures Documentation',
        'Environment Tagging Standards'
    ]
    
    documentation_updates['runbooks_revised'] = [
        'Development Environment Maintenance',
        'Feature Flag Management',
        'Temporary Tag Cleanup Procedures'
    ]
    
    documentation_updates['team_wikis_updated'] = [
        'Frontend Team Development Wiki',
        'Data Science Team Experiment Tracking',
        'QA Team Testing Environment Guide',
        'Release Team Staging Procedures'
    ]
    
    print(f"✓ Updated {len(documentation_updates['documentation_updated'])} documentation items")
    return documentation_updates

def notify_development_teams(cleanup_config: Dict, removal_results: Dict):
    """Notify development teams of cleanup results"""
    
    team_notifications = {
        'notifications_sent': [],
        'team_summaries': {},
        'action_items': {}
    }
    
    print("Notifying development teams...")
    
    # Group results by team
    teams = set(env.development_team for env in cleanup_config['target_environments'].values())
    
    for team in teams:
        team_instances = [
            instance for instance, env in cleanup_config['target_environments'].items()
            if env.development_team == team
        ]
        
        team_results = {
            instance: removal_results.get(instance, {})
            for instance in team_instances
        }
        
        team_notifications['team_summaries'][team] = {
            'affected_instances': team_instances,
            'successful_cleanups': len([r for r in team_results.values() if r.get('success', False)]),
            'failed_cleanups': len([r for r in team_results.values() if not r.get('success', False)]),
            'preserved_experiments': len([r for r in team_results.values() if r.get('reason') == 'preservation_requirements_not_met'])
        }
        
        # Create action items for each team
        team_notifications['action_items'][team] = generate_team_action_items(team, team_results, cleanup_config)
        team_notifications['notifications_sent'].append(f"{team} cleanup summary")
    
    print(f"✓ Notifications sent to {len(teams)} development teams")
    return team_notifications

def generate_team_action_items(team: str, team_results: Dict, cleanup_config: Dict):
    """Generate action items for development team"""
    
    action_items = []
    
    # Check for failed cleanups
    failed_instances = [
        instance for instance, result in team_results.items()
        if not result.get('success', False)
    ]
    
    if failed_instances:
        action_items.append(f"Review cleanup failures for: {', '.join(failed_instances)}")
    
    # Check for preserved experiments
    preserved_instances = [
        instance for instance, result in team_results.items()
        if result.get('reason') == 'preservation_requirements_not_met'
    ]
    
    if preserved_instances:
        action_items.append(f"Plan future cleanup for preserved instances: {', '.join(preserved_instances)}")
    
    # General recommendations
    action_items.extend([
        "Review temporary tag usage policies",
        "Update feature flag cleanup procedures",
        "Document active experiment timelines"
    ])
    
    return action_items

def setup_post_cleanup_monitoring(cleanup_config: Dict):
    """Setup monitoring after cleanup"""
    
    monitoring_setup = {
        'monitors_configured': [],
        'alerts_created': [],
        'dashboards_updated': []
    }
    
    print("Setting up post-cleanup monitoring...")
    
    monitoring_setup['monitors_configured'] = [
        'Development environment health monitoring',
        'Preserved experiment functionality monitoring',
        'Tag compliance monitoring',
        'Development team activity monitoring'
    ]
    
    monitoring_setup['alerts_created'] = [
        'Unauthorized temporary tag additions',
        'Experimental feature failures',
        'Development environment degradation'
    ]
    
    monitoring_setup['dashboards_updated'] = [
        'Development Environment Overview',
        'Experimental Features Status',
        'Tag Lifecycle Management',
        'Team Development Activity'
    ]
    
    print(f"✓ Monitoring configured for {len(monitoring_setup['monitors_configured'])} areas")
    return monitoring_setup

# Execute temporary tag removal for development cleanup
if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = remove_temporary_tags_dev_cleanup()
    if success:
        print("🎉 Development environment cleanup completed successfully")
    else:
        print("❌ Development environment cleanup failed or partially completed")

    stop-deployment

    Attempts to stop an ongoing deployment to a compute platform for an application. This command allows you to halt deployments that are in progress, which is useful for preventing the rollout of problematic releases or responding to critical issues discovered during deployment.

    Syntax

    aws deploy stop-deployment
--deployment-id <value>
[--auto-rollback-enabled | --no-auto-rollback-enabled]
[--cli-input-json <value>]
[--generate-cli-skeleton <value>]

    Parameters

    --deployment-id (string)

    The unique ID of a deployment to stop.

    --auto-rollback-enabled | --no-auto-rollback-enabled (boolean)

    Indicates whether to automatically roll back the deployment if it fails to stop.

    --cli-input-json (string)

    Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided.

    --generate-cli-skeleton (string)

    Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request.

    Attributes

    • deployment-id: Required parameter that specifies which deployment to stop
    • auto-rollback-enabled: Optional parameter to enable automatic rollback on stop failure
    • synchronous-operation: Command waits for stop operation to complete
    • emergency-stop: Can be used as emergency brake for problematic deployments

    Methods

    • Deployment Halt: Immediately stops the deployment process
    • Instance State Management: Manages instance states during stop operation
    • Rollback Coordination: Coordinates automatic rollback if enabled
    • Status Notification: Notifies about deployment stop status

    Return Value

    Returns information about the stopped deployment including:

    • deploymentId: The ID of the stopped deployment
    • stopStatus: Status of the stop operation (Pending, Succeeded, Failed)
    • stopMessage: Additional information about the stop operation
    • rollbackInfo: Information about automatic rollback if triggered

    Examples

    Example 1: Emergency Stop of Django Production Deployment

    Stop a Django application deployment that's causing critical production issues.

    #!/bin/bash
# Emergency stop of Django production deployment
aws deploy stop-deployment \
  --deployment-id d-A1B2C3D4E \
  --auto-rollback-enabled \
  --region us-east-1
    { "deploymentId": "d-A1B2C3D4E", "stopStatus": "Succeeded" }
    Django Integration:

    Emergency deployment stop with comprehensive incident response:

    # Emergency deployment stop for Django production issues
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict, Tuple, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass
import json
import logging

@dataclass
class IncidentContext:
    """Production incident context"""
    incident_id: str
    severity_level: str
    affected_services: List[str]
    error_indicators: List[str]
    impact_assessment: str
    stakeholders_notified: List[str]
    emergency_contacts: List[str]

@dataclass
class DeploymentStopContext:
    """Deployment stop context"""
    deployment_id: str
    application_name: str
    deployment_group: str
    deployment_start_time: datetime
    instances_affected: List[str]
    rollback_enabled: bool
    stop_reason: str

def emergency_stop_django_deployment():
    """Emergency stop of Django production deployment with incident response"""
    
    # Emergency stop configuration
    emergency_config = {
        'incident': IncidentContext(
            incident_id='INC-2026-0115-001',
            severity_level='critical',
            affected_services=['jobpost_web', 'jobpost_api', 'user_authentication'],
            error_indicators=[
                'database_connection_timeouts',
                'memory_leak_detected',
                'response_time_degradation',
                '500_error_rate_spike'
            ],
            impact_assessment='production_user_experience_severely_degraded',
            stakeholders_notified=['ops_team', 'dev_team', 'product_management'],
            emergency_contacts=['on_call_engineer', 'site_reliability_lead', 'cto']
        ),
        'deployment_stop': DeploymentStopContext(
            deployment_id='d-A1B2C3D4E',
            application_name='jobpost-web-app',
            deployment_group='production-web-servers',
            deployment_start_time=datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=15),
            instances_affected=[
                'jobpost-web-01', 'jobpost-web-02', 'jobpost-web-03',
                'jobpost-api-01', 'jobpost-api-02'
            ],
            rollback_enabled=True,
            stop_reason='critical_production_issues_detected'
        ),
        'emergency_procedures': {
            'immediate_actions_required': True,
            'stakeholder_communication': True,
            'incident_documentation': True,
            'rollback_preparation': True,
            'health_monitoring': True
        },
        'production_safeguards': {
            'traffic_management': True,
            'database_protection': True,
            'user_session_preservation': True,
            'data_integrity_verification': True
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🚨 EMERGENCY: Stopping Django production deployment")
        print(f"Incident ID: {emergency_config['incident'].incident_id}")
        print(f"Deployment ID: {emergency_config['deployment_stop'].deployment_id}")
        print(f"Severity: {emergency_config['incident'].severity_level.upper()}")
        
        # Immediate incident response initialization
        incident_response = initialize_emergency_response(emergency_config)
        if not incident_response['response_initialized']:
            print("❌ Emergency response initialization failed")
            return False
        
        # Validate emergency stop prerequisites
        stop_validation = validate_emergency_stop_prerequisites(emergency_config)
        if not stop_validation['stop_validated']:
            print("❌ Emergency stop validation failed")
            return False
        
        # Implement production safeguards
        safeguards = implement_production_safeguards(emergency_config)
        
        # Execute emergency deployment stop
        stop_result = execute_emergency_deployment_stop(emergency_config)
        if not stop_result['stop_successful']:
            print("❌ Emergency deployment stop failed")
            return False
        
        # Coordinate automatic rollback if needed
        rollback_coordination = coordinate_emergency_rollback(emergency_config, stop_result)
        
        # Monitor post-stop system health
        health_monitoring = monitor_post_stop_health(emergency_config)
        
        # Document incident and actions taken
        incident_documentation = document_emergency_incident(emergency_config, stop_result)
        
        # Communicate with stakeholders
        stakeholder_communication = communicate_emergency_status(emergency_config, stop_result)
        
        # Prepare recovery procedures
        recovery_preparation = prepare_recovery_procedures(emergency_config)
        
        if (stop_result['stop_successful'] and 
            health_monitoring['system_stabilized'] and
            incident_documentation['documentation_complete']):
            print(f"✅ Emergency deployment stop completed successfully")
            return True
        else:
            print(f"⚠️ Emergency stop completed with issues requiring attention")
            return False
            
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"🚨 CRITICAL: Emergency deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

def initialize_emergency_response(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Initialize emergency response procedures"""
    
    incident_response = {
        'response_initialized': False,
        'incident_created': False,
        'war_room_established': False,
        'emergency_contacts_notified': False,
        'communication_channels_activated': False
    }
    
    print("🚨 Initializing emergency response...")
    
    try:
        # Create incident record
        incident_creation = create_emergency_incident_record(emergency_config)
        incident_response['incident_created'] = incident_creation['incident_created']
        
        # Establish war room
        war_room_setup = establish_emergency_war_room(emergency_config)
        incident_response['war_room_established'] = war_room_setup['war_room_active']
        
        # Notify emergency contacts
        emergency_notifications = notify_emergency_contacts(emergency_config)
        incident_response['emergency_contacts_notified'] = emergency_notifications['contacts_notified']
        
        # Activate communication channels
        communication_setup = activate_emergency_communication(emergency_config)
        incident_response['communication_channels_activated'] = communication_setup['channels_active']
        
        # Overall response initialization
        incident_response['response_initialized'] = all([
            incident_response['incident_created'],
            incident_response['war_room_established'],
            incident_response['emergency_contacts_notified'],
            incident_response['communication_channels_activated']
        ])
        
        print(f"Emergency Response Status:")
        print(f"  Incident Created: {'✅' if incident_response['incident_created'] else '❌'}")
        print(f"  War Room Active: {'✅' if incident_response['war_room_established'] else '❌'}")
        print(f"  Contacts Notified: {'✅' if incident_response['emergency_contacts_notified'] else '❌'}")
        print(f"  Communications Active: {'✅' if incident_response['communication_channels_activated'] else '❌'}")
        
        return incident_response
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Emergency response initialization failed: {str(e)}")
        incident_response['response_initialized'] = False
        return incident_response

def create_emergency_incident_record(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Create emergency incident record"""
    
    incident = emergency_config['incident']
    
    incident_record = {
        'incident_id': incident.incident_id,
        'creation_timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
        'severity': incident.severity_level,
        'title': 'Critical Production Deployment Issue',
        'description': f"Emergency deployment stop required due to {incident.impact_assessment}",
        'affected_services': incident.affected_services,
        'error_indicators': incident.error_indicators,
        'incident_commander': 'on_call_engineer',
        'status': 'active'
    }
    
    print(f"  📋 Incident record created: {incident_record['incident_id']}")
    return {'incident_created': True, 'incident_record': incident_record}

def establish_emergency_war_room(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Establish emergency war room"""
    
    war_room_config = {
        'room_id': f"war-room-{emergency_config['incident'].incident_id}",
        'video_conference_url': f"https://meet.anihpj.com/emergency/{emergency_config['incident'].incident_id}",
        'slack_channel': f"#incident-{emergency_config['incident'].incident_id}",
        'shared_documents': f"https://docs.anihpj.com/incidents/{emergency_config['incident'].incident_id}",
        'monitoring_dashboard': f"https://monitoring.anihpj.com/incidents/{emergency_config['incident'].incident_id}"
    }
    
    print(f"  🏢 War room established: {war_room_config['room_id']}")
    return {'war_room_active': True, 'war_room_config': war_room_config}

def notify_emergency_contacts(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Notify emergency contacts"""
    
    incident = emergency_config['incident']
    
    notification_results = {
        'contacts_notified': True,
        'notification_methods': ['sms', 'phone_call', 'slack', 'email'],
        'notification_timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
        'escalation_triggered': True
    }
    
    # Simulate emergency notifications
    for contact in incident.emergency_contacts:
        print(f"  📱 Emergency notification sent to: {contact}")
    
    for stakeholder in incident.stakeholders_notified:
        print(f"  📧 Stakeholder notification sent to: {stakeholder}")
    
    return notification_results

def activate_emergency_communication(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Activate emergency communication channels"""
    
    communication_channels = {
        'channels_active': True,
        'active_channels': [
            'incident_slack_channel',
            'emergency_conference_bridge',
            'sms_notification_system',
            'status_page_updates'
        ],
        'communication_protocols': [
            'regular_status_updates',
            'escalation_procedures',
            'stakeholder_briefings'
        ]
    }
    
    print(f"  📢 Emergency communication channels activated")
    return communication_channels

def validate_emergency_stop_prerequisites(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Validate emergency stop prerequisites"""
    
    stop_validation = {
        'stop_validated': False,
        'validation_checks': {},
        'critical_issues': [],
        'warnings': []
    }
    
    print("🔍 Validating emergency stop prerequisites...")
    
    # Check deployment status
    deployment_status_check = check_deployment_status(emergency_config)
    stop_validation['validation_checks']['deployment_status'] = deployment_status_check
    
    # Check system health indicators
    health_indicators_check = check_system_health_indicators(emergency_config)
    stop_validation['validation_checks']['health_indicators'] = health_indicators_check
    
    # Check rollback readiness
    rollback_readiness_check = check_rollback_readiness(emergency_config)
    stop_validation['validation_checks']['rollback_readiness'] = rollback_readiness_check
    
    # Check traffic management readiness
    traffic_management_check = check_traffic_management_readiness(emergency_config)
    stop_validation['validation_checks']['traffic_management'] = traffic_management_check
    
    # Collect critical issues and warnings
    for check_name, check_result in stop_validation['validation_checks'].items():
        if not check_result['passed']:
            if check_result['severity'] == 'critical':
                stop_validation['critical_issues'].append(f"{check_name}: {check_result['message']}")
            else:
                stop_validation['warnings'].append(f"{check_name}: {check_result['message']}")
    
    # Validation passes if no critical issues
    stop_validation['stop_validated'] = len(stop_validation['critical_issues']) == 0
    
    print(f"Emergency Stop Validation:")
    print(f"  Validation Passed: {'✅' if stop_validation['stop_validated'] else '❌'}")
    print(f"  Critical Issues: {len(stop_validation['critical_issues'])}")
    print(f"  Warnings: {len(stop_validation['warnings'])}")
    
    for issue in stop_validation['critical_issues']:
        print(f"    🚨 {issue}")
    
    return stop_validation

def check_deployment_status(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Check current deployment status"""
    
    deployment_stop = emergency_config['deployment_stop']
    
    # Simulate deployment status check
    return {
        'passed': True,
        'severity': 'info',
        'message': f"Deployment {deployment_stop.deployment_id} is in progress and can be stopped",
        'deployment_state': 'in_progress',
        'instances_affected': len(deployment_stop.instances_affected)
    }

def check_system_health_indicators(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Check system health indicators"""
    
    incident = emergency_config['incident']
    
    # Check if health indicators confirm critical issues
    critical_indicators = ['database_connection_timeouts', 'memory_leak_detected', '500_error_rate_spike']
    detected_critical = [indicator for indicator in incident.error_indicators if indicator in critical_indicators]
    
    return {
        'passed': len(detected_critical) > 0,  # Pass if critical issues detected (justifies emergency stop)
        'severity': 'critical' if len(detected_critical) == 0 else 'info',
        'message': f"Critical health indicators detected: {', '.join(detected_critical)}",
        'indicators_detected': len(detected_critical),
        'severity_justified': len(detected_critical) > 0
    }

def check_rollback_readiness(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Check rollback readiness"""
    
    deployment_stop = emergency_config['deployment_stop']
    
    return {
        'passed': deployment_stop.rollback_enabled,
        'severity': 'critical' if not deployment_stop.rollback_enabled else 'info',
        'message': 'Automatic rollback enabled' if deployment_stop.rollback_enabled else 'Automatic rollback not configured',
        'rollback_available': deployment_stop.rollback_enabled
    }

def check_traffic_management_readiness(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Check traffic management readiness"""
    
    return {
        'passed': emergency_config['production_safeguards']['traffic_management'],
        'severity': 'warning',
        'message': 'Traffic management systems ready for emergency stop',
        'load_balancer_ready': True,
        'dns_failover_ready': True
    }

def implement_production_safeguards(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Implement production safeguards during emergency stop"""
    
    safeguards = {
        'safeguards_implemented': [],
        'traffic_management_active': False,
        'database_protection_active': False,
        'session_preservation_active': False,
        'data_integrity_monitoring_active': False
    }
    
    print("🛡️ Implementing production safeguards...")
    
    # Implement traffic management
    traffic_safeguards = implement_traffic_management_safeguards(emergency_config)
    safeguards['traffic_management_active'] = traffic_safeguards['safeguards_active']
    safeguards['safeguards_implemented'].extend(traffic_safeguards['safeguards_list'])
    
    # Implement database protection
    database_safeguards = implement_database_protection_safeguards(emergency_config)
    safeguards['database_protection_active'] = database_safeguards['protection_active']
    safeguards['safeguards_implemented'].extend(database_safeguards['protection_list'])
    
    # Implement session preservation
    session_safeguards = implement_session_preservation_safeguards(emergency_config)
    safeguards['session_preservation_active'] = session_safeguards['preservation_active']
    safeguards['safeguards_implemented'].extend(session_safeguards['preservation_list'])
    
    # Implement data integrity monitoring
    integrity_safeguards = implement_data_integrity_monitoring(emergency_config)
    safeguards['data_integrity_monitoring_active'] = integrity_safeguards['monitoring_active']
    safeguards['safeguards_implemented'].extend(integrity_safeguards['monitoring_list'])
    
    print(f"Production Safeguards:")
    for safeguard in safeguards['safeguards_implemented']:
        print(f"  🛡️ {safeguard}")
    
    return safeguards

def implement_traffic_management_safeguards(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Implement traffic management safeguards"""
    
    traffic_safeguards = {
        'safeguards_active': True,
        'safeguards_list': [
            'Load balancer health check frequency increased',
            'Traffic routing rules updated for emergency mode',
            'CDN cache invalidation prepared',
            'DNS failover mechanisms activated'
        ]
    }
    
    return traffic_safeguards

def implement_database_protection_safeguards(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Implement database protection safeguards"""
    
    database_safeguards = {
        'protection_active': True,
        'protection_list': [
            'Database connection pooling limits enforced',
            'Read replica failover prepared',
            'Database backup verification completed',
            'Transaction logging enhanced for emergency period'
        ]
    }
    
    return database_safeguards

def implement_session_preservation_safeguards(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Implement user session preservation safeguards"""
    
    session_safeguards = {
        'preservation_active': True,
        'preservation_list': [
            'Redis session store backup initiated',
            'Session replication to backup systems enabled',
            'User authentication state preservation activated',
            'Session timeout extended for emergency period'
        ]
    }
    
    return session_safeguards

def implement_data_integrity_monitoring(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Implement data integrity monitoring"""
    
    integrity_safeguards = {
        'monitoring_active': True,
        'monitoring_list': [
            'Data consistency checks activated',
            'Transaction rollback monitoring enhanced',
            'Database constraint violation alerts enabled',
            'Data corruption detection systems activated'
        ]
    }
    
    return integrity_safeguards

def execute_emergency_deployment_stop(emergency_config: Dict):
    """Execute emergency deployment stop"""
    
    deployment_stop = emergency_config['deployment_stop']
    
    stop_result = {
        'stop_successful': False,
        'stop_timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
        'deployment_id': deployment_stop.deployment_id,
        'stop_status': None,
        'affected_instances': [],
        'rollback_triggered': False
    }
    
    print(f"⛔ Executing emergency deployment stop...")
    
    try:
        # Execute the deployment stop
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.stop_deployment(
            deploymentId=deployment_stop.deployment_id,
            autoRollbackEnabled=deployment_stop.rollback_enabled
        )
        
        stop_result.update({
            'stop_successful': True,
            'stop_status': 'Succeeded',
            'affected_instances': deployment_stop.instances_affected,
            'rollback_triggered': deployment_stop.rollback_enabled
        })
        
        print(f"✅ Emergency deployment stop executed successfully")
        print(f"  Deployment ID: {stop_result['deployment_id']}")
        print(f"  Stop Status: {stop_result['stop_status']}")
        print(f"  Affected Instances: {len(stop_result['affected_instances'])}")
        print(f"  Rollback Enabled: {'Yes' if stop_result['rollback_triggered'] else 'No'}")
        
        return stop_result
        
    except Exception as e:
        stop_result.update({
            'stop_successful': False,
            'stop_status': 'Failed',
            'error': str(e)
        })
        
        print(f"❌ Emergency deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return stop_result

def coordinate_emergency_rollback(emergency_config: Dict, stop_result: Dict):
    """Coordinate emergency rollback if needed"""
    
    rollback_coordination = {
        'rollback_coordinated': False,
        'rollback_initiated': False,
        'rollback_monitoring_active': False,
        'rollback_status': 'not_required'
    }
    
    if stop_result['rollback_triggered']:
        print("🔄 Coordinating emergency rollback...")
        
        # Monitor rollback progress
        rollback_monitoring = monitor_rollback_progress(emergency_config, stop_result)
        rollback_coordination['rollback_monitoring_active'] = rollback_monitoring['monitoring_active']
        
        # Coordinate rollback validation
        rollback_validation = validate_rollback_execution(emergency_config, stop_result)
        rollback_coordination['rollback_initiated'] = rollback_validation['rollback_successful']
        
        rollback_coordination['rollback_coordinated'] = (
            rollback_coordination['rollback_monitoring_active'] and
            rollback_coordination['rollback_initiated']
        )
        
        rollback_coordination['rollback_status'] = 'in_progress' if rollback_coordination['rollback_coordinated'] else 'failed'
        
        print(f"Rollback Coordination:")
        print(f"  Rollback Initiated: {'✅' if rollback_coordination['rollback_initiated'] else '❌'}")
        print(f"  Monitoring Active: {'✅' if rollback_coordination['rollback_monitoring_active'] else '❌'}")
    else:
        print("ℹ️ No automatic rollback configured")
        rollback_coordination['rollback_status'] = 'disabled'
    
    return rollback_coordination

def monitor_rollback_progress(emergency_config: Dict, stop_result: Dict):
    """Monitor rollback progress"""
    
    rollback_monitoring = {
        'monitoring_active': True,
        'rollback_progress': 0,
        'instances_rolled_back': [],
        'rollback_eta': datetime.now() + timedelta(minutes=10)
    }
    
    # Simulate rollback monitoring
    deployment_stop = emergency_config['deployment_stop']
    
    for instance in deployment_stop.instances_affected:
        rollback_monitoring['instances_rolled_back'].append({
            'instance': instance,
            'rollback_status': 'in_progress',
            'estimated_completion': datetime.now() + timedelta(minutes=5)
        })
    
    print(f"  🔄 Monitoring rollback for {len(rollback_monitoring['instances_rolled_back'])} instances")
    return rollback_monitoring

def validate_rollback_execution(emergency_config: Dict, stop_result: Dict):
    """Validate rollback execution"""
    
    rollback_validation = {
        'rollback_successful': True,
        'validation_checks': {
            'instance_health': 'passed',
            'service_availability': 'passed',
            'data_consistency': 'passed',
            'user_access': 'passed'
        }
    }
    
    print(f"  ✅ Rollback validation checks passed")
    return rollback_validation

# Continue with the rest of the emergency deployment stop implementation...

# Execute emergency deployment stop
if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = emergency_stop_django_deployment()
    if success:
        print("🎉 Emergency deployment stop completed successfully")
    else:
        print("❌ Emergency deployment stop failed")
    Example 2: Stop Deployment for Django Security Patch Rollback

    Stop a deployment when security patches cause unexpected system behavior requiring investigation.

    #!/bin/bash
# Stop deployment for Django security patch rollback
aws deploy stop-deployment \
  --deployment-id d-B2C3D4E5F \
  --no-auto-rollback-enabled \
  --region us-east-1
    { "deploymentId": "d-B2C3D4E5F", "stopStatus": "Succeeded" }
    Django Integration:

    Security patch deployment stop with security analysis:

    # Security patch deployment stop for Django security analysis
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict, Tuple, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass 
class SecurityPatchContext:
    """Security patch deployment context"""
    patch_id: str
    security_bulletins: List[str]
    affected_components: List[str]
    patch_severity: str
    deployment_urgency: str
    testing_completed: bool
    regression_detected: bool

def stop_security_patch_deployment():
    """Stop security patch deployment for investigation and analysis"""
    
    # Security patch stop configuration
    patch_config = {
        'security_patch': SecurityPatchContext(
            patch_id='SEC-2026-001',
            security_bulletins=['CVE-2026-0123', 'CVE-2026-0124'],
            affected_components=['django_auth', 'session_management', 'user_permissions'],
            patch_severity='high',
            deployment_urgency='immediate',
            testing_completed=True,
            regression_detected=True
        ),
        'deployment_context': {
            'deployment_id': 'd-B2C3D4E5F',
            'application_name': 'jobpost-security-patches',
            'deployment_group': 'production-security-group',
            'instances_affected': [
                'jobpost-web-01', 'jobpost-web-02', 'jobpost-web-03',
                'jobpost-api-01', 'jobpost-api-02', 'jobpost-auth-01'
            ],
            'rollback_disabled_reason': 'security_analysis_required'
        },
        'regression_indicators': {
            'authentication_failures': 'increased_15_percent',
            'session_corruption': 'detected_multiple_instances',
            'permission_escalation': 'potential_vulnerability',
            'performance_degradation': 'response_time_increased_30_percent'
        },
        'security_analysis_requirements': {
            'vulnerability_assessment': True,
            'patch_validation': True,
            'regression_analysis': True,
            'security_team_review': True
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔒 Stopping security patch deployment for analysis")
        print(f"Patch ID: {patch_config['security_patch'].patch_id}")
        print(f"Deployment ID: {patch_config['deployment_context']['deployment_id']}")
        print(f"Security Bulletins: {', '.join(patch_config['security_patch'].security_bulletins)}")
        
        # Validate security stop requirements
        security_validation = validate_security_stop_requirements(patch_config)
        if not security_validation['stop_validated']:
            print("❌ Security stop requirements not met")
            return False
        
        # Analyze regression indicators
        regression_analysis = analyze_security_regression_indicators(patch_config)
        
        # Execute security deployment stop
        stop_result = execute_security_deployment_stop(patch_config)
        if not stop_result['stop_successful']:
            print("❌ Security deployment stop failed")
            return False
        
        # Initiate security analysis procedures
        security_analysis = initiate_security_analysis_procedures(patch_config)
        
        # Preserve security evidence
        evidence_preservation = preserve_security_evidence(patch_config, stop_result)
        
        # Setup security monitoring
        security_monitoring = setup_enhanced_security_monitoring(patch_config)
        
        # Document security findings
        security_documentation = document_security_findings(patch_config, regression_analysis)
        
        # Coordinate with security team
        security_coordination = coordinate_security_team_response(patch_config)
        
        if (stop_result['stop_successful'] and 
            security_analysis['analysis_initiated'] and
            evidence_preservation['evidence_preserved']):
            print(f"✅ Security patch deployment stopped successfully for analysis")
            return True
        else:
            print(f"⚠️ Security deployment stop completed with analysis issues")
            return False
            
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Security deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

def validate_security_stop_requirements(patch_config: Dict):
    """Validate security deployment stop requirements"""
    
    security_validation = {
        'stop_validated': False,
        'security_checks': {},
        'critical_security_issues': [],
        'security_warnings': []
    }
    
    print("🔍 Validating security stop requirements...")
    
    # Check regression detection
    regression_check = validate_regression_detection(patch_config)
    security_validation['security_checks']['regression_detection'] = regression_check
    
    # Check security team availability
    security_team_check = validate_security_team_availability(patch_config)
    security_validation['security_checks']['security_team'] = security_team_check
    
    # Check analysis environment readiness
    analysis_env_check = validate_analysis_environment_readiness(patch_config)
    security_validation['security_checks']['analysis_environment'] = analysis_env_check
    
    # Check evidence preservation capabilities
    evidence_check = validate_evidence_preservation_capabilities(patch_config)
    security_validation['security_checks']['evidence_preservation'] = evidence_check
    
    # Collect issues and warnings
    for check_name, check_result in security_validation['security_checks'].items():
        if not check_result['passed']:
            if check_result['severity'] == 'critical':
                security_validation['critical_security_issues'].append(f"{check_name}: {check_result['message']}")
            else:
                security_validation['security_warnings'].append(f"{check_name}: {check_result['message']}")
    
    security_validation['stop_validated'] = len(security_validation['critical_security_issues']) == 0
    
    print(f"Security Stop Validation:")
    print(f"  Validation Passed: {'✅' if security_validation['stop_validated'] else '❌'}")
    print(f"  Critical Issues: {len(security_validation['critical_security_issues'])}")
    
    return security_validation

def validate_regression_detection(patch_config: Dict):
    """Validate regression detection"""
    
    security_patch = patch_config['security_patch']
    
    return {
        'passed': security_patch.regression_detected,
        'severity': 'critical' if not security_patch.regression_detected else 'info',
        'message': 'Regression indicators detected, analysis required' if security_patch.regression_detected else 'No regression detected',
        'regression_indicators': patch_config['regression_indicators']
    }

def validate_security_team_availability(patch_config: Dict):
    """Validate security team availability"""
    
    return {
        'passed': True,
        'severity': 'info',
        'message': 'Security team available for analysis',
        'team_members_available': ['security_analyst_01', 'security_engineer_02', 'security_lead']
    }

def validate_analysis_environment_readiness(patch_config: Dict):
    """Validate analysis environment readiness"""
    
    return {
        'passed': True,
        'severity': 'info',
        'message': 'Security analysis environment ready',
        'analysis_tools_available': ['vulnerability_scanner', 'code_analysis', 'regression_testing']
    }

def validate_evidence_preservation_capabilities(patch_config: Dict):
    """Validate evidence preservation capabilities"""
    
    return {
        'passed': True,
        'severity': 'warning',
        'message': 'Evidence preservation systems ready',
        'preservation_systems': ['log_archival', 'configuration_snapshots', 'forensic_imaging']
    }

def analyze_security_regression_indicators(patch_config: Dict):
    """Analyze security regression indicators"""
    
    regression_analysis = {
        'analysis_completed': False,
        'indicator_assessments': {},
        'severity_analysis': {},
        'impact_analysis': {},
        'recommended_actions': []
    }
    
    print("🔍 Analyzing security regression indicators...")
    
    # Analyze each regression indicator
    for indicator, status in patch_config['regression_indicators'].items():
        indicator_assessment = assess_regression_indicator(indicator, status)
        regression_analysis['indicator_assessments'][indicator] = indicator_assessment
    
    # Perform severity analysis
    regression_analysis['severity_analysis'] = analyze_regression_severity(patch_config)
    
    # Perform impact analysis
    regression_analysis['impact_analysis'] = analyze_security_impact(patch_config)
    
    # Generate recommended actions
    regression_analysis['recommended_actions'] = generate_security_recommendations(patch_config, regression_analysis)
    
    regression_analysis['analysis_completed'] = True
    
    print(f"Regression Analysis Summary:")
    for indicator, assessment in regression_analysis['indicator_assessments'].items():
        print(f"  {indicator}: {assessment['risk_level']} risk")
    
    return regression_analysis

def assess_regression_indicator(indicator: str, status: str):
    """Assess individual regression indicator"""
    
    risk_mapping = {
        'increased_15_percent': 'medium',
        'detected_multiple_instances': 'high',
        'potential_vulnerability': 'critical',
        'response_time_increased_30_percent': 'medium'
    }
    
    return {
        'indicator': indicator,
        'status': status,
        'risk_level': risk_mapping.get(status, 'unknown'),
        'requires_investigation': True,
        'analysis_priority': 'high' if risk_mapping.get(status) in ['high', 'critical'] else 'medium'
    }

def analyze_regression_severity(patch_config: Dict):
    """Analyze overall regression severity"""
    
    high_risk_indicators = [
        indicator for indicator, status in patch_config['regression_indicators'].items()
        if status in ['potential_vulnerability', 'detected_multiple_instances']
    ]
    
    return {
        'overall_severity': 'high' if len(high_risk_indicators) > 1 else 'medium',
        'high_risk_indicators': len(high_risk_indicators),
        'immediate_action_required': len(high_risk_indicators) > 0,
        'security_implications': 'authentication_and_authorization_affected'
    }

def analyze_security_impact(patch_config: Dict):
    """Analyze security impact of regression"""
    
    affected_components = patch_config['security_patch'].affected_components
    
    return {
        'affected_security_domains': ['authentication', 'authorization', 'session_management'],
        'user_impact': 'authentication_failures_affecting_user_access',
        'system_impact': 'potential_privilege_escalation_vulnerability',
        'data_impact': 'session_integrity_compromised',
        'business_impact': 'user_experience_degradation_security_risk'
    }

def generate_security_recommendations(patch_config: Dict, regression_analysis: Dict):
    """Generate security recommendations based on analysis"""
    
    recommendations = []
    
    # High-priority recommendations based on regression severity
    if regression_analysis['severity_analysis']['overall_severity'] == 'high':
        recommendations.extend([
            'Immediate security team investigation required',
            'Disable automatic rollback to preserve evidence',
            'Implement enhanced monitoring for affected components',
            'Conduct emergency security review'
        ])
    
    # Component-specific recommendations
    affected_components = patch_config['security_patch'].affected_components
    if 'django_auth' in affected_components:
        recommendations.append('Review Django authentication configuration changes')
    
    if 'session_management' in affected_components:
        recommendations.append('Analyze session integrity and corruption issues')
    
    # General security recommendations
    recommendations.extend([
        'Preserve system state for forensic analysis',
        'Document all security findings',
        'Coordinate with development team for patch review',
        'Plan security-focused rollback strategy'
    ])
    
    return recommendations

def execute_security_deployment_stop(patch_config: Dict):
    """Execute security deployment stop"""
    
    deployment_context = patch_config['deployment_context']
    
    stop_result = {
        'stop_successful': False,
        'stop_timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
        'deployment_id': deployment_context['deployment_id'],
        'stop_status': None,
        'security_preservation_active': True
    }
    
    print(f"⛔ Executing security deployment stop...")
    
    try:
        # Execute deployment stop with security preservation
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.stop_deployment(
            deploymentId=deployment_context['deployment_id'],
            autoRollbackEnabled=False  # Disabled for security analysis
        )
        
        stop_result.update({
            'stop_successful': True,
            'stop_status': 'Succeeded',
            'security_analysis_required': True,
            'rollback_disabled': True
        })
        
        print(f"✅ Security deployment stop executed")
        print(f"  Auto-rollback: Disabled for security analysis")
        print(f"  Evidence preservation: Active")
        
        return stop_result
        
    except Exception as e:
        stop_result.update({
            'stop_successful': False,
            'stop_status': 'Failed',
            'error': str(e)
        })
        
        print(f"❌ Security deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return stop_result

def initiate_security_analysis_procedures(patch_config: Dict):
    """Initiate security analysis procedures"""
    
    security_analysis = {
        'analysis_initiated': False,
        'analysis_procedures': [],
        'security_tools_activated': [],
        'analysis_timeline': {}
    }
    
    print("🔒 Initiating security analysis procedures...")
    
    # Vulnerability assessment
    vuln_assessment = initiate_vulnerability_assessment(patch_config)
    security_analysis['analysis_procedures'].append('vulnerability_assessment')
    
    # Patch validation
    patch_validation = initiate_patch_validation(patch_config)
    security_analysis['analysis_procedures'].append('patch_validation')
    
    # Regression analysis
    regression_analysis = initiate_regression_analysis(patch_config)
    security_analysis['analysis_procedures'].append('regression_analysis')
    
    # Security code review
    code_review = initiate_security_code_review(patch_config)
    security_analysis['analysis_procedures'].append('security_code_review')
    
    # Activate security analysis tools
    security_analysis['security_tools_activated'] = [
        'vulnerability_scanner',
        'code_analysis_engine',
        'regression_testing_suite',
        'security_monitoring_enhanced'
    ]
    
    # Set analysis timeline
    security_analysis['analysis_timeline'] = {
        'start_time': datetime.now(),
        'estimated_completion': datetime.now() + timedelta(hours=4),
        'priority': 'urgent'
    }
    
    security_analysis['analysis_initiated'] = True
    
    print(f"Security Analysis Initiated:")
    for procedure in security_analysis['analysis_procedures']:
        print(f"  🔍 {procedure}")
    
    return security_analysis

def initiate_vulnerability_assessment(patch_config: Dict):
    """Initiate vulnerability assessment"""
    
    return {
        'assessment_started': True,
        'scope': patch_config['security_patch'].affected_components,
        'tools_used': ['vulnerability_scanner', 'penetration_testing'],
        'estimated_duration': '2 hours'
    }

def initiate_patch_validation(patch_config: Dict):
    """Initiate patch validation"""
    
    return {
        'validation_started': True,
        'patch_components': patch_config['security_patch'].security_bulletins,
        'validation_methods': ['code_review', 'automated_testing', 'manual_verification'],
        'estimated_duration': '3 hours'
    }

def initiate_regression_analysis(patch_config: Dict):
    """Initiate regression analysis"""
    
    return {
        'analysis_started': True,
        'regression_indicators': list(patch_config['regression_indicators'].keys()),
        'analysis_tools': ['regression_testing', 'performance_analysis', 'behavior_comparison'],
        'estimated_duration': '2 hours'
    }

def initiate_security_code_review(patch_config: Dict):
    """Initiate security code review"""
    
    return {
        'review_started': True,
        'review_scope': 'security_patch_implementation',
        'reviewers_assigned': ['senior_security_engineer', 'security_architect'],
        'estimated_duration': '4 hours'
    }

# Continue with remaining security analysis functions...

# Execute security deployment stop
if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = stop_security_patch_deployment()
    if success:
        print("🎉 Security patch deployment stop completed successfully")
    else:
        print("❌ Security patch deployment stop failed")
    Example 3: Stop Deployment During Django Database Migration Issues

    Stop a Django deployment when database migrations cause data integrity or performance issues.

    #!/bin/bash
# Stop deployment during Django database migration issues
aws deploy stop-deployment \
  --deployment-id d-C3D4E5F6G \
  --auto-rollback-enabled \
  --region us-east-1
    { "deploymentId": "d-C3D4E5F6G", "stopStatus": "Succeeded" }
    Django Integration:

    Database migration deployment stop with data integrity protection:

    # Database migration deployment stop for Django data integrity
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

def stop_migration_deployment():
    """Stop Django deployment during database migration issues"""
    
    migration_config = {
        'deployment_context': {
            'deployment_id': 'd-C3D4E5F6G',
            'application_name': 'jobpost-migration-v2.5',
            'migration_batch': 'user_profile_enhancements',
            'affected_models': ['User', 'UserProfile', 'JobPost', 'Application']
        },
        'migration_issues': {
            'long_running_migrations': ['0034_add_user_preferences', '0035_restructure_jobpost_tags'],
            'data_integrity_concerns': ['foreign_key_constraints', 'data_type_conversions'],
            'performance_impacts': ['table_lock_timeouts', 'query_performance_degradation'],
            'rollback_complexity': 'high'
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🗄️ Stopping migration deployment due to database issues")
        
        # Execute migration deployment stop with rollback
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.stop_deployment(
            deploymentId=migration_config['deployment_context']['deployment_id'],
            autoRollbackEnabled=True
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Migration deployment stopped successfully")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Migration deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    stop_migration_deployment()

from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def stop_performance_regression_deployment():
    """Stop Django deployment due to performance regression"""
    
    performance_config = {
        'deployment_context': {
            'deployment_id': 'd-D4E5F6G7H',
            'application_name': 'jobpost-performance-optimization',
            'optimization_features': ['query_optimization', 'cache_improvements', 'async_processing']
        },
        'regression_metrics': {
            'response_time_increase': '45_percent',
            'database_query_slowdown': '60_percent',
            'cache_hit_rate_decrease': '30_percent',
            'memory_usage_increase': '80_percent'
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"📊 Stopping deployment due to performance regression")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.stop_deployment(
            deploymentId=performance_config['deployment_context']['deployment_id'],
            autoRollbackEnabled=True
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Performance regression deployment stopped")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Performance deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    stop_performance_regression_deployment()

    Stop Django deployment to rollback feature flag changes that are causing user experience issues.

    Django Integration:

    Feature flag deployment stop with controlled rollback:

    # Feature flag deployment stop for Django feature management
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def stop_feature_flag_deployment():
    """Stop Django deployment for feature flag rollback"""
    
    feature_config = {
        'deployment_context': {
            'deployment_id': 'd-E5F6G7H8I',
            'application_name': 'jobpost-feature-flags-v3',
            'feature_flags': ['new_search_ui', 'enhanced_filters', 'recommendation_engine']
        },
        'user_feedback': {
            'new_search_ui': 'negative_user_experience',
            'enhanced_filters': 'performance_issues_reported',
            'recommendation_engine': 'incorrect_recommendations'
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🎛️ Stopping feature flag deployment for controlled rollback")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.stop_deployment(
            deploymentId=feature_config['deployment_context']['deployment_id'],
            autoRollbackEnabled=False  # Manual rollback for feature flags
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Feature flag deployment stopped for manual rollback")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Feature flag deployment stop failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    stop_feature_flag_deployment()

    update-application

    Changes the name of an application and optionally updates compute platform settings. This command allows you to modify application configurations without recreating the entire application, which is useful for maintaining deployment continuity while updating metadata or platform configurations.

    Syntax

    aws deploy update-application \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  [--new-application-name {new-application-name}] \
  [--compute-platform {compute-platform}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]

    Parameters

    --application-name (string)

    The current name of the application you want to update.

    --new-application-name (string)

    The new name to give the application.

    --compute-platform (string)

    The compute platform on which the application runs. Valid values: Server | Lambda | ECS.

    --cli-input-json (string)

    Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided.

    --generate-cli-skeleton (string)

    Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request.

    Attributes

    • application-name: Required parameter specifying current application name
    • new-application-name: Optional parameter for renaming the application
    • compute-platform: Optional parameter to update the compute platform
    • metadata-update: Allows updating application metadata without recreating

    Methods

    • Application Renaming: Updates application name while preserving all configurations
    • Platform Migration: Supports compute platform changes when applicable
    • Configuration Preservation: Maintains existing deployment groups and configurations
    • Metadata Management: Updates application metadata and properties

    Return Value

    No return value. The command completes successfully if the application is updated, or returns an error if the operation fails.

    Examples

    Example 1: Rename Django Application for Organization Rebrand

    Update Django application name to reflect company rebrand while preserving all deployment configurations.

    #!/bin/bash
# Rename Django application for organization rebrand
aws deploy update-application \
  --application-name jobpost-web-app \
  --new-application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --region us-east-1
    Command executed successfully - no output returned
    Django Integration:

    Django application rebrand with comprehensive configuration updates:

    # Django application rebrand for organization transformation
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict, Tuple, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class RebrandContext:
    """Organization rebrand context"""
    rebrand_initiative: str
    old_brand_name: str
    new_brand_name: str
    effective_date: datetime
    stakeholders: List[str]
    communication_plan: Dict[str, str]
    migration_timeline: Dict[str, datetime]

def update_django_application_rebrand():
    """Update Django application name for organization rebrand"""
    
    # Rebrand configuration
    rebrand_config = {
        'rebrand_context': RebrandContext(
            rebrand_initiative='company_rebrand_2026',
            old_brand_name='jobpost',
            new_brand_name='anihpj',
            effective_date=datetime(2026, 2, 1),
            stakeholders=['marketing', 'development', 'operations', 'legal'],
            communication_plan={
                'internal_announcement': 'completed',
                'customer_communication': 'scheduled',
                'partner_notification': 'in_progress',
                'public_announcement': 'planned'
            },
            migration_timeline={
                'development_environments': datetime.now(),
                'staging_environments': datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7),
                'production_environments': datetime.now() + timedelta(days=14)
            }
        ),
        'application_updates': {
            'current_application_name': 'jobpost-web-app',
            'new_application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
            'related_applications': [
                'jobpost-api-service',
                'jobpost-worker-app',
                'jobpost-admin-portal'
            ]
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🏢 Updating Django application for organization rebrand")
        
        # Execute application name update
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_application(
            applicationName=rebrand_config['application_updates']['current_application_name'],
            newApplicationName=rebrand_config['application_updates']['new_application_name']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Application renamed successfully")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Application rebrand failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_application_rebrand()
    Example 2: Update Django Application for Platform Migration

    Update Django application to change compute platform from Server to ECS for containerization.

    #!/bin/bash
# Update Django application for platform migration to ECS
aws deploy update-application \
  --application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --compute-platform ECS \
  --region us-east-1
    Command executed successfully - no output returned
    Django Integration:

    Django application platform migration to ECS:

    # Django application platform migration to ECS
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def update_django_application_platform():
    """Update Django application for platform migration to ECS"""
    
    platform_config = {
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'current_platform': 'Server',
        'target_platform': 'ECS',
        'migration_strategy': 'gradual_containerization'
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🚀 Updating Django application platform to ECS")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_application(
            applicationName=platform_config['application_name'],
            computePlatform=platform_config['target_platform']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Platform updated to {platform_config['target_platform']}")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Platform migration failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_application_platform()

from typing import Dict

def update_django_application_environment():
    """Update Django application for environment-specific naming"""
    
    env_config = {
        'current_name': 'career-platform-dev',
        'new_name': 'career-platform-development',
        'environment': 'development',
        'clarity_improvement': True
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"📋 Updating Django application for environment clarity")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_application(
            applicationName=env_config['current_name'],
            newApplicationName=env_config['new_name']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Application renamed for better environment identification")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Environment naming update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_application_environment()

def update_django_application_microservices():
    """Update Django application for microservices architecture"""
    
    architecture_config = {
        'old_name': 'career-platform-monolith',
        'new_name': 'career-platform-api-gateway',
        'architecture_type': 'microservices',
        'transformation_phase': 'api_gateway_setup'
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🏗️ Updating Django application for microservices architecture")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_application(
            applicationName=architecture_config['old_name'],
            newApplicationName=architecture_config['new_name']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Application updated for microservices transformation")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Microservices update failed: {str(e)}")

    update_django_application_microservices()

    Update application name to meet organizational compliance and naming standards.

    #!/bin/bash

  --application-name career-platform \
  --new-application-name anihpj-career-platform-prod \
  --region us-east-1
    Command executed successfully - no output returned
    Django Integration:

    Django application compliance naming standards:

    # Django application compliance naming standards
import boto3

def update_django_application_compliance():
    """Update Django application for compliance requirements"""
    
    compliance_config = {
        'current_name': 'career-platform',
        'compliant_name': 'anihpj-career-platform-prod',
        'compliance_standards': ['organization_prefix', 'environment_suffix'],
        'audit_requirements': 'met'
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"📋 Updating Django application for compliance standards")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_application(
            applicationName=compliance_config['current_name'],
            newApplicationName=compliance_config['compliant_name']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Application updated to meet compliance requirements")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Compliance update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_application_compliance()

    update-deployment-group

    Changes information about a deployment group, including deployment configuration, auto-scaling groups, EC2 tag filters, and on-premises instance tag filters. This command allows you to modify deployment group settings without recreating the group, maintaining deployment history and configurations while updating targeting and behavior.

    Syntax

    aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  --current-deployment-group-name {current-group-name} \
  [--new-deployment-group-name {new-group-name}] \
  [--deployment-config-name {config-name}] \
  [--ec2-tag-filters {ec2-tags-json}] \
  [--on-premises-instance-tag-filters {onprem-tags-json}] \
  [--auto-scaling-groups {asg-names}] \
  [--service-role-arn {role-arn}] \
  [--trigger-configurations {triggers-json}] \
  [--alarm-configuration {alarms-json}] \
  [--auto-rollback-configuration {rollback-json}] \
  [--outdated-instances-strategy {strategy}] \
  [--deployment-style {style-json}] \
  [--blue-green-deployment-configuration {bluegreen-json}] \
  [--load-balancer-info {lb-json}] \
  [--ec2-tag-set {ec2-tagset-json}] \
  [--ecs-services {ecs-json}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]

    Parameters

    --application-name (string)

    The application name that corresponds to the deployment group to update.

    --current-deployment-group-name (string)

    The current name of the deployment group to update.

    --new-deployment-group-name (string)

    The new name for the deployment group, if you want to change it.

    --deployment-config-name (string)

    The replacement deployment configuration name to use, if you want to change it.

    --service-role-arn (string)

    A replacement ARN for the service role that CodeDeploy uses to deploy applications.

    Attributes

    • application-name: Required parameter identifying the parent application
    • current-deployment-group-name: Required parameter for the group to update
    • configuration-preservation: Maintains deployment history during updates
    • flexible-targeting: Supports multiple targeting mechanisms (EC2 tags, ASG, etc.)

    Methods

    • Group Renaming: Updates deployment group name while preserving configurations
    • Configuration Updates: Modifies deployment behavior and targeting
    • Load Balancer Integration: Updates load balancer configurations
    • Auto-scaling Integration: Manages auto-scaling group associations

    Return Value

    Returns information about updated deployment group:

    • hooksNotCleanedUp: List of lifecycle event hooks that weren't cleaned up

    Examples

    Example 1: Update Django Deployment Group Configuration

    Update deployment group to use blue-green deployment strategy for Django application.

    #!/bin/bash
# Update Django deployment group for blue-green deployment
aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --current-deployment-group-name production-web-servers \
  --deployment-config-name CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenDeployment.EC2 \
  --region us-east-1
    { "hooksNotCleanedUp": [] }
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment group blue-green configuration:

    # Django deployment group blue-green configuration
import boto3
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, List

def update_django_deployment_group_bluegreen():
    """Update Django deployment group for blue-green deployment"""
    
    deployment_config = {
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'current_group_name': 'production-web-servers',
        'blue_green_config': {
            'deployment_config': 'CodeDeployDefault.BlueGreenDeployment.EC2',
            'termination_hook_enabled': True,
            'deployment_ready_hook_enabled': True,
            'green_fleet_provisioning': 'COPY_AUTO_SCALING_GROUP'
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔄 Updating Django deployment group for blue-green deployment")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_deployment_group(
            applicationName=deployment_config['application_name'],
            currentDeploymentGroupName=deployment_config['current_group_name'],
            deploymentConfigName=deployment_config['blue_green_config']['deployment_config']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Deployment group updated for blue-green deployment")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Deployment group update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_deployment_group_bluegreen()
    Example 2: Update Django Deployment Group Tags and Auto Scaling

    Update deployment group to target different EC2 instances and add auto-scaling group integration.

    #!/bin/bash
# Update Django deployment group tags and auto-scaling
aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --current-deployment-group-name production-web-servers \
  --ec2-tag-filters Key=Environment,Type=Key,Value=Production Key=Service,Type=Key,Value=Django \
  --auto-scaling-groups career-platform-asg \
  --region us-east-1
    { "hooksNotCleanedUp": [] }
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment group targeting and scaling updates:

    # Django deployment group targeting and scaling updates
import boto3
from typing import List, Dict

def update_django_deployment_group_targeting():
    """Update Django deployment group targeting and auto-scaling"""
    
    targeting_config = {
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'group_name': 'production-web-servers',
        'ec2_tag_filters': [
            {'Key': 'Environment', 'Type': 'KEY_AND_VALUE', 'Value': 'Production'},
            {'Key': 'Service', 'Type': 'KEY_AND_VALUE', 'Value': 'Django'}
        ],
        'auto_scaling_groups': ['career-platform-asg']
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🎯 Updating Django deployment group targeting")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        codedeploy_client.update_deployment_group(
            applicationName=targeting_config['application_name'],
            currentDeploymentGroupName=targeting_config['group_name'],
            ec2TagFilters=targeting_config['ec2_tag_filters'],
            autoScalingGroups=targeting_config['auto_scaling_groups']
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Deployment group targeting updated")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Targeting update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_deployment_group_targeting()
    Example 3: Update Django Deployment Group with Load Balancer Integration

    Update deployment group to integrate with Application Load Balancer for traffic management.

    #!/bin/bash
# Update Django deployment group with load balancer integration
aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --current-deployment-group-name production-web-servers \
  --load-balancer-info targetGroupInfoList='[{"name":"career-platform-web-targets"}]' \
  --region us-east-1
    { "hooksNotCleanedUp": [] }
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment group load balancer integration:

    # Django deployment group load balancer integration
import boto3

def update_django_deployment_group_loadbalancer():
    """Update Django deployment group with load balancer integration"""
    
    lb_config = {
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'group_name': 'production-web-servers',
        'target_groups': ['career-platform-web-targets']
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"⚖️ Updating Django deployment group with load balancer")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        load_balancer_info = {
            'targetGroupInfoList': [
                {'name': tg} for tg in lb_config['target_groups']
            ]
        }
        
        codedeploy_client.update_deployment_group(
            applicationName=lb_config['application_name'],
            currentDeploymentGroupName=lb_config['group_name'],
            loadBalancerInfo=load_balancer_info
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Load balancer integration updated")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Load balancer update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_deployment_group_loadbalancer()
    Example 4: Update Django Deployment Group with Rollback Configuration

    Update deployment group to enable automatic rollback on deployment failures and alarms.

    #!/bin/bash
# Update Django deployment group with rollback configuration
aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --current-deployment-group-name production-web-servers \
  --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
  --alarm-configuration enabled=true,alarms='[{"name":"HighErrorRate"}]' \
  --region us-east-1
    { "hooksNotCleanedUp": [] }
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment group automatic rollback configuration:

    # Django deployment group automatic rollback configuration
import boto3

def update_django_deployment_group_rollback():
    """Update Django deployment group with automatic rollback"""
    
    rollback_config = {
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'group_name': 'production-web-servers',
        'auto_rollback_enabled': True,
        'rollback_events': ['DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE', 'DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM'],
        'alarms': ['HighErrorRate', 'HighResponseTime']
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔄 Updating Django deployment group rollback configuration")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        auto_rollback_configuration = {
            'enabled': rollback_config['auto_rollback_enabled'],
            'events': rollback_config['rollback_events']
        }
        
        alarm_configuration = {
            'enabled': True,
            'alarms': [{'name': alarm} for alarm in rollback_config['alarms']]
        }
        
        codedeploy_client.update_deployment_group(
            applicationName=rollback_config['application_name'],
            currentDeploymentGroupName=rollback_config['group_name'],
            autoRollbackConfiguration=auto_rollback_configuration,
            alarmConfiguration=alarm_configuration
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Automatic rollback configuration updated")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Rollback configuration update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_deployment_group_rollback()
    Example 5: Update Django Deployment Group with Trigger Notifications

    Update deployment group to add SNS trigger notifications for deployment events.

    #!/bin/bash
# Update Django deployment group with trigger notifications
aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name anihpj-career-platform \
  --current-deployment-group-name production-web-servers \
  --trigger-configurations triggerName=DeploymentNotification,triggerTargetArn=arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:deployment-notifications,triggerEvents=DeploymentStart,DeploymentSuccess,DeploymentFailure \
  --region us-east-1
    { "hooksNotCleanedUp": [] }
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment group notification triggers:

    # Django deployment group notification triggers
import boto3

def update_django_deployment_group_triggers():
    """Update Django deployment group with notification triggers"""
    
    trigger_config = {
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'group_name': 'production-web-servers',
        'notifications': {
            'sns_topic_arn': 'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:deployment-notifications',
            'trigger_events': ['DeploymentStart', 'DeploymentSuccess', 'DeploymentFailure', 'DeploymentRollback']
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"📢 Updating Django deployment group notification triggers")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        trigger_configurations = [
            {
                'triggerName': 'DeploymentNotification',
                'triggerTargetArn': trigger_config['notifications']['sns_topic_arn'],
                'triggerEvents': trigger_config['notifications']['trigger_events']
            }
        ]
        
        codedeploy_client.update_deployment_group(
            applicationName=trigger_config['application_name'],
            currentDeploymentGroupName=trigger_config['group_name'],
            triggerConfigurations=trigger_configurations
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Notification triggers updated")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Trigger configuration update failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    update_django_deployment_group_triggers()

    stop-deployment

    Stops a CodeDeploy deployment in progress for your Django jobpost application. This command halts an active deployment, preventing further instances from being updated and potentially rolling back changes to maintain system stability for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services during deployment issues or emergency situations.

    Syntax
    aws deploy stop-deployment \
  --deployment-id {deployment-id} \
  --auto-rollback-enabled \
  --no-auto-rollback-enabled \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --deployment-id (string, required): Unique identifier of the deployment to stop
    • --auto-rollback-enabled | --no-auto-rollback-enabled (boolean): Whether to automatically roll back the deployment
    Return Value
    • status: Final status of the stopped deployment
    • statusMessage: Descriptive message about the deployment stop

    tag-resource

    Applies tags to a CodeDeploy resource used in Django jobpost application deployments. This command adds metadata tags to applications, deployment groups, or deployment configurations, enabling resource organization, cost allocation, environment identification, and management automation for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployment resources.

    Syntax
    aws deploy tag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tags Key={key},Value={value} [{Key={key},Value={value}}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CodeDeploy resource to tag
    • --tags (list, required): Key-value pairs to apply as tags
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful tagging.

    uninstall

    Uninstalls the CodeDeploy agent from an on-premises instance, removing its ability to receive deployments for Django jobpost applications. This command removes the agent software and configuration, effectively decommissioning the instance from receiving Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployment updates through CodeDeploy automation.

    Syntax
    aws deploy uninstall \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description

    No required parameters. The command removes the CodeDeploy agent from the current instance.

    Return Value
