    No output returned on successful uninstallation.

    untag-resource

    Removes tags from a CodeDeploy resource used in Django jobpost application deployments. This command removes specific metadata tags from applications, deployment groups, or deployment configurations, enabling resource organization cleanup and tag management for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployment resources.

    Syntax
    aws deploy untag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tag-keys {tag-key} [{tag-key}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CodeDeploy resource to remove tags from
    • --tag-keys (list, required): Keys of the tags to remove
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful tag removal.

    update-application

    Updates the settings for an existing CodeDeploy application managing Django jobpost deployments. This command modifies application configuration including name changes and settings updates for applications managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments across different environments and infrastructure configurations.

    Syntax
    aws deploy update-application \
  --application-name {current-application-name} \
  --new-application-name {new-application-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --application-name (string, required): Current name of the CodeDeploy application to update
    • --new-application-name (string): New name for the application if changing the name
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful update.

    update-deployment-group

    Updates the configuration for an existing deployment group within a CodeDeploy application managing Django jobpost deployments. This command modifies deployment group settings including auto scaling groups, load balancers, deployment configurations, and service roles for deployment groups managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services across various infrastructure environments.

    Syntax
    aws deploy update-deployment-group \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  --current-deployment-group-name {current-group-name} \
  --new-deployment-group-name {new-group-name} \
  --deployment-config-name {deployment-config} \
  --auto-scaling-groups {auto-scaling-group} [{auto-scaling-group}...] \
  --service-role-arn {service-role-arn} \
  --trigger-configurations {trigger-config} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --application-name (string, required): Name of the CodeDeploy application containing the deployment group
    • --current-deployment-group-name (string, required): Current name of the deployment group to update
    • --new-deployment-group-name (string): New name for the deployment group if changing the name
    • --deployment-config-name (string): Deployment configuration to use for the group
    • --service-role-arn (string): ARN of the service role for CodeDeploy to use
    • --auto-scaling-groups (list): Auto scaling groups to associate with the deployment group
    Return Value
    • hooksNotCleanedUp: Information about lifecycle event hooks that weren't cleaned up during the update

    wait deployment-successful

    Wait until a deployment is successful. This command polls the deployment status and blocks until the deployment completes successfully or fails. It provides a convenient way to synchronize automation workflows and ensure deployment completion before proceeding with subsequent operations for your Django jobpost application with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

    Syntax
    aws deploy wait deployment-successful \
  --deployment-ids {deployment-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]

    Parameters

    --deployment-ids (list)

    A list of deployment IDs to wait for successful completion. Multiple deployments can be monitored simultaneously.

    --cli-input-json (string)

    Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided.

    --generate-cli-skeleton (string)

    Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request.

    Attributes

    • deployment-ids: Required parameter specifying deployments to monitor
    • blocking-operation: Command blocks until completion or failure
    • polling-mechanism: Automatically polls deployment status at intervals
    • timeout-handling: Supports timeout configuration for long-running deployments

    Methods

    • Status Polling: Continuously monitors deployment progress
    • Success Detection: Identifies successful deployment completion
    • Error Handling: Detects and reports deployment failures
    • Synchronization: Provides workflow synchronization capabilities

    Return Value

    The command exits with status code 0 when all deployments succeed, or non-zero status code if any deployment fails or times out.

    Examples

    Example 1: Wait for Django Production Deployment Success

    Wait for Django production deployment to complete successfully before proceeding with post-deployment tasks.

    #!/bin/bash
# Wait for Django production deployment success
aws deploy wait deployment-successful \
  --deployment-ids d-A1B2C3D4E \
  --region us-east-1
    Command waits until deployment is successful - no output returned
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment success monitoring with post-deployment automation:

    # Django deployment success monitoring with automation
import boto3
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict

def wait_django_deployment_success():
    """Wait for Django deployment success with monitoring"""
    
    deployment_config = {
        'deployment_id': 'd-A1B2C3D4E',
        'application_name': 'anihpj-career-platform',
        'deployment_group': 'production-web-servers',
        'monitoring_enabled': True,
        'post_deployment_tasks': [
            'cache_warmup',
            'health_checks',
            'performance_validation',
            'stakeholder_notification'
        ]
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"⏳ Waiting for Django deployment success: {deployment_config['deployment_id']}")
        
        # Start monitoring
        start_time = datetime.now()
        monitoring_active = True
        
        while monitoring_active:
            # Check deployment status
            codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
            
            response = codedeploy_client.get_deployment(
                deploymentId=deployment_config['deployment_id']
            )
            
            deployment_status = response['deploymentInfo']['status']
            
            if deployment_status == 'Succeeded':
                print(f"✅ Deployment completed successfully")
                
                # Execute post-deployment tasks
                post_deployment_success = execute_post_deployment_tasks(deployment_config)
                return post_deployment_success
                
            elif deployment_status in ['Failed', 'Stopped']:
                print(f"❌ Deployment failed with status: {deployment_status}")
                return False
                
            elif deployment_status in ['InProgress', 'Pending', 'Queued']:
                print(f"🔄 Deployment in progress: {deployment_status}")
                time.sleep(30)  # Wait 30 seconds before next check
                
            # Check for timeout
            elapsed_time = datetime.now() - start_time
            if elapsed_time > timedelta(hours=2):
                print(f"⏰ Deployment timeout after {elapsed_time}")
                return False
        
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Deployment monitoring failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

def execute_post_deployment_tasks(deployment_config: Dict):
    """Execute post-deployment tasks after successful deployment"""
    
    print(f"🚀 Executing post-deployment tasks...")
    
    # Cache warmup
    cache_warmup_success = execute_cache_warmup(deployment_config)
    
    # Health checks
    health_check_success = execute_health_checks(deployment_config)
    
    # Performance validation
    performance_validation_success = execute_performance_validation(deployment_config)
    
    # Stakeholder notification
    notification_success = send_deployment_success_notification(deployment_config)
    
    all_tasks_successful = all([
        cache_warmup_success,
        health_check_success,
        performance_validation_success,
        notification_success
    ])
    
    if all_tasks_successful:
        print(f"✅ All post-deployment tasks completed successfully")
    else:
        print(f"⚠️ Some post-deployment tasks had issues")
    
    return all_tasks_successful

def execute_cache_warmup(deployment_config: Dict):
    """Execute cache warmup after deployment"""
    
    print(f"  🔥 Warming up Django application cache...")
    
    # Simulate cache warmup for Django application
    cache_endpoints = [
        '/api/jobs/popular',
        '/api/companies/featured', 
        '/api/skills/trending',
        '/api/locations/active'
    ]
    
    for endpoint in cache_endpoints:
        print(f"    Warming cache for: {endpoint}")
        # Simulate HTTP request to warm cache
        time.sleep(1)
    
    print(f"  ✅ Cache warmup completed")
    return True

def execute_health_checks(deployment_config: Dict):
    """Execute comprehensive health checks"""
    
    print(f"  🏥 Executing post-deployment health checks...")
    
    health_checks = {
        'application_health': check_application_health(),
        'database_connectivity': check_database_connectivity(),
        'cache_connectivity': check_cache_connectivity(),
        'external_services': check_external_services()
    }
    
    all_healthy = all(health_checks.values())
    
    if all_healthy:
        print(f"  ✅ All health checks passed")
    else:
        failed_checks = [check for check, result in health_checks.items() if not result]
        print(f"  ❌ Failed health checks: {', '.join(failed_checks)}")
    
    return all_healthy

def check_application_health():
    """Check Django application health"""
    # Simulate application health check
    return True

def check_database_connectivity():
    """Check database connectivity"""
    # Simulate database connectivity check
    return True

def check_cache_connectivity():
    """Check cache connectivity"""
    # Simulate cache connectivity check
    return True

def check_external_services():
    """Check external services connectivity"""
    # Simulate external services check
    return True

def execute_performance_validation(deployment_config: Dict):
    """Execute performance validation tests"""
    
    print(f"  📊 Executing performance validation...")
    
    performance_metrics = {
        'response_time_ms': 150,
        'throughput_rps': 1000,
        'error_rate_percent': 0.1,
        'cpu_utilization_percent': 45
    }
    
    # Validate performance thresholds
    performance_acceptable = (
        performance_metrics['response_time_ms'] < 200 and
        performance_metrics['throughput_rps'] > 500 and
        performance_metrics['error_rate_percent'] < 1.0 and
        performance_metrics['cpu_utilization_percent'] < 80
    )
    
    if performance_acceptable:
        print(f"  ✅ Performance validation passed")
    else:
        print(f"  ⚠️ Performance validation failed")
    
    return performance_acceptable

def send_deployment_success_notification(deployment_config: Dict):
    """Send deployment success notification to stakeholders"""
    
    print(f"  📢 Sending deployment success notifications...")
    
    stakeholders = [
        'development_team@anihpj.com',
        'operations_team@anihpj.com',
        'product_team@anihpj.com'
    ]
    
    for stakeholder in stakeholders:
        print(f"    Notification sent to: {stakeholder}")
    
    print(f"  ✅ Success notifications sent")
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = wait_django_deployment_success()
    if success:
        print("🎉 Django deployment completed with all validations")
    else:
        print("❌ Django deployment or validations failed")
    Example 2: Wait for Multiple Django Deployment Success

    Wait for multiple Django deployments to complete successfully across different environments.

    #!/bin/bash
# Wait for multiple Django deployments success
aws deploy wait deployment-successful \
  --deployment-ids d-A1B2C3D4E d-B2C3D4E5F d-C3D4E5F6G \
  --region us-east-1
    Command waits until all deployments are successful - no output returned
    Django Integration:

    Multiple Django deployment coordination:

    # Multiple Django deployment coordination
import boto3
import asyncio
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from typing import List, Dict

async def wait_multiple_django_deployments():
    """Wait for multiple Django deployments with coordination"""
    
    deployment_config = {
        'deployments': [
            {'id': 'd-A1B2C3D4E', 'environment': 'production', 'priority': 'high'},
            {'id': 'd-B2C3D4E5F', 'environment': 'staging', 'priority': 'medium'},
            {'id': 'd-C3D4E5F6G', 'environment': 'development', 'priority': 'low'}
        ],
        'coordination_strategy': 'parallel_monitoring'
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"⏳ Monitoring {len(deployment_config['deployments'])} Django deployments")
        
        # Monitor all deployments concurrently
        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3) as executor:
            deployment_futures = []
            
            for deployment in deployment_config['deployments']:
                future = executor.submit(monitor_single_deployment, deployment)
                deployment_futures.append(future)
            
            # Wait for all deployments to complete
            results = [future.result() for future in deployment_futures]
            
            all_successful = all(results)
            
            if all_successful:
                print(f"✅ All Django deployments completed successfully")
            else:
                print(f"⚠️ Some Django deployments failed")
            
            return all_successful
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Multiple deployment monitoring failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

def monitor_single_deployment(deployment: Dict):
    """Monitor individual deployment"""
    
    print(f"🔍 Monitoring deployment: {deployment['id']} ({deployment['environment']})")
    
    # Simulate deployment monitoring
    import time
    time.sleep(10)  # Simulate deployment time
    
    print(f"✅ Deployment {deployment['id']} completed")
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio
    success = asyncio.run(wait_multiple_django_deployments())
    print("🎉 Multiple deployments coordination completed" if success else "❌ Coordination failed")
    Example 3: Wait for Django Deployment with Timeout Handling

    Wait for Django deployment with custom timeout and retry logic.

    #!/bin/bash
# Wait for Django deployment with timeout
DEPLOYMENT_ID="d-D4E5F6G7H"

echo "Waiting for Django deployment with timeout handling..."

timeout 3600 aws deploy wait deployment-successful \
  --deployment-ids $DEPLOYMENT_ID \
  --region us-east-1

if [ $? -eq 124 ]; then
    echo "Deployment timed out after 1 hour"
    exit 1
else
    echo "Deployment completed successfully"
fi
    Waiting for Django deployment with timeout handling... Deployment completed successfully
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment timeout and retry handling:

    # Django deployment timeout and retry handling
import boto3
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def wait_django_deployment_with_timeout():
    """Wait for Django deployment with timeout and retry logic"""
    
    deployment_config = {
        'deployment_id': 'd-D4E5F6G7H',
        'max_timeout_minutes': 60,
        'retry_attempts': 3,
        'retry_delay_seconds': 300
    }
    
    for attempt in range(deployment_config['retry_attempts']):
        try:
            print(f"🔄 Deployment wait attempt {attempt + 1}/{deployment_config['retry_attempts']}")
            
            start_time = datetime.now()
            max_wait_time = timedelta(minutes=deployment_config['max_timeout_minutes'])
            
            while datetime.now() - start_time < max_wait_time:
                codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
                
                response = codedeploy_client.get_deployment(
                    deploymentId=deployment_config['deployment_id']
                )
                
                status = response['deploymentInfo']['status']
                
                if status == 'Succeeded':
                    print(f"✅ Deployment succeeded on attempt {attempt + 1}")
                    return True
                elif status in ['Failed', 'Stopped']:
                    print(f"❌ Deployment failed: {status}")
                    break
                
                time.sleep(30)
            
            print(f"⏰ Attempt {attempt + 1} timed out")
            
            if attempt < deployment_config['retry_attempts'] - 1:
                print(f"⏳ Waiting {deployment_config['retry_delay_seconds']} seconds before retry...")
                time.sleep(deployment_config['retry_delay_seconds'])
        
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Attempt {attempt + 1} failed: {str(e)}")
    
    print(f"❌ All retry attempts exhausted")
    return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = wait_django_deployment_with_timeout()
    print("🎉 Deployment wait completed" if success else "❌ Deployment wait failed")
    Example 4: Wait for Django Blue-Green Deployment Success

    Wait for Django blue-green deployment with specialized monitoring and validation.

    #!/bin/bash
# Wait for Django blue-green deployment success
aws deploy wait deployment-successful \
  --deployment-ids d-E5F6G7H8I \
  --region us-east-1
    Command waits until blue-green deployment is successful - no output returned
    Django Integration:

    Django blue-green deployment monitoring:

    # Django blue-green deployment monitoring
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

def wait_django_bluegreen_deployment():
    """Wait for Django blue-green deployment with specialized monitoring"""
    
    bluegreen_config = {
        'deployment_id': 'd-E5F6G7H8I',
        'deployment_type': 'blue_green',
        'green_fleet_validation': True,
        'traffic_shift_monitoring': True
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔵🟢 Monitoring Django blue-green deployment")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        # Monitor deployment phases
        phases = ['Provisioning', 'Deploying', 'Validating', 'Shifting', 'Terminating']
        
        for phase in phases:
            print(f"📋 Phase: {phase}")
            
            if phase == 'Validating':
                validation_success = validate_green_fleet(bluegreen_config)
                if not validation_success:
                    print(f"❌ Green fleet validation failed")
                    return False
            
            elif phase == 'Shifting':
                traffic_shift_success = monitor_traffic_shift(bluegreen_config)
                if not traffic_shift_success:
                    print(f"❌ Traffic shift monitoring failed")
                    return False
        
        print(f"✅ Blue-green deployment completed successfully")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Blue-green deployment monitoring failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

def validate_green_fleet(config: dict):
    """Validate green fleet before traffic shift"""
    print(f"  🟢 Validating green fleet...")
    # Simulate green fleet validation
    return True

def monitor_traffic_shift(config: dict):
    """Monitor traffic shift process"""
    print(f"  🔄 Monitoring traffic shift...")
    # Simulate traffic shift monitoring
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = wait_django_bluegreen_deployment()
    print("🎉 Blue-green deployment completed" if success else "❌ Blue-green deployment failed")
    Example 5: Wait for Django Deployment with CI/CD Pipeline Integration

    Wait for Django deployment as part of automated CI/CD pipeline with comprehensive reporting.

    #!/bin/bash
# Wait for Django deployment in CI/CD pipeline
DEPLOYMENT_ID="d-F6G7H8I9J"
PIPELINE_ID="pipeline-2026-001"

echo "CI/CD Pipeline: Waiting for Django deployment..."

aws deploy wait deployment-successful \
  --deployment-ids $DEPLOYMENT_ID \
  --region us-east-1

DEPLOYMENT_STATUS=$?

if [ $DEPLOYMENT_STATUS -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ Pipeline deployment successful"
    echo "deployment_status=success" >> pipeline_results.txt
else
    echo "❌ Pipeline deployment failed"
    echo "deployment_status=failed" >> pipeline_results.txt
fi

exit $DEPLOYMENT_STATUS
    CI/CD Pipeline: Waiting for Django deployment... ✅ Pipeline deployment successful
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment CI/CD pipeline integration:

    # Django deployment CI/CD pipeline integration
import boto3
import json
from datetime import datetime

def wait_django_deployment_cicd():
    """Wait for Django deployment with CI/CD pipeline integration"""
    
    pipeline_config = {
        'deployment_id': 'd-F6G7H8I9J',
        'pipeline_id': 'pipeline-2026-001',
        'stage': 'production_deployment',
        'reporting_enabled': True,
        'metrics_collection': True
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔄 CI/CD Pipeline: Monitoring Django deployment")
        
        # Initialize pipeline reporting
        pipeline_report = initialize_pipeline_report(pipeline_config)
        
        # Monitor deployment
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        deployment_start = datetime.now()
        
        # Wait for deployment completion
        waiter = codedeploy_client.get_waiter('deployment_successful')
        waiter.wait(deploymentIds=[pipeline_config['deployment_id']])
        
        deployment_end = datetime.now()
        deployment_duration = deployment_end - deployment_start
        
        # Update pipeline report
        pipeline_report.update({
            'deployment_status': 'success',
            'deployment_duration': str(deployment_duration),
            'completion_time': deployment_end.isoformat()
        })
        
        # Generate CI/CD metrics
        cicd_metrics = generate_cicd_metrics(pipeline_config, pipeline_report)
        
        # Save pipeline results
        save_pipeline_results(pipeline_report, cicd_metrics)
        
        print(f"✅ CI/CD Pipeline deployment completed successfully")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        # Handle deployment failure in pipeline
        pipeline_report = {
            'deployment_status': 'failed',
            'error': str(e),
            'failure_time': datetime.now().isoformat()
        }
        
        save_pipeline_results(pipeline_report, {})
        
        print(f"❌ CI/CD Pipeline deployment failed: {str(e)}")
        return False

def initialize_pipeline_report(config: dict):
    """Initialize pipeline report"""
    return {
        'pipeline_id': config['pipeline_id'],
        'deployment_id': config['deployment_id'],
        'stage': config['stage'],
        'start_time': datetime.now().isoformat()
    }

def generate_cicd_metrics(config: dict, report: dict):
    """Generate CI/CD metrics"""
    return {
        'deployment_frequency': 'daily',
        'lead_time': report.get('deployment_duration', '0'),
        'mttr': '15 minutes',
        'change_failure_rate': '2%'
    }

def save_pipeline_results(report: dict, metrics: dict):
    """Save pipeline results for reporting"""
    results = {
        'report': report,
        'metrics': metrics,
        'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat()
    }
    
    # Save to file (simulated)
    print(f"💾 Pipeline results saved")
    
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = wait_django_deployment_cicd()
    print("🎉 CI/CD deployment completed" if success else "❌ CI/CD deployment failed")

    batch-get-deployment-groups

    Get details about multiple deployment groups in CodeDeploy for your Django jobpost application. This command retrieves comprehensive information about deployment configurations including auto scaling groups, load balancers, and deployment settings for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model deployments.

    Syntax
    aws deploy batch-get-deployment-groups \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  --deployment-group-names {deployment-group-name} [{deployment-group-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]

    Parameters:

    • --application-name (string): The name of the application.
    • --deployment-group-names (list): A list of deployment group names.

    Attributes:

    • deploymentGroupsInfo: Information about the deployment groups
    • errorMessage: Error messages for failed retrievals

    Methods:

    • get_deployment_group_details()
    • validate_deployment_groups()
    • parse_group_configurations()

    Return Value:

    Returns deployment group information including configurations and service roles.

    Examples:

    Example 1: Get Django Application Deployment Groups

    Retrieve multiple deployment groups for Django application.

    # Get Django deployment groups
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-groups \
  --application-name django-social-platform \
  --deployment-group-names production staging development \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "deploymentGroupsInfo": [
        {
            "applicationName": "django-social-platform",
            "deploymentGroupName": "production",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.EC2AllInstancesOneAtATimeBlueGreen",
            "ec2TagFilters": [
                {
                    "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE",
                    "Key": "Environment",
                    "Value": "production"
                }
            ],
            "serviceRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole",
            "loadBalancerInfo": {
                "targetGroupInfoList": [
                    {
                        "name": "django-prod-tg"
                    }
                ]
            }
        },
        {
            "applicationName": "django-social-platform",
            "deploymentGroupName": "staging",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.EC2AllInstancesOneAtATime",
            "ec2TagFilters": [
                {
                    "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE",
                    "Key": "Environment",
                    "Value": "staging"
                }
            ],
            "serviceRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole"
        },
        {
            "applicationName": "django-social-platform",
            "deploymentGroupName": "development",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.EC2AllInstancesOneAtATime",
            "ec2TagFilters": [
                {
                    "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE",
                    "Key": "Environment",
                    "Value": "development"
                }
            ],
            "serviceRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole"
        }
    ]
}
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment groups management:

    # Django deployment groups management
import boto3
import json

def get_django_deployment_groups():
    """Get multiple Django deployment groups information"""
    
    django_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-social-platform',
        'deployment_groups': ['production', 'staging', 'development']
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"📦 Retrieving Django deployment groups")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.batch_get_deployment_groups(
            applicationName=django_config['application_name'],
            deploymentGroupNames=django_config['deployment_groups']
        )
        
        deployment_groups_info = response.get('deploymentGroupsInfo', [])
        
        # Process deployment groups
        groups_summary = process_deployment_groups(deployment_groups_info)
        
        # Validate group configurations
        validation_results = validate_group_configurations(deployment_groups_info)
        
        print(f"✅ Retrieved {len(deployment_groups_info)} deployment groups")
        
        return {
            'deployment_groups': deployment_groups_info,
            'groups_summary': groups_summary,
            'validation_results': validation_results
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed to retrieve Django deployment groups: {str(e)}")
        return None

def process_deployment_groups(groups_info: list):
    """Process deployment groups information"""
    summary = {
        'total_groups': len(groups_info),
        'environments': [],
        'deployment_configs': {},
        'service_roles': set()
    }
    
    for group in groups_info:
        # Extract environment information
        group_name = group.get('deploymentGroupName', '')
        summary['environments'].append(group_name)
        
        # Count deployment configurations
        config_name = group.get('deploymentConfigName', '')
        summary['deployment_configs'][config_name] = summary['deployment_configs'].get(config_name, 0) + 1
        
        # Collect service roles
        service_role = group.get('serviceRoleArn', '')
        if service_role:
            summary['service_roles'].add(service_role)
    
    summary['service_roles'] = list(summary['service_roles'])
    
    return summary

def validate_group_configurations(groups_info: list):
    """Validate deployment group configurations"""
    validation_results = {
        'valid_groups': [],
        'invalid_groups': [],
        'missing_configurations': []
    }
    
    required_fields = ['deploymentGroupName', 'serviceRoleArn', 'deploymentConfigName']
    
    for group in groups_info:
        group_name = group.get('deploymentGroupName', 'Unknown')
        
        # Check required fields
        missing_fields = [field for field in required_fields if not group.get(field)]
        
        if missing_fields:
            validation_results['invalid_groups'].append({
                'group_name': group_name,
                'missing_fields': missing_fields
            })
        else:
            validation_results['valid_groups'].append(group_name)
        
        # Check for missing EC2 filters or Load Balancer info
        if not group.get('ec2TagFilters') and not group.get('onPremisesInstanceTagFilters'):
            validation_results['missing_configurations'].append({
                'group_name': group_name,
                'issue': 'No instance targeting configured'
            })
    
    return validation_results

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = get_django_deployment_groups()
    if result:
        print("🎉 Django deployment groups retrieved successfully")
    else:
        print("❌ Failed to retrieve Django deployment groups")
    Example 2: Get Django Microservices Deployment Groups

    Retrieve deployment groups for Django microservices architecture.

    # Get Django microservices deployment groups
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-groups \
  --application-name django-microservices \
  --deployment-group-names api-service user-service content-service payment-service \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "deploymentGroupsInfo": [
        {
            "applicationName": "django-microservices",
            "deploymentGroupName": "api-service",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.ECSAllAtOnceBlueGreen",
            "ecsServices": [
                {
                    "serviceName": "django-api-service",
                    "clusterName": "microservices-cluster"
                }
            ],
            "serviceRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployECSRole"
        },
        {
            "applicationName": "django-microservices",
            "deploymentGroupName": "user-service",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.ECSAllAtOnceBlueGreen",
            "ecsServices": [
                {
                    "serviceName": "django-user-service",
                    "clusterName": "microservices-cluster"
                }
            ],
            "serviceRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployECSRole"
        }
    ]
}
    Django Integration:

    Django microservices deployment groups:

    # Django microservices deployment groups
import boto3
from typing import Dict, List

def get_django_microservices_groups():
    """Get Django microservices deployment groups"""
    
    microservices_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-microservices',
        'services': ['api-service', 'user-service', 'content-service', 'payment-service'],
        'cluster_name': 'microservices-cluster',
        'deployment_type': 'ECS_BLUE_GREEN'
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔧 Retrieving Django microservices deployment groups")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.batch_get_deployment_groups(
            applicationName=microservices_config['application_name'],
            deploymentGroupNames=microservices_config['services']
        )
        
        # Process microservices groups
        services_info = process_microservices_groups(response.get('deploymentGroupsInfo', []))
        
        # Analyze service dependencies
        dependencies = analyze_service_dependencies(services_info)
        
        # Generate deployment plan
        deployment_plan = generate_microservices_deployment_plan(services_info, dependencies)
        
        print(f"✅ Retrieved {len(services_info)} microservices deployment groups")
        
        return {
            'services_info': services_info,
            'dependencies': dependencies,
            'deployment_plan': deployment_plan
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed to retrieve microservices groups: {str(e)}")
        return None

def process_microservices_groups(groups_info: List[Dict]):
    """Process microservices deployment groups"""
    services = {}
    
    for group in groups_info:
        service_name = group.get('deploymentGroupName', '')
        
        services[service_name] = {
            'deployment_config': group.get('deploymentConfigName', ''),
            'ecs_services': group.get('ecsServices', []),
            'service_role': group.get('serviceRoleArn', ''),
            'auto_rollback': group.get('autoRollbackConfiguration', {}),
            'alarm_configuration': group.get('alarmConfiguration', {})
        }
    
    return services

def analyze_service_dependencies(services_info: Dict):
    """Analyze microservices dependencies"""
    dependencies = {
        'api-service': [],  # Independent service
        'user-service': ['api-service'],  # Depends on API
        'content-service': ['user-service'],  # Depends on User
        'payment-service': ['user-service', 'api-service']  # Depends on multiple
    }
    
    return dependencies

def generate_microservices_deployment_plan(services_info: Dict, dependencies: Dict):
    """Generate microservices deployment plan"""
    deployment_plan = {
        'phases': [],
        'total_services': len(services_info),
        'estimated_duration': '45 minutes'
    }
    
    # Phase 1: Independent services
    deployment_plan['phases'].append({
        'phase': 1,
        'services': ['api-service'],
        'parallel': False,
        'duration': '10 minutes'
    })
    
    # Phase 2: Services depending on API
    deployment_plan['phases'].append({
        'phase': 2,
        'services': ['user-service'],
        'parallel': False,
        'duration': '15 minutes'
    })
    
    # Phase 3: Dependent services
    deployment_plan['phases'].append({
        'phase': 3,
        'services': ['content-service', 'payment-service'],
        'parallel': True,
        'duration': '20 minutes'
    })
    
    return deployment_plan

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = get_django_microservices_groups()
    if result:
        print("🎉 Microservices deployment groups retrieved")
    else:
        print("❌ Failed to retrieve microservices groups")
    Example 3: Get Django Regional Deployment Groups

    Retrieve deployment groups across multiple AWS regions for Django application.

    # Get Django regional deployment groups
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-groups \
  --application-name django-global-app \
  --deployment-group-names us-east-prod us-west-prod eu-central-prod asia-pacific-prod \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "deploymentGroupsInfo": [
        {
            "applicationName": "django-global-app",
            "deploymentGroupName": "us-east-prod",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.EC2AllInstancesOneAtATimeBlueGreen",
            "ec2TagFilters": [
                {
                    "Type": "KEY_AND_VALUE",
                    "Key": "Region",
                    "Value": "us-east-1"
                }
            ],
            "loadBalancerInfo": {
                "targetGroupInfoList": [
                    {
                        "name": "django-us-east-tg"
                    }
                ]
            }
        }
    ]
}
    Django Integration:

    Django regional deployment groups management:

    # Django regional deployment groups
import boto3
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
import threading

def get_django_regional_groups():
    """Get Django deployment groups across regions"""
    
    regional_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-global-app',
        'regions': {
            'us-east-1': ['us-east-prod', 'us-east-staging'],
            'us-west-2': ['us-west-prod', 'us-west-staging'],
            'eu-central-1': ['eu-central-prod'],
            'ap-southeast-1': ['asia-pacific-prod']
        }
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🌍 Retrieving Django regional deployment groups")
        
        # Get groups from all regions concurrently
        regional_results = get_groups_from_all_regions(regional_config)
        
        # Consolidate regional information
        consolidated_info = consolidate_regional_groups(regional_results)
        
        # Analyze regional deployment strategy
        deployment_strategy = analyze_regional_deployment(consolidated_info)
        
        print(f"✅ Retrieved deployment groups from {len(regional_results)} regions")
        
        return {
            'regional_results': regional_results,
            'consolidated_info': consolidated_info,
            'deployment_strategy': deployment_strategy
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed to retrieve regional groups: {str(e)}")
        return None

def get_groups_from_all_regions(config: dict):
    """Get deployment groups from all regions concurrently"""
    regional_results = {}
    
    def get_region_groups(region, group_names):
        """Get groups for a specific region"""
        try:
            codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy', region_name=region)
            
            response = codedeploy_client.batch_get_deployment_groups(
                applicationName=config['application_name'],
                deploymentGroupNames=group_names
            )
            
            return {
                'region': region,
                'groups': response.get('deploymentGroupsInfo', []),
                'success': True
            }
        except Exception as e:
            return {
                'region': region,
                'groups': [],
                'success': False,
                'error': str(e)
            }
    
    # Use ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent region queries
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
        futures = []
        
        for region, group_names in config['regions'].items():
            future = executor.submit(get_region_groups, region, group_names)
            futures.append(future)
        
        for future in futures:
            result = future.result()
            regional_results[result['region']] = result
    
    return regional_results

def consolidate_regional_groups(regional_results: dict):
    """Consolidate regional deployment group information"""
    consolidated = {
        'total_regions': len(regional_results),
        'total_groups': 0,
        'successful_regions': [],
        'failed_regions': [],
        'deployment_configs': {},
        'load_balancers': []
    }
    
    for region, result in regional_results.items():
        if result['success']:
            consolidated['successful_regions'].append(region)
            consolidated['total_groups'] += len(result['groups'])
            
            # Analyze deployment configs per region
            for group in result['groups']:
                config_name = group.get('deploymentConfigName', '')
                if config_name not in consolidated['deployment_configs']:
                    consolidated['deployment_configs'][config_name] = []
                consolidated['deployment_configs'][config_name].append(region)
                
                # Extract load balancer information
                lb_info = group.get('loadBalancerInfo', {})
                if lb_info:
                    consolidated['load_balancers'].append({
                        'region': region,
                        'group': group.get('deploymentGroupName', ''),
                        'target_groups': lb_info.get('targetGroupInfoList', [])
                    })
        else:
            consolidated['failed_regions'].append({
                'region': region,
                'error': result.get('error', 'Unknown error')
            })
    
    return consolidated

def analyze_regional_deployment(consolidated_info: dict):
    """Analyze regional deployment strategy"""
    strategy = {
        'deployment_approach': 'multi_region',
        'rollout_strategy': 'sequential',
        'regions_order': [],
        'estimated_duration': '2 hours'
    }
    
    # Determine deployment order based on region priority
    region_priority = ['us-east-1', 'us-west-2', 'eu-central-1', 'ap-southeast-1']
    
    for region in region_priority:
        if region in consolidated_info['successful_regions']:
            strategy['regions_order'].append(region)
    
    # Add any additional regions not in priority list
    for region in consolidated_info['successful_regions']:
        if region not in strategy['regions_order']:
            strategy['regions_order'].append(region)
    
    return strategy

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = get_django_regional_groups()
    if result:
        print("🎉 Regional deployment groups retrieved")
    else:
        print("❌ Failed to retrieve regional groups")
    Example 4: Get Django Blue-Green Deployment Groups

    Retrieve blue-green deployment groups with advanced configuration analysis.

    # Get Django blue-green deployment groups
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-groups \
  --application-name django-ecommerce \
  --deployment-group-names blue-production green-production canary-testing \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "deploymentGroupsInfo": [
        {
            "applicationName": "django-ecommerce",
            "deploymentGroupName": "blue-production",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.EC2AllInstancesOneAtATimeBlueGreen",
            "blueGreenDeploymentConfiguration": {
                "deploymentReadyOption": {
                    "actionOnTimeout": "CONTINUE_DEPLOYMENT"
                },
                "greenFleetProvisioningOption": {
                    "action": "COPY_AUTO_SCALING_GROUP"
                },
                "terminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess": {
                    "action": "TERMINATE",
                    "terminationWaitTimeInMinutes": 5
                }
            }
        }
    ]
}
    Django Integration:

    Django blue-green deployment groups analysis:

    # Django blue-green deployment groups analysis
import boto3
import json

def get_django_bluegreen_groups():
    """Get Django blue-green deployment groups with configuration analysis"""
    
    bluegreen_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-ecommerce',
        'deployment_groups': ['blue-production', 'green-production', 'canary-testing'],
        'deployment_type': 'blue_green',
        'traffic_management': True
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🔵🟢 Retrieving Django blue-green deployment groups")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.batch_get_deployment_groups(
            applicationName=bluegreen_config['application_name'],
            deploymentGroupNames=bluegreen_config['deployment_groups']
        )
        
        # Analyze blue-green configurations
        bluegreen_analysis = analyze_bluegreen_configurations(
            response.get('deploymentGroupsInfo', [])
        )
        
        # Validate deployment readiness
        readiness_check = validate_deployment_readiness(bluegreen_analysis)
        
        # Generate traffic shift strategy
        traffic_strategy = generate_traffic_shift_strategy(bluegreen_analysis)
        
        print(f"✅ Analyzed {len(bluegreen_analysis['groups'])} blue-green groups")
        
        return {
            'bluegreen_analysis': bluegreen_analysis,
            'readiness_check': readiness_check,
            'traffic_strategy': traffic_strategy
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed to retrieve blue-green groups: {str(e)}")
        return None

def analyze_bluegreen_configurations(groups_info: list):
    """Analyze blue-green deployment configurations"""
    analysis = {
        'groups': {},
        'deployment_strategies': {},
        'termination_policies': {},
        'readiness_options': {}
    }
    
    for group in groups_info:
        group_name = group.get('deploymentGroupName', '')
        bg_config = group.get('blueGreenDeploymentConfiguration', {})
        
        analysis['groups'][group_name] = {
            'deployment_config': group.get('deploymentConfigName', ''),
            'blue_green_config': bg_config,
            'auto_scaling_groups': group.get('autoScalingGroups', []),
            'load_balancer_info': group.get('loadBalancerInfo', {})
        }
        
        # Analyze deployment ready options
        ready_option = bg_config.get('deploymentReadyOption', {})
        analysis['readiness_options'][group_name] = {
            'action_on_timeout': ready_option.get('actionOnTimeout', 'CONTINUE_DEPLOYMENT'),
            'wait_time_minutes': ready_option.get('waitTimeInMinutes', 0)
        }
        
        # Analyze termination policies
        termination_config = bg_config.get('terminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess', {})
        analysis['termination_policies'][group_name] = {
            'action': termination_config.get('action', 'TERMINATE'),
            'wait_time': termination_config.get('terminationWaitTimeInMinutes', 5)
        }
    
    return analysis

def validate_deployment_readiness(analysis: dict):
    """Validate blue-green deployment readiness"""
    readiness = {
        'ready_groups': [],
        'issues': [],
        'recommendations': []
    }
    
    for group_name, config in analysis['groups'].items():
        issues_found = []
        
        # Check for required configurations
        if not config.get('blue_green_config'):
            issues_found.append('Missing blue-green configuration')
        
        if not config.get('load_balancer_info'):
            issues_found.append('No load balancer configuration')
        
        if not config.get('auto_scaling_groups'):
            issues_found.append('No auto scaling groups configured')
        
        if issues_found:
            readiness['issues'].append({
                'group': group_name,
                'issues': issues_found
            })
        else:
            readiness['ready_groups'].append(group_name)
    
    # Generate recommendations
    if len(readiness['issues']) > 0:
        readiness['recommendations'] = [
            'Review blue-green configuration for all groups',
            'Ensure load balancers are properly configured',
            'Verify auto scaling group settings'
        ]
    
    return readiness

def generate_traffic_shift_strategy(analysis: dict):
    """Generate traffic shift strategy for blue-green deployment"""
    strategy = {
        'deployment_order': [],
        'traffic_shift_plan': {},
        'monitoring_requirements': [],
        'rollback_strategy': {}
    }
    
    # Define deployment order based on group priority
    group_priority = ['canary-testing', 'blue-production', 'green-production']
    
    for group in group_priority:
        if group in analysis['groups']:
            strategy['deployment_order'].append(group)
    
    # Generate traffic shift plan
    for group_name in strategy['deployment_order']:
        termination_policy = analysis['termination_policies'].get(group_name, {})
        
        strategy['traffic_shift_plan'][group_name] = {
            'shift_percentage': 100 if 'production' in group_name else 25,
            'monitoring_duration': '10 minutes',
            'termination_wait': termination_policy.get('wait_time', 5)
        }
    
    # Define monitoring requirements
    strategy['monitoring_requirements'] = [
        'Application response time < 200ms',
        'Error rate < 1%',
        'Database connection pool healthy',
        'Cache hit rate > 95%'
    ]
    
    return strategy

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = get_django_bluegreen_groups()
    if result:
        print("🎉 Blue-green deployment groups analyzed")
    else:
        print("❌ Failed to analyze blue-green groups")
    Example 5: Get Django Auto-Scaling Deployment Groups

    Retrieve deployment groups with auto-scaling configuration analysis.

    # Get Django auto-scaling deployment groups
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-groups \
  --application-name django-scalable-app \
  --deployment-group-names web-tier app-tier data-tier \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "deploymentGroupsInfo": [
        {
            "applicationName": "django-scalable-app",
            "deploymentGroupName": "web-tier",
            "deploymentConfigName": "CodeDeployDefault.EC2AllInstancesOneAtATime",
            "autoScalingGroups": [
                {
                    "name": "django-web-asg",
                    "hook": "django-web-lifecycle-hook"
                }
            ],
            "alarmConfiguration": {
                "enabled": true,
                "alarms": [
                    {
                        "name": "django-high-cpu"
                    },
                    {
                        "name": "django-high-memory"
                    }
                ]
            }
        }
    ]
}
    Django Integration:

    Django auto-scaling deployment groups management:

    # Django auto-scaling deployment groups
import boto3
from typing import Dict, List, Any

def get_django_autoscaling_groups():
    """Get Django auto-scaling deployment groups with comprehensive analysis"""
    
    autoscaling_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-scalable-app',
        'deployment_groups': ['web-tier', 'app-tier', 'data-tier'],
        'scaling_strategy': 'dynamic',
        'performance_monitoring': True
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"📈 Retrieving Django auto-scaling deployment groups")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        autoscaling_client = boto3.client('autoscaling')
        cloudwatch_client = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
        
        # Get deployment groups
        response = codedeploy_client.batch_get_deployment_groups(
            applicationName=autoscaling_config['application_name'],
            deploymentGroupNames=autoscaling_config['deployment_groups']
        )
        
        groups_info = response.get('deploymentGroupsInfo', [])
        
        # Analyze auto-scaling configurations
        autoscaling_analysis = analyze_autoscaling_configs(groups_info, autoscaling_client)
        
        # Get CloudWatch metrics
        metrics_analysis = get_cloudwatch_metrics(groups_info, cloudwatch_client)
        
        # Generate scaling recommendations
        scaling_recommendations = generate_scaling_recommendations(
            autoscaling_analysis, metrics_analysis
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Analyzed {len(groups_info)} auto-scaling deployment groups")
        
        return {
            'groups_info': groups_info,
            'autoscaling_analysis': autoscaling_analysis,
            'metrics_analysis': metrics_analysis,
            'scaling_recommendations': scaling_recommendations
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed to retrieve auto-scaling groups: {str(e)}")
        return None

def analyze_autoscaling_configs(groups_info: List[Dict], autoscaling_client):
    """Analyze auto-scaling group configurations"""
    analysis = {
        'asg_details': {},
        'scaling_policies': {},
        'lifecycle_hooks': {},
        'capacity_analysis': {}
    }
    
    for group in groups_info:
        group_name = group.get('deploymentGroupName', '')
        asg_list = group.get('autoScalingGroups', [])
        
        for asg in asg_list:
            asg_name = asg.get('name', '')
            
            try:
                # Get ASG details
                asg_response = autoscaling_client.describe_auto_scaling_groups(
                    AutoScalingGroupNames=[asg_name]
                )
                
                if asg_response['AutoScalingGroups']:
                    asg_details = asg_response['AutoScalingGroups'][0]
                    
                    analysis['asg_details'][asg_name] = {
                        'min_size': asg_details.get('MinSize', 0),
                        'max_size': asg_details.get('MaxSize', 0),
                        'desired_capacity': asg_details.get('DesiredCapacity', 0),
                        'availability_zones': asg_details.get('AvailabilityZones', []),
                        'health_check_type': asg_details.get('HealthCheckType', ''),
                        'health_check_grace_period': asg_details.get('HealthCheckGracePeriod', 0)
                    }
                
                # Get scaling policies
                policies_response = autoscaling_client.describe_policies(
                    AutoScalingGroupName=asg_name
                )
                
                analysis['scaling_policies'][asg_name] = [
                    {
                        'policy_name': policy.get('PolicyName', ''),
                        'policy_type': policy.get('PolicyType', ''),
                        'adjustment_type': policy.get('AdjustmentType', ''),
                        'cooldown': policy.get('Cooldown', 0)
                    }
                    for policy in policies_response.get('ScalingPolicies', [])
                ]
                
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Warning: Could not analyze ASG {asg_name}: {str(e)}")
                continue
    
    return analysis

def get_cloudwatch_metrics(groups_info: List[Dict], cloudwatch_client):
    """Get CloudWatch metrics for deployment groups"""
    metrics = {
        'cpu_utilization': {},
        'memory_utilization': {},
        'network_io': {},
        'alarm_status': {}
    }
    
    for group in groups_info:
        group_name = group.get('deploymentGroupName', '')
        alarm_config = group.get('alarmConfiguration', {})
        
        if alarm_config.get('enabled'):
            alarms = alarm_config.get('alarms', [])
            
            for alarm in alarms:
                alarm_name = alarm.get('name', '')
                
                try:
                    # Get alarm state
                    alarm_response = cloudwatch_client.describe_alarms(
                        AlarmNames=[alarm_name]
                    )
                    
                    if alarm_response['MetricAlarms']:
                        alarm_details = alarm_response['MetricAlarms'][0]
                        
                        metrics['alarm_status'][alarm_name] = {
                            'state': alarm_details.get('StateValue', ''),
                            'reason': alarm_details.get('StateReason', ''),
                            'metric_name': alarm_details.get('MetricName', ''),
                            'threshold': alarm_details.get('Threshold', 0)
                        }
                
                except Exception as e:
                    print(f"Warning: Could not get alarm {alarm_name}: {str(e)}")
                    continue
    
    return metrics

def generate_scaling_recommendations(autoscaling_analysis: Dict, metrics_analysis: Dict):
    """Generate scaling recommendations based on analysis"""
    recommendations = {
        'capacity_recommendations': [],
        'policy_recommendations': [],
        'monitoring_recommendations': [],
        'cost_optimization': []
    }
    
    # Analyze capacity settings
    for asg_name, details in autoscaling_analysis['asg_details'].items():
        min_size = details.get('min_size', 0)
        max_size = details.get('max_size', 0)
        desired = details.get('desired_capacity', 0)
        
        # Capacity recommendations
        if max_size - min_size < 3:
            recommendations['capacity_recommendations'].append({
                'asg': asg_name,
                'recommendation': 'Increase scaling range for better elasticity',
                'suggested_min': min_size,
                'suggested_max': max_size + 5
            })
        
        if desired == max_size:
            recommendations['capacity_recommendations'].append({
                'asg': asg_name,
                'recommendation': 'Consider increasing max capacity to handle traffic spikes',
                'current_max': max_size,
                'suggested_max': max_size * 2
            })
    
    # Policy recommendations
    for asg_name, policies in autoscaling_analysis['scaling_policies'].items():
        if not policies:
            recommendations['policy_recommendations'].append({
                'asg': asg_name,
                'recommendation': 'Configure automatic scaling policies',
                'suggested_policies': ['target_tracking_cpu', 'target_tracking_memory']
            })
    
    # Monitoring recommendations
    recommendations['monitoring_recommendations'] = [
        'Enable detailed CloudWatch monitoring',
        'Set up custom Django application metrics',
        'Configure ALB target health monitoring',
        'Implement database connection monitoring'
    ]
    
    # Cost optimization
    recommendations['cost_optimization'] = [
        'Use mixed instance types in ASG',
        'Implement predictive scaling for known traffic patterns',
        'Consider spot instances for non-critical workloads',
        'Right-size instances based on actual utilization'
    ]
    
    return recommendations

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = get_django_autoscaling_groups()
    if result:
        print("🎉 Auto-scaling deployment groups analyzed")
    else:
        print("❌ Failed to analyze auto-scaling groups")

    batch-get-deployment-instances

    Get details about deployment instances in CodeDeploy deployments for your Django jobpost application. This command provides instance-level deployment information including status, lifecycle events, and health data for EC2 instances running Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services.

    Syntax
    aws deploy batch-get-deployment-instances \
  --deployment-id {deployment-id} \
  --instance-ids {instance-id} [{instance-id}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]

    Parameters:

    • --deployment-id (string): The unique deployment identifier.
    • --instance-ids (list): List of instance IDs to retrieve information for.

    Attributes:

    • instancesSummary: Summary information about deployment instances
    • deploymentInfo: Details about the deployment
    • instanceStatus: Status of each instance in the deployment

    Methods:

    • get_instance_details()
    • monitor_instance_deployment()
    • validate_instance_health()

    Return Value:

    Returns detailed information about specific instances in a deployment.

    Examples:

    Example 1: Get Django Deployment Instance Status

    Retrieve status information for specific Django deployment instances.

    # Get Django deployment instance status
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-instances \
  --deployment-id d-A1B2C3D4E \
  --instance-ids i-0123456789abcdef0 i-0987654321fedcba0 i-0abcdef123456789 \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "instancesSummary": [
        {
            "deploymentId": "d-A1B2C3D4E",
            "instanceId": "i-0123456789abcdef0",
            "status": "Succeeded",
            "lastUpdatedAt": 1735689600.0,
            "lifecycleEvents": [
                {
                    "lifecycleEventName": "ApplicationStart",
                    "status": "Succeeded",
                    "startTime": 1735689300.0,
                    "endTime": 1735689400.0
                },
                {
                    "lifecycleEventName": "ApplicationStop",
                    "status": "Succeeded",
                    "startTime": 1735689200.0,
                    "endTime": 1735689250.0
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "deploymentId": "d-A1B2C3D4E",
            "instanceId": "i-0987654321fedcba0",
            "status": "InProgress",
            "lastUpdatedAt": 1735689500.0,
            "lifecycleEvents": [
                {
                    "lifecycleEventName": "ApplicationStart",
                    "status": "InProgress",
                    "startTime": 1735689400.0
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}
    Django Integration:

    Django deployment instance monitoring:

    # Django deployment instance monitoring
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def get_django_deployment_instances():
    """Get Django deployment instance status with detailed monitoring"""
    
    deployment_config = {
        'deployment_id': 'd-A1B2C3D4E',
        'instance_ids': [
            'i-0123456789abcdef0',  # Web server 1
            'i-0987654321fedcba0',  # Web server 2
            'i-0abcdef123456789'   # Web server 3
        ],
        'django_app': 'social-platform',
        'monitoring_enabled': True
    }
    
    try:
        print(f"🖥️ Monitoring Django deployment instances")
        
        codedeploy_client = boto3.client('deploy')
        
        response = codedeploy_client.batch_get_deployment_instances(
            deploymentId=deployment_config['deployment_id'],
            instanceIds=deployment_config['instance_ids']
        )
        
        instances_summary = response.get('instancesSummary', [])
        
        # Analyze instance deployment status
        deployment_analysis = analyze_instance_deployment_status(instances_summary)
        
        # Monitor lifecycle events
        lifecycle_monitoring = monitor_lifecycle_events(instances_summary)
        
        # Check Django application health
        django_health = check_django_application_health(deployment_analysis)
        
        print(f"✅ Monitored {len(instances_summary)} Django instances")
        
        return {
            'instances_summary': instances_summary,
            'deployment_analysis': deployment_analysis,
            'lifecycle_monitoring': lifecycle_monitoring,
            'django_health': django_health
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed to retrieve deployment instances: {str(e)}")
        return None

def analyze_instance_deployment_status(instances_summary: list):
    """Analyze deployment status across instances"""
    analysis = {
        'total_instances': len(instances_summary),
        'status_distribution': {},
        'deployment_progress': {},
        'failed_instances': [],
        'successful_instances': []
    }
    
    for instance in instances_summary:
        instance_id = instance.get('instanceId', '')
        status = instance.get('status', '')
        
        # Count status distribution
        analysis['status_distribution'][status] = analysis['status_distribution'].get(status, 0) + 1
        
        # Track progress for each instance
        lifecycle_events = instance.get('lifecycleEvents', [])
        completed_events = sum(1 for event in lifecycle_events if event.get('status') == 'Succeeded')
        total_events = len(lifecycle_events) if lifecycle_events else 1
        
        analysis['deployment_progress'][instance_id] = {
            'status': status,
            'progress_percentage': (completed_events / total_events) * 100 if total_events > 0 else 0,
            'completed_events': completed_events,
            'total_events': total_events
        }
        
        # Categorize instances
        if status == 'Failed':
            analysis['failed_instances'].append(instance_id)
        elif status == 'Succeeded':
            analysis['successful_instances'].append(instance_id)
    
    return analysis

def monitor_lifecycle_events(instances_summary: list):
    """Monitor lifecycle events across instances"""
    monitoring = {
        'lifecycle_stages': {},
        'event_timeline': [],
        'performance_metrics': {},
        'critical_events': []
    }
    
    # Define Django-specific lifecycle stages
    django_lifecycle_stages = [
        'ApplicationStop',
        'DownloadBundle',
        'BeforeInstall',
        'Install',
        'AfterInstall',
        'ApplicationStart',
        'ValidateService'
    ]
    
    for stage in django_lifecycle_stages:
        monitoring['lifecycle_stages'][stage] = {
            'completed': 0,
            'failed': 0,
            'in_progress': 0,
            'average_duration': 0
        }
    
    # Process each instance's events
    for instance in instances_summary:
        instance_id = instance.get('instanceId', '')
        lifecycle_events = instance.get('lifecycleEvents', [])
        
        for event in lifecycle_events:
            event_name = event.get('lifecycleEventName', '')
            status = event.get('status', '')
            start_time = event.get('startTime')
            end_time = event.get('endTime')
            
            if event_name in monitoring['lifecycle_stages']:
                # Count status
                if status == 'Succeeded':
                    monitoring['lifecycle_stages'][event_name]['completed'] += 1
                elif status == 'Failed':
                    monitoring['lifecycle_stages'][event_name]['failed'] += 1
                    monitoring['critical_events'].append({
                        'instance_id': instance_id,
                        'event': event_name,
                        'status': status,
                        'error_code': event.get('diagnostics', {}).get('errorCode', '')
                    })
                elif status == 'InProgress':
                    monitoring['lifecycle_stages'][event_name]['in_progress'] += 1
                
                # Calculate duration
                if start_time and end_time:
                    duration = end_time - start_time
                    monitoring['event_timeline'].append({
                        'instance_id': instance_id,
                        'event': event_name,
                        'duration': duration,
                        'timestamp': datetime.fromtimestamp(start_time, timezone.utc)
                    })
    
    return monitoring

def check_django_application_health(deployment_analysis: dict):
    """Check Django application health after deployment"""
    health_check = {
        'overall_health': 'Unknown',
        'healthy_instances': 0,
        'unhealthy_instances': 0,
        'health_checks': {},
        'recommendations': []
    }
    
    successful_instances = len(deployment_analysis['successful_instances'])
    failed_instances = len(deployment_analysis['failed_instances'])
    total_instances = deployment_analysis['total_instances']
    
    # Calculate health metrics
    health_check['healthy_instances'] = successful_instances
    health_check['unhealthy_instances'] = failed_instances
    
    # Determine overall health
    if successful_instances == total_instances:
        health_check['overall_health'] = 'Healthy'
    elif successful_instances > total_instances * 0.7:
        health_check['overall_health'] = 'Degraded'
    else:
        health_check['overall_health'] = 'Unhealthy'
    
    # Django-specific health checks
    health_check['health_checks'] = {
        'django_server_running': successful_instances > 0,
        'database_connectivity': successful_instances > 0,  # Simplified check
        'static_files_serving': True,  # Assumed if deployment succeeded
        'cache_connectivity': True     # Assumed if deployment succeeded
    }
    
    # Generate recommendations
    if failed_instances > 0:
        health_check['recommendations'].append(
            f"Investigate {failed_instances} failed instances"
        )
    
    if successful_instances < total_instances:
        health_check['recommendations'].append(
            "Consider rolling back deployment if critical functionality is affected"
        )
    
    if health_check['overall_health'] != 'Healthy':
        health_check['recommendations'].append(
            "Monitor application metrics and user experience closely"
        )
    
    return health_check

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = get_django_deployment_instances()
    if result:
        print("🎉 Django deployment instances monitored successfully")
    else:
        print("❌ Failed to monitor deployment instances")
    Example 2: Get Django Blue-Green Deployment Instances

    Monitor instances during Django blue-green deployment with fleet comparison.

    # Get Django blue-green deployment instances
aws deploy batch-get-deployment-instances \
  --deployment-id d-B2C3D4E5F \
  --instance-ids i-blue01 i-blue02 i-green01 i-green02 \
  --region us-east-1
    {
    "instancesSummary": [
        {
            "deploymentId": "d-B2C3D4E5F",
            "instanceId": "i-blue01",
