            'total_capacity': region_data['total_instances'],
            'availability_percentage': round((region_data['active_instances'] / region_data['total_instances']) * 100, 2) if region_data['total_instances'] > 0 else 0
        }
    
    # Django global state analysis
    user_count = User.objects.count()
    jobpost_count = JobPost.objects.count()
    company_count = Company.objects.count()
    application_count = Application.objects.count()
    
    # Estimate regional data distribution
    locations_by_region = {
        'us-east': Location.objects.filter(country__in=['US', 'CA']).count(),
        'eu-west': Location.objects.filter(country__in=['GB', 'DE', 'FR', 'IT', 'ES']).count(),
        'ap-south': Location.objects.filter(country__in=['IN', 'SG', 'AU', 'JP']).count()
    }
    
    regional_coordination['django_global_state'] = {
        'total_users_global': user_count,
        'total_jobposts_global': jobpost_count,
        'total_companies_global': company_count,
        'total_applications_global': application_count,
        'regional_data_distribution': locations_by_region,
        'data_localization_compliance': {
            'gdpr_compliant': locations_by_region['eu-west'] > 0,
            'ccpa_compliant': locations_by_region['us-east'] > 0,
            'data_residency_configured': True
        },
        'cross_region_sync_required': sum(locations_by_region.values()) != max(locations_by_region.values()) * len(locations_by_region)
    }
    
    # Deployment synchronization planning
    deployment_windows = []
    for region, region_data in regional_coordination['regions'].items():
        if region != 'global' and region_data['deployment_window']:
            deployment_windows.append({
                'region': region,
                'window': region_data['deployment_window'],
                'local_time': region_data['local_time']
            })
    
    regional_coordination['deployment_synchronization'] = {
        'coordinated_deployment_possible': len(deployment_windows) == len([r for r in regional_coordination['regions'] if r != 'global']),
        'deployment_windows': deployment_windows,
        'recommended_sequence': [
            'ap-south (deploys first - IST timezone)',
            'eu-west (deploys second - GMT timezone)', 
            'us-east (deploys last - EST timezone)'
        ],
        'rollback_coordination_time': '4-6 hours for global rollback',
        'blue_green_feasibility': total_web_servers >= 6  # Need at least 2 servers per region for blue-green
    }
    
    # Performance metrics
    avg_uptime = sum(instance_data['uptime_hours'] for region_data in regional_coordination['regions'].values() 
                    for instance_data in region_data['instances'] if instance_data['uptime_hours']) / \
                sum(1 for region_data in regional_coordination['regions'].values() 
                   for instance_data in region_data['instances'] if instance_data['uptime_hours'])
    
    regional_coordination['performance_metrics'] = {
        'average_uptime_hours': round(avg_uptime, 2),
        'global_availability_target': 99.9,
        'current_availability_estimate': round((regional_coordination['cross_region_analysis']['total_web_servers'] / (regional_coordination['cross_region_analysis']['total_web_servers'] + 0.1)) * 100, 2),
        'latency_optimization_needed': True,
        'cdn_coverage': len(regional_coordination['regions'].get('global', {}).get('instances', [])) > 0
    }
    
    # Failover planning
    regional_coordination['failover_planning'] = {
        'primary_region': 'us-east',
        'secondary_regions': ['eu-west', 'ap-south'],
        'failover_scenarios': [
            {
                'scenario': 'us-east region failure',
                'failover_target': 'eu-west',
                'estimated_rto': '15 minutes',
                'estimated_rpo': '5 minutes',
                'data_sync_required': True
            },
            {
                'scenario': 'eu-west region failure',
                'failover_target': 'us-east',
                'estimated_rto': '10 minutes',
                'estimated_rpo': '5 minutes',
                'data_sync_required': True
            },
            {
                'scenario': 'ap-south region failure',
                'failover_target': 'eu-west',
                'estimated_rto': '20 minutes',
                'estimated_rpo': '10 minutes',
                'data_sync_required': True
            }
        ],
        'automated_failover_configured': False,
        'manual_failover_procedures': 'Documented and tested monthly'
    }
    
    # Generate optimization recommendations
    if regional_coordination['performance_metrics']['current_availability_estimate'] < 99.9:
        regional_coordination['optimization_recommendations'].append('Add redundancy to achieve 99.9% availability target')
    
    if not regional_coordination['deployment_synchronization']['blue_green_feasibility']:
        regional_coordination['optimization_recommendations'].append('Scale web servers to enable blue-green deployments')
    
    if regional_coordination['django_global_state']['cross_region_sync_required']:
        regional_coordination['optimization_recommendations'].append('Implement data synchronization across regions')
    
    if not regional_coordination['failover_planning']['automated_failover_configured']:
        regional_coordination['optimization_recommendations'].append('Implement automated failover mechanisms')
    
    regional_coordination['optimization_recommendations'].extend([
        'Configure global load balancing',
        'Implement cross-region monitoring',
        'Set up automated deployment coordination',
        'Establish data residency compliance procedures'
    ])
    
    return regional_coordination

# Execute multi-region coordination analysis
coordination_data = coordinate_multi_region_on_premises()

print("\nMulti-Region On-Premises Coordination Analysis:")
print(f"Deployment: {coordination_data['global_deployment_name']}")
print(f"Analysis Time: {coordination_data['coordination_timestamp']}")

print("\nRegional Status:")
for region, data in coordination_data['regions'].items():
    if region == 'global':
        print(f"  • Global Services: {len(data['instances'])} instances")
    else:
        print(f"  • {region.upper()}: {data['active_instances']}/{data['total_instances']} active ({data['registration_health']})")
        print(f"    Local Time: {data['local_time']}")
        print(f"    Deployment Window: {data['deployment_window']}")
        print(f"    Web Servers: {len(data['web_servers'])}, Database Servers: {len(data['database_servers'])}")

print("\nCross-Region Analysis:")
print(f"  • Total Regions: {coordination_data['cross_region_analysis']['total_regions']}")
print(f"  • Total Web Servers: {coordination_data['cross_region_analysis']['total_web_servers']}")
print(f"  • Total Database Servers: {coordination_data['cross_region_analysis']['total_database_servers']}")
print(f"  • Blue-Green Feasible: {'✅' if coordination_data['deployment_synchronization']['blue_green_feasibility'] else '❌'}")

print("\nDjango Global State:")
print(f"  • Total Users: {coordination_data['django_global_state']['total_users_global']:,}")
print(f"  • Total Job Posts: {coordination_data['django_global_state']['total_jobposts_global']:,}")
print(f"  • Regional Data Distribution:")
for region, count in coordination_data['django_global_state']['regional_data_distribution'].items():
    print(f"    - {region}: {count:,} locations")
print(f"  • GDPR Compliant: {'✅' if coordination_data['django_global_state']['data_localization_compliance']['gdpr_compliant'] else '❌'}")

print("\nDeployment Coordination:")
print(f"  • Coordinated Deployment: {'✅' if coordination_data['deployment_synchronization']['coordinated_deployment_possible'] else '❌'}")
print("  • Recommended Sequence:")
for i, step in enumerate(coordination_data['deployment_synchronization']['recommended_sequence'], 1):
    print(f"    {i}. {step}")

print("\nFailover Planning:")
print(f"  • Primary Region: {coordination_data['failover_planning']['primary_region']}")
print(f"  • Automated Failover: {'✅' if coordination_data['failover_planning']['automated_failover_configured'] else '❌'}")
for scenario in coordination_data['failover_planning']['failover_scenarios'][:2]:
    print(f"  • {scenario['scenario']}: RTO {scenario['estimated_rto']}, RPO {scenario['estimated_rpo']}")

print("\nOptimization Recommendations:")
for i, rec in enumerate(coordination_data['optimization_recommendations'][:5], 1):
    print(f"  {i}. {rec}")

    Example 5: On-Premises Instance Lifecycle Management

    # Manage on-premises instance lifecycle for Django application deployment
aws deploy batch-get-on-premises-instances \
    --instance-names "django-migrate-web-01" "django-migrate-worker-01" "django-migrate-db-01" \
    --output json

# Django equivalent for comprehensive lifecycle management
from myapp.models import User, JobPost, Company, Location, Skill, Application
from django.db import connection
from django.core.management import call_command
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import json
from collections import defaultdict

def manage_instance_lifecycle():
    """Comprehensive lifecycle management for on-premises Django instances"""
    client = boto3.client('codedeploy')
    
    # Get instances for lifecycle management
    response = client.batch_get_on_premises_instances(
        instanceNames=[
            'django-migrate-web-01',
            'django-migrate-worker-01', 
            'django-migrate-db-01',
            'django-migrate-cache-01',
            'django-migrate-monitor-01',
            'django-legacy-web-01',  # To be decommissioned
            'django-legacy-worker-01'  # To be decommissioned
        ]
    )
    
    lifecycle_management = {
        'management_timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        'lifecycle_phase': 'Django-Application-Migration',
        'instance_inventory': {},
        'lifecycle_stages': {},
        'migration_planning': {},
        'capacity_management': {},
        'data_migration_status': {},
        'django_application_continuity': {},
        'decommission_planning': {},
        'optimization_insights': {},
        'action_plan': []
    }
    
    # Analyze each instance for lifecycle stage
    for instance_info in response['instanceInfoList']:
        instance_name = instance_info['instanceName']
        register_time = instance_info.get('registerTime')
        deregister_time = instance_info.get('deregisterTime')
        iam_session_arn = instance_info.get('iamSessionArn')
        tags = instance_info.get('tags', [])
        
        # Determine instance purpose and lifecycle stage
        if 'migrate' in instance_name:
            instance_type = 'migration_target'
            lifecycle_stage = 'provisioning' if register_time and (datetime.utcnow() - register_time).total_seconds() < 86400 else 'active'
        elif 'legacy' in instance_name:
            instance_type = 'legacy_source'
            lifecycle_stage = 'decommission_planned' if not deregister_time else 'decommissioned'
        else:
            instance_type = 'standard'
            lifecycle_stage = 'active' if not deregister_time else 'decommissioned'
        
        instance_data = {
            'name': instance_name,
            'type': instance_type,
            'service_role': 'web' if 'web' in instance_name else 'worker' if 'worker' in instance_name else 'database' if 'db' in instance_name else 'cache' if 'cache' in instance_name else 'monitoring',
            'lifecycle_stage': lifecycle_stage,
            'registration_time': register_time,
            'deregistration_time': deregister_time,
            'age_days': None,
            'iam_configured': bool(iam_session_arn),
            'deployment_ready': False,
            'migration_priority': 'high',
            'capacity_metrics': {},
            'health_status': 'unknown'
        }
        
        # Calculate age and deployment readiness
        if register_time:
            if deregister_time:
                age = (deregister_time - register_time).total_seconds() / 86400
            else:
                age = (datetime.utcnow() - register_time).total_seconds() / 86400
            instance_data['age_days'] = round(age, 2)
            
            # Determine deployment readiness
            instance_data['deployment_ready'] = (
                not deregister_time and
                bool(iam_session_arn) and
                age >= 0.1  # At least 2.4 hours old
            )
            
            # Set migration priority
            if instance_type == 'legacy_source':
                instance_data['migration_priority'] = 'high'  # Legacy needs to be migrated first
            elif instance_type == 'migration_target':
                if instance_data['service_role'] in ['database', 'cache']:
                    instance_data['migration_priority'] = 'critical'  # Data services first
                elif instance_data['service_role'] == 'web':
                    instance_data['migration_priority'] = 'medium'   # Web services after data
                else:
                    instance_data['migration_priority'] = 'low'     # Supporting services last
        
        # Set health status based on multiple factors
        if deregister_time:
            instance_data['health_status'] = 'decommissioned'
        elif not iam_session_arn:
            instance_data['health_status'] = 'configuration_needed'
        elif not instance_data['deployment_ready']:
            instance_data['health_status'] = 'provisioning'
        else:
            instance_data['health_status'] = 'healthy'
        
        lifecycle_management['instance_inventory'][instance_name] = instance_data
    
    # Group instances by lifecycle stage
    stage_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for instance_name, instance_data in lifecycle_management['instance_inventory'].items():
        stage_groups[instance_data['lifecycle_stage']].append(instance_data)
    
    lifecycle_management['lifecycle_stages'] = {
        stage: {
            'instance_count': len(instances),
            'instances': [inst['name'] for inst in instances],
            'health_summary': defaultdict(int),
            'migration_priority_summary': defaultdict(int),
            'estimated_migration_time': 0
        }
        for stage, instances in stage_groups.items()
    }
    
    # Calculate summaries for each stage
    for stage, stage_data in lifecycle_management['lifecycle_stages'].items():
        instances = stage_groups[stage]
        
        for instance in instances:
            stage_data['health_summary'][instance['health_status']] += 1
            stage_data['migration_priority_summary'][instance['migration_priority']] += 1
        
        # Estimate migration time based on service types
        db_instances = len([i for i in instances if i['service_role'] == 'database'])
        web_instances = len([i for i in instances if i['service_role'] == 'web'])
        worker_instances = len([i for i in instances if i['service_role'] == 'worker'])
        
        # Time estimates: DB=4h, Web=2h, Worker=1h, Others=0.5h
        estimated_time = (db_instances * 4) + (web_instances * 2) + (worker_instances * 1) + ((len(instances) - db_instances - web_instances - worker_instances) * 0.5)
        stage_data['estimated_migration_time'] = estimated_time
    
    # Django application continuity analysis
    user_count = User.objects.count()
    active_jobposts = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count()
    recent_applications = Application.objects.filter(applied_at__gte=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=7)).count()
    
    # Assess current Django application state
    migration_targets = [inst for inst in lifecycle_management['instance_inventory'].values() if inst['type'] == 'migration_target']
    legacy_sources = [inst for inst in lifecycle_management['instance_inventory'].values() if inst['type'] == 'legacy_source']
    
    lifecycle_management['django_application_continuity'] = {
        'current_user_count': user_count,
        'active_jobposts': active_jobposts,
        'recent_applications': recent_applications,
        'daily_activity_impact': {
            'users_potentially_affected': user_count,
            'jobposts_requiring_migration': active_jobposts,
            'applications_in_transition': recent_applications,
            'estimated_downtime_minutes': len(migration_targets) * 30  # 30 min per target instance
        },
        'service_continuity_plan': {
            'web_service_redundancy': len([i for i in migration_targets if i['service_role'] == 'web']),
            'database_backup_strategy': 'hot_standby' if len([i for i in migration_targets if i['service_role'] == 'database']) > 0 else 'cold_backup',
            'worker_process_continuity': len([i for i in migration_targets if i['service_role'] == 'worker']) > 0,
            'zero_downtime_possible': len(migration_targets) >= len(legacy_sources)
        },
        'data_consistency_requirements': {
            'user_profile_sync': True,
            'jobpost_content_sync': True,
            'application_state_sync': True,
            'media_file_migration': True,
            'session_preservation': False  # Sessions will be reset during migration
        }
    }
    
    # Migration planning
    migration_phases = []
    
    # Phase 1: Database and Cache Migration
    db_cache_instances = [inst for inst in migration_targets if inst['service_role'] in ['database', 'cache']]
    if db_cache_instances:
        migration_phases.append({
            'phase': 1,
            'name': 'Data Layer Migration',
            'instances': [inst['name'] for inst in db_cache_instances],
            'estimated_duration': '4-6 hours',
            'django_impact': 'Read-only mode during migration',
            'prerequisites': ['Database backup verification', 'Cache warming preparation'],
            'rollback_time': '2 hours'
        })
    
    # Phase 2: Web Services Migration  
    web_instances = [inst for inst in migration_targets if inst['service_role'] == 'web']
    if web_instances:
        migration_phases.append({
            'phase': 2,
            'name': 'Web Services Migration',
            'instances': [inst['name'] for inst in web_instances],
            'estimated_duration': '2-3 hours',
            'django_impact': 'Gradual traffic shift with load balancer',
            'prerequisites': ['Database migration complete', 'Load balancer configuration'],
            'rollback_time': '30 minutes'
        })
    
    # Phase 3: Worker and Supporting Services
    worker_support_instances = [inst for inst in migration_targets if inst['service_role'] in ['worker', 'monitoring']]
    if worker_support_instances:
        migration_phases.append({
            'phase': 3,
            'name': 'Background Services Migration',
            'instances': [inst['name'] for inst in worker_support_instances],
            'estimated_duration': '1-2 hours',
            'django_impact': 'Background job processing may be delayed',
            'prerequisites': ['Web services stable', 'Queue system ready'],
            'rollback_time': '15 minutes'
        })
    
    lifecycle_management['migration_planning'] = {
        'total_phases': len(migration_phases),
        'migration_phases': migration_phases,
        'total_estimated_time': sum(int(phase['estimated_duration'].split('-')[1].split(' ')[0]) for phase in migration_phases),
        'maintenance_window_required': '8-12 hours',
        'business_impact_assessment': {
            'user_facing_downtime': '30-60 minutes',
            'admin_functionality_downtime': '4-6 hours',
            'background_processing_delay': '2-4 hours',
            'search_indexing_downtime': '1-2 hours'
        }
    }
    
    # Decommission planning for legacy instances
    lifecycle_management['decommission_planning'] = {
        'legacy_instances_count': len(legacy_sources),
        'decommission_timeline': [
            {'week': 1, 'action': 'Complete migration validation'},
            {'week': 2, 'action': 'Redirect all traffic to new instances'},
            {'week': 3, 'action': 'Monitor for issues and performance'},
            {'week': 4, 'action': 'Begin deregistration of legacy instances'},
            {'week': 6, 'action': 'Complete data archival and cleanup'},
            {'week': 8, 'action': 'Full decommissioning of legacy hardware'}
        ],
        'data_retention_requirements': {
            'audit_logs': '7 years',
            'user_data_backup': '5 years', 
            'application_metrics': '2 years',
            'deployment_history': '1 year'
        },
        'cost_savings_estimate': {
            'monthly_hosting_reduction': len(legacy_sources) * 500,  # $500 per instance
            'annual_maintenance_savings': len(legacy_sources) * 2000,  # $2000 per instance
            'power_consumption_reduction': f"{len(legacy_sources) * 1.5:.1f} kW/month"
        }
    }
    
    # Generate action plan
    lifecycle_management['action_plan'] = [
        'Week 1: Complete pre-migration testing and validation',
        f"Week 2-3: Execute {len(migration_phases)} migration phases",
        'Week 4: Post-migration monitoring and optimization',
        'Week 5-6: Legacy instance traffic redirection',
        'Week 7-8: Legacy instance decommissioning',
        'Week 9: Migration post-mortem and documentation',
        'Week 10+: Ongoing optimization and capacity planning'
    ]
    
    if any(inst['health_status'] == 'configuration_needed' for inst in lifecycle_management['instance_inventory'].values()):
        lifecycle_management['action_plan'].insert(0, 'Immediate: Complete IAM configuration for all instances')
    
    if lifecycle_management['django_application_continuity']['service_continuity_plan']['zero_downtime_possible']:
        lifecycle_management['action_plan'].insert(1, 'Priority: Implement zero-downtime migration strategy')
    
    return lifecycle_management

# Execute lifecycle management analysis
lifecycle_data = manage_instance_lifecycle()

print("\nOn-Premises Instance Lifecycle Management:")
print(f"Management Phase: {lifecycle_data['lifecycle_phase']}")
print(f"Analysis Time: {lifecycle_data['management_timestamp']}")

print("\nInstance Inventory:")
for stage, stage_data in lifecycle_data['lifecycle_stages'].items():
    print(f"  • {stage.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {stage_data['instance_count']} instances")
    print(f"    Instances: {', '.join(stage_data['instances'])}")
    print(f"    Estimated Migration Time: {stage_data['estimated_migration_time']} hours")

print("\nDjango Application Continuity:")
print(f"  • Current Users: {lifecycle_data['django_application_continuity']['current_user_count']:,}")
print(f"  • Active Job Posts: {lifecycle_data['django_application_continuity']['active_jobposts']:,}")
print(f"  • Recent Applications: {lifecycle_data['django_application_continuity']['recent_applications']:,}")
print(f"  • Estimated Downtime: {lifecycle_data['django_application_continuity']['daily_activity_impact']['estimated_downtime_minutes']} minutes")
print(f"  • Zero Downtime Possible: {'✅' if lifecycle_data['django_application_continuity']['service_continuity_plan']['zero_downtime_possible'] else '❌'}")

print("\nMigration Planning:")
print(f"  • Total Phases: {lifecycle_data['migration_planning']['total_phases']}")
print(f"  • Total Time: {lifecycle_data['migration_planning']['total_estimated_time']} hours")
print(f"  • Maintenance Window: {lifecycle_data['migration_planning']['maintenance_window_required']}")

for phase in lifecycle_data['migration_planning']['migration_phases']:
    print(f"  • Phase {phase['phase']}: {phase['name']} ({phase['estimated_duration']})")
    print(f"    Instances: {', '.join(phase['instances'])}")
    print(f"    Django Impact: {phase['django_impact']}")

print("\nDecommission Planning:")
print(f"  • Legacy Instances: {lifecycle_data['decommission_planning']['legacy_instances_count']}")
print(f"  • Monthly Savings: ${lifecycle_data['decommission_planning']['cost_savings_estimate']['monthly_hosting_reduction']:,}")
print(f"  • Annual Savings: ${lifecycle_data['decommission_planning']['cost_savings_estimate']['annual_maintenance_savings']:,}")
print(f"  • Power Reduction: {lifecycle_data['decommission_planning']['cost_savings_estimate']['power_consumption_reduction']}")

print("\nAction Plan:")
for i, action in enumerate(lifecycle_data['action_plan'], 1):
    print(f"  {i}. {action}")

    create-application

    Full description: Creates a new application in CodeDeploy. Applications are logical groupings of deployment configurations, deployment groups, and revisions. This command creates the foundation for organizing your Django application deployments, enabling version control and deployment management across different environments and infrastructure types.

    Syntax:

    aws deploy create-application \
  --application-name {application-name} \
  [--compute-platform {compute-platform}] \
  [--tags Key={key},Value={value}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]

    Parameters description:

    • --application-name: Name of the application to create (must be unique within AWS account and region)
    • --compute-platform: Compute platform for deployment (Server, Lambda, or ECS)
    • --tags: Metadata tags to assign to the application for organization and billing
    • --cli-input-json: JSON string or file path for input parameters
    • --generate-cli-skeleton: Prints skeleton structure without making API call

    Attributes:

    • applicationId: Unique identifier assigned to the application
    • applicationName: Name of the created application
    • createTime: Timestamp when application was created
    • linkedToGitHub: Whether application is linked to GitHub repository
    • gitHubAccountName: GitHub account name (if linked)
    • computePlatform: The compute platform (Server, Lambda, or ECS)

    Methods:

    • create_application(): Creates new application with specified configuration
    • validate_application_name(): Validates application name meets requirements
    • assign_tags(): Adds metadata tags for organization
    • configure_compute_platform(): Sets appropriate compute platform
    • setup_github_integration(): Links application to GitHub repository

    Return value: Returns applicationId of the newly created application, which can be used for subsequent deployment operations and configuration management.

    Example 1: Create Django Job Portal Application

    # Create new Django job portal application in CodeDeploy
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name "django-job-portal" \
    --compute-platform "Server" \
    --tags Key="Environment",Value="Production" Key="Project",Value="JobPortal" Key="Framework",Value="Django"

# Django equivalent for application setup and validation
from myapp.models import User, JobPost, Company, Location, Skill, Application
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import connection
import boto3
from datetime import datetime
import json

def create_django_application_deployment():
    """Create Django job portal application in CodeDeploy with comprehensive setup"""
    client = boto3.client('codedeploy')
    
    application_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-job-portal',
        'compute_platform': 'Server',
        'environment': 'Production',
        'project_info': {
            'framework': 'Django',
            'version': '5.2.5',
            'python_version': '3.11+',
            'database': 'PostgreSQL',
            'cache': 'Redis'
        },
        'deployment_requirements': {
            'min_instances': 3,
            'load_balancer_required': True,
            'database_migrations': True,
            'static_files_deployment': True,
            'environment_variables_config': True
        }
    }
    
    try:
        # Create the application
        response = client.create_application(
            applicationName=application_config['application_name'],
            computePlatform=application_config['compute_platform'],
            tags=[
                {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': application_config['environment']},
                {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'JobPortal'},
                {'Key': 'Framework', 'Value': application_config['project_info']['framework']},
                {'Key': 'DjangoVersion', 'Value': application_config['project_info']['version']},
                {'Key': 'Database', 'Value': application_config['project_info']['database']},
                {'Key': 'Cache', 'Value': application_config['project_info']['cache']},
                {'Key': 'CreatedBy', 'Value': 'CodeDeploy-Django-Setup'},
                {'Key': 'LastModified', 'Value': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()}
            ]
        )
        
        application_id = response['applicationId']
        
        # Validate Django application readiness
        django_readiness = {
            'application_id': application_id,
            'application_name': application_config['application_name'],
            'models_validated': True,
            'database_connectivity': True,
            'static_files_ready': True,
            'environment_configuration': {},
            'deployment_validation': {}
        }
        
        # Check Django model structure
        model_counts = {
            'users': User.objects.count(),
            'job_posts': JobPost.objects.count(),
            'companies': Company.objects.count(),
            'locations': Location.objects.count(),
            'skills': Skill.objects.count(),
            'applications': Application.objects.count()
        }
        
        # Validate environment configuration
        django_readiness['environment_configuration'] = {
            'debug_mode': getattr(settings, 'DEBUG', True),
            'allowed_hosts': getattr(settings, 'ALLOWED_HOSTS', []),
            'database_engine': settings.DATABASES['default']['ENGINE'],
            'static_url': getattr(settings, 'STATIC_URL', '/static/'),
            'media_url': getattr(settings, 'MEDIA_URL', '/media/'),
            'secret_key_configured': bool(getattr(settings, 'SECRET_KEY', None)),
            'time_zone': getattr(settings, 'TIME_ZONE', 'UTC')
        }
        
        # Deployment validation checks
        deployment_checks = {
            'database_migrations_needed': False,
            'static_collection_needed': True,
            'requirements_updated': True,
            'nginx_configuration_ready': True,
            'gunicorn_configuration_ready': True,
            'supervisor_configuration_ready': True
        }
        
        # Check if migrations are needed
        try:
            with connection.cursor() as cursor:
                cursor.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM django_migrations")
                migration_count = cursor.fetchone()[0]
                deployment_checks['database_migrations_needed'] = migration_count == 0
        except Exception:
            deployment_checks['database_migrations_needed'] = True
        
        django_readiness['deployment_validation'] = deployment_checks
        
        # Generate deployment summary
        deployment_summary = {
            'application_created': True,
            'application_id': application_id,
            'django_models_count': model_counts,
            'readiness_score': 0,
            'critical_issues': [],
            'recommendations': []
        }
        
        # Calculate readiness score (0-100)
        score = 0
        
        # Environment checks (30 points)
        if not django_readiness['environment_configuration']['debug_mode']:
            score += 10
        if django_readiness['environment_configuration']['secret_key_configured']:
            score += 10
        if django_readiness['environment_configuration']['allowed_hosts']:
            score += 10
        
        # Deployment checks (40 points)
        deployment_ready_count = sum(1 for check, ready in deployment_checks.items() 
                                   if ready and check != 'database_migrations_needed')
        score += (deployment_ready_count / (len(deployment_checks) - 1)) * 40
        
        # Model data checks (30 points)
        if model_counts['users'] > 0:
            score += 10
        if model_counts['companies'] > 0:
            score += 10  
        if model_counts['job_posts'] > 0:
            score += 10
        
        deployment_summary['readiness_score'] = round(score, 2)
        
        # Generate recommendations
        if django_readiness['environment_configuration']['debug_mode']:
            deployment_summary['critical_issues'].append('DEBUG=True in production environment')
            deployment_summary['recommendations'].append('Set DEBUG=False for production deployment')
        
        if not django_readiness['environment_configuration']['allowed_hosts']:
            deployment_summary['critical_issues'].append('ALLOWED_HOSTS not configured')
            deployment_summary['recommendations'].append('Configure ALLOWED_HOSTS for production domains')
        
        if deployment_checks['database_migrations_needed']:
            deployment_summary['recommendations'].append('Run database migrations before deployment')
        
        if deployment_checks['static_collection_needed']:
            deployment_summary['recommendations'].append('Collect static files for production')
        
        deployment_summary['recommendations'].extend([
            'Configure automated deployment pipeline',
            'Set up monitoring and logging',
            'Implement health check endpoints',
            'Configure backup and recovery procedures'
        ])
        
        return {
            'codedeploy_response': response,
            'django_readiness': django_readiness,
            'deployment_summary': deployment_summary
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        return {
            'error': f"Failed to create application: {str(e)}",
            'application_config': application_config
        }

# Execute application creation
result = create_django_application_deployment()

if 'error' not in result:
    print("✅ Django Job Portal Application Created Successfully")
    print(f"Application ID: {result['codedeploy_response']['applicationId']}")
    print(f"Application Name: {result['deployment_summary']['application_id']}")
    
    print("\nDjango Model Data Summary:")
    for model, count in result['deployment_summary']['django_models_count'].items():
        print(f"  • {model.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {count:,}")
    
    print(f"\nDeployment Readiness Score: {result['deployment_summary']['readiness_score']}/100")
    
    print("\nEnvironment Configuration:")
    env_config = result['django_readiness']['environment_configuration']
    print(f"  • Debug Mode: {env_config['debug_mode']}")
    print(f"  • Database Engine: {env_config['database_engine']}")
    print(f"  • Allowed Hosts: {len(env_config['allowed_hosts'])} configured")
    print(f"  • Secret Key: {'✅ Configured' if env_config['secret_key_configured'] else '❌ Missing'}")
    
    if result['deployment_summary']['critical_issues']:
        print("\n⚠️ Critical Issues:")
        for issue in result['deployment_summary']['critical_issues']:
            print(f"  • {issue}")
    
    print("\n📋 Recommendations:")
    for i, rec in enumerate(result['deployment_summary']['recommendations'][:5], 1):
        print(f"  {i}. {rec}")
        
else:
    print(f"❌ Application Creation Failed: {result['error']}")
    print(f"Attempted Configuration: {result['application_config']}")

    Example 2: Multi-Environment Application Setup

    # Create Django application with multi-environment support and comprehensive tagging
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name "django-jobpost-multi-env" \
    --compute-platform "Server" \
    --tags Key="Environment",Value="Multi" Key="Project",Value="JobPost" Key="Framework",Value="Django" Key="Team",Value="DevOps" Key="CostCenter",Value="Engineering"

# Django equivalent for multi-environment application setup
from myapp.models import User, JobPost, Company, Location, Skill, Application
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import connection
from django.core.management import call_command
import boto3
from datetime import datetime
import json
from collections import defaultdict

def setup_multi_environment_application():
    """Set up Django application for multi-environment deployment (dev, staging, production)"""
    client = boto3.client('codedeploy')
    
    # Multi-environment configuration
    environments = {
        'development': {
            'application_name': 'django-jobpost-dev',
            'compute_platform': 'Server',
            'instance_requirements': {
                'min_instances': 1,
                'max_instances': 2,
                'instance_type': 't3.medium',
                'storage_gb': 20
            },
            'django_settings': {
                'debug': True,
                'log_level': 'DEBUG',
                'database_pool_size': 5,
                'cache_timeout': 300
            }
        },
        'staging': {
            'application_name': 'django-jobpost-staging',
            'compute_platform': 'Server',
            'instance_requirements': {
                'min_instances': 2,
                'max_instances': 4,
                'instance_type': 't3.large',
                'storage_gb': 50
            },
            'django_settings': {
                'debug': False,
                'log_level': 'INFO',
                'database_pool_size': 10,
                'cache_timeout': 600
            }
        },
        'production': {
            'application_name': 'django-jobpost-prod',
            'compute_platform': 'Server',
            'instance_requirements': {
                'min_instances': 3,
                'max_instances': 10,
                'instance_type': 'c5.xlarge',
                'storage_gb': 100
            },
            'django_settings': {
                'debug': False,
                'log_level': 'WARNING',
                'database_pool_size': 20,
                'cache_timeout': 3600
            }
        }
    }
    
    deployment_results = {
        'multi_env_setup': {
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'total_environments': len(environments),
            'environments_created': [],
            'environments_failed': [],
            'overall_success': True
        },
        'django_compatibility': {},
        'resource_planning': {},
        'deployment_strategy': {}
    }
    
    # Create applications for each environment
    for env_name, env_config in environments.items():
        try:
            response = client.create_application(
                applicationName=env_config['application_name'],
                computePlatform=env_config['compute_platform'],
                tags=[
                    {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': env_name.title()},
                    {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'JobPost'},
                    {'Key': 'Framework', 'Value': 'Django'},
                    {'Key': 'Team', 'Value': 'DevOps'},
                    {'Key': 'CostCenter', 'Value': 'Engineering'},
                    {'Key': 'AutoShutdown', 'Value': 'true' if env_name == 'development' else 'false'},
                    {'Key': 'BackupRequired', 'Value': 'true' if env_name == 'production' else 'false'},
                    {'Key': 'MonitoringLevel', 'Value': 'high' if env_name == 'production' else 'medium'},
                    {'Key': 'CreatedDate', 'Value': datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}
                ]
            )
            
            env_result = {
                'environment': env_name,
                'application_id': response['applicationId'],
                'application_name': env_config['application_name'],
                'creation_success': True,
                'configuration': env_config,
                'django_readiness': {}
            }
            
            # Validate Django configuration for each environment
            django_validation = {
                'settings_compatibility': True,
                'database_configuration': True,
                'static_files_strategy': True,
                'caching_strategy': True,
                'security_configuration': True,
                'performance_optimization': {}
            }
            
            # Environment-specific Django validation
            if env_name == 'development':
                django_validation['settings_compatibility'] = env_config['django_settings']['debug']
                django_validation['performance_optimization'] = {
                    'debug_toolbar_enabled': True,
                    'sql_logging_enabled': True,
                    'template_caching_disabled': True,
                    'compression_disabled': True
                }
            elif env_name == 'staging':
                django_validation['settings_compatibility'] = not env_config['django_settings']['debug']
                django_validation['performance_optimization'] = {
                    'debug_toolbar_enabled': False,
                    'sql_logging_enabled': False,
                    'template_caching_enabled': True,
                    'compression_enabled': True,
                    'test_data_populated': True
                }
            else:  # production
                django_validation['settings_compatibility'] = not env_config['django_settings']['debug']
                django_validation['performance_optimization'] = {
                    'debug_toolbar_enabled': False,
                    'sql_logging_enabled': False,
                    'template_caching_enabled': True,
                    'compression_enabled': True,
                    'cdn_configured': True,
                    'database_connection_pooling': True,
                    'redis_cluster_mode': True
                }
            
            env_result['django_readiness'] = django_validation
            deployment_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_created'].append(env_result)
            
        except Exception as e:
            error_result = {
                'environment': env_name,
                'error': str(e),
                'configuration': env_config
            }
            deployment_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_failed'].append(error_result)
            deployment_results['multi_env_setup']['overall_success'] = False
    
    # Analyze Django compatibility across environments
    if deployment_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_created']:
        model_compatibility = {
            'user_model_scaling': {},
            'jobpost_model_scaling': {},
            'application_model_scaling': {},
            'company_model_scaling': {},
            'cross_environment_considerations': []
        }
        
        # Get current Django model statistics
        current_stats = {
            'users': User.objects.count(),
            'job_posts': JobPost.objects.count(),
            'companies': Company.objects.count(),
            'applications': Application.objects.count(),
            'locations': Location.objects.count(),
            'skills': Skill.objects.count()
        }
        
        # Calculate scaling requirements for each environment
        for env_data in deployment_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_created']:
            env_name = env_data['environment']
            
            if env_name == 'development':
                # Development: Small subset of production data
                scaling_factor = 0.1  # 10% of production data
            elif env_name == 'staging':
                # Staging: Larger subset for realistic testing
                scaling_factor = 0.3  # 30% of production data
            else:
                # Production: Full dataset
                scaling_factor = 1.0
            
            model_compatibility[f'{env_name}_estimated_data'] = {
                'users': int(current_stats['users'] * scaling_factor),
                'job_posts': int(current_stats['job_posts'] * scaling_factor),
                'companies': int(current_stats['companies'] * scaling_factor),
                'applications': int(current_stats['applications'] * scaling_factor),
                'storage_requirement_gb': int((current_stats['users'] * 0.001 + current_stats['job_posts'] * 0.002) * scaling_factor),  # Rough estimate
                'database_connections_needed': env_data['configuration']['django_settings']['database_pool_size']
            }
        
        # Cross-environment considerations
        model_compatibility['cross_environment_considerations'] = [
            'Implement data anonymization for development/staging',
            'Set up automated data refresh from production to staging',
            'Configure environment-specific media file handling',
            'Implement environment-specific email backend configuration',
            'Set up cross-environment monitoring and alerting',
            'Configure environment-specific third-party service endpoints'
        ]
        
        deployment_results['django_compatibility'] = model_compatibility
    
    # Resource planning analysis
    total_min_instances = sum(env['instance_requirements']['min_instances'] for env in environments.values())
    total_max_instances = sum(env['instance_requirements']['max_instances'] for env in environments.values())
    total_storage = sum(env['instance_requirements']['storage_gb'] for env in environments.values())
    
    deployment_results['resource_planning'] = {
        'compute_resources': {
            'total_min_instances': total_min_instances,
            'total_max_instances': total_max_instances,
            'instance_types_used': list(set(env['instance_requirements']['instance_type'] for env in environments.values())),
            'estimated_monthly_cost': {
                'development': 150,  # t3.medium * 2 instances
                'staging': 400,      # t3.large * 4 instances
                'production': 1200,   # c5.xlarge * 10 instances
                'total': 1750
            }
        },
        'storage_resources': {
            'total_storage_gb': total_storage,
            'backup_storage_gb': total_storage * 2,  # 2x for backups
            'estimated_storage_cost_monthly': total_storage * 0.10  # $0.10 per GB
        },
        'network_resources': {
            'load_balancers_needed': 3,  # One per environment
            'cdn_required': True,
            'ssl_certificates_needed': 3,
            'estimated_network_cost_monthly': 200
        }
    }
    
    # Deployment strategy
    deployment_results['deployment_strategy'] = {
        'promotion_pipeline': [
            'Development → Feature Testing',
            'Staging → Integration Testing & QA',
            'Production → Live Deployment'
        ],
        'deployment_order': ['development', 'staging', 'production'],
        'rollback_strategy': {
            'development': 'Immediate rollback allowed',
            'staging': 'Rollback after QA approval',
            'production': 'Rollback requires change management approval'
        },
        'automation_recommendations': [
            'Implement CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions',
            'Set up automated testing between environments',
            'Configure blue-green deployment for production',
            'Implement automated rollback triggers',
            'Set up cross-environment monitoring dashboards'
        ]
    }
    
    return deployment_results

# Execute multi-environment setup
setup_results = setup_multi_environment_application()

print("\n🌐 Multi-Environment Django Application Setup")
print(f"Setup Time: {setup_results['multi_env_setup']['timestamp']}")
print(f"Total Environments: {setup_results['multi_env_setup']['total_environments']}")
print(f"Success Rate: {len(setup_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_created'])}/{setup_results['multi_env_setup']['total_environments']}")

print("\n✅ Successfully Created Applications:")
for env in setup_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_created']:
    print(f"  • {env['environment'].title()}: {env['application_name']} (ID: {env['application_id']})")
    config = env['configuration']
    print(f"    - Instances: {config['instance_requirements']['min_instances']}-{config['instance_requirements']['max_instances']} ({config['instance_requirements']['instance_type']})")
    print(f"    - Django Debug: {config['django_settings']['debug']}")
    print(f"    - DB Pool Size: {config['django_settings']['database_pool_size']}")

if setup_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_failed']:
    print("\n❌ Failed Applications:")
    for failed in setup_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_failed']:
        print(f"  • {failed['environment']}: {failed['error']}")

print("\n📊 Resource Planning:")
compute = setup_results['resource_planning']['compute_resources']
print(f"  • Total Instances: {compute['total_min_instances']}-{compute['total_max_instances']}")
print(f"  • Instance Types: {', '.join(compute['instance_types_used'])}")
print(f"  • Estimated Monthly Cost: ${compute['estimated_monthly_cost']['total']:,}")

storage = setup_results['resource_planning']['storage_resources']
print(f"  • Total Storage: {storage['total_storage_gb']} GB")
print(f"  • Backup Storage: {storage['backup_storage_gb']} GB")

if 'django_compatibility' in setup_results:
    print("\n🐍 Django Environment Scaling:")
    for env in setup_results['multi_env_setup']['environments_created']:
        env_name = env['environment']
        data_key = f"{env_name}_estimated_data"
        if data_key in setup_results['django_compatibility']:
            data = setup_results['django_compatibility'][data_key]
            print(f"  • {env_name.title()}:")
            print(f"    - Users: {data['users']:,}")
            print(f"    - Job Posts: {data['job_posts']:,}")
            print(f"    - DB Connections: {data['database_connections_needed']}")

print("\n🚀 Deployment Strategy:")
strategy = setup_results['deployment_strategy']
print("  • Promotion Pipeline:")
for i, step in enumerate(strategy['promotion_pipeline'], 1):
    print(f"    {i}. {step}")

print("\n💡 Automation Recommendations:")
for i, rec in enumerate(strategy['automation_recommendations'][:3], 1):
    print(f"  {i}. {rec}")

    Example 3: Enterprise Application with Advanced Configuration

    # Create enterprise Django application with advanced configuration and compliance tags
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name "django-enterprise-jobpost" \
    --compute-platform "Server" \
    --tags Key="Environment",Value="Enterprise" Key="Compliance",Value="SOC2" Key="DataClassification",Value="Confidential" Key="BackupRetention",Value="7years"

# Django equivalent for enterprise application setup with compliance and governance
from myapp.models import User, JobPost, Company, Location, Skill, Application
from django.contrib.auth.models import Group, Permission
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import connection
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import json
from collections import defaultdict

def setup_enterprise_application():
    """Set up enterprise Django application with advanced configuration and compliance"""
    client = boto3.client('codedeploy')
    
    # Enterprise application configuration
    enterprise_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-enterprise-jobpost',
        'compute_platform': 'Server',
        'compliance_requirements': {
            'soc2_compliance': True,
            'gdpr_compliance': True,
            'hipaa_compliance': False,
            'data_encryption_required': True,
            'audit_logging_required': True,
            'backup_retention_years': 7
        },
        'security_configuration': {
            'multi_factor_auth': True,
            'role_based_access': True,
            'data_classification': 'Confidential',
            'network_isolation': True,
            'ssl_termination': True,
            'waf_protection': True
        },
        'performance_requirements': {
            'availability_sla': 99.9,
            'response_time_ms': 200,
            'concurrent_users': 10000,
            'throughput_rps': 1000,
            'disaster_recovery_rto': 60,  # minutes
            'disaster_recovery_rpo': 15   # minutes
        },
        'monitoring_configuration': {
            'application_monitoring': True,
            'infrastructure_monitoring': True,
            'security_monitoring': True,
            'performance_monitoring': True,
            'compliance_monitoring': True
        }
    }
    
    try:
        # Create enterprise application with comprehensive tags
        response = client.create_application(
            applicationName=enterprise_config['application_name'],
            computePlatform=enterprise_config['compute_platform'],
            tags=[
                # Core application tags
                {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': 'Enterprise'},
                {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'JobPost'},
                {'Key': 'Framework', 'Value': 'Django'},
                {'Key': 'ApplicationTier', 'Value': 'Production'},
                
                # Compliance tags
                {'Key': 'Compliance', 'Value': 'SOC2'},
                {'Key': 'DataClassification', 'Value': enterprise_config['security_configuration']['data_classification']},
                {'Key': 'BackupRetention', 'Value': f"{enterprise_config['compliance_requirements']['backup_retention_years']}years"},
                {'Key': 'EncryptionRequired', 'Value': str(enterprise_config['compliance_requirements']['data_encryption_required']).lower()},
                {'Key': 'AuditLogging', 'Value': str(enterprise_config['compliance_requirements']['audit_logging_required']).lower()},
                
                # Security tags
                {'Key': 'SecurityZone', 'Value': 'Restricted'},
                {'Key': 'NetworkIsolation', 'Value': str(enterprise_config['security_configuration']['network_isolation']).lower()},
                {'Key': 'MFARequired', 'Value': str(enterprise_config['security_configuration']['multi_factor_auth']).lower()},
                {'Key': 'WAFProtection', 'Value': str(enterprise_config['security_configuration']['waf_protection']).lower()},
                
                # Performance tags
                {'Key': 'SLA', 'Value': f"{enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['availability_sla']}%"},
                {'Key': 'RTO', 'Value': f"{enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['disaster_recovery_rto']}min"},
                {'Key': 'RPO', 'Value': f"{enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['disaster_recovery_rpo']}min"},
                
                # Operational tags
                {'Key': 'CostCenter', 'Value': 'Enterprise-IT'},
                {'Key': 'BusinessOwner', 'Value': 'HR-Operations'},
                {'Key': 'TechnicalOwner', 'Value': 'Platform-Engineering'},
                {'Key': 'MaintenanceWindow', 'Value': 'Sunday-02:00-06:00-UTC'},
                {'Key': 'CreatedDate', 'Value': datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')},
                {'Key': 'LastReviewed', 'Value': datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}
            ]
        )
        
        application_id = response['applicationId']
        
        # Enterprise application analysis
        enterprise_analysis = {
            'application_id': application_id,
            'creation_timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'django_enterprise_readiness': {},
            'compliance_assessment': {},
            'security_configuration_status': {},
            'performance_validation': {},
            'governance_framework': {},
            'monitoring_setup': {},
            'recommendations': []
        }
        
        # Django enterprise readiness assessment
        current_user_count = User.objects.count()
        current_company_count = Company.objects.count()
        current_jobpost_count = JobPost.objects.count()
        current_application_count = Application.objects.count()
        
        # Check Django authentication and authorization setup
        admin_group_exists = Group.objects.filter(name='Administrators').exists()
        hr_group_exists = Group.objects.filter(name='HR_Managers').exists()
        user_permissions = Permission.objects.filter(user__isnull=False).count()
        
        enterprise_analysis['django_enterprise_readiness'] = {
            'user_management': {
                'total_users': current_user_count,
                'admin_group_configured': admin_group_exists,
                'hr_group_configured': hr_group_exists,
                'permission_assignments': user_permissions,
                'multi_factor_auth_ready': False,  # Would need additional package
                'single_sign_on_ready': False      # Would need SAML/OAuth setup
            },
            'data_volume_assessment': {
                'companies': current_company_count,
                'job_posts': current_jobpost_count,
                'applications': current_application_count,
                'estimated_storage_gb': (current_user_count * 0.001) + (current_jobpost_count * 0.002),
                'scaling_capacity': 'High' if current_user_count > 1000 else 'Medium' if current_user_count > 100 else 'Low'
            },
            'enterprise_features': {
                'role_based_permissions': admin_group_exists and hr_group_exists,
                'audit_trail_enabled': hasattr(settings, 'AUDIT_LOG_ENABLED'),
                'data_encryption_configured': hasattr(settings, 'FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY'),
                'backup_procedures_configured': hasattr(settings, 'BACKUP_CONFIGURATION'),
                'monitoring_integration': hasattr(settings, 'MONITORING_ENABLED')
            }
        }
        
        # Compliance assessment
        compliance_checks = {
            'soc2_requirements': {
                'access_controls': admin_group_exists,
                'audit_logging': hasattr(settings, 'AUDIT_LOG_ENABLED'),
                'data_encryption': hasattr(settings, 'FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY'),
                'backup_procedures': hasattr(settings, 'BACKUP_CONFIGURATION'),
                'incident_response_plan': False,  # Would need manual verification
                'security_monitoring': hasattr(settings, 'SECURITY_MONITORING_ENABLED')
            },
            'gdpr_requirements': {
                'data_consent_tracking': False,  # Would need consent model
                'data_deletion_capability': True,  # Django models support deletion
                'data_portability': False,        # Would need export functionality
                'privacy_by_design': False,       # Would need privacy impact assessment
                'data_breach_notification': False # Would need notification system
            },
            'general_data_protection': {
                'personal_data_identified': True,  # User model contains PII
                'data_minimization': False,        # Would need data retention policies
                'purpose_limitation': False,       # Would need usage policies
                'storage_limitation': False       # Would need automated cleanup
            }
        }
        
        # Calculate compliance scores
        soc2_score = sum(1 for check, status in compliance_checks['soc2_requirements'].items() if status)
        gdpr_score = sum(1 for check, status in compliance_checks['gdpr_requirements'].items() if status)
        
        enterprise_analysis['compliance_assessment'] = {
            'soc2_compliance_score': round((soc2_score / len(compliance_checks['soc2_requirements'])) * 100, 2),
            'gdpr_compliance_score': round((gdpr_score / len(compliance_checks['gdpr_requirements'])) * 100, 2),
            'overall_compliance_status': 'Needs Work' if (soc2_score + gdpr_score) < 8 else 'Partially Compliant',
            'compliance_gaps': [],
            'compliance_checks': compliance_checks
        }
        
        # Identify compliance gaps
        for category, checks in compliance_checks.items():
            for check, status in checks.items():
                if not status:
                    enterprise_analysis['compliance_assessment']['compliance_gaps'].append({
                        'category': category,
                        'requirement': check.replace('_', ' ').title(),
                        'priority': 'High' if category == 'soc2_requirements' else 'Medium'
                    })
        
        # Security configuration status
        enterprise_analysis['security_configuration_status'] = {
            'network_security': {
                'ssl_configured': True,  # Assume HTTPS is configured
                'firewall_rules': False, # Would need AWS Security Groups
                'waf_enabled': enterprise_config['security_configuration']['waf_protection'],
                'ddos_protection': False # Would need AWS Shield
            },
            'application_security': {
                'csrf_protection': True,  # Django has built-in CSRF
                'sql_injection_protection': True,  # Django ORM provides protection
                'xss_protection': True,  # Django template system provides protection
                'session_security': hasattr(settings, 'SESSION_SECURITY_CONFIGURED'),
                'password_policies': hasattr(settings, 'AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS')
            },
            'data_security': {
                'encryption_at_rest': enterprise_config['compliance_requirements']['data_encryption_required'],
                'encryption_in_transit': True,  # HTTPS
                'key_management': False,  # Would need AWS KMS integration
                'data_masking': False,    # Would need field-level encryption
                'secure_backup': hasattr(settings, 'ENCRYPTED_BACKUP_ENABLED')
            }
        }
        
        # Performance validation
        estimated_load = enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['concurrent_users']
        estimated_rps = enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['throughput_rps']
        
        enterprise_analysis['performance_validation'] = {
            'capacity_planning': {
                'target_concurrent_users': estimated_load,
                'target_requests_per_second': estimated_rps,
                'estimated_instance_count': max(3, estimated_load // 1000),  # 1000 users per instance
                'database_connection_pool': estimated_load // 100,  # 100 users per connection
                'cache_memory_gb': max(4, estimated_load // 2500)  # 2500 users per GB cache
            },
            'performance_requirements': {
                'response_time_target_ms': enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['response_time_ms'],
                'availability_target': enterprise_config['performance_requirements']['availability_sla'],
                'scalability_testing_needed': True,
                'load_testing_required': True,
                'performance_monitoring_setup': enterprise_config['monitoring_configuration']['performance_monitoring']
            },
            'optimization_recommendations': [
                'Implement database query optimization',
                'Configure Redis cluster for session storage',
                'Set up CDN for static file delivery',
                'Implement application-level caching',
                'Configure auto-scaling policies',
                'Set up database read replicas'
            ]
        }
        
        # Generate comprehensive recommendations
        enterprise_analysis['recommendations'] = [
            'Implement SOC2 compliance framework immediately',
            'Set up comprehensive audit logging system',
            'Configure data encryption at rest and in transit',
            'Implement multi-factor authentication',
            'Set up GDPR-compliant data handling procedures',
            'Configure automated backup and disaster recovery',
            'Implement comprehensive monitoring and alerting',
            'Set up security incident response procedures',
            'Configure load testing and performance monitoring',
            'Implement data retention and deletion policies'
        ]
        
        return {
            'success': True,
            'codedeploy_response': response,
            'enterprise_analysis': enterprise_analysis,
            'enterprise_config': enterprise_config
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        return {
            'success': False,
            'error': str(e),
            'enterprise_config': enterprise_config
        }

# Execute enterprise application setup
enterprise_result = setup_enterprise_application()

if enterprise_result['success']:
    print("\n🏢 Enterprise Django Application Created Successfully")
    analysis = enterprise_result['enterprise_analysis']
    
    print(f"Application ID: {enterprise_result['codedeploy_response']['applicationId']}")
    print(f"Creation Time: {analysis['creation_timestamp']}")
    
    print("\n👥 Django Enterprise Readiness:")
    user_mgmt = analysis['django_enterprise_readiness']['user_management']
    print(f"  • Total Users: {user_mgmt['total_users']:,}")
    print(f"  • Admin Group: {'✅' if user_mgmt['admin_group_configured'] else '❌'} {'Configured' if user_mgmt['admin_group_configured'] else 'Missing'}")
    print(f"  • HR Group: {'✅' if user_mgmt['hr_group_configured'] else '❌'} {'Configured' if user_mgmt['hr_group_configured'] else 'Missing'}")
    print(f"  • Permission Assignments: {user_mgmt['permission_assignments']}")
    
    data_vol = analysis['django_enterprise_readiness']['data_volume_assessment']
    print(f"\n📊 Data Volume Assessment:")
    print(f"  • Companies: {data_vol['companies']:,}")
    print(f"  • Job Posts: {data_vol['job_posts']:,}")
    print(f"  • Applications: {data_vol['applications']:,}")
    print(f"  • Scaling Capacity: {data_vol['scaling_capacity']}")
    
    print(f"\n🔒 Compliance Assessment:")
    compliance = analysis['compliance_assessment']
    print(f"  • SOC2 Compliance: {compliance['soc2_compliance_score']}%")
    print(f"  • GDPR Compliance: {compliance['gdpr_compliance_score']}%")
    print(f"  • Overall Status: {compliance['overall_compliance_status']}")
    
    if compliance['compliance_gaps']:
        print(f"\n⚠️ Compliance Gaps ({len(compliance['compliance_gaps'])} identified):")
        for gap in compliance['compliance_gaps'][:5]:
            print(f"  • {gap['requirement']} ({gap['priority']} Priority)")
    
    print(f"\n⚡ Performance Requirements:")
    perf = analysis['performance_validation']['capacity_planning']
    print(f"  • Target Users: {perf['target_concurrent_users']:,}")
    print(f"  • Target RPS: {perf['target_requests_per_second']:,}")
    print(f"  • Estimated Instances: {perf['estimated_instance_count']}")
    print(f"  • DB Pool Size: {perf['database_connection_pool']}")
    print(f"  • Cache Memory: {perf['cache_memory_gb']} GB")
    
    print(f"\n💡 Priority Recommendations:")
    for i, rec in enumerate(analysis['recommendations'][:5], 1):
        print(f"  {i}. {rec}")
else:
    print(f"❌ Enterprise Application Creation Failed: {enterprise_result['error']}")

    Example 4: Microservices Application Architecture

    # Create Django microservices application with service-oriented architecture
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name "django-microservices-jobpost" \
    --compute-platform "Server" \
    --tags Key="Architecture",Value="Microservices" Key="ServiceMesh",Value="Required" Key="Project",Value="JobPost"

# Django equivalent for microservices application architecture setup
from myapp.models import User, JobPost, Company, Location, Skill, Application
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import connection
import boto3
from datetime import datetime
import json

def setup_microservices_application():
    """Set up Django application with microservices architecture for scalable job portal"""
    client = boto3.client('codedeploy')
    
    # Define microservices architecture
    microservices = {
        'user-service': {
            'application_name': 'django-user-service',
            'description': 'User authentication, profile management, and user operations',
            'django_models': ['User'],
            'scaling_profile': {'min_instances': 2, 'max_instances': 8}
        },
        'job-service': {
            'application_name': 'django-job-service', 
            'description': 'Job posting, search, and job-related operations',
            'django_models': ['JobPost', 'Skill'],
            'scaling_profile': {'min_instances': 3, 'max_instances': 12}
        },
        'company-service': {
            'application_name': 'django-company-service',
            'description': 'Company profiles and management',
            'django_models': ['Company', 'Location'],
            'scaling_profile': {'min_instances': 2, 'max_instances': 6}
        }
    }
    
    results = {'services_created': [], 'architecture_analysis': {}}
    
    # Create applications for each microservice
    for service_name, config in microservices.items():
        try:
            response = client.create_application(
                applicationName=config['application_name'],
                computePlatform='Server',
                tags=[
                    {'Key': 'Architecture', 'Value': 'Microservices'},
                    {'Key': 'ServiceName', 'Value': service_name},
                    {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'JobPost'},
                    {'Key': 'Framework', 'Value': 'Django'}
                ]
            )
            
            results['services_created'].append({
                'service_name': service_name,
                'application_id': response['applicationId'],
                'description': config['description'],
                'django_models': config['django_models'],
                'scaling_config': config['scaling_profile']
            })
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Failed to create {service_name}: {e}")
    
    # Architecture analysis
    total_min = sum(s['scaling_config']['min_instances'] for s in results['services_created'])
    total_max = sum(s['scaling_config']['max_instances'] for s in results['services_created'])
    
    results['architecture_analysis'] = {
        'total_services': len(results['services_created']),
        'instance_range': f"{total_min}-{total_max}",
        'estimated_cost': total_min * 200,  # $200/instance/month
        'communication_type': 'HTTP/REST with service mesh'
    }
    
    return results

# Execute microservices setup
microservices_result = setup_microservices_application()

print("\n🔬 Microservices Django Application Architecture")
print(f"Services Created: {len(microservices_result['services_created'])}")

for service in microservices_result['services_created']:
    print(f"  • {service['service_name']}: {service['application_id']}")
    print(f"    Models: {', '.join(service['django_models'])}")
    print(f"    Scaling: {service['scaling_config']['min_instances']}-{service['scaling_config']['max_instances']} instances")

analysis = microservices_result['architecture_analysis']
print(f"\nArchitecture Summary:")
print(f"  • Total Services: {analysis['total_services']}")
print(f"  • Instance Range: {analysis['instance_range']}")
print(f"  • Estimated Monthly Cost: ${analysis['estimated_cost']:,}")
print(f"  • Communication: {analysis['communication_type']}")

    Example 5: CI/CD Integrated Application with GitHub

    # Create Django application with CI/CD integration and automated deployment
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name "django-cicd-jobpost" \
    --compute-platform "Server" \
    --tags Key="CICD",Value="Enabled" Key="Repository",Value="GitHub" Key="AutoDeploy",Value="True"

# Django equivalent for CI/CD integrated application setup
from myapp.models import User, JobPost, Company, Location, Skill, Application
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import connection
import boto3
from datetime import datetime
import json

def setup_cicd_integrated_application():
    """Set up Django application with comprehensive CI/CD integration"""
    client = boto3.client('codedeploy')
    
    # CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
    cicd_config = {
        'application_name': 'django-cicd-jobpost',
        'pipeline_stages': {
            'source': {'provider': 'GitHub', 'branch': 'main'},
            'build': {'python_version': '3.11', 'test_coverage': 85},
            'test': ['unit_tests', 'integration_tests', 'security_tests'],
            'deploy': {'strategy': 'blue_green', 'rollback': True}
        },
        'environments': {
            'development': {'auto_deploy': True, 'approval': False},
            'staging': {'auto_deploy': True, 'approval': True},
            'production': {'auto_deploy': False, 'approval': True}
        }
    }
    
    try:
        # Create CI/CD integrated application
        response = client.create_application(
            applicationName=cicd_config['application_name'],
            computePlatform='Server',
            tags=[
                {'Key': 'CICD', 'Value': 'Enabled'},
                {'Key': 'Repository', 'Value': 'GitHub'},
                {'Key': 'AutoDeploy', 'Value': 'True'},
                {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'JobPost'},
                {'Key': 'DeploymentStrategy', 'Value': 'blue_green'},
                {'Key': 'TestCoverage', 'Value': '85'},
                {'Key': 'Environments', 'Value': str(len(cicd_config['environments']))}
            ]
        )
        
        application_id = response['applicationId']
        
        # CI/CD readiness assessment
        django_readiness = {
            'requirements_file': True,  # requirements.txt exists
            'settings_configured': hasattr(settings, 'DATABASES'),
            'static_files_ready': hasattr(settings, 'STATIC_ROOT'),
            'tests_present': True,  # Django test suite
            'migrations_current': True
        }
        
        readiness_score = sum(1 for check in django_readiness.values() if check)
        readiness_percentage = (readiness_score / len(django_readiness)) * 100
        
        # Pipeline analysis
        pipeline_analysis = {
            'total_stages': len(cicd_config['pipeline_stages']),
            'automated_stages': 3,  # source, build, test
            'manual_stages': 1,     # deploy (with approval)
            'estimated_duration': '15-25 minutes',
            'parallel_execution': True
        }
        
        # Environment analysis
        env_analysis = {
            'auto_deploy_envs': len([env for env, cfg in cicd_config['environments'].items() if cfg['auto_deploy']]),
            'manual_deploy_envs': len([env for env, cfg in cicd_config['environments'].items() if not cfg['auto_deploy']]),
            'approval_required_envs': len([env for env, cfg in cicd_config['environments'].items() if cfg['approval']])
        }
        
        # Current Django model statistics
        current_stats = {
            'users': User.objects.count(),
            'companies': Company.objects.count(),
            'job_posts': JobPost.objects.count(),
            'applications': Application.objects.count()
        }
        
        return {
            'success': True,
            'application_id': application_id,
            'django_readiness': django_readiness,
            'readiness_percentage': readiness_percentage,
            'pipeline_analysis': pipeline_analysis,
            'env_analysis': env_analysis,
            'current_data': current_stats,
            'cicd_config': cicd_config
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)}

# Execute CI/CD setup
cicd_result = setup_cicd_integrated_application()

if cicd_result['success']:
    print("\n🚀 CI/CD Integrated Django Application Created")
    print(f"Application ID: {cicd_result['application_id']}")
    
    print(f"\n🐍 Django CI/CD Readiness: {cicd_result['readiness_percentage']:.1f}%")
    for check, status in cicd_result['django_readiness'].items():
        print(f"  • {check.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {'✅' if status else '❌'}")
    
    pipeline = cicd_result['pipeline_analysis']
    print(f"\n📋 Pipeline Configuration:")
    print(f"  • Total Stages: {pipeline['total_stages']}")
    print(f"  • Automated: {pipeline['automated_stages']}, Manual: {pipeline['manual_stages']}")
    print(f"  • Duration: {pipeline['estimated_duration']}")
    print(f"  • Parallel Execution: {'✅' if pipeline['parallel_execution'] else '❌'}")
    
    env = cicd_result['env_analysis']
    print(f"\n🌐 Environment Strategy:")
    print(f"  • Auto-Deploy Environments: {env['auto_deploy_envs']}")
    print(f"  • Manual Deploy Environments: {env['manual_deploy_envs']}")
    print(f"  • Approval Required: {env['approval_required_envs']}")
    
    print(f"\n📊 Current Data Volume:")
    data = cicd_result['current_data']
    print(f"  • Users: {data['users']:,}")
    print(f"  • Companies: {data['companies']:,}")
    print(f"  • Job Posts: {data['job_posts']:,}")
    print(f"  • Applications: {data['applications']:,}")
    
    print(f"\n💡 CI/CD Recommendations:")
    print(f"  1. Implement automated database migration testing")
    print(f"  2. Set up comprehensive monitoring and alerting")
    print(f"  3. Configure automated rollback triggers")
    print(f"  4. Implement feature flags for safer deployments")
    print(f"  5. Set up performance regression testing")
    
else:
    print(f"❌ CI/CD Application Creation Failed: {cicd_result['error']}")

    AWS CodeCommit

    Fully managed source control service for secure, scalable Git-based repositories

    📋 CodeCommit Overview

    AWS CodeCommit is a fully managed source control service that hosts secure Git-based repositories. It allows you to store and manage your anihpj Django application source code with full Git functionality, integrated security, and seamless AWS service integration. CodeCommit repositories store your jobpost application code with models for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost data.

    Core CodeCommit Operations for Django Development

    create-repository - Create new Git repositories
    get-repository - Get repository information
    list-repositories - List all repositories
    create-branch - Create new branches
    list-branches - List repository branches
    create-pull-request - Create pull requests for code review
    merge-pull-request-by-fast-forward - Merge approved PRs
    create-commit - Create commits programmatically
    get-file - Retrieve file contents
    put-file - Add or update files
    create-approval-rule-template - Define code review requirements
    put-repository-triggers - Configure CI/CD triggers

    associate-approval-rule-template-with-repository

    Associates an approval rule template with a specified repository in AWS CodeCommit. This command links predefined approval workflows to your Django jobpost application repository, ensuring code changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models undergo proper review processes before merging. Essential for maintaining code quality and compliance in team environments.

    Syntax
