    aws codecommit associate-approval-rule-template-with-repository \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template to associate with the repository. Templates define approval requirements like minimum approvers and approval pool members.
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the CodeCommit repository to associate with the template. This should be your Django application repository.
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful association.

    batch-associate-approval-rule-template-with-repositories

    Associates an approval rule template with multiple repositories in a single operation. This command efficiently applies consistent approval workflows across all Django jobpost microservices repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models, ensuring uniform code review standards across your entire application ecosystem.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit batch-associate-approval-rule-template-with-repositories \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --repository-names {repository-name} [{repository-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template to associate with multiple repositories
    • --repository-names (list, required): List of repository names to associate with the template
    Return Value
    • associatedRepositoryNames: List of repositories successfully associated
    • errors: Array of error details for failed associations

    batch-describe-merge-conflicts

    Returns information about merge conflicts in multiple files between two commits. This command provides detailed conflict analysis for Django jobpost application merges, helping developers understand and resolve conflicts in Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files during feature branch integrations and pull request reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit batch-describe-merge-conflicts \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --merge-option {merge-option} \
  [--max-merge-hunks {max-hunks}] \
  [--max-conflict-files {max-files}] \
  [--file-paths {file-path} [{file-path}...]] \
  [--conflict-detail-level {detail-level}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {strategy}] \
  [--next-token {pagination-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the CodeCommit repository containing the commits to analyze
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Commit specifier for the destination branch (typically main or develop)
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Commit specifier for the source branch being merged
    • --merge-option (string, required): Merge strategy to use: FAST_FORWARD_MERGE, SQUASH_MERGE, THREE_WAY_MERGE
    • --max-merge-hunks (integer): Maximum number of merge hunks to return per file
    • --max-conflict-files (integer): Maximum number of conflict files to return
    • --conflict-detail-level (string): Level of conflict detail: FILE_LEVEL, LINE_LEVEL
    Return Value
    • conflicts: Array of conflict metadata for each file with conflicts
    • destinationCommitId: Full commit ID of the destination commit
    • sourceCommitId: Full commit ID of the source commit
    • baseCommitId: Full commit ID of the merge base
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results
    • errors: Array of error details for files that couldn't be analyzed

    batch-disassociate-approval-rule-template-from-repositories

    Removes an approval rule template association from multiple repositories in a single operation. This command efficiently removes approval requirements from Django jobpost repositories when reorganizing workflows or decommissioning approval templates for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model development processes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit batch-disassociate-approval-rule-template-from-repositories \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --repository-names {repository-name} [{repository-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template to disassociate
    • --repository-names (list, required): List of repository names to disassociate from the template
    Return Value
    • disassociatedRepositoryNames: List of repositories successfully disassociated
    • errors: Array of error details for failed disassociations

    batch-get-commits

    Returns information about multiple commits in a repository. This command retrieves detailed commit metadata for tracking changes to your Django jobpost application, including author information, commit messages, and parent commits for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model modifications across your development history.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit batch-get-commits \
  --commit-ids {commit-id} [{commit-id}...] \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --commit-ids (list, required): List of full commit IDs to retrieve information about
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the CodeCommit repository containing the commits
    Return Value
    • commits: Array of commit objects with full metadata
    • errors: Array of error details for commits that couldn't be retrieved

    batch-get-repositories

    Returns information about multiple repositories in AWS CodeCommit. This command retrieves comprehensive repository metadata for all Django jobpost application repositories, including configuration details, clone URLs, and metadata for repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models across your development environment.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit batch-get-repositories \
  --repository-names {repository-name} [{repository-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-names (list, required): List of repository names to retrieve information about
    Return Value
    • repositories: Array of repository metadata objects
    • repositoriesNotFound: List of repository names that were not found

    create-approval-rule-template

    Creates a template for approval rules that can be associated with repositories. This command establishes reusable approval workflows for Django jobpost application code reviews, defining minimum approver requirements and approval pools for changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models to ensure code quality and compliance.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-approval-rule-template \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --approval-rule-template-content {template-content} \
  [--approval-rule-template-description {description}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Unique name for the approval rule template
    • --approval-rule-template-content (string, required): JSON content defining approval requirements including number of approvals and approval pool members
    • --approval-rule-template-description (string): Description of the template's purpose and usage
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplate: Object containing template details including templateId, name, content, description, and timestamps

    create-branch

    Creates a new branch in a CodeCommit repository. This command enables feature branch development workflows for your Django jobpost application, allowing isolated development of new features for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models before merging into the main codebase through pull requests.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-branch \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --branch-name {branch-name} \
  --commit-id {commit-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the CodeCommit repository where the branch will be created
    • --branch-name (string, required): Name for the new branch (e.g., feature/add-skills-api, bugfix/jobpost-validation)
    • --commit-id (string, required): Full commit ID to use as the starting point for the new branch
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful branch creation.

    create-commit

    Creates a commit for a repository with specified file changes. This command programmatically creates commits in your Django jobpost application repository, enabling automated file modifications, additions, and deletions for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files through CI/CD pipelines and automation scripts.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-commit \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --branch-name {branch-name} \
  [--parent-commit-id {parent-commit}] \
  [--author-name {author-name}] \
  [--email {author-email}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--put-files {file-info}] \
  [--delete-files {file-path}] \
  [--set-file-modes {file-mode-info}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the CodeCommit repository for the commit
    • --branch-name (string, required): Name of the branch where the commit will be created
    • --parent-commit-id (string): Full commit ID of the parent commit for non-initial commits
    • --author-name (string): Name of the commit author
    • --email (string): Email address of the commit author
    • --commit-message (string): Descriptive message for the commit
    • --put-files (list): Files to add or update in the commit
    • --delete-files (list): Files to delete in the commit
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the created commit
    • treeId: Tree ID associated with the commit
    • filesAdded: List of files added in the commit
    • filesUpdated: List of files updated in the commit
    • filesDeleted: List of files deleted in the commit

    create-pull-request

    Creates a pull request in a CodeCommit repository for code review. This command initiates the review process for Django jobpost application changes, enabling team collaboration on modifications to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models before merging into the main branch with proper approval workflows.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-pull-request \
  --title {pull-request-title} \
  --targets repositoryName={repository},sourceReference={source-branch},destinationReference={destination-branch} \
  [--description {description}] \
  [--client-request-token {idempotency-token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --title (string, required): Title for the pull request describing the changes
    • --targets (list, required): Target specifications including repository name, source branch, and destination branch
    • --description (string): Detailed description of the changes and their purpose
    • --client-request-token (string): Unique token for idempotent requests
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Object containing pull request details including pullRequestId, title, description, targets, and status

    create-pull-request-approval-rule

    Creates an approval rule for a specific pull request in CodeCommit. This command adds custom approval requirements to individual pull requests for your Django jobpost application, ensuring critical changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models receive appropriate review and approval before merging.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-pull-request-approval-rule \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --approval-rule-name {rule-name} \
  --approval-rule-content {rule-content} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --approval-rule-name (string, required): Name for the approval rule
    • --approval-rule-content (string, required): JSON content specifying approval requirements including minimum approvals and approval pool
    Return Value
    • approvalRule: Object containing rule details including approvalRuleId, name, content, and timestamps

    create-repository

    Creates a new CodeCommit repository for storing source code. This command initializes a new Git repository for your Django jobpost application, providing version control for all source files including models for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost with full AWS integration for CI/CD workflows.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-repository \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--repository-description {description}] \
  [--tags {key=value}] \
  [--kms-key-id {kms-key}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Unique name for the new repository
    • --repository-description (string): Description of the repository's purpose
    • --tags (map): Key-value pairs for resource tagging and organization
    • --kms-key-id (string): AWS KMS key ID for repository encryption
    Return Value
    • repositoryMetadata: Object containing repository details including repositoryId, cloneUrlHttp, cloneUrlSsh, and ARN

    create-unreferenced-merge-commit

    Creates an unreferenced merge commit between two branches for conflict resolution testing. This command simulates merges for your Django jobpost application branches, allowing you to preview and resolve conflicts in Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes before performing the actual merge.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit create-unreferenced-merge-commit \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  --merge-option {FAST_FORWARD_MERGE|SQUASH_MERGE|THREE_WAY_MERGE} \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--author-name {author}] \
  [--email {email}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--conflict-resolution {conflictResolution}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the branches
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, HEAD, or commit ID for the source
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, HEAD, or commit ID for the destination
    • --merge-option (string, required): Type of merge to perform
    • --conflict-resolution-strategy (string): Strategy for automatic conflict resolution
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the unreferenced merge commit
    • treeId: Tree ID associated with the merge commit

    credential-helper

    Provides a Git credential helper for authenticating with AWS CodeCommit repositories. This command integrates with Git to automatically provide credentials when cloning, pushing, or pulling from your Django jobpost application repository, enabling seamless authentication for accessing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code without storing passwords.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit credential-helper {get|store|erase}
git config --global credential.helper '!aws codecommit credential-helper $@'
git config --global credential.UseHttpPath true
    Parameters Description
    • get: Retrieves credentials for the Git repository URL
    • store: Stores credentials (not used by CodeCommit helper)
    • erase: Erases stored credentials (not used by CodeCommit helper)
    Return Value
    • username: AWS access key ID used for authentication
    • password: Temporary password generated using AWS credentials and SigV4

    delete-approval-rule-template

    Deletes an approval rule template from AWS CodeCommit. This command removes obsolete approval workflow templates no longer needed for your Django jobpost application development, cleaning up templates that were previously used for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit delete-approval-rule-template \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template to delete
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplateId: System-generated ID of the deleted template

    delete-branch

    Deletes a branch from a CodeCommit repository. This command removes merged or abandoned feature branches from your Django jobpost application repository, maintaining a clean branch structure after completing work on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model features.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit delete-branch \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --branch-name {branch-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the branch
    • --branch-name (string, required): Name of the branch to delete
    Return Value
    • deletedBranch: Object containing deleted branch details including branchName and commitId

    delete-comment-content

    Deletes the content of a comment in a CodeCommit pull request or commit. This command removes inappropriate or outdated comments from code reviews in your Django jobpost application repository, maintaining clean discussion threads for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit delete-comment-content \
  --comment-id {comment-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --comment-id (string, required): System-generated unique ID of the comment to delete
    Return Value
    • comment: Object containing the deleted comment details with empty content

    delete-file

    Deletes a file from a branch in a CodeCommit repository and creates a new commit. This command removes obsolete files from your Django jobpost application codebase, enabling programmatic cleanup of deprecated Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files or configurations.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit delete-file \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --branch-name {branch-name} \
  --file-path {file-path} \
  --parent-commit-id {parent-commit} \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--name {committer-name}] \
  [--email {committer-email}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the file
    • --branch-name (string, required): Name of the branch where the file will be deleted
    • --file-path (string, required): Full path to the file to delete
    • --parent-commit-id (string, required): Full commit ID of the parent commit
    • --commit-message (string): Commit message for the deletion
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the deletion commit
    • blobId: Blob ID of the deleted file
    • treeId: Tree ID of the commit
    • filePath: Path of the deleted file

    delete-pull-request-approval-rule

    Deletes an approval rule from a pull request in CodeCommit. This command removes unnecessary approval rules from pull requests for your Django jobpost application, allowing flexibility in the review process for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit delete-pull-request-approval-rule \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --approval-rule-name {rule-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --approval-rule-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule to delete
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleId: ID of the deleted approval rule

    delete-repository

    Permanently deletes a CodeCommit repository and all its contents. This command removes deprecated or test repositories for your Django jobpost application development, including all branches, commits, and history for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code. This action is irreversible.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit delete-repository \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository to permanently delete
    Return Value
    • repositoryId: ID of the deleted repository

    describe-merge-conflicts

    Returns detailed information about merge conflicts for a specific file between branches. This command provides comprehensive conflict analysis for your Django jobpost application merges, showing line-by-line differences in Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files requiring manual resolution.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit describe-merge-conflicts \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --merge-option {FAST_FORWARD_MERGE|SQUASH_MERGE|THREE_WAY_MERGE} \
  --file-path {file-path} \
  [--max-merge-hunks {number}] \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository with merge conflicts
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for the destination
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for the source
    • --merge-option (string, required): Type of merge being performed
    • --file-path (string, required): Path to the file with conflicts
    • --conflict-detail-level (string): Level of conflict detail (FILE_LEVEL or LINE_LEVEL)
    Return Value
    • conflictMetadata: Object containing file metadata and conflict information
    • mergeHunks: Array of merge hunks showing source and destination content
    • destinationCommitId: Commit ID of the destination
    • sourceCommitId: Commit ID of the source

    describe-pull-request-events

    Returns information about events that have occurred in a pull request. This command tracks the complete activity history for pull requests in your Django jobpost application, showing status changes, comments, approvals, and merge events for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit describe-pull-request-events \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  [--pull-request-event-type {PULL_REQUEST_CREATED|PULL_REQUEST_STATUS_CHANGED|...}] \
  [--actor-arn {arn}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --pull-request-event-type (string): Filter events by type (PULL_REQUEST_CREATED, PULL_REQUEST_STATUS_CHANGED, PULL_REQUEST_SOURCE_REFERENCE_UPDATED, PULL_REQUEST_MERGE_STATE_CHANGED, PULL_REQUEST_APPROVAL_RULE_CREATED, PULL_REQUEST_APPROVAL_RULE_UPDATED, PULL_REQUEST_APPROVAL_RULE_DELETED, PULL_REQUEST_APPROVAL_RULE_OVERRIDDEN, PULL_REQUEST_APPROVAL_STATE_CHANGED)
    • --actor-arn (string): Filter events by the ARN of the actor
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of events to return
    Return Value
    • pullRequestEvents: Array of event objects with eventDate, eventType, actorArn, and event-specific data
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    disassociate-approval-rule-template-from-repository

    Removes the association between an approval rule template and a repository. This command disconnects approval workflow templates from your Django jobpost application repositories, allowing different approval processes for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit disassociate-approval-rule-template-from-repository \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template to disassociate
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository to disassociate
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful disassociation.

    evaluate-pull-request-approval-rules

    Evaluates whether a pull request has met all approval rule requirements. This command checks if pull requests for your Django jobpost application have received sufficient approvals, verifying compliance with approval rules before merging Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit evaluate-pull-request-approval-rules \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --revision-id {revision-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --revision-id (string, required): Revision ID of the pull request (latest revision)
    Return Value
    • evaluation: Object containing approved status, overridden status, and approvalRulesSatisfied/approvalRulesNotSatisfied arrays

    get-approval-rule-template

    Returns information about an approval rule template. This command retrieves the configuration details of approval rule templates used for your Django jobpost application code reviews, showing requirements for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model change approvals.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-approval-rule-template \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplate: Object containing templateId, name, description, content, creationDate, and lastModifiedDate

    get-blob

    Returns the base-64 encoded content of a blob object in a repository. This command retrieves binary or text file contents from your Django jobpost application repository, enabling programmatic access to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model source files by their blob ID.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-blob \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --blob-id {blob-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the blob
    • --blob-id (string, required): SHA ID of the blob object
    Return Value
    • content: Base64-encoded content of the blob

    get-branch

    Returns information about a branch in a CodeCommit repository. This command retrieves details about specific branches in your Django jobpost application repository, including the latest commit ID for branches managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model development.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-branch \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--branch-name {branch-name}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the branch
    • --branch-name (string): Name of the branch to retrieve (defaults to default branch)
    Return Value
    • branch: Object containing branchName and commitId

    get-comment

    Returns the content of a comment made on a diff or pull request. This command retrieves specific code review comments from your Django jobpost application repository, accessing feedback on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes for reference or analysis.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-comment \
  --comment-id {comment-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --comment-id (string, required): System-generated unique ID of the comment
    Return Value
    • comment: Object containing commentId, content, authorArn, inReplyTo, creationDate, and callerReactions

    get-comment-reactions

    Returns information about reactions to a comment. This command retrieves emoji reactions on code review comments in your Django jobpost application repository, showing team engagement with feedback on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-comment-reactions \
  --comment-id {comment-id} \
  [--reaction-user-arn {user-arn}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --comment-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the comment
    • --reaction-user-arn (string): Filter reactions by a specific user ARN
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of reactions to return
    Return Value
    • reactionsForComment: Array of reaction objects with emoji, shortCode, and users list
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    get-comments-for-compared-commit

    Returns comments made on the comparison between two commits. This command retrieves all code review comments for commit comparisons in your Django jobpost application, showing discussion threads about Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes between versions.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-comments-for-compared-commit \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --after-commit-id {after-commit-id} \
  [--before-commit-id {before-commit-id}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --after-commit-id (string, required): Commit ID of the after commit
    • --before-commit-id (string): Commit ID of the before commit (omit for first commit)
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of comment threads to return
    Return Value
    • commentsForComparedCommitData: Array of comment threads with location, comments, and metadata
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    get-comments-for-pull-request

    Returns comments made on a pull request. This command retrieves all code review discussion threads for pull requests in your Django jobpost application, showing feedback and conversations about Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes under review.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-comments-for-pull-request \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  [--repository-name {repository-name}] \
  [--before-commit-id {before-commit-id}] \
  [--after-commit-id {after-commit-id}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --repository-name (string): Name of the repository (for filtering)
    • --before-commit-id (string): Before commit ID for comparison filtering
    • --after-commit-id (string): After commit ID for comparison filtering
    Return Value
    • commentsForPullRequestData: Array of comment threads with location, comments, and PR metadata
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    get-commit

    Returns information about a commit in a CodeCommit repository. This command retrieves detailed metadata for specific commits in your Django jobpost application repository, including author information and commit message for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-commit \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --commit-id {commit-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the commit
    • --commit-id (string, required): Full commit ID to retrieve
    Return Value
    • commit: Object containing commitId, treeId, parents, message, author, committer, and additionalData

    get-differences

    Returns information about the differences in paths between two commits. This command lists all file changes between versions in your Django jobpost application repository, showing additions, modifications, and deletions of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-differences \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --after-commit-specifier {after-commit} \
  [--before-commit-specifier {before-commit}] \
  [--before-path {path}] \
  [--after-path {path}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --after-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, HEAD, or commit ID for the after state
    • --before-commit-specifier (string): Branch, tag, HEAD, or commit ID for the before state
    • --before-path (string): Filter to files in this path before the change
    • --after-path (string): Filter to files in this path after the change
    Return Value
    • differences: Array of difference objects with beforeBlob, afterBlob, and changeType
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    get-file

    Returns the contents of a file in a CodeCommit repository. This command retrieves file content and metadata from your Django jobpost application repository, accessing source code for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models at specific commits or branches.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-file \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --file-path {file-path} \
  [--commit-specifier {commit}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository containing the file
    • --file-path (string, required): Full path to the file
    • --commit-specifier (string): Branch, tag, HEAD, or commit ID (defaults to HEAD)
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the specified version
    • blobId: Blob ID of the file
    • filePath: Path to the file
    • fileMode: File mode (EXECUTABLE, NORMAL, SYMLINK)
    • fileSize: Size of the file in bytes
    • fileContent: Base64-encoded file content

    get-folder

    Returns the contents of a folder in a CodeCommit repository. This command lists all files and subfolders in a directory of your Django jobpost application repository, enabling navigation of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model source code structure.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-folder \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --folder-path {folder-path} \
  [--commit-specifier {commit}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --folder-path (string, required): Path to the folder (use "/" for root)
    • --commit-specifier (string): Branch, tag, HEAD, or commit ID (defaults to HEAD)
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the specified version
    • folderPath: Path to the folder
    • files: Array of file objects with path, blobId, and mode
    • subFolders: Array of subfolder objects with path and treeId
    • symbolicLinks: Array of symbolic link objects
    • subModules: Array of submodule objects

    get-merge-commit

    Returns information about a merge commit created by one of the merge options. This command retrieves details about merge commits in your Django jobpost application repository, showing how Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model branches were combined.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-merge-commit \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for source
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for destination
    • --conflict-detail-level (string): Level of conflict detail to return
    Return Value
    • sourceCommitId: Full commit ID of the source
    • destinationCommitId: Full commit ID of the destination
    • baseCommitId: Full commit ID of the merge base
    • mergedCommitId: Full commit ID of the merge commit

    get-merge-conflicts

    Returns information about merge conflicts between branches for a specified merge. This command identifies all files with conflicts in your Django jobpost application merge operations, listing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files requiring manual resolution.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-merge-conflicts \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --merge-option {FAST_FORWARD_MERGE|SQUASH_MERGE|THREE_WAY_MERGE} \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--max-conflict-files {number}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository with conflicts
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for destination
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for source
    • --merge-option (string, required): Type of merge to evaluate
    • --conflict-detail-level (string): Level of conflict detail to return
    Return Value
    • mergeable: Boolean indicating if branches can be merged automatically
    • destinationCommitId: Full commit ID of the destination
    • sourceCommitId: Full commit ID of the source
    • baseCommitId: Full commit ID of the merge base
    • conflictMetadataList: Array of conflict metadata objects

    get-merge-options

    Returns information about available merge options between two branches. This command determines which merge strategies are possible for your Django jobpost application branches, helping choose the best approach for combining Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-merge-options \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for source
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for destination
    Return Value
    • mergeOptions: Array of available merge options (FAST_FORWARD_MERGE, SQUASH_MERGE, THREE_WAY_MERGE)
    • sourceCommitId: Full commit ID of the source
    • destinationCommitId: Full commit ID of the destination
    • baseCommitId: Full commit ID of the merge base

    get-pull-request

    Returns information about a pull request in CodeCommit. This command retrieves comprehensive details about pull requests in your Django jobpost application repository, including status, targets, and approval state for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes under review.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-pull-request \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Object containing pullRequestId, title, description, targets, authorArn, pullRequestStatus, creationDate, and approvalRules

    get-pull-request-approval-states

    Returns information about the approval states for a pull request. This command shows who has approved or requested changes to pull requests in your Django jobpost application, tracking review progress for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model modifications.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-pull-request-approval-states \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --revision-id {revision-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --revision-id (string, required): Revision ID of the pull request
    Return Value
    • approvals: Array of approval objects with userArn and approvalState (APPROVE, REVOKE)

    get-pull-request-override-state

    Returns information about whether approval rules have been overridden for a pull request. This command checks if approval requirements have been bypassed for pull requests in your Django jobpost application, showing override status for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-pull-request-override-state \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --revision-id {revision-id} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --revision-id (string, required): Revision ID of the pull request
    Return Value
    • overridden: Boolean indicating if approval rules have been overridden
    • overrider: ARN of the user who overrode the rules (if applicable)

    get-repository

    Returns information about a repository in CodeCommit. This command retrieves comprehensive metadata for your Django jobpost application repository, including clone URLs, ARN, and configuration details for the repository storing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-repository \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository to retrieve
    Return Value
    • repositoryMetadata: Object containing repositoryId, repositoryName, repositoryDescription, cloneUrlHttp, cloneUrlSsh, Arn, defaultBranch, and kmsKeyId

    get-repository-triggers

    Returns information about triggers configured for a repository. This command lists all event triggers for your Django jobpost application repository, showing SNS or Lambda integrations triggered by push events to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model branches.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit get-repository-triggers \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    Return Value
    • configurationId: System-generated ID for the trigger configuration
    • triggers: Array of trigger objects with name, destinationArn, customData, branches, and events

    list-approval-rule-templates

    Lists all approval rule templates in an AWS account. This command enumerates all reusable approval workflow templates available for your Django jobpost application repositories, showing templates that define code review requirements for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-approval-rule-templates \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of templates to return
    • --next-token (string): Pagination token for additional results
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplateNames: Array of approval rule template names
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-associated-approval-rule-templates-for-repository

    Lists all approval rule templates associated with a repository. This command shows which approval workflow templates are linked to your Django jobpost application repository, identifying active approval requirements for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model pull requests.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-associated-approval-rule-templates-for-repository \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of templates to return
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplateNames: Array of associated template names
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-branches

    Lists all branches in a CodeCommit repository. This command enumerates all branches in your Django jobpost application repository, showing feature branches, release branches, and development branches for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model work.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-branches \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --next-token (string): Pagination token for additional results
    Return Value
    • branches: Array of branch names
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-file-commit-history

    Returns a list of commits that modified a specified file. This command tracks the complete change history of individual files in your Django jobpost application repository, showing all commits that modified Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-file-commit-history \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --file-path {file-path} \
  [--commit-specifier {commit}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --file-path (string, required): Path to the file to get history for
    • --commit-specifier (string): Branch, tag, or commit ID to start history from
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of commits to return
    Return Value
    • revisionDag: Array of file revision information objects
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-pull-requests

    Returns a list of pull requests for a specified repository. This command enumerates all pull requests in your Django jobpost application repository, showing open, closed, or merged requests for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-pull-requests \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--author-arn {author-arn}] \
  [--pull-request-status {OPEN|CLOSED}] \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --author-arn (string): Filter by the ARN of the pull request author
    • --pull-request-status (string): Filter by status (OPEN or CLOSED)
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of pull requests to return
    Return Value
    • pullRequestIds: Array of pull request IDs
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-repositories

    Lists all CodeCommit repositories in an AWS account. This command enumerates all repositories available for your Django jobpost application development, showing repository names and IDs for all projects managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-repositories \
  [--sort-by {repositoryName|lastModifiedDate}] \
  [--order {ascending|descending}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --sort-by (string): Field to sort by (repositoryName or lastModifiedDate)
    • --order (string): Sort order (ascending or descending)
    • --next-token (string): Pagination token for additional results
    Return Value
    • repositories: Array of repository objects with repositoryName and repositoryId
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-repositories-for-approval-rule-template

    Lists all repositories associated with a specified approval rule template. This command shows which repositories use a particular approval workflow for your Django jobpost application, identifying where Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code reviews follow specific approval requirements.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-repositories-for-approval-rule-template \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  [--max-results {number}] \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule template
    • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of repositories to return
    Return Value
    • repositoryNames: Array of associated repository names
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    list-tags-for-resource

    Lists all tags associated with a CodeCommit resource. This command retrieves resource tags for your Django jobpost application repositories, showing organization and cost allocation tags applied to repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit list-tags-for-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  [--next-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CodeCommit resource
    • --next-token (string): Pagination token for additional results
    Return Value
    • tags: Map of tag keys to tag values
    • nextToken: Pagination token for additional results

    merge-branches-by-fast-forward

    Merges two branches using the fast-forward merge strategy. This command performs fast-forward merges in your Django jobpost application repository when there are no conflicting changes, efficiently combining Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model work from feature branches.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit merge-branches-by-fast-forward \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  [--target-branch {branch-name}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for source
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for destination
    • --target-branch (string): Branch where merge result will be committed
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the merge result
    • treeId: Tree ID of the merge commit

    merge-branches-by-squash

    Merges two branches using the squash merge strategy. This command combines all commits from a feature branch into a single commit in your Django jobpost application repository, creating a clean history for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit merge-branches-by-squash \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  [--target-branch {branch-name}] \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--author-name {author}] \
  [--email {email}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--conflict-resolution {conflictResolution}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for source
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for destination
    • --commit-message (string): Commit message for the squashed merge
    • --conflict-resolution-strategy (string): Strategy for automatic conflict resolution
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the squash merge result
    • treeId: Tree ID of the merge commit

    merge-branches-by-three-way

    Merges two branches using a three-way merge strategy. This command performs standard merges in your Django jobpost application repository, preserving full commit history from feature branches while combining Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit merge-branches-by-three-way \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --source-commit-specifier {source-commit} \
  --destination-commit-specifier {destination-commit} \
  [--target-branch {branch-name}] \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--author-name {author}] \
  [--email {email}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--conflict-resolution {conflictResolution}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --source-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for source
    • --destination-commit-specifier (string, required): Branch, tag, or commit ID for destination
    • --commit-message (string): Commit message for the merge
    • --conflict-resolution-strategy (string): Strategy for automatic conflict resolution
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the three-way merge result
    • treeId: Tree ID of the merge commit

    merge-pull-request-by-fast-forward

    Attempts to merge a pull request using the fast-forward merge strategy. This command closes pull requests with fast-forward merges in your Django jobpost application, integrating approved Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes into the destination branch.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit merge-pull-request-by-fast-forward \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--source-commit-id {source-commit}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --source-commit-id (string): Full commit ID of the source (for verification)
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Updated pull request object with CLOSED status and merge metadata

    merge-pull-request-by-squash

    Attempts to merge a pull request using the squash merge strategy. This command closes pull requests with squash merges in your Django jobpost application, combining all commits into one for cleaner history of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit merge-pull-request-by-squash \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--source-commit-id {source-commit}] \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--author-name {author}] \
  [--email {email}] \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--conflict-resolution {conflictResolution}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --commit-message (string): Commit message for the squashed merge
    • --conflict-resolution-strategy (string): Strategy for automatic conflict resolution
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Updated pull request object with CLOSED status and merge metadata

    merge-pull-request-by-three-way

    Attempts to merge a pull request using the three-way merge strategy. This command closes pull requests with standard three-way merges in your Django jobpost application, preserving full commit history while integrating Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit merge-pull-request-by-three-way \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--source-commit-id {source-commit}] \
  [--conflict-detail-level {FILE_LEVEL|LINE_LEVEL}] \
  [--conflict-resolution-strategy {NONE|ACCEPT_SOURCE|ACCEPT_DESTINATION|AUTOMERGE}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--author-name {author}] \
  [--email {email}] \
  [--keep-empty-folders] \
  [--conflict-resolution {conflictResolution}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --commit-message (string): Commit message for the merge
    • --conflict-resolution-strategy (string): Strategy for automatic conflict resolution
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Updated pull request object with CLOSED status and merge metadata

    override-pull-request-approval-rules

    Overrides approval rules for a pull request, allowing it to be merged without meeting all requirements. This command bypasses approval rules in emergency situations for your Django jobpost application, enabling urgent Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model fixes to be merged quickly.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit override-pull-request-approval-rules \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --revision-id {revision-id} \
  --override-status {OVERRIDE|REVOKE} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --revision-id (string, required): Revision ID of the pull request
    • --override-status (string, required): OVERRIDE to bypass rules, REVOKE to remove override
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful override.

    post-comment-for-compared-commit

    Posts a comment on a specific comparison between commits. This command adds code review comments to commit comparisons in your Django jobpost application repository, enabling feedback on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes outside of pull requests.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit post-comment-for-compared-commit \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --after-commit-id {after-commit-id} \
  --content {comment-content} \
  [--before-commit-id {before-commit-id}] \
  [--location filePath={path},filePosition={position},relativeFileVersion={BEFORE|AFTER}] \
  [--client-request-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --after-commit-id (string, required): Full commit ID of the after commit
    • --content (string, required): Content of the comment
    • --before-commit-id (string): Full commit ID of the before commit
    • --location (structure): Location in the file for line-specific comments
    Return Value
    • repositoryName: Name of the repository
    • beforeCommitId: Before commit ID of the comparison
    • afterCommitId: After commit ID of the comparison
    • comment: Comment object with commentId, content, and metadata

    post-comment-for-pull-request

    Posts a comment on a pull request. This command adds code review comments to pull requests in your Django jobpost application, enabling team feedback and discussion on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes under review.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit post-comment-for-pull-request \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --before-commit-id {before-commit-id} \
  --after-commit-id {after-commit-id} \
  --content {comment-content} \
  [--location filePath={path},filePosition={position},relativeFileVersion={BEFORE|AFTER}] \
  [--client-request-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --before-commit-id (string, required): Full commit ID of the before commit
    • --after-commit-id (string, required): Full commit ID of the after commit
    • --content (string, required): Content of the comment
    • --location (structure): Location in the file for line-specific comments
    Return Value
    • repositoryName: Name of the repository
    • pullRequestId: ID of the pull request
    • comment: Comment object with commentId, content, and metadata

    post-comment-reply

    Posts a reply to an existing comment in a pull request or commit comparison. This command enables threaded discussions on code reviews in your Django jobpost application, allowing detailed conversations about Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit post-comment-reply \
  --in-reply-to {parent-comment-id} \
  --content {reply-content} \
  [--client-request-token {token}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --in-reply-to (string, required): System-generated ID of the comment to reply to
    • --content (string, required): Content of the reply
    • --client-request-token (string): Unique token for idempotency
    Return Value
    • comment: Comment object with commentId, content, inReplyTo, and metadata

    put-comment-reaction

    Adds or updates an emoji reaction to a comment. This command enables team members to react to code review comments in your Django jobpost application with emojis, providing quick feedback on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model change discussions.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit put-comment-reaction \
  --comment-id {comment-id} \
  --reaction-value {emoji-shortcode} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --comment-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the comment
    • --reaction-value (string, required): Emoji shortcode for the reaction (e.g., :thumbsup:, :smile:)
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful reaction.

    put-file

    Adds or updates a file in a branch and automatically creates a commit. This command programmatically uploads files to your Django jobpost application repository, enabling automated updates to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model files through CI/CD pipelines and scripts.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit put-file \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --branch-name {branch-name} \
  --file-content {file-content|fileb://path} \
  --file-path {file-path} \
  [--file-mode {EXECUTABLE|NORMAL|SYMLINK}] \
  [--parent-commit-id {parent-commit}] \
  [--commit-message {message}] \
  [--name {committer-name}] \
  [--email {committer-email}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --branch-name (string, required): Name of the branch for the commit
    • --file-content (blob, required): Content of the file (base64 or fileb:// path)
    • --file-path (string, required): Path where the file will be created or updated
    • --file-mode (string): File mode (EXECUTABLE, NORMAL, or SYMLINK)
    • --commit-message (string): Commit message for the change
    Return Value
    • commitId: Full commit ID of the created commit
    • blobId: Blob ID of the uploaded file
    • treeId: Tree ID of the commit

    put-repository-triggers

    Configures triggers for a CodeCommit repository to invoke Lambda functions or publish to SNS topics. This command sets up automated event notifications for your Django jobpost application repository, triggering CI/CD pipelines when Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code is pushed.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit put-repository-triggers \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --triggers name={trigger-name},destinationArn={arn},customData={data},branches={branch1},{branch2},events={event1},{event2} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --triggers (list, required): Array of trigger configurations with name, destinationArn, branches, and events
    Return Value
    • configurationId: System-generated ID for the trigger configuration

    tag-resource

    Adds tags to a CodeCommit resource. This command applies metadata tags to your Django jobpost application repositories, enabling organization, cost allocation, and access control for repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit tag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tags {key1=value1,key2=value2} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CodeCommit resource to tag
    • --tags (map, required): Map of tag keys to tag values
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful tagging.

    test-repository-triggers

    Tests repository triggers to verify they are configured correctly. This command validates trigger configurations for your Django jobpost application repository, ensuring Lambda functions and SNS topics will be properly invoked when Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code is pushed.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit test-repository-triggers \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --triggers name={trigger-name},destinationArn={arn},branches={branch1},events={event1} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --triggers (list, required): Array of trigger configurations to test
    Return Value
    • successfulExecutions: Array of trigger names that executed successfully
    • failedExecutions: Array of failed trigger details with trigger name and failure message

    untag-resource

    Removes tags from a CodeCommit resource. This command deletes metadata tags from your Django jobpost application repositories, enabling cleanup of obsolete organization and cost allocation tags from repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit untag-resource \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --tag-keys {key1} {key2} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the CodeCommit resource to untag
    • --tag-keys (list, required): List of tag keys to remove
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful tag removal.

    update-approval-rule-template-content

    Updates the content of an approval rule template. This command modifies approval workflow requirements for your Django jobpost application code reviews, changing approval counts or approval pool members for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model change reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-approval-rule-template-content \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --new-rule-content {rule-content-json} \
  [--existing-rule-content-sha256 {sha256}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the template to update
    • --new-rule-content (string, required): New JSON content defining approval requirements
    • --existing-rule-content-sha256 (string): SHA-256 hash of existing content for optimistic locking
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplate: Updated template object with new content and timestamps

    update-approval-rule-template-description

    Updates the description of an approval rule template. This command modifies the description of approval workflow templates for your Django jobpost application, providing clearer documentation of approval requirements for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-approval-rule-template-description \
  --approval-rule-template-name {template-name} \
  --approval-rule-template-description {description} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Name of the template to update
    • --approval-rule-template-description (string, required): New description for the template
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplate: Updated template object with new description

    update-approval-rule-template-name

    Updates the name of an approval rule template. This command renames approval workflow templates for your Django jobpost application, allowing better organization of templates used for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code review requirements.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-approval-rule-template-name \
  --old-approval-rule-template-name {old-name} \
  --new-approval-rule-template-name {new-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --old-approval-rule-template-name (string, required): Current name of the template
    • --new-approval-rule-template-name (string, required): New name for the template
    Return Value
    • approvalRuleTemplate: Updated template object with new name

    update-comment

    Updates the content of a comment. This command modifies existing code review comments in your Django jobpost application repository, allowing correction or clarification of feedback on Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-comment \
  --comment-id {comment-id} \
  --content {new-content} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --comment-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the comment to update
    • --content (string, required): New content for the comment
    Return Value
    • comment: Updated comment object with new content and lastModifiedDate

    update-default-branch

    Sets or updates the default branch for a repository. This command changes which branch is used by default for your Django jobpost application repository, specifying the primary branch for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model development workflows.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-default-branch \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --default-branch-name {branch-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository
    • --default-branch-name (string, required): Name of the branch to set as default
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful update.

    update-pull-request-approval-rule-content

    Updates the content of an approval rule for a specific pull request. This command modifies approval requirements on individual pull requests in your Django jobpost application, changing requirements for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model change reviews.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-pull-request-approval-rule-content \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --approval-rule-name {rule-name} \
  --new-rule-content {rule-content-json} \
  [--existing-rule-content-sha256 {sha256}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --approval-rule-name (string, required): Name of the approval rule to update
    • --new-rule-content (string, required): New JSON content defining approval requirements
    • --existing-rule-content-sha256 (string): SHA-256 hash for optimistic locking
    Return Value
    • approvalRule: Updated approval rule object with new content

    update-pull-request-approval-state

    Updates the approval state for a user on a specified pull request. This command submits approval or revocation on pull requests in your Django jobpost application, enabling reviewers to approve or request changes to Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model modifications.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-pull-request-approval-state \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --revision-id {revision-id} \
  --approval-state {APPROVE|REVOKE} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --revision-id (string, required): Revision ID of the pull request
    • --approval-state (string, required): APPROVE to approve, REVOKE to remove approval
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful approval state update.

    update-pull-request-description

    Updates the description of a pull request. This command modifies pull request descriptions in your Django jobpost application, allowing updates to explain Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes more clearly or add additional context.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-pull-request-description \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --description {new-description} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --description (string, required): New description for the pull request
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Updated pull request object with new description

    update-pull-request-status

    Updates the status of a pull request. This command closes or reopens pull requests in your Django jobpost application repository, managing the lifecycle of code reviews for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-pull-request-status \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --pull-request-status {OPEN|CLOSED} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --pull-request-status (string, required): New status (OPEN or CLOSED)
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Updated pull request object with new status

    update-pull-request-title

    Updates the title of a pull request. This command modifies pull request titles in your Django jobpost application, allowing clearer naming of code reviews for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model changes.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-pull-request-title \
  --pull-request-id {pull-request-id} \
  --title {new-title} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --pull-request-id (string, required): System-generated ID of the pull request
    • --title (string, required): New title for the pull request
    Return Value
    • pullRequest: Updated pull request object with new title

    update-repository-description

    Updates the description of a CodeCommit repository. This command modifies repository descriptions for your Django jobpost application, providing clearer documentation of repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-repository-description \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--repository-description {description}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository to update
    • --repository-description (string): New description (omit to remove existing description)
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful update.

    update-repository-encryption-key
