    Updates the AWS KMS encryption key used to encrypt data in a repository. This command changes the encryption key for your Django jobpost application repository, enhancing security for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code with customer-managed keys.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-repository-encryption-key \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --kms-key-id {kms-key-id-or-arn} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --repository-name (string, required): Name of the repository to update
    • --kms-key-id (string, required): ID, ARN, or alias of the AWS KMS key to use
    Return Value
    • repositoryId: ID of the updated repository
    • kmsKeyId: The new KMS key ID for the repository
    • originalKmsKeyId: The previous KMS key ID

    update-repository-name

    Renames a CodeCommit repository. This command changes the name of your Django jobpost application repository, allowing reorganization of repositories managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model code to follow naming conventions.

    Syntax
    aws codecommit update-repository-name \
  --old-name {old-repository-name} \
  --new-name {new-repository-name} \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --old-name (string, required): Current name of the repository
    • --new-name (string, required): New name for the repository
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful rename.

    ⚙️ Management & Governance

    AWS Management services help you monitor, manage, and govern your infrastructure. This section covers CloudWatch for monitoring, CloudFormation for infrastructure as code, Systems Manager for operational management, and CloudTrail for auditing.

    AWS CloudWatch

    Monitoring and observability service for tracking metrics, logs, and application performance

    📊 CloudWatch Overview

    AWS CloudWatch provides comprehensive monitoring and observability for your Django jobpost application. Track application metrics, monitor logs, set alarms, and create dashboards to visualize the performance of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models. CloudWatch helps you detect issues, optimize performance, and maintain application health across your infrastructure.

    Core CloudWatch Operations for Django Monitoring

    put-metric-alarm - Create performance alarms
    put-metric-data - Send custom metrics
    get-metric-statistics - Retrieve metric data
    describe-alarms - List configured alarms
    put-dashboard - Create monitoring dashboards
    get-metric-data - Query multiple metrics
    list-metrics - Discover available metrics
    set-alarm-state - Test alarm notifications

    delete-alarms

    Deletes the specified alarms from AWS CloudWatch. This command removes alarm configurations for your Django jobpost application, cleaning up alarms that monitor Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model metrics when they're no longer needed or need to be reconfigured.

    Syntax
    aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names {alarm-name} [{alarm-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --alarm-names (list, required): Names of the alarms to delete. Can specify multiple alarm names separated by spaces.
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Attributes
    • AlarmName (string): Name identifier for the alarm being deleted
    • StateValue (string): State of alarm before deletion (OK, ALARM, INSUFFICIENT_DATA)
    • Namespace (string): Metric namespace the alarm monitors
    • MetricName (string): Name of the metric being monitored
    Methods

    DeleteAlarms - Permanently removes CloudWatch alarms from your account

    Syntax
    aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names {alarm-name-1} {alarm-name-2} \
  --region {region}
    Parameters
    • AlarmNames (list of strings, required): Array of alarm names to delete. Each name must match exactly. Maximum 100 alarms per request.
    • Region (string, optional): AWS region where alarms exist. Defaults to configured region.
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful deletion. HTTP 200 status code indicates success. If an alarm doesn't exist, the operation succeeds without error.

    5 Detailed CloudWatch delete-alarms Examples
    Example 1: Delete Single JobPost Creation Rate Alarm

    Remove a CloudWatch alarm that monitors JobPost creation rates in your Django application. This alarm was tracking when job posting activity drops below threshold, indicating potential application issues affecting Author productivity.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete alarm monitoring JobPost creation rate
aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names "jobpost-low-creation-rate" \
  --region us-east-1

echo "Alarm deleted successfully"
    ✓ Alarm deleted successfully No output returned - operation completed successfully. HTTP Status: 200 OK Deleted alarm details: - Name: jobpost-low-creation-rate - Type: Metric Alarm - Namespace: Custom/JobPost - Metric: JobPostCreationsPerHour - Previous State: OK - Actions: SNS notification to ops-team
    # Django QuerySet - Verify alarm deletion and check JobPost metrics from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta # Check recent JobPost creation activity last_hour = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) recent_posts = JobPost.objects.filter(created_at__gte=last_hour) print("JobPost Creation Monitoring (Alarm Deleted)") print("="*60) print(f"JobPosts created in last hour: {recent_posts.count()}") # Output: JobPosts created in last hour: 0 # Verify alarm was monitoring this metric posts_by_author = recent_posts.values('author__user__username').annotate( post_count=Count('id') ).order_by('-post_count') print("\nAuthor Activity (Last Hour):") for author_data in posts_by_author[:5]: print(f" {author_data['author__user__username']}: {author_data['post_count']} posts") # Output: (empty - no recent posts) # Check if alarm threshold was justified daily_average = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(days=7) ).count() / 7 print(f"\nDaily average JobPosts: {daily_average:.1f}") # Output: Daily average JobPosts: 0.0 # Skills associated with recent posts skills_in_use = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__in=recent_posts ).distinct() print(f"Active Skills in recent posts: {skills_in_use.count()}") # Output: Active Skills in recent posts: 0 # Location distribution location_stats = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__in=recent_posts ).values('city', 'country').annotate( post_count=Count('jobpost') ).order_by('-post_count') print("\nTop Locations:") for loc in location_stats[:3]: print(f" {loc['city']}, {loc['country']}: {loc['post_count']} posts") # Output: (empty - no recent posts) print("\n✓ Alarm monitoring removed - manual monitoring recommended")
    Example 2: Bulk Delete Development Environment Alarms

    Clean up multiple test alarms created during Django application development. Remove alarms for Skills queries, User authentication failures, and Author activity that were used for testing CloudWatch integration.

    #!/bin/bash
# Bulk delete development/test alarms
aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names \
    "dev-skills-query-latency" \
    "dev-user-login-failures" \
    "dev-author-post-rate" \
    "dev-location-search-errors" \
    "dev-jobpost-database-connections" \
  --region us-east-1

# Verify deletion by attempting to describe deleted alarms
aws cloudwatch describe-alarms \
  --alarm-names \
    "dev-skills-query-latency" \
    "dev-user-login-failures" \
  --region us-east-1
    ✓ 5 development alarms deleted successfully Deleted Alarms: 1. dev-skills-query-latency - Monitored: Skills.objects.filter() query times - Threshold: > 500ms average latency 2. dev-user-login-failures - Monitored: Failed User authentication attempts - Threshold: > 5 failures per minute 3. dev-author-post-rate - Monitored: Author.objects.create() success rate - Threshold: < 95% success rate 4. dev-location-search-errors - Monitored: Location search API errors - Threshold: > 2% error rate 5. dev-jobpost-database-connections - Monitored: Database connection pool usage - Threshold: > 80% connections in use Verification Output: { "MetricAlarms": [], "CompositeAlarms": [] } ✓ Confirmed: All alarms successfully deleted
    # Django QuerySet - Performance metrics after alarm deletion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Avg, Count, Q from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta import time User = get_user_model() print("Development Environment Metrics (Post-Alarm Deletion)") print("="*60) # 1. Skills query performance (was monitored by dev-skills-query-latency) start_time = time.time() top_skills = Skills.objects.annotate( job_count=Count('jobpost') ).order_by('-job_count')[:10] query_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000 print(f"\n1. Skills Query Performance:") print(f" Query time: {query_time:.2f}ms") print(f" Total skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f" Top skills returned: {top_skills.count()}") # Output: # Query time: 2.45ms # Total skills: 0 # Top skills returned: 0 # 2. User authentication metrics (was monitored by dev-user-login-failures) last_hour = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) active_users = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=last_hour, is_active=True ).count() failed_login_attempts = 0 # Would come from auth logs print(f"\n2. User Authentication Status:") print(f" Active users (last hour): {active_users}") print(f" Failed login attempts: {failed_login_attempts}") print(f" Success rate: N/A") # Output: # Active users (last hour): 0 # Failed login attempts: 0 # Success rate: N/A # 3. Author posting activity (was monitored by dev-author-post-rate) recent_authors = Author.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=last_hour ).distinct() author_post_success = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=last_hour, author__isnull=False ).count() print(f"\n3. Author Activity Metrics:") print(f" Active authors: {recent_authors.count()}") print(f" Successful posts: {author_post_success}") print(f" Average posts per author: N/A") # Output: # Active authors: 0 # Successful posts: 0 # Average posts per author: N/A # 4. Location search performance (was monitored by dev-location-search-errors) location_searches = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=last_hour ).distinct().count() print(f"\n4. Location Search Metrics:") print(f" Unique locations searched: {location_searches}") print(f" Search error rate: 0.00%") # Output: # Unique locations searched: 0 # Search error rate: 0.00% # 5. Database connection usage (was monitored by dev-jobpost-database-connections) from django.db import connection queries_executed = len(connection.queries) print(f"\n5. Database Connection Metrics:") print(f" Queries executed: {queries_executed}") print(f" Connection pool: Django default (not pooled)") # Output: # Queries executed: 5 # Connection pool: Django default (not pooled) print("\n✓ All development alarms removed") print("⚠ Consider implementing application-level monitoring")
    Example 3: Delete Alarms for Deprecated Metrics with Error Handling

    Remove CloudWatch alarms that monitor deprecated Django model metrics. Handle cases where alarms may already be deleted or never existed, ensuring cleanup script is idempotent for Skills rating and legacy User profile metrics.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete deprecated metric alarms with error handling
ALARMS_TO_DELETE=(
  "legacy-skills-rating-low"
  "deprecated-user-profile-completion"
  "old-author-reputation-score"
  "removed-location-geocoding-failures"
  "archived-jobpost-expiration-rate"
)

echo "Attempting to delete ${#ALARMS_TO_DELETE[@]} deprecated alarms..."

# Delete alarms (CloudWatch doesn't error if alarm doesn't exist)
aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names "${ALARMS_TO_DELETE[@]}" \
  --region us-east-1 2>&1

EXIT_CODE=$?

if [ $EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ Deletion command completed successfully"
  
  # Verify which alarms actually existed
  for alarm in "${ALARMS_TO_DELETE[@]}"; do
    ALARM_EXISTS=$(aws cloudwatch describe-alarms \
      --alarm-names "$alarm" \
      --query 'length(MetricAlarms)' \
      --output text \
      --region us-east-1)
    
    if [ "$ALARM_EXISTS" -eq "0" ]; then
      echo "  ✓ $alarm - confirmed deleted/never existed"
    else
      echo "  ✗ $alarm - still exists (unexpected)"
    fi
  done
else
  echo "✗ Deletion command failed with exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi
    Attempting to delete 5 deprecated alarms... ✓ Deletion command completed successfully Verification Results: ✓ legacy-skills-rating-low - confirmed deleted/never existed ✓ deprecated-user-profile-completion - confirmed deleted/never existed ✓ old-author-reputation-score - confirmed deleted/never existed ✓ removed-location-geocoding-failures - confirmed deleted/never existed ✓ archived-jobpost-expiration-rate - confirmed deleted/never existed Summary: - Total alarms processed: 5 - Successfully deleted/verified: 5 - Errors encountered: 0 - Exit code: 0 Cleanup Details: 1. legacy-skills-rating-low - Original metric: Custom/Skills/RatingScore - Replaced by: Skills.avg_rating field in Django 2. deprecated-user-profile-completion - Original metric: Custom/User/ProfileCompleteness - Replaced by: User profile model redesign 3. old-author-reputation-score - Original metric: Custom/Author/ReputationPoints - Replaced by: Author.reputation_score property 4. removed-location-geocoding-failures - Original metric: Custom/Location/GeocodingErrors - Replaced by: Third-party geocoding service 5. archived-jobpost-expiration-rate - Original metric: Custom/JobPost/ExpirationRate - Replaced by: Automated archival system ✓ All deprecated alarms successfully removed
    # Django QuerySet - Verify deprecated metrics replaced by model fields from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Avg, F, Q, Count from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta print("Deprecated Metrics → Django Model Fields Migration") print("="*60) # 1. Skills rating (replaced CloudWatch metric) # Old: CloudWatch custom metric "Skills/RatingScore" # New: Django model field avg_rating skills_with_ratings = Skills.objects.exclude( Q(avg_rating__isnull=True) | Q(avg_rating=0) ) print("\n1. Skills Rating System:") print(f" Skills with ratings: {skills_with_ratings.count()}") if skills_with_ratings.exists(): avg_skill_rating = skills_with_ratings.aggregate( avg=Avg('avg_rating') )['avg'] print(f" Average rating: {avg_skill_rating:.2f}/5.0") else: print(" Average rating: N/A (no ratings yet)") # Output: # Skills with ratings: 0 # Average rating: N/A (no ratings yet) # 2. User profile completion (replaced CloudWatch metric) # Old: CloudWatch custom metric "User/ProfileCompleteness" # New: Django User model with profile fields total_users = User.objects.count() users_with_complete_profiles = User.objects.filter( first_name__isnull=False, last_name__isnull=False, email__isnull=False ).exclude( Q(first_name='') | Q(last_name='') | Q(email='') ).count() print(f"\n2. User Profile Completion:") print(f" Total users: {total_users}") print(f" Complete profiles: {users_with_complete_profiles}") if total_users > 0: completion_rate = (users_with_complete_profiles / total_users) * 100 print(f" Completion rate: {completion_rate:.1f}%") else: print(" Completion rate: N/A") # Output: # Total users: 0 # Complete profiles: 0 # Completion rate: N/A # 3. Author reputation score (replaced CloudWatch metric) # Old: CloudWatch custom metric "Author/ReputationPoints" # New: Django Author model with reputation_score property authors_with_reputation = Author.objects.exclude( reputation_score__isnull=True ) print(f"\n3. Author Reputation System:") print(f" Authors with reputation: {authors_with_reputation.count()}") if authors_with_reputation.exists(): top_authors = authors_with_reputation.order_by('-reputation_score')[:5] print(" Top 5 authors:") for author in top_authors: print(f" - {author.user.username}: {author.reputation_score} pts") else: print(" Top authors: None yet") # Output: # Authors with reputation: 0 # Top authors: None yet # 4. Location geocoding (replaced CloudWatch metric) # Old: CloudWatch custom metric "Location/GeocodingErrors" # New: Location model with validated coordinates locations_with_coords = Location.objects.exclude( Q(latitude__isnull=True) | Q(longitude__isnull=True) ) print(f"\n4. Location Geocoding Status:") print(f" Total locations: {Location.objects.count()}") print(f" Geocoded locations: {locations_with_coords.count()}") if Location.objects.count() > 0: geocoding_success = (locations_with_coords.count() / Location.objects.count()) * 100 print(f" Geocoding success rate: {geocoding_success:.1f}%") else: print(" Geocoding success rate: N/A") # Output: # Total locations: 0 # Geocoded locations: 0 # Geocoding success rate: N/A # 5. JobPost expiration tracking (replaced CloudWatch metric) # Old: CloudWatch custom metric "JobPost/ExpirationRate" # New: Django JobPost model with expires_at field active_posts = JobPost.objects.filter( expires_at__gt=timezone.now(), status='published' ) expired_posts = JobPost.objects.filter( expires_at__lte=timezone.now(), status='published' ) print(f"\n5. JobPost Expiration Tracking:") print(f" Active posts: {active_posts.count()}") print(f" Expired posts: {expired_posts.count()}") print(f" Auto-archive enabled: ✓") # Output: # Active posts: 0 # Expired posts: 0 # Auto-archive enabled: ✓ print("\n✓ All deprecated CloudWatch metrics migrated to Django models") print("✓ Alarm deletion confirmed - using Django-native monitoring")
    Example 4: Delete All Alarms Matching Pattern for Testing Environment

    Remove all CloudWatch alarms with a specific prefix pattern used in testing environment. First list alarms to delete, then batch delete them for comprehensive cleanup of test monitoring for Skills, Users, Authors, Locations, and JobPost test data.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete all alarms matching test-jobpost-* pattern

# Step 1: List all alarms with test prefix
echo "Finding alarms with 'test-jobpost-' prefix..."
TEST_ALARMS=$(aws cloudwatch describe-alarms \
  --alarm-name-prefix "test-jobpost-" \
  --query 'MetricAlarms[].AlarmName' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

if [ -z "$TEST_ALARMS" ]; then
  echo "No test alarms found"
  exit 0
fi

# Convert space-separated string to array
ALARM_ARRAY=($TEST_ALARMS)
ALARM_COUNT=${#ALARM_ARRAY[@]}

echo "Found $ALARM_COUNT test alarms to delete:"
printf '  - %s\n' "${ALARM_ARRAY[@]}"

# Step 2: Delete alarms in batches of 100 (AWS limit)
BATCH_SIZE=100
for ((i=0; i<$ALARM_COUNT; i+=BATCH_SIZE)); do
  BATCH=("${ALARM_ARRAY[@]:i:BATCH_SIZE}")
  BATCH_NUM=$((i/BATCH_SIZE + 1))
  
  echo "Deleting batch $BATCH_NUM (${#BATCH[@]} alarms)..."
  aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
    --alarm-names "${BATCH[@]}" \
    --region us-east-1
    
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ Batch $BATCH_NUM deleted successfully"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Batch $BATCH_NUM failed"
  fi
done

# Step 3: Verify deletion
REMAINING=$(aws cloudwatch describe-alarms \
  --alarm-name-prefix "test-jobpost-" \
  --query 'length(MetricAlarms)' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Verification: $REMAINING alarms remaining"
    Finding alarms with 'test-jobpost-' prefix... Found 23 test alarms to delete: - test-jobpost-skills-query-high-latency - test-jobpost-skills-cache-miss-rate - test-jobpost-user-registration-errors - test-jobpost-user-login-latency - test-jobpost-user-session-timeout - test-jobpost-author-post-failures - test-jobpost-author-update-errors - test-jobpost-author-query-performance - test-jobpost-location-search-latency - test-jobpost-location-api-errors - test-jobpost-location-geocoding-failures - test-jobpost-jobpost-creation-rate - test-jobpost-jobpost-deletion-errors - test-jobpost-jobpost-update-latency - test-jobpost-jobpost-search-performance - test-jobpost-database-connection-pool - test-jobpost-redis-cache-hit-rate - test-jobpost-api-response-time - test-jobpost-celery-task-failures - test-jobpost-email-delivery-failures - test-jobpost-file-upload-errors - test-jobpost-image-processing-latency - test-jobpost-pdf-generation-errors Deleting batch 1 (23 alarms)... ✓ Batch 1 deleted successfully Verification: 0 alarms remaining Summary: - Total test alarms found: 23 - Successfully deleted: 23 - Failed deletions: 0 - Remaining alarms: 0 ✓ All test environment alarms cleaned up successfully
    # Django QuerySet - Test environment cleanup verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Avg, Max, Min from django.utils import timezone from django.core.cache import cache import time print("Test Environment Metrics (Post-Alarm Cleanup)") print("="*60) # Verify test data still exists after alarm deletion print("\n1. Skills Test Data:") test_skills = Skills.objects.filter( name__istartswith='test_' ) print(f" Test skills remaining: {test_skills.count()}") print(f" Total skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Output: # Test skills remaining: 0 # Total skills: 0 # Check User test accounts print("\n2. User Test Accounts:") test_users = User.objects.filter( Q(username__istartswith='test_') | Q(email__icontains='test@') ) print(f" Test users: {test_users.count()}") print(f" Total users: {User.objects.count()}") # Output: # Test users: 0 # Total users: 0 # Author test data print("\n3. Author Test Profiles:") test_authors = Author.objects.filter( user__username__istartswith='test_' ) print(f" Test authors: {test_authors.count()}") print(f" Total authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Output: # Test authors: 0 # Total authors: 0 # Location test data print("\n4. Location Test Data:") test_locations = Location.objects.filter( city__istartswith='Test' ) print(f" Test locations: {test_locations.count()}") print(f" Total locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Output: # Test locations: 0 # Total locations: 0 # JobPost test data print("\n5. JobPost Test Data:") test_jobposts = JobPost.objects.filter( Q(title__istartswith='[TEST]') | Q(author__user__username__istartswith='test_') ) print(f" Test job posts: {test_jobposts.count()}") print(f" Total job posts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") # Output: # Test job posts: 0 # Total job posts: 0 # Performance metrics (previously monitored by deleted alarms) print("\n6. Performance Metrics (Now Unmonitored):") # Skills query performance start = time.time() Skills.objects.all()[:100] skills_query_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000 print(f" Skills query time: {skills_query_time:.2f}ms") # Cache performance cache_key = 'test_metric' cache.set(cache_key, 'test_value', 300) cache_hit = cache.get(cache_key) is not None print(f" Cache operational: {'✓' if cache_hit else '✗'}") # Database connection from django.db import connection print(f" DB queries executed: {len(connection.queries)}") # Output: # Skills query time: 1.23ms # Cache operational: ✓ # DB queries executed: 6 print("\n7. Cleanup Summary:") print(" ✓ 23 test alarms deleted") print(" ✓ Test data preserved (if any)") print(" ✓ Application functioning normally") print(" ⚠ Test metrics no longer monitored by CloudWatch") print(" → Recommendation: Set up production alarms for active metrics") # Suggestions for production monitoring print("\n8. Production Monitoring Recommendations:") production_metrics = [ "Skills query latency (p95 > 200ms)", "User authentication failure rate (> 5%)", "Author post creation errors (> 2%)", "Location API timeout rate (> 1%)", "JobPost database deadlocks (> 0)" ] print(" Consider creating alarms for:") for metric in production_metrics: print(f" • {metric}")
    Example 5: Delete Composite Alarm and All Child Alarms

    Remove a composite CloudWatch alarm that aggregates multiple Django application health metrics, along with all its child alarms monitoring Skills availability, User authentication, Author services, Location API, and JobPost database performance.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete composite alarm and all child alarms

COMPOSITE_ALARM="jobpost-application-health-critical"

# Step 1: Describe composite alarm to find child alarms
echo "Retrieving composite alarm configuration..."
CHILD_ALARMS=$(aws cloudwatch describe-alarms \
  --alarm-names "$COMPOSITE_ALARM" \
  --query 'CompositeAlarms[0].AlarmRule' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1 | \
  grep -oP 'ALARM\(\K[^)]+')

# Extract individual alarm names from rule
CHILD_ALARM_ARRAY=()
while IFS= read -r alarm; do
  CHILD_ALARM_ARRAY+=("$alarm")
done <<< "$CHILD_ALARMS"

echo "Found composite alarm with ${#CHILD_ALARM_ARRAY[@]} child alarms"

# Step 2: Delete composite alarm first
echo "Deleting composite alarm: $COMPOSITE_ALARM"
aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names "$COMPOSITE_ALARM" \
  --region us-east-1

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ Composite alarm deleted"
else
  echo "✗ Failed to delete composite alarm"
  exit 1
fi

# Step 3: Delete all child alarms
echo "Deleting ${#CHILD_ALARM_ARRAY[@]} child alarms..."
aws cloudwatch delete-alarms \
  --alarm-names "${CHILD_ALARM_ARRAY[@]}" \
  --region us-east-1

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ All child alarms deleted"
else
  echo "✗ Some child alarms may not have been deleted"
fi

# Step 4: Verify complete deletion
echo "Verifying deletion..."
ALL_ALARMS=("$COMPOSITE_ALARM" "${CHILD_ALARM_ARRAY[@]}")
for alarm in "${ALL_ALARMS[@]}"; do
  EXISTS=$(aws cloudwatch describe-alarms \
    --alarm-names "$alarm" \
    --query 'length(MetricAlarms + CompositeAlarms)' \
    --output text \
    --region us-east-1)
    
  if [ "$EXISTS" -eq "0" ]; then
    echo "  ✓ $alarm - deleted"
  else
    echo "  ✗ $alarm - still exists"
  fi
done
    Retrieving composite alarm configuration... Found composite alarm with 5 child alarms: 1. jobpost-skills-service-unhealthy 2. jobpost-user-auth-failing 3. jobpost-author-api-degraded 4. jobpost-location-service-down 5. jobpost-database-high-connections Composite Alarm Rule: ALARM(jobpost-skills-service-unhealthy) OR ALARM(jobpost-user-auth-failing) OR ALARM(jobpost-author-api-degraded) OR ALARM(jobpost-location-service-down) OR ALARM(jobpost-database-high-connections) Deleting composite alarm: jobpost-application-health-critical ✓ Composite alarm deleted Deleting 5 child alarms... ✓ All child alarms deleted Verifying deletion... ✓ jobpost-application-health-critical - deleted ✓ jobpost-skills-service-unhealthy - deleted ✓ jobpost-user-auth-failing - deleted ✓ jobpost-author-api-degraded - deleted ✓ jobpost-location-service-down - deleted ✓ jobpost-database-high-connections - deleted Summary: - Composite alarm: 1 deleted - Child alarms: 5 deleted - Total alarms removed: 6 - Verification: All confirmed deleted Previous Alarm Configuration: ================================== Composite: jobpost-application-health-critical - Purpose: Aggregate Django app health monitoring - Logic: Trigger if ANY child alarm enters ALARM state - Actions: SNS → PagerDuty, Email to ops@anihpj.com Child Alarms: 1. Skills Service: API response time > 1s 2. User Auth: Login failure rate > 10% 3. Author API: Error rate > 5% 4. Location Service: HTTP 500 errors > 3/min 5. Database: Active connections > 90% of max ✓ Complete alarm hierarchy removed successfully
    # Django QuerySet - Application health check after alarm deletion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Avg from django.db import connection from django.core.cache import cache from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta import time print("Django Application Health Check (Post-Alarm Deletion)") print("="*60) # 1. Skills Service Health (was: jobpost-skills-service-unhealthy) print("\n1. Skills Service Health:") start = time.time() try: skills_count = Skills.objects.count() skills_with_jobs = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct().count() response_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000 print(f" Status: ✓ Operational") print(f" Response time: {response_time:.2f}ms") print(f" Total skills: {skills_count}") print(f" Skills in use: {skills_with_jobs}") except Exception as e: print(f" Status: ✗ Error - {str(e)}") # Output: # Status: ✓ Operational # Response time: 2.15ms # Total skills: 0 # Skills in use: 0 # 2. User Authentication Health (was: jobpost-user-auth-failing) print("\n2. User Authentication Service:") try: total_users = User.objects.count() active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() recent_logins = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) ).count() print(f" Status: ✓ Operational") print(f" Total users: {total_users}") print(f" Active users: {active_users}") print(f" Recent logins (1h): {recent_logins}") print(f" Auth failure rate: 0.00%") except Exception as e: print(f" Status: ✗ Error - {str(e)}") # Output: # Status: ✓ Operational # Total users: 0 # Active users: 0 # Recent logins (1h): 0 # Auth failure rate: 0.00% # 3. Author API Health (was: jobpost-author-api-degraded) print("\n3. Author API Service:") try: total_authors = Author.objects.count() active_authors = Author.objects.filter( user__is_active=True ).count() recent_posts = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) ).count() print(f" Status: ✓ Operational") print(f" Total authors: {total_authors}") print(f" Active authors: {active_authors}") print(f" Posts created (1h): {recent_posts}") print(f" API error rate: 0.00%") except Exception as e: print(f" Status: ✗ Error - {str(e)}") # Output: # Status: ✓ Operational # Total authors: 0 # Active authors: 0 # Posts created (1h): 0 # API error rate: 0.00% # 4. Location Service Health (was: jobpost-location-service-down) print("\n4. Location Service:") try: total_locations = Location.objects.count() locations_with_jobs = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct().count() # Test geocoding functionality test_location = Location.objects.first() geocoding_available = test_location is not None and \ test_location.latitude is not None print(f" Status: ✓ Operational") print(f" Total locations: {total_locations}") print(f" Locations with jobs: {locations_with_jobs}") print(f" Geocoding: {'✓ Available' if geocoding_available else '⚠ No data'}") print(f" Service errors: 0") except Exception as e: print(f" Status: ✗ Error - {str(e)}") # Output: # Status: ✓ Operational # Total locations: 0 # Locations with jobs: 0 # Geocoding: ⚠ No data # Service errors: 0 # 5. Database Connection Health (was: jobpost-database-high-connections) print("\n5. Database Connection Pool:") try: # Get current connection info queries_count = len(connection.queries) # Test database responsiveness start = time.time() JobPost.objects.count() db_response = (time.time() - start) * 1000 print(f" Status: ✓ Operational") print(f" Response time: {db_response:.2f}ms") print(f" Queries executed: {queries_count}") print(f" Connection pool: Django default") print(f" Pool utilization: N/A (no pooling)") except Exception as e: print(f" Status: ✗ Error - {str(e)}") # Output: # Status: ✓ Operational # Response time: 1.05ms # Queries executed: 5 # Connection pool: Django default # Pool utilization: N/A (no pooling) # Overall Application Health Summary print("\n" + "="*60) print("OVERALL APPLICATION HEALTH SUMMARY") print("="*60) print("✓ Skills Service: Operational") print("✓ User Authentication: Operational") print("✓ Author API: Operational") print("✓ Location Service: Operational") print("✓ Database: Operational") print("\n⚠ CloudWatch Alarms Removed:") print(" - Composite alarm: jobpost-application-health-critical") print(" - Child alarms: 5 monitoring alarms") print("\n→ Recommendation:") print(" Consider implementing alternative monitoring:") print(" • Django health check endpoints") print(" • Application Performance Monitoring (APM)") print(" • Custom Django middleware for metrics") print(" • Database query logging and analysis") print("\n✓ All services operational - manual monitoring enabled")

    delete-anomaly-detector

    Deletes an anomaly detector from CloudWatch. This command removes machine learning-based anomaly detection configurations for Django jobpost application metrics, stopping automatic detection of unusual patterns in Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model performance data.

    Syntax
    aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
  [--namespace {namespace}] \
  [--metric-name {metric-name}] \
  [--dimensions {dimensions-json}] \
  [--stat {statistic}] \
  [--single-metric-anomaly-detector {detector-config}] \
  [--metric-math-anomaly-detector {math-detector-config}] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --namespace (string, optional): Namespace of the metric (e.g., AWS/EC2, Custom/Django)
    • --metric-name (string, optional): Name of the metric monitored by the detector
    • --dimensions (list, optional): Dimensions of the metric for precise detector identification
    • --stat (string, optional): Statistic type (Average, Sum, Maximum, Minimum, SampleCount)
    • --single-metric-anomaly-detector (structure, optional): Configuration for single metric detector
    • --metric-math-anomaly-detector (structure, optional): Configuration for metric math detector
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Attributes
    • Namespace (string): Metric namespace where anomaly detector operates
    • MetricName (string): Name of metric being monitored for anomalies
    • Stat (string): Statistical function used (Average, Sum, Maximum, etc.)
    • Dimensions (list): Metric dimensions for precise detector identification
    Methods

    DeleteAnomalyDetector - Removes ML-based anomaly detection from CloudWatch metrics

    Syntax
    aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
  --namespace {namespace} \
  --metric-name {metric-name} \
  --stat {statistic} \
  --dimensions {dimensions-json}
    Parameters
    • Namespace (string, required): Metric namespace (Custom/Django, AWS/EC2, etc.)
    • MetricName (string, required): Specific metric name to stop monitoring
    • Stat (string, required): Statistic type the detector was monitoring
    • Dimensions (list, optional): Dimension filters for the metric
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful deletion. HTTP 200 indicates success. Detector configuration is permanently removed.

    5 Detailed CloudWatch delete-anomaly-detector Examples
    Example 1: Delete Anomaly Detector for Skills Query Response Time

    Remove anomaly detection from Skills model query performance metrics. This detector was using machine learning to identify unusual patterns in Skills.objects.filter() query response times in your Django jobpost application.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete anomaly detector monitoring Skills query latency
aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
  --namespace "Custom/Django" \
  --metric-name "SkillsQueryResponseTime" \
  --stat "Average" \
  --dimensions Name=Model,Value=Skills Name=Operation,Value=filter \
  --region us-east-1

echo "Anomaly detector deleted"
    ✓ Anomaly detector deleted successfully Detector Configuration (Before Deletion): - Namespace: Custom/Django - Metric: SkillsQueryResponseTime - Statistic: Average - Dimensions: * Model: Skills * Operation: filter - Training Period: 14 days - Detection Threshold: 3 standard deviations - State: TRAINED Anomaly Detection History: - Total anomalies detected: 3 - Last anomaly: 2026-01-10 14:23:45 UTC - Reason: Query time 1,234ms (expected: 45-85ms) HTTP Status: 200 OK ✓ Detector removed from CloudWatch
    # Django QuerySet - Skills query performance after detector deletion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Q, Avg from django.utils import timezone import time print("Skills Query Performance Analysis") print("="*60) # Test Skills query performance (was monitored by anomaly detector) start_time = time.time() skills_query = Skills.objects.filter( Q(name__icontains='python') | Q(name__icontains='django') ).select_related('category')[:100] query_time_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000 print(f"\nSkills Filter Query:") print(f" Query time: {query_time_ms:.2f}ms") print(f" Results returned: {skills_query.count()}") print(f" Status: {'✓ Normal' if query_time_ms < 100 else '⚠ Slow'}") # Output: # Query time: 2.34ms # Results returned: 0 # Status: ✓ Normal # Check Skills usage in JobPosts skills_in_use = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct() print(f"\nSkills Usage:") print(f" Total skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f" Skills in use: {skills_in_use.count()}") print(f" Unused skills: {Skills.objects.count() - skills_in_use.count()}") # Output: # Total skills: 0 # Skills in use: 0 # Unused skills: 0 # JobPost by Skills analysis jobposts_with_skills = JobPost.objects.filter( skills__isnull=False ).distinct().count() print(f"\nJobPost Statistics:") print(f" Posts with skills: {jobposts_with_skills}") print(f" Posts without skills: {JobPost.objects.filter(skills__isnull=True).count()}") # Output: # Posts with skills: 0 # Posts without skills: 0 # Performance metrics print(f"\n⚠ Note: Anomaly detection disabled") print(f" Manual monitoring required for Skills query performance") print(f" Consider setting static alarm thresholds")
    Example 2: Delete User Login Failure Rate Anomaly Detector

    Remove ML-based anomaly detection from User authentication failure metrics. This detector was automatically identifying unusual spikes in failed login attempts that might indicate security issues or application problems.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete User authentication anomaly detector
aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
  --namespace "Custom/Django/Auth" \
  --metric-name "UserLoginFailureRate" \
  --stat "Sum" \
  --dimensions Name=Model,Value=User Name=Action,Value=authentication \
  --region us-east-1

# Verify deletion
aws cloudwatch describe-anomaly-detectors \
  --namespace "Custom/Django/Auth" \
  --metric-name "UserLoginFailureRate" \
  --region us-east-1
    ✓ User authentication anomaly detector deleted Detector Details: - Namespace: Custom/Django/Auth - Metric: UserLoginFailureRate - Statistic: Sum (total failures per period) - Evaluation Period: 5 minutes - Dimensions: * Model: User * Action: authentication Historical Anomalies Detected: 1. 2026-01-08 03:45 - Spike to 47 failures (expected: 2-5) → Action: Security team notified 2. 2026-01-10 19:12 - Spike to 33 failures (expected: 2-5) → Action: Rate limiting applied 3. 2026-01-12 11:30 - Drop to 0 failures (expected: 2-5) → Action: Confirmed maintenance window Verification Output: { "AnomalyDetectors": [] } ✓ Detector confirmed deleted ⚠ Manual monitoring recommended for security metrics
    # Django QuerySet - User authentication monitoring post-detector from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta User = get_user_model() print("User Authentication Analysis (Anomaly Detection Disabled)") print("="*60) # Recent login activity last_hour = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) recent_logins = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=last_hour ).count() print(f"\nRecent Login Activity (Last Hour):") print(f" Successful logins: {recent_logins}") print(f" Active sessions: {recent_logins}") # Output: # Successful logins: 0 # Active sessions: 0 # User account status total_users = User.objects.count() active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() inactive_users = total_users - active_users print(f"\nUser Account Status:") print(f" Total users: {total_users}") print(f" Active accounts: {active_users}") print(f" Inactive accounts: {inactive_users}") # Output: # Total users: 0 # Active accounts: 0 # Inactive accounts: 0 # Authors linked to User accounts authors_with_users = Author.objects.filter( user__isnull=False, user__is_active=True ).count() print(f"\nAuthor-User Relationship:") print(f" Authors with accounts: {authors_with_users}") print(f" Total authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Output: # Authors with accounts: 0 # Total authors: 0 # JobPost creation by authenticated users posts_by_users = JobPost.objects.filter( author__user__isnull=False ).count() print(f"\nContent Created by Authenticated Users:") print(f" JobPosts by registered users: {posts_by_users}") print(f" Total JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") # Output: # JobPosts by registered users: 0 # Total JobPosts: 0 # Security recommendations print(f"\n⚠ Security Monitoring Status:") print(f" CloudWatch Anomaly Detection: Disabled") print(f" Manual threshold alarms: Required") print(f" → Recommendation: Implement fixed-threshold alarm") print(f" → Set alarm: Login failures > 10/minute") print(f" → Enable: Failed login attempt logging")
    Example 3: Bulk Delete All Anomaly Detectors for Django Models

    Remove all anomaly detectors monitoring Django jobpost application metrics. Clean up ML-based monitoring for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations when migrating to static threshold alarms.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete all Django model anomaly detectors

DETECTORS=(
  "Custom/Django:SkillsQueryResponseTime:Average"
  "Custom/Django/Auth:UserLoginFailureRate:Sum"
  "Custom/Django:AuthorPostCreationRate:Count"
  "Custom/Django:LocationSearchLatency:Average"
  "Custom/Django:JobPostDatabaseErrors:Sum"
)

echo "Deleting ${#DETECTORS[@]} anomaly detectors..."

for detector in "${DETECTORS[@]}"; do
  IFS=':' read -r namespace metric stat <<< "$detector"
  
  echo "Deleting: $metric in $namespace ($stat)"
  aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
    --namespace "$namespace" \
    --metric-name "$metric" \
    --stat "$stat" \
    --region us-east-1
    
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ Deleted successfully"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Failed to delete"
  fi
done

echo "Verifying deletion..."
REMAINING=$(aws cloudwatch describe-anomaly-detectors \
  --namespace "Custom/Django" \
  --query 'length(AnomalyDetectors)' \
  --output text)

echo "Remaining detectors: $REMAINING"
    Deleting 5 anomaly detectors... Deleting: SkillsQueryResponseTime in Custom/Django (Average) ✓ Deleted successfully Deleting: UserLoginFailureRate in Custom/Django/Auth (Sum) ✓ Deleted successfully Deleting: AuthorPostCreationRate in Custom/Django (Average) ✓ Deleted successfully Deleting: LocationSearchLatency in Custom/Django (Average) ✓ Deleted successfully Deleting: JobPostDatabaseErrors in Custom/Django (Sum) ✓ Deleted successfully Verifying deletion... Remaining detectors: 0 Deletion Summary: ================================ Total Detectors Processed: 5 Successfully Deleted: 5 Failed Deletions: 0 Deleted Detectors Details: 1. SkillsQueryResponseTime - Monitored: Skills model query performance - Training data: 21 days - Anomalies found: 2 2. UserLoginFailureRate - Monitored: Authentication failures - Training data: 30 days - Anomalies found: 3 3. AuthorPostCreationRate - Monitored: Content creation patterns - Training data: 14 days - Anomalies found: 1 4. LocationSearchLatency - Monitored: Location query speed - Training data: 14 days - Anomalies found: 0 5. JobPostDatabaseErrors - Monitored: Database operation failures - Training data: 30 days - Anomalies found: 4 ✓ All anomaly detectors removed from CloudWatch ⚠ Manual monitoring required for all metrics
    # Django QuerySet - Comprehensive model monitoring post-cleanup from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Avg, Max, Min, Q from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta import time print("Django Model Monitoring (Post-Anomaly Detection Cleanup)") print("="*60) # 1. Skills Performance print("\n1. Skills Model Metrics:") start = time.time() skills_count = Skills.objects.count() query_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000 skills_in_posts = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct().count() print(f" Query time: {query_time:.2f}ms") print(f" Total skills: {skills_count}") print(f" Skills in use: {skills_in_posts}") print(f" Status: {'✓ Normal' if query_time < 100 else '⚠ Slow'}") # Output: # Query time: 1.89ms # Total skills: 0 # Skills in use: 0 # Status: ✓ Normal # 2. User Authentication print("\n2. User Model Metrics:") total_users = User.objects.count() active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() recent_logins = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) ).count() print(f" Total users: {total_users}") print(f" Active users: {active_users}") print(f" Recent logins: {recent_logins}") print(f" Login failure rate: N/A (no tracking)") # Output: # Total users: 0 # Active users: 0 # Recent logins: 0 # Login failure rate: N/A (no tracking) # 3. Author Activity print("\n3. Author Model Metrics:") total_authors = Author.objects.count() active_authors = Author.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(days=7) ).distinct().count() posts_last_week = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(days=7) ).count() print(f" Total authors: {total_authors}") print(f" Active authors (7d): {active_authors}") print(f" Posts created (7d): {posts_last_week}") if active_authors > 0: print(f" Avg posts per author: {posts_last_week / active_authors:.1f}") else: print(f" Avg posts per author: N/A") # Output: # Total authors: 0 # Active authors (7d): 0 # Posts created (7d): 0 # Avg posts per author: N/A # 4. Location Performance print("\n4. Location Model Metrics:") start = time.time() location_count = Location.objects.count() location_query_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000 locations_with_posts = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct().count() print(f" Query time: {location_query_time:.2f}ms") print(f" Total locations: {location_count}") print(f" Locations with jobs: {locations_with_posts}") print(f" Status: {'✓ Normal' if location_query_time < 100 else '⚠ Slow'}") # Output: # Query time: 1.45ms # Total locations: 0 # Locations with jobs: 0 # Status: ✓ Normal # 5. JobPost Operations print("\n5. JobPost Model Metrics:") total_posts = JobPost.objects.count() published_posts = JobPost.objects.filter(status='published').count() draft_posts = JobPost.objects.filter(status='draft').count() print(f" Total posts: {total_posts}") print(f" Published: {published_posts}") print(f" Drafts: {draft_posts}") print(f" Database errors: 0 (no tracking)") # Output: # Total posts: 0 # Published: 0 # Drafts: 0 # Database errors: 0 (no tracking) print("\n" + "="*60) print("MONITORING STATUS SUMMARY") print("="*60) print("✓ All Django models operational") print("✗ CloudWatch anomaly detection disabled") print("⚠ Manual monitoring required") print("\nRecommended Actions:") print(" 1. Implement static threshold alarms") print(" 2. Set up application-level logging") print(" 3. Configure Django middleware for metrics") print(" 4. Enable database query monitoring") print(" 5. Set up health check endpoints")
    Example 4: Delete Anomaly Detector with JSON Input File

    Remove anomaly detector using JSON configuration file for Author content creation metrics. This approach is useful when detector configuration is complex or needs to be version-controlled in your Django application infrastructure.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create JSON configuration for detector deletion
cat > delete-detector-config.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Namespace": "Custom/Django/Content",
  "MetricName": "AuthorContentCreationRate",
  "Stat": "Sum",
  "Dimensions": [
    {
      "Name": "Model",
      "Value": "Author"
    },
    {
      "Name": "Action",
      "Value": "create_jobpost"
    },
    {
      "Name": "Environment",
      "Value": "production"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

# Delete using JSON file
aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
  --cli-input-json file://delete-detector-config.json \
  --region us-east-1

echo "Detector deleted using JSON configuration"

# Clean up
rm delete-detector-config.json
    ✓ Detector deleted using JSON configuration JSON Configuration: { "Namespace": "Custom/Django/Content", "MetricName": "AuthorContentCreationRate", "Stat": "Sum", "Dimensions": [ { "Name": "Model", "Value": "Author" }, { "Name": "Action", "Value": "create_jobpost" }, { "Name": "Environment", "Value": "production" } ] } Detector Details (Before Deletion): - Model: Author - Metric: Content creation rate per hour - Training Period: 21 days - Baseline: 5-15 JobPosts/hour - Anomalies Detected: 2 * 2026-01-05: Spike to 47 posts/hour (campaign launch) * 2026-01-09: Drop to 1 post/hour (holiday) Dimension Filters: - Model=Author: Specific to Author operations - Action=create_jobpost: Only creation events - Environment=production: Production metrics only HTTP Status: 200 OK ✓ Configuration file processed successfully ✓ Detector removed from CloudWatch
    # Django QuerySet - Author content creation analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Q from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta import json print("Author Content Creation Analysis") print("="*60) # Load detector configuration for reference detector_config = { "Namespace": "Custom/Django/Content", "MetricName": "AuthorContentCreationRate", "Dimensions": { "Model": "Author", "Action": "create_jobpost", "Environment": "production" } } print(f"\nDeleted Detector Configuration:") print(f" Namespace: {detector_config['Namespace']}") print(f" Metric: {detector_config['MetricName']}") print(f" Monitoring: {detector_config['Dimensions']['Model']} model") # Analyze Author content creation patterns last_24h = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=24) posts_last_24h = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=last_24h, author__isnull=False ) posts_per_hour = posts_last_24h.count() / 24 print(f"\nContent Creation Metrics (Last 24 Hours):") print(f" Total posts created: {posts_last_24h.count()}") print(f" Average posts/hour: {posts_per_hour:.1f}") print(f" Status: {'✓ Normal' if 5 <= posts_per_hour <= 15 else '⚠ Outside baseline'}") # Output: # Total posts created: 0 # Average posts/hour: 0.0 # Status: ⚠ Outside baseline # Top contributing authors top_authors = Author.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=last_24h ).annotate( post_count=Count('jobpost') ).order_by('-post_count')[:5] print(f"\nTop Authors (Last 24 Hours):") for author in top_authors: print(f" - {author.user.username}: {author.post_count} posts") if not top_authors.exists(): print(" (No recent activity)") # Output: (No recent activity) # Content quality metrics posts_with_skills = posts_last_24h.filter( skills__isnull=False ).distinct().count() posts_with_location = posts_last_24h.filter( location__isnull=False ).count() print(f"\nContent Quality:") print(f" Posts with skills: {posts_with_skills}") print(f" Posts with location: {posts_with_location}") if posts_last_24h.count() > 0: skills_percentage = (posts_with_skills / posts_last_24h.count()) * 100 location_percentage = (posts_with_location / posts_last_24h.count()) * 100 print(f" Skills coverage: {skills_percentage:.1f}%") print(f" Location coverage: {location_percentage:.1f}%") else: print(" Skills coverage: N/A") print(" Location coverage: N/A") # Output: # Posts with skills: 0 # Posts with location: 0 # Skills coverage: N/A # Location coverage: N/A # User engagement with author content authors_with_users = Author.objects.filter( user__is_active=True, jobpost__created_at__gte=last_24h ).distinct().count() print(f"\nAuthor Engagement:") print(f" Active authors: {authors_with_users}") print(f" Total registered authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Output: # Active authors: 0 # Total registered authors: 0 print(f"\n⚠ Anomaly Detection Status:") print(f" CloudWatch ML Detection: Disabled") print(f" Manual baseline: 5-15 posts/hour") print(f" Current rate: {posts_per_hour:.1f} posts/hour") print(f" → Consider static threshold alarm") print(f" → Alert if rate < 3 or > 20 posts/hour")
    Example 5: Delete Anomaly Detector and Verify with Describe Command

    Remove Location search latency anomaly detector and verify deletion by attempting to describe it. Demonstrates complete cleanup workflow for JobPost location-based search features in Django application.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete Location search latency anomaly detector with verification

NAMESPACE="Custom/Django/Search"
METRIC="LocationSearchLatency"
STAT="p99"

echo "Step 1: Check if detector exists..."
DETECTOR_EXISTS=$(aws cloudwatch describe-anomaly-detectors \
  --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
  --metric-name "$METRIC" \
  --query 'length(AnomalyDetectors)' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Found $DETECTOR_EXISTS detector(s)"

if [ "$DETECTOR_EXISTS" -gt "0" ]; then
  echo "Step 2: Deleting detector..."
  aws cloudwatch delete-anomaly-detector \
    --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
    --metric-name "$METRIC" \
    --stat "$STAT" \
    --dimensions Name=Model,Value=Location Name=Operation,Value=search \
    --region us-east-1
    
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ Deletion successful"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Deletion failed"
    exit 1
  fi
else
  echo "  ℹ Detector does not exist or already deleted"
fi

echo "Step 3: Verifying deletion..."
sleep 2

VERIFY=$(aws cloudwatch describe-anomaly-detectors \
  --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
  --metric-name "$METRIC" \
  --query 'length(AnomalyDetectors)' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

if [ "$VERIFY" -eq "0" ]; then
  echo "  ✓ Deletion confirmed - detector not found"
else
  echo "  ✗ Detector still exists (unexpected)"
fi

echo "Step 4: Cleanup complete"
    Step 1: Check if detector exists... Found 1 detector(s) Detector Configuration: - Namespace: Custom/Django/Search - Metric: LocationSearchLatency - Statistic: p99 (99th percentile) - Dimensions: * Model: Location * Operation: search - State: TRAINED - Training Data Points: 2,016 (14 days × 144 periods/day) Step 2: Deleting detector... ✓ Deletion successful Step 3: Verifying deletion... ✓ Deletion confirmed - detector not found Verification Details: { "AnomalyDetectors": [] } Step 4: Cleanup complete Historical Performance: - Baseline p99: 45-65ms - Anomalies detected: 2 * 2026-01-07 14:30: 312ms (database index issue) * 2026-01-11 09:15: 198ms (high concurrent searches) Impact Assessment: - Total searches monitored: ~50,000 - Anomaly rate: 0.004% - Manual threshold recommended: p99 > 100ms ✓ Location search anomaly detection removed ⚠ Configure static alarm for p99 latency
    # Django QuerySet - Location search performance analysis from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Q, Prefetch from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta import time print("Location Search Performance Analysis") print("="*60) # Simulate location search queries search_terms = ['New York', 'San Francisco', 'London', 'Remote'] search_results = {} print("\nLocation Search Tests:") for term in search_terms: start = time.time() results = Location.objects.filter( Q(city__icontains=term) | Q(country__icontains=term) | Q(state__icontains=term) ).prefetch_related( Prefetch('jobpost_set', queryset=JobPost.objects.filter(status='published')) )[:20] query_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000 search_results[term] = { 'count': results.count(), 'time_ms': query_time } print(f" '{term}':") print(f" Results: {results.count()}") print(f" Time: {query_time:.2f}ms") print(f" Status: {'✓ Fast' if query_time < 100 else '⚠ Slow'}") # Output: # 'New York': # Results: 0 # Time: 2.15ms # Status: ✓ Fast # 'San Francisco': # Results: 0 # Time: 1.98ms # Status: ✓ Fast # ... etc # Calculate percentiles times = [r['time_ms'] for r in search_results.values()] if times: times_sorted = sorted(times) p50_index = len(times_sorted) // 2 p99_index = int(len(times_sorted) * 0.99) print(f"\nPercentile Analysis:") print(f" p50 (median): {times_sorted[p50_index]:.2f}ms") print(f" p99: {times_sorted[p99_index]:.2f}ms") print(f" Max: {max(times):.2f}ms") else: print("\nPercentile Analysis: N/A (no data)") # Location-JobPost relationship locations_with_jobs = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__status='published' ).annotate( job_count=Count('jobpost') ).order_by('-job_count')[:10] print(f"\nTop Locations by JobPost Count:") for loc in locations_with_jobs: print(f" {loc.city}, {loc.country}: {loc.job_count} jobs") if not locations_with_jobs.exists(): print(" (No locations with jobs)") # Output: (No locations with jobs) # Geographic distribution total_locations = Location.objects.count() locations_with_coords = Location.objects.exclude( Q(latitude__isnull=True) | Q(longitude__isnull=True) ).count() print(f"\nGeographic Data:") print(f" Total locations: {total_locations}") print(f" Geocoded locations: {locations_with_coords}") if total_locations > 0: geocoding_rate = (locations_with_coords / total_locations) * 100 print(f" Geocoding coverage: {geocoding_rate:.1f}%") else: print(" Geocoding coverage: N/A") # Output: # Total locations: 0 # Geocoded locations: 0 # Geocoding coverage: N/A # Authors and Users by location authors_by_location = Author.objects.values( 'location__city', 'location__country' ).annotate( author_count=Count('id') ).order_by('-author_count')[:5] print(f"\nAuthors by Location:") for loc_data in authors_by_location: city = loc_data['location__city'] or 'Unknown' country = loc_data['location__country'] or 'Unknown' print(f" {city}, {country}: {loc_data['author_count']} authors") if not authors_by_location.exists(): print(" (No location data)") # Output: (No location data) print(f"\n⚠ Performance Monitoring:") print(f" Anomaly Detection: Disabled") print(f" Current p99: {times_sorted[p99_index]:.2f}ms" if times else " Current p99: N/A") print(f" Recommended threshold: p99 > 100ms") print(f" Status: {'✓ Within normal range' if not times or max(times) < 100 else '⚠ Review required'}")

    delete-dashboards

    Deletes CloudWatch dashboards from your account. This command removes visualization dashboards that display Django jobpost application metrics, cleaning up dashboards monitoring Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model performance when no longer needed.

    Syntax
    aws cloudwatch delete-dashboards \
  --dashboard-names {dashboard-name} [{dashboard-name}...] \
  [--cli-input-json {json-file}] \
  [--generate-cli-skeleton {skeleton-format}]
    Parameters Description
    • --dashboard-names (list, required): Names of dashboards to delete. Can specify multiple dashboard names.
    • --cli-input-json (string, optional): Reads arguments from JSON file
    • --generate-cli-skeleton (string, optional): Generates JSON skeleton for input
    Attributes
    • DashboardName (string): Unique identifier for the CloudWatch dashboard
    • DashboardArn (string): Amazon Resource Name for the dashboard
    • DashboardBody (JSON): Widget configuration defining metrics and visualizations
    • Size (integer): Dashboard body size in bytes
    • LastModified (timestamp): Last modification timestamp
    Methods

    DeleteDashboards - Removes one or more CloudWatch dashboards permanently from account

    Syntax
    aws cloudwatch delete-dashboards \
  --dashboard-names {dashboard-name-1} {dashboard-name-2}
    Parameters
    • DashboardNames (list, required): Array of dashboard names to delete (1-100 dashboards per request)
    Return Value

    No output returned on successful deletion. HTTP 200 status indicates success. Deleted dashboards cannot be recovered.

    5 Detailed CloudWatch delete-dashboards Examples
    Example 1: Delete Single Django Models Monitoring Dashboard

    Remove the primary monitoring dashboard for Django jobpost application. This dashboard contains widgets tracking Skills queries, User authentication, Author activity, Location searches, and JobPost creation metrics across your production environment.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete main Django monitoring dashboard
aws cloudwatch delete-dashboards \
  --dashboard-names "Django-JobPost-Production-Metrics" \
  --region us-east-1

echo "Dashboard deleted successfully"
    ✓ Dashboard deleted successfully Dashboard Details (Before Deletion): - Name: Django-JobPost-Production-Metrics - Created: 2025-12-15 10:30:45 UTC - Last Modified: 2026-01-12 14:22:13 UTC - Size: 15,234 bytes - Widgets: 12 Widget Summary: 1. Skills Query Performance (Line Chart) - Metrics: Average response time, p99 latency 2. User Authentication Rate (Metric Display) - Successful logins, failed attempts 3. Author Content Creation (Bar Chart) - JobPosts created per hour by author 4. Location Search Latency (Heatmap) - Search performance by region 5. JobPost Database Operations (Stacked Area) - Reads, writes, updates, deletes 6. Skills Model Error Rate (Alarm Widget) - Database errors, validation failures 7. User Session Duration (Histogram) - Distribution of active sessions 8. Author Performance Comparison (Table) - Top 10 authors by activity 9. Location Geographic Distribution (Map Widget) - Jobs by location worldwide 10. JobPost Status Overview (Pie Chart) - Published, draft, archived distribution 11. System Health Composite (Number widgets) - Overall system metrics 12. Custom Annotations (Text widget) - Deployment notes and incidents HTTP Status: 200 OK ✓ Dashboard permanently removed
    # Django QuerySet - Dashboard metrics that were being monitored from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Avg, Max, Q from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta print("Metrics Previously Displayed on Deleted Dashboard") print("="*60) # 1. Skills Query Performance (Widget 1) print("\n1. Skills Model Metrics:") skills_total = Skills.objects.count() skills_used = Skills.objects.filter(jobpost__isnull=False).distinct().count() usage_rate = (skills_used / skills_total * 100) if skills_total > 0 else 0 print(f" Total skills: {skills_total}") print(f" Skills in use: {skills_used}") print(f" Usage rate: {usage_rate:.1f}%") # Output: # Total skills: 0 # Skills in use: 0 # Usage rate: 0.0% # 2. User Authentication (Widget 2) print("\n2. User Authentication Metrics:") last_hour = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=1) recent_logins = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=last_hour ).count() active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f" Recent logins (1h): {recent_logins}") print(f" Active users: {active_users}") print(f" Total users: {User.objects.count()}") # Output: # Recent logins (1h): 0 # Active users: 0 # Total users: 0 # 3. Author Content Creation (Widget 3) print("\n3. Author Content Creation:") last_24h = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=24) posts_created = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=last_24h ).count() active_authors = Author.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=last_24h ).distinct().count() print(f" Posts created (24h): {posts_created}") print(f" Active authors: {active_authors}") if active_authors > 0: print(f" Avg posts per author: {posts_created / active_authors:.1f}") else: print(f" Avg posts per author: N/A") # Output: # Posts created (24h): 0 # Active authors: 0 # Avg posts per author: N/A # 4. Location Search Performance (Widget 4) print("\n4. Location Search Metrics:") total_locations = Location.objects.count() locations_with_jobs = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__status='published' ).distinct().count() print(f" Total locations: {total_locations}") print(f" Locations with jobs: {locations_with_jobs}") if total_locations > 0: coverage = (locations_with_jobs / total_locations) * 100 print(f" Job coverage: {coverage:.1f}%") else: print(f" Job coverage: N/A") # Output: # Total locations: 0 # Locations with jobs: 0 # Job coverage: N/A # 5. JobPost Database Operations (Widget 5) print("\n5. JobPost Metrics:") published = JobPost.objects.filter(status='published').count() draft = JobPost.objects.filter(status='draft').count() archived = JobPost.objects.filter(status='archived').count() print(f" Published: {published}") print(f" Draft: {draft}") print(f" Archived: {archived}") print(f" Total: {published + draft + archived}") # Output: # Published: 0 # Draft: 0 # Archived: 0 # Total: 0 # 6. Top Authors (Widget 8) print("\n6. Top Authors by Activity:") top_authors = Author.objects.annotate( post_count=Count('jobpost') ).order_by('-post_count')[:5] for idx, author in enumerate(top_authors, 1): username = author.user.username if author.user else 'Unknown' print(f" {idx}. {username}: {author.post_count} posts") if not top_authors.exists(): print(" (No authors)") # Output: (No authors) print(f"\n⚠ Dashboard Status:") print(f" Dashboard: Django-JobPost-Production-Metrics") print(f" Status: DELETED") print(f" Monitoring: Disabled") print(f" → Create new dashboard or use AWS Console for visualization") print(f" → Run manual queries for current metrics")
    Example 2: Delete Multiple Development and Testing Dashboards

    Clean up development, staging, and testing dashboards for Django application. Removes temporary monitoring dashboards used during feature development and QA testing of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost functionality.

    #!/bin/bash
# Delete multiple non-production dashboards
aws cloudwatch delete-dashboards \
  --dashboard-names \
    "Django-Dev-Skills-Testing" \
    "Django-Staging-UserAuth" \
    "Django-QA-LocationSearch" \
    "Django-Dev-JobPostMetrics" \
  --region us-east-1

# Verify deletion
echo "Verifying deletion..."
for dashboard in "Django-Dev-Skills-Testing" "Django-Staging-UserAuth" "Django-QA-LocationSearch" "Django-Dev-JobPostMetrics"; do
  RESULT=$(aws cloudwatch get-dashboard --dashboard-name "$dashboard" 2>&1)
  if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "ResourceNotFound"; then
    echo "  ✓ $dashboard deleted"
  else
    echo "  ✗ $dashboard still exists"
  fi
done
    ✓ Dashboards deleted successfully Deleted Dashboards: 1. Django-Dev-Skills-Testing - Environment: Development - Purpose: Skills model query optimization testing - Widgets: 5 (query latency, index usage, N+1 detection) - Created: 2026-01-05 - Last Used: 2026-01-10 2. Django-Staging-UserAuth - Environment: Staging - Purpose: User authentication flow testing - Widgets: 7 (login rate, failure tracking, session management) - Created: 2025-12-20 - Last Used: 2026-01-11 3. Django-QA-LocationSearch - Environment: QA - Purpose: Location-based search performance - Widgets: 4 (search latency, result accuracy, geocoding) - Created: 2026-01-08 - Last Used: 2026-01-12 4. Django-Dev-JobPostMetrics - Environment: Development - Purpose: JobPost CRUD operation monitoring - Widgets: 8 (create/read/update/delete metrics) - Created: 2026-01-03 - Last Used: 2026-01-09 Verifying deletion... ✓ Django-Dev-Skills-Testing deleted ✓ Django-Staging-UserAuth deleted ✓ Django-QA-LocationSearch deleted ✓ Django-Dev-JobPostMetrics deleted HTTP Status: 200 OK ✓ All 4 dashboards permanently removed ✓ Development and testing dashboards cleaned up
    # Django QuerySet - Multi-environment metric verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Q, Avg from django.utils import timezone print("Multi-Environment Metrics (Post-Dashboard Cleanup)") print("="*60) # Environment configuration environments = { 'development': {'db_name': 'jobpost_dev'}, 'staging': {'db_name': 'jobpost_staging'}, 'qa': {'db_name': 'jobpost_qa'}, 'production': {'db_name': 'jobpost_prod'} } print("\nDeleted Dashboard Environments:") for env, config in environments.items(): print(f" {env.capitalize()}: {config['db_name']}") # 1. Skills Testing Metrics (Dev Dashboard 1) print("\n1. Skills Model (Development Testing):") skills_with_posts = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct() print(f" Total skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f" Skills with jobs: {skills_with_posts.count()}") print(f" Empty skills: {Skills.objects.filter(jobpost__isnull=True).count()}") # Output: # Total skills: 0 # Skills with jobs: 0 # Empty skills: 0 # 2. User Authentication (Staging Dashboard 2) print("\n2. User Authentication (Staging):") total_users = User.objects.count() authenticated_users = User.objects.filter( is_active=True, last_login__isnull=False ).count() print(f" Total users: {total_users}") print(f" Authenticated: {authenticated_users}") print(f" Never logged in: {total_users - authenticated_users}") # Output: # Total users: 0 # Authenticated: 0 # Never logged in: 0 # 3. Location Search (QA Dashboard 3) print("\n3. Location Search (QA Testing):") locations_geocoded = Location.objects.exclude( Q(latitude__isnull=True) | Q(longitude__isnull=True) ).count() locations_with_jobs = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__status='published' ).distinct().count() print(f" Total locations: {Location.objects.count()}") print(f" Geocoded: {locations_geocoded}") print(f" With active jobs: {locations_with_jobs}") # Output: # Total locations: 0 # Geocoded: 0 # With active jobs: 0 # 4. JobPost CRUD Operations (Dev Dashboard 4) print("\n4. JobPost Operations (Development):") post_stats = { 'published': JobPost.objects.filter(status='published').count(), 'draft': JobPost.objects.filter(status='draft').count(), 'archived': JobPost.objects.filter(status='archived').count() } print(f" Published: {post_stats['published']}") print(f" Draft: {post_stats['draft']}") print(f" Archived: {post_stats['archived']}") print(f" Total: {sum(post_stats.values())}") # Output: # Published: 0 # Draft: 0 # Archived: 0 # Total: 0 # Cross-model relationships print("\n5. Cross-Model Relationships:") authors_with_posts = Author.objects.filter( jobpost__isnull=False ).distinct().count() posts_with_skills = JobPost.objects.filter( skills__isnull=False ).distinct().count() posts_with_location = JobPost.objects.filter( location__isnull=False ).count() print(f" Authors with content: {authors_with_posts}") print(f" Posts with skills: {posts_with_skills}") print(f" Posts with location: {posts_with_location}") # Output: # Authors with content: 0 # Posts with skills: 0 # Posts with location: 0 print(f"\n⚠ Dashboard Cleanup Summary:") print(f" Deleted: 4 dashboards") print(f" Environments affected: Dev, Staging, QA") print(f" Production dashboard: Retained") print(f" → Use production dashboard for ongoing monitoring") print(f" → Recreate dev/staging dashboards if needed for new features")
    Example 3: Delete Dashboard with JSON Input Configuration

    Remove CloudWatch dashboard using JSON configuration file for Author performance monitoring. This approach is useful for infrastructure-as-code deployments and version-controlled dashboard lifecycle management in Django jobpost application.

    #!/bin/bash
# Create JSON configuration for dashboard deletion
cat > delete-dashboard.json << 'EOF'
{
  "DashboardNames": [
    "Django-Author-Performance-2026Q1"
  ]
}
EOF

# Delete using JSON configuration
aws cloudwatch delete-dashboards \
  --cli-input-json file://delete-dashboard.json \
  --region us-east-1

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "Dashboard deleted via JSON configuration"
  
  # Log deletion to audit file
  cat >> dashboard-audit.log << LOG
$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - DELETED: Django-Author-Performance-2026Q1
User: $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query 'Arn' --output text)
Reason: End of Q1 2026 reporting period
LOG
  
  echo "Deletion logged to dashboard-audit.log"
fi

# Cleanup
rm delete-dashboard.json
    ✓ Dashboard deleted via JSON configuration Dashboard Details: { "DashboardName": "Django-Author-Performance-2026Q1", "DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudwatch:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/Django-Author-Performance-2026Q1", "Size": 12847, "LastModified": "2026-01-15T09:45:22.000Z" } Widget Configuration (Before Deletion): - Total Widgets: 9 Widget Breakdown: 1. Author Registration Rate - Metric: Count of new Author objects per day 2. Content Creation Velocity - Metric: JobPosts created per Author per week 3. Author User Linkage Status - Metrics: Authors with/without User accounts 4. Top Contributing Authors - Table: Top 20 authors by JobPost count 5. Author Activity Heatmap - Visualization: Post creation by hour of day 6. Author Engagement Score - Custom Metric: Composite engagement calculation 7. Author-Location Distribution - Geographic: Authors by Location 8. Skills Coverage by Author - Metric: Avg skills per JobPost by author 9. Author Retention Metrics - Trend: Active vs inactive authors over time Audit Log Entry: 2026-01-15 14:23:45 - DELETED: Django-Author-Performance-2026Q1 User: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/django-admin Reason: End of Q1 2026 reporting period ✓ Deletion logged to dashboard-audit.log HTTP Status: 200 OK
    # Django QuerySet - Author performance metrics from deleted dashboard from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count, Avg, Q, Max, Min from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta import json print("Author Performance Metrics (Q1 2026 Dashboard - Deleted)") print("="*60) # Load deleted dashboard config dashboard_config = { "name": "Django-Author-Performance-2026Q1", "period": "Q1 2026", "deleted": "2026-01-15", "widgets": 9 } print(f"\nDashboard Info:") print(f" Name: {dashboard_config['name']}") print(f" Period: {dashboard_config['period']}") print(f" Status: Deleted on {dashboard_config['deleted']}") # 1. Author Registration Rate (Widget 1) print("\n1. Author Registration:") q1_start = timezone.datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc) q1_end = timezone.datetime(2026, 3, 31, tzinfo=timezone.utc) authors_q1 = Author.objects.filter( created_at__gte=q1_start, created_at__lte=q1_end ).count() if hasattr(Author, 'created_at') else 0 print(f" New authors (Q1): {authors_q1}") print(f" Total authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Output: # New authors (Q1): 0 # Total authors: 0 # 2. Content Creation Velocity (Widget 2) print("\n2. Content Creation Velocity:") authors_with_posts = Author.objects.annotate( post_count=Count('jobpost'), posts_last_week=Count( 'jobpost', filter=Q(jobpost__created_at__gte=timezone.now() - timedelta(weeks=1)) ) ).filter(post_count__gt=0) if authors_with_posts.exists(): avg_posts = authors_with_posts.aggregate( avg=Avg('post_count') )['avg'] print(f" Avg posts per author: {avg_posts:.1f}") else: print(f" Avg posts per author: N/A") total_posts = JobPost.objects.count() print(f" Total JobPosts: {total_posts}") # Output: # Avg posts per author: N/A # Total JobPosts: 0 # 3. Author-User Linkage (Widget 3) print("\n3. Author-User Linkage:") authors_with_users = Author.objects.filter(user__isnull=False).count() authors_without_users = Author.objects.filter(user__isnull=True).count() print(f" Authors with accounts: {authors_with_users}") print(f" Authors without accounts: {authors_without_users}") if (authors_with_users + authors_without_users) > 0: linkage_rate = (authors_with_users / (authors_with_users + authors_without_users)) * 100 print(f" Linkage rate: {linkage_rate:.1f}%") else: print(f" Linkage rate: N/A") # Output: # Authors with accounts: 0 # Authors without accounts: 0 # Linkage rate: N/A # 4. Top Contributing Authors (Widget 4) print("\n4. Top Authors (Q1 2026):") top_authors = Author.objects.annotate( post_count=Count('jobpost') ).filter(post_count__gt=0).order_by('-post_count')[:5] for idx, author in enumerate(top_authors, 1): username = author.user.username if author.user else 'No account' print(f" {idx}. {username}: {author.post_count} posts") if not top_authors.exists(): print(" (No active authors)") # Output: (No active authors) # 5. Skills Coverage by Author (Widget 8) print("\n5. Skills Coverage:") posts_with_skills = JobPost.objects.filter( skills__isnull=False ).distinct() if posts_with_skills.exists(): avg_skills = posts_with_skills.annotate( skill_count=Count('skills') ).aggregate(avg=Avg('skill_count'))['avg'] print(f" Avg skills per post: {avg_skills:.1f}") else: print(f" Avg skills per post: N/A") print(f" Posts with skills: {posts_with_skills.count()}") # Output: # Avg skills per post: N/A # Posts with skills: 0 # 6. Author-Location Distribution (Widget 7) print("\n6. Geographic Distribution:") authors_by_location = Author.objects.filter( location__isnull=False ).values( 'location__city', 'location__country' ).annotate(count=Count('id')).order_by('-count')[:3] for loc in authors_by_location: city = loc['location__city'] or 'Unknown' country = loc['location__country'] or 'Unknown' print(f" {city}, {country}: {loc['count']} authors") if not authors_by_location.exists(): print(" (No location data)") # Output: (No location data) print(f"\n⚠ Dashboard Status:") print(f" Q1 2026 reporting dashboard deleted") print(f" Historical data: Still available in CloudWatch Metrics") print(f" → Create Q2 2026 dashboard for continued monitoring") print(f" → Export Q1 metrics before creating new dashboard")
    Example 4: Delete All Dashboards with Name Pattern Matching

    Bulk delete all temporary and archived CloudWatch dashboards for Django jobpost application. Identifies and removes dashboards matching specific naming patterns for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost monitoring across different time periods.

    #!/bin/bash
# List and delete all temporary/archived dashboards

echo "Finding dashboards to delete..."

# Get all dashboard names matching patterns
TEMP_DASHBOARDS=$(aws cloudwatch list-dashboards \
  --query 'DashboardEntries[?starts_with(DashboardName, `Django-Temp-`) || starts_with(DashboardName, `Django-Archive-`)].DashboardName' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

if [ -z "$TEMP_DASHBOARDS" ]; then
  echo "No matching dashboards found"
  exit 0
fi

# Convert to array
DASHBOARD_ARRAY=($TEMP_DASHBOARDS)
echo "Found ${#DASHBOARD_ARRAY[@]} dashboards to delete:"
printf '  - %s\n' "${DASHBOARD_ARRAY[@]}"

# Delete in batches of 10 (AWS limit per request)
BATCH_SIZE=10
for ((i=0; i<${#DASHBOARD_ARRAY[@]}; i+=BATCH_SIZE)); do
  BATCH=("${DASHBOARD_ARRAY[@]:i:BATCH_SIZE}")
  
  echo "Deleting batch $(($i/$BATCH_SIZE + 1))..."
  aws cloudwatch delete-dashboards \
    --dashboard-names "${BATCH[@]}" \
    --region us-east-1
    
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ Batch deleted successfully"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Batch deletion failed"
  fi
  
  sleep 1  # Rate limiting
done

echo "✓ All matching dashboards deleted"
    Finding dashboards to delete... Query Results: DashboardEntries matching patterns: - Django-Temp-Skills-Debug-20260110 - Django-Temp-UserAuth-Test-20260112 - Django-Temp-LocationGeo-20260108 - Django-Archive-2025Q4-AuthorMetrics - Django-Archive-2025Q3-JobPostStats - Django-Temp-SkillsOptimization - Django-Archive-2025Q2-AllModels Found 7 dashboards to delete: - Django-Temp-Skills-Debug-20260110 - Django-Temp-UserAuth-Test-20260112 - Django-Temp-LocationGeo-20260108 - Django-Archive-2025Q4-AuthorMetrics - Django-Archive-2025Q3-JobPostStats - Django-Temp-SkillsOptimization - Django-Archive-2025Q2-AllModels Deleting batch 1... Dashboards in batch: 1. Django-Temp-Skills-Debug-20260110 - Purpose: Skills query debugging - Created: 2026-01-10 - Size: 3,421 bytes 2. Django-Temp-UserAuth-Test-20260112 - Purpose: Authentication testing - Created: 2026-01-12 - Size: 2,876 bytes 3. Django-Temp-LocationGeo-20260108 - Purpose: Geocoding validation - Created: 2026-01-08 - Size: 4,102 bytes 4. Django-Archive-2025Q4-AuthorMetrics - Purpose: Q4 2025 reporting - Created: 2025-10-01 - Size: 15,234 bytes 5.
